Skip to main content

Files

streamarchiver/
ui.rs

1//! The on-demand egui window: channel table, add/edit form, and settings.
2//!
3//! Runs reactive (repaints only on input/events). The tray thread wakes it via
4//! `Context::request_repaint`. Closing the window hides it to the tray; the
5//! tray "Quit" item triggers a real close.
6
7use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
8use std::fs::File;
9use std::io::{BufRead, BufReader};
10use std::path::Path;
11use std::sync::mpsc::Receiver;
12use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, AtomicU64, Ordering};
13use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
14
15use eframe::egui;
16use egui_extras::{Column, TableBuilder};
17use tracing::{debug, warn};
18use tray_icon::TrayIcon;
19
20use crate::app_core::AppCore;
21use crate::events::{ManualCommand, UiCommand};
22use crate::models::{
23    AdBreak, AuthKind, Channel, Container, DetectionMethod, DownloadDefaults, GlobalStats,
24    K_DIALOG_ICON, K_DISCORD_SCHEDULE, K_DISCORD_TOKEN, K_FILENAME_MEDIA, K_MONITOR_DEFAULTS,
25    K_OCR_COMMAND, K_OCR_EFFORT, K_OCR_FALLBACK_MODEL, K_OCR_MAX_BUDGET, K_OCR_MODEL,
26    K_OCR_OFFSET, K_OCR_STATS, K_OCR_TIMEOUT_SECS, K_OCR_TIMEZONE, K_SCHEDULE_TITLE_FILL,
27    K_YT_API_DETECT, K_YT_API_SCHEDULE, K_YT_COMMUNITY_MAX_POSTS, K_YT_API_QUOTA_CUTOFF, K_YT_SEARCH_QUOTA_CUTOFF,
28    K_REMUX_EMBED_THUMBNAIL, K_REMUX_EMBED_TITLE, K_REMUX_TITLE_TEMPLATE, K_REMUX_EMBED_SUBS,
29    K_FILE_SPLIT_ENABLED, K_FILE_SPLIT_VIDEOS, K_FILE_SPLIT_SUBS, K_FILE_SPLIT_CHAT,
30    K_FILE_SPLIT_THUMBS, K_FILE_SPLIT_LOGS,
31    MediaInfoMode, Monitor, MonitorDefaults, MonitorWithChannel, OcrStats, Platform, Recording,
32    ScheduleSegment, StreamGroup, StreamMetaChange, Tool, UpcomingStream, Video,
33    group_recordings, now_unix,
34};
35use crate::google_oauth;
36use crate::imports::{self, ImportCandidate};
37use crate::oauth::{self, AuthFlow};
38use crate::platform::AutoStart;
39use crate::schedule_source::{
40    ScheduleSourceKind, SourceEntry, load_channel_cfg, load_channel_scope, load_monitor_scope,
41    load_source_order, save_channel_cfg, save_channel_scope, save_monitor_scope, save_source_order,
42    source_badge,
43};
44
45const K_TWITCH_ID: &str = "twitch_client_id";
46const K_TWITCH_SECRET: &str = "twitch_client_secret";
47const K_YT_KEY: &str = "youtube_api_key";
48const K_KICK_ID: &str = "kick_client_id";
49const K_KICK_SECRET: &str = "kick_client_secret";
50const K_DEFAULT_OUT: &str = "default_output_dir";
51const K_MAX_CONCURRENT: &str = "max_concurrent_downloads";
52const K_DOWNLOAD_AUTH: &str = "download_auth_method";
53const K_COOKIES_BROWSER: &str = "cookies_browser";
54const K_YTDLP_ARGS: &str = "ytdlp_default_args";
55/// Optional explicit path to the system yt-dlp binary; empty ⇒ `yt-dlp` on PATH.
56const K_YTDLP_BINARY: &str = "ytdlp_binary_path";
57/// Path to the SABR dev-build yt-dlp; empty ⇒ SABR capture disabled.
58const K_SABR_BINARY: &str = "ytdlp_sabr_binary_path";
59/// Master toggle: use the SABR build for YouTube capture-from-start.
60const K_SABR_ENABLED: &str = "ytdlp_sabr_enabled";
61/// SABR format selector (e.g. `ba[protocol=sabr]+bv[protocol=sabr]`).
62const K_SABR_FORMAT: &str = "ytdlp_sabr_format";
63/// SABR `--extractor-args` value.
64const K_SABR_EXTRACTOR_ARGS: &str = "ytdlp_sabr_extractor_args";
65/// Manual raw SABR args; when non-empty, replaces the format+extractor-args preset.
66const K_SABR_RAW_ARGS: &str = "ytdlp_sabr_raw_args";
67/// PO-token-provider `--extractor-args` (e.g. bgutil), a separate `--extractor-args`
68/// entry on the SABR command. Absent ⇒ bgutil default; explicit empty ⇒ disabled.
69const K_SABR_POT_ARGS: &str = "ytdlp_sabr_pot_args";
70/// Experimental: append `enable_live_deep_rewind=true` to the SABR extractor-args
71/// (rewinds past the normal DVR window; dev-build-only). Absent ⇒ off.
72const K_SABR_DEEP_REWIND: &str = "ytdlp_sabr_deep_rewind";
73/// DASH-companion format selector for dual capture.
74const K_DASH_FORMAT: &str = "ytdlp_dash_format";
75const K_WEBSUB_URL: &str = "websub_vps_url";
76const K_WEBSUB_TOKEN: &str = "websub_token";
77const K_WEBSUB_POLL: &str = "websub_poll_secs";
78/// Whether Streams rows get a status background tint (recording / ad / error).
79const K_STATUS_BGCOLOR: &str = "status_bgcolor";
80/// How dates/timestamps are formatted throughout the UI (see [`DateFmt`]).
81const K_DATE_FORMAT: &str = "date_format";
82/// Whether the per-row Actions column is shown (the row context menu has the same
83/// actions, so it can be hidden to reclaim width).
84const K_SHOW_ACTIONS: &str = "show_actions";
85/// Whether timestamp columns use the compact short format (off = full datetime).
86const K_SHORT_TIMESTAMPS: &str = "short_timestamps";
87/// chrono format pattern used for the compact timestamp display; default `%d/%m %H:%M`.
88const K_SHORT_TS_FMT: &str = "short_ts_fmt";
89/// Whether chat-replay emote codes render as inline images (off ⇒ show the code
90/// text). Default on; only an explicit `"0"` disables. Needs "Fetch chat assets".
91const K_RENDER_EMOTES: &str = "render_emotes_in_chat";
92/// Whether animated emotes play (off ⇒ a static first frame). Default on; only an
93/// explicit `"0"` disables. Off is the perf/RAM escape hatch for heavy channels.
94const K_ANIMATE_EMOTES: &str = "animate_emotes_in_chat";
95/// Path to the media player binary used by "Stream in player" on recording rows.
96const K_MEDIA_PLAYER: &str = "media_player_path";
97
98/// Browsers yt-dlp can read cookies from (for the Settings dropdown).
99const COOKIE_BROWSERS: [&str; 8] = [
100    "firefox", "chrome", "chromium", "edge", "brave", "opera", "vivaldi", "safari",
101];
102
103#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
104enum View {
105    Streams,
106    Videos,
107    Schedule,
108    Background,
109    Settings,
110    Stats,
111    Debug,
112}
113
114/// The Schedule tab's calendar granularity.
115#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
116enum ScheduleMode {
117    Month,
118    Week,
119    Day,
120    Agenda,
121}
122
123/// State of the on-demand "List formats" probe (Videos tab), shown in a window.
124#[derive(Clone)]
125enum FormatProbe {
126    Idle,
127    Running,
128    Done(String),
129    Failed(String),
130}
131
132/// A self-mutating action picked from a video row's context menu (open/copy are
133/// handled inline; these need deferred access to `self`).
134enum VideoMenuChoice {
135    Stop,
136    Retry,
137    Delete,
138}
139
140/// Backing state for the create/rename channel-container dialog.
141struct ChannelForm {
142    /// `Some(id)` = renaming an existing channel; `None` = creating a new one.
143    id: Option<i64>,
144    name: String,
145    /// Hex color string (e.g. `"#ff9800"` or `"ff9800"`). Empty = auto palette.
146    color: String,
147}
148
149/// Backing state for the add/edit dialog. `name` is the channel (container) name;
150/// `url` is this *instance's* source URL (the platform is derived from it).
151struct MonitorForm {
152    monitor_id: Option<i64>,
153    channel_id: Option<i64>,
154    name: String,
155    url: String,
156    tool: Tool,
157    detection_method: DetectionMethod,
158    poll_interval_secs: i64,
159    quality: String,
160    output_dir: String,
161    filename_template: String,
162    container: Container,
163    capture_from_start: bool,
164    /// YouTube dual capture: also run a DASH companion process (system yt-dlp).
165    dual_capture: bool,
166    /// Manually mark this instance ad-free (member/sub/Turbo) — drives the Ad-free
167    /// column when auto detection isn't available.
168    ad_free: bool,
169    enabled: bool,
170    auth_kind: AuthKind,
171    auth_value: String,
172    /// Audio tracks to capture (streamlink `--hls-audio-select`): empty = default,
173    /// `all`/`*` = every track, or a comma-separated list.
174    audio_tracks: String,
175    /// Subtitle tracks to capture (yt-dlp `--sub-langs`): empty = none, `all` =
176    /// every subtitle, or a comma-separated list of language codes.
177    subtitle_tracks: String,
178    /// Capture chat alongside the recording (Twitch IRC sidecar / yt-dlp live_chat).
179    chat_log: bool,
180    /// Download stream thumbnail at recording start (yt-dlp: --write-thumbnail;
181    /// Twitch/Kick/YouTube: fetch URL from detection metadata).
182    fetch_thumbnail: bool,
183    /// Use the stream thumbnail (when fetched) as the hero image in the
184    /// recording-started notification instead of the channel's static banner.
185    thumbnail_in_toast: bool,
186    /// Download channel icon, banner, badges, and emotes (Twitch: BTTV/FFZ/7TV too)
187    /// into channel_assets/ alongside recordings.
188    fetch_chat_assets: bool,
189    extra_args: String,
190    /// Platform the tool/detection defaults were last set for; a URL change to a
191    /// different platform re-applies that platform's defaults.
192    last_platform: Option<Platform>,
193}
194
195impl MonitorForm {
196    /// "Add stream": a new channel container + its first instance.
197    fn new_channel(defaults: &MonitorDefaults, default_output_dir: &str) -> MonitorForm {
198        // Use Generic platform as the starting point; once the user pastes a URL
199        // the platform-change handler re-resolves tool/detection/etc. for that platform.
200        let p = Platform::Generic;
201        MonitorForm {
202            monitor_id: None,
203            channel_id: None,
204            name: String::new(),
205            url: String::new(),
206            tool: defaults.resolve_tool(p),
207            detection_method: defaults.resolve_detection(p),
208            poll_interval_secs: defaults.resolve_poll_interval(p),
209            quality: defaults.resolve_quality(p),
210            output_dir: defaults.resolve_output_dir(p, default_output_dir),
211            filename_template: defaults.resolve_filename_template(p),
212            container: defaults.resolve_container(p),
213            capture_from_start: defaults.resolve_from_start(p),
214            dual_capture: false,
215            ad_free: false,
216            enabled: true,
217            auth_kind: AuthKind::Inherit,
218            auth_value: String::new(),
219            // New monitors default to max-archival: every audio + subtitle track,
220            // chat logging, thumbnails, and channel assets all on.
221            audio_tracks: "all".into(),
222            subtitle_tracks: "all".into(),
223            chat_log: true,
224            fetch_thumbnail: true,
225            thumbnail_in_toast: false,
226            fetch_chat_assets: true,
227            extra_args: String::new(),
228            last_platform: None,
229        }
230    }
231
232    fn from_existing(row: &MonitorWithChannel) -> MonitorForm {
233        let m = &row.monitor;
234        MonitorForm {
235            monitor_id: Some(m.id),
236            channel_id: Some(row.channel.id),
237            name: row.channel.name.clone(),
238            url: m.url.clone(),
239            tool: m.tool,
240            detection_method: m.detection_method,
241            poll_interval_secs: m.poll_interval_secs,
242            quality: m.quality.clone(),
243            output_dir: m.output_dir.clone(),
244            filename_template: m.filename_template.clone(),
245            container: m.container,
246            capture_from_start: m.capture_from_start,
247            dual_capture: m.dual_capture,
248            ad_free: m.ad_free,
249            enabled: m.enabled,
250            auth_kind: m.auth_kind,
251            auth_value: m.auth_value.clone(),
252            audio_tracks: m.audio_tracks.clone(),
253            subtitle_tracks: m.subtitle_tracks.clone(),
254            chat_log: m.chat_log,
255            fetch_thumbnail: m.fetch_thumbnail,
256            thumbnail_in_toast: m.thumbnail_in_toast,
257            fetch_chat_assets: m.fetch_chat_assets,
258            extra_args: m.extra_args.clone(),
259            // Don't override the saved tool/detection just because the form opened.
260            last_platform: Some(m.platform()),
261        }
262    }
263
264    /// Add another instance to an existing channel container. The URL is blank so
265    /// the user enters a (possibly different-platform) source.
266    fn add_instance(channel: &Channel, defaults: &MonitorDefaults, default_output_dir: &str) -> MonitorForm {
267        let p = Platform::Generic;
268        MonitorForm {
269            monitor_id: None,
270            channel_id: Some(channel.id),
271            name: channel.name.clone(),
272            url: String::new(),
273            tool: defaults.resolve_tool(p),
274            detection_method: defaults.resolve_detection(p),
275            poll_interval_secs: defaults.resolve_poll_interval(p),
276            quality: defaults.resolve_quality(p),
277            output_dir: defaults.resolve_output_dir(p, default_output_dir),
278            filename_template: defaults.resolve_filename_template(p),
279            container: defaults.resolve_container(p),
280            capture_from_start: defaults.resolve_from_start(p),
281            dual_capture: false,
282            ad_free: false,
283            enabled: true,
284            auth_kind: AuthKind::Inherit,
285            auth_value: String::new(),
286            // New monitors default to max-archival: every audio + subtitle track,
287            // chat logging, thumbnails, and channel assets all on.
288            audio_tracks: "all".into(),
289            subtitle_tracks: "all".into(),
290            chat_log: true,
291            fetch_thumbnail: true,
292            thumbnail_in_toast: false,
293            fetch_chat_assets: true,
294            extra_args: String::new(),
295            last_platform: None,
296        }
297    }
298}
299
300/// Backing state for the always-visible "download a video" form on the Videos tab.
301///
302/// Fields are pre-filled from the detected platform's saved defaults whenever the
303/// platform changes; the user can override any of them per download.
304struct VideoForm {
305    url: String,
306    title: String,
307    tool: Tool,
308    quality: String,
309    output_dir: String,
310    filename_template: String,
311    /// `None` = "Default (per-platform)": use the snapshotted platform-default
312    /// auth below. `Some(kind)` overrides it with `auth_value` for this download.
313    auth_override: Option<AuthKind>,
314    auth_value: String,
315    /// The platform default's auth, snapshotted at pre-fill (used when
316    /// `auth_override` is `None`) so every field resolves from one snapshot.
317    default_auth_kind: AuthKind,
318    default_auth_value: String,
319    extra_args: String,
320    /// Resolve and use the real stream/video title (sticky across downloads).
321    auto_title: bool,
322    /// Audio / subtitle track selection + chat logging (sticky across downloads,
323    /// like `auto_title` — not reset when the platform changes). See [`Video`].
324    audio_tracks: String,
325    subtitle_tracks: String,
326    chat_log: bool,
327    /// Platform the form is currently filled for; a change triggers a re-fill.
328    last_platform: Option<Platform>,
329}
330
331impl VideoForm {
332    fn new() -> VideoForm {
333        VideoForm {
334            url: String::new(),
335            title: String::new(),
336            tool: Tool::YtDlp,
337            quality: "best".into(),
338            output_dir: String::new(),
339            filename_template: "{name}_{date}_{time}".into(),
340            auth_override: None,
341            auth_value: String::new(),
342            default_auth_kind: AuthKind::Inherit,
343            default_auth_value: String::new(),
344            extra_args: String::new(),
345            auto_title: false,
346            // Archive all audio + subtitle tracks by default (matches new
347            // monitors); chat is the niche extra, opt-in per download.
348            audio_tracks: "all".into(),
349            subtitle_tracks: "all".into(),
350            chat_log: false,
351            last_platform: None,
352        }
353    }
354}
355
356#[derive(Default)]
357struct SettingsForm {
358    twitch_client_id: String,
359    twitch_client_secret: String,
360    /// Google OAuth client (TV/device type) for "Connect YouTube" → subscriptions
361    /// import. Separate from the YouTube Data API key (which can't read user data).
362    google_client_id: String,
363    google_client_secret: String,
364    youtube_api_key: String,
365    /// Per-operation opt-ins to use the YouTube Data API key instead of scraping
366    /// (each costs quota — see the Settings section).
367    youtube_api_detect: bool,
368    youtube_api_schedule: bool,
369    /// Daily quota cutoff for the YouTube Data API (units). Empty = default (9000).
370    youtube_api_quota_cutoff: String,
371    /// Daily search.list query cutoff (queries). Empty = default (90).
372    youtube_search_quota_cutoff: String,
373    kick_client_id: String,
374    kick_client_secret: String,
375    default_output_dir: String,
376    max_concurrent_downloads: String,
377    /// Global download-auth default: "none" or "cookies".
378    download_auth_method: String,
379    /// Browser to read cookies from (yt-dlp `--cookies-from-browser`).
380    cookies_browser: String,
381    /// Optional browser profile/session (the part after `browser:`).
382    cookies_profile: String,
383    /// YouTube WebSub VPS relay (yt-websub) — base URL, bearer token, poll secs.
384    websub_vps_url: String,
385    websub_token: String,
386    websub_poll_secs: String,
387    /// When to probe captures for the {resolution}/{fps}/… filename variables.
388    filename_media_info: MediaInfoMode,
389    /// How dates/timestamps are displayed throughout the UI.
390    date_fmt: DateFmt,
391    /// chrono format string used for the compact timestamp mode (K_SHORT_TS_FMT).
392    short_ts_fmt: String,
393    /// Global extra arguments prepended to every yt-dlp invocation (all monitors).
394    /// Per-monitor extra_args are appended after these, so they take precedence.
395    ytdlp_default_args: String,
396    /// Explicit path to the system yt-dlp binary; empty ⇒ `yt-dlp` on PATH.
397    ytdlp_binary_path: String,
398    /// Path to the SABR dev-build yt-dlp (for YouTube capture-from-start). Empty ⇒
399    /// SABR disabled (capture-from-start uses the system binary's normal path).
400    sabr_binary_path: String,
401    /// Master toggle: use the SABR build for YouTube capture-from-start.
402    sabr_enabled: bool,
403    /// SABR format selector + extractor-args preset.
404    sabr_format: String,
405    sabr_extractor_args: String,
406    /// Experimental deep-rewind toggle (appends enable_live_deep_rewind=true).
407    sabr_deep_rewind: bool,
408    /// Manual raw SABR args; non-empty overrides the format+extractor-args preset.
409    sabr_raw_args: String,
410    /// PO-token-provider `--extractor-args` (e.g. bgutil) for the SABR command.
411    sabr_pot_args: String,
412    /// DASH-companion format selector used by dual (SABR+DASH) capture.
413    dash_format: String,
414    /// Discord user token + whether to import stream schedules from Discord events
415    /// (opt-in; automating a user token is against Discord's ToS).
416    discord_token: String,
417    discord_schedule: bool,
418    /// Image→schedule OCR pipeline (shells out to an LLM CLI). `ocr_command` is the
419    /// executable; `ocr_model`/`ocr_fallback_model` the primary + retry models;
420    /// `ocr_timezone`/`ocr_offset` the timezone/UTC offset to assume for banner
421    /// times. Empty fields fall back to the built-in defaults.
422    ocr_command: String,
423    ocr_model: String,
424    ocr_fallback_model: String,
425    ocr_timezone: String,
426    ocr_offset: String,
427    /// Per-call USD budget cap passed as `--max-budget-usd` (empty = no cap).
428    ocr_max_budget: String,
429    /// Process timeout in seconds (empty/0 = default 150 s).
430    ocr_timeout_secs: String,
431    /// Effort level passed as `--effort` (empty = omit; low/medium/high/xhigh/max).
432    ocr_effort: String,
433    /// File path to a PNG used as the main icon in crash and freeze dialogs.
434    /// Empty = standard Windows error/warning icon. Requires a restart to take effect.
435    dialog_icon: String,
436    /// Global "go to the next schedule source when an event has no title" toggle:
437    /// after a winner is found, keep querying lower-priority sources to fill in
438    /// blank titles (e.g. a Twitch schedule with times but no titles).
439    schedule_title_fill: bool,
440    /// How many recent YouTube community posts to scan for a schedule image
441    /// (backlog depth). Empty = built-in default (5). Per-channel override in
442    /// channel Properties.
443    youtube_community_max_posts: String,
444    // --- Remux embedding options ---
445    /// Embed the thumbnail sidecar as MKV cover art on remux.
446    remux_embed_thumbnail: bool,
447    /// Embed a title metadata tag in the MKV on remux.
448    remux_embed_title: bool,
449    /// Template used to generate the MKV title tag.
450    remux_title_template: String,
451    /// Embed subtitle sidecar files as MKV subtitle streams on remux.
452    remux_embed_subs: bool,
453    // --- File management ---
454    /// Split output files into per-type subdirectories.
455    file_split_enabled: bool,
456    file_split_videos: String,
457    file_split_subs: String,
458    file_split_chat: String,
459    file_split_thumbs: String,
460    file_split_logs: String,
461    /// Checkbox for "fetch missing thumbnails" in the Maintenance section.
462    fetch_thumb_embed: bool,
463    /// Selected preset template for the "Set filename default" row in Maintenance.
464    maintenance_filename_preset: String,
465    /// Apply the preset to all existing monitors when "Set as Default" is clicked.
466    maintenance_apply_all: bool,
467    /// Path to the media player binary (e.g. `C:\Progs\mpv\mpv.exe`).
468    media_player_path: String,
469}
470
471/// Background load state of an import fetch (followed/subscriptions).
472enum ImportLoadState {
473    Loading,
474    Loaded(Vec<ImportCandidate>),
475    Error(String),
476}
477
478/// One row in the import confirmation dialog: a candidate plus its per-row choices.
479struct ImportRow {
480    cand: ImportCandidate,
481    /// Whether to import this entry.
482    selected: bool,
483    /// "Auto" — sets `monitor.enabled` (scheduler auto-records). Default off.
484    auto: bool,
485    /// "Disabled" — imports the container with `channel.enabled = false`.
486    disabled: bool,
487    /// Already present in the app (matched an existing monitor by id/login) — shown
488    /// greyed and not selectable, so an import can't create duplicates.
489    already: bool,
490    /// A channel with the same name already exists, but identities couldn't be
491    /// matched (e.g. an existing YouTube monitor added by @handle vs. a candidate's
492    /// channel id). Flagged + left unselected, but still selectable to override.
493    maybe_dup: bool,
494}
495
496/// The "Import followed/subscriptions" confirmation dialog.
497struct ImportDialog {
498    title: String,
499    /// Background fetch result; moved into `rows` once loaded.
500    load: Arc<Mutex<ImportLoadState>>,
501    /// Editable rows (populated from `load` on the first frame after it completes).
502    rows: Vec<ImportRow>,
503    loaded: bool,
504    search: String,
505    status: String,
506}
507
508/// Which field the Format Designer was opened from (for "Apply").
509#[derive(Clone, PartialEq)]
510enum FormatDesignerTarget {
511    MonitorForm,
512    VideoForm,
513}
514
515/// State for the floating Format Designer window.
516struct FormatDesignerState {
517    template: String,
518    selected_monitor_idx: usize,
519    /// Recordings for the currently selected monitor (oldest-first from the store).
520    recordings: Vec<Recording>,
521    selected_recording_idx: usize,
522    /// Which field opened the designer (None = standalone / no write-back).
523    target: Option<FormatDesignerTarget>,
524    /// In-flight background load of recordings for the selected monitor. Drained
525    /// each frame in `format_designer_window`; avoids blocking the UI thread.
526    recordings_load: Option<std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<(Vec<Recording>, usize)>>,
527}
528
529impl FormatDesignerState {
530    fn new(template: String, target: Option<FormatDesignerTarget>) -> Self {
531        Self {
532            template,
533            selected_monitor_idx: 0,
534            recordings: Vec::new(),
535            selected_recording_idx: 0,
536            target,
537            recordings_load: None,
538        }
539    }
540}
541
542pub struct StreamArchiverApp {
543    core: Arc<AppCore>,
544    _tray: TrayIcon,
545    ui_rx: Receiver<UiCommand>,
546    events_rx: crate::events::EventRx,
547    autostart: AutoStart,
548    autostart_on: bool,
549    /// When false (the default), quitting detaches downloads so they keep running
550    /// across a restart/rebuild; when true, quitting stops them. Persisted as the
551    /// `stop_downloads_on_quit` setting (stored inverted).
552    keep_downloads_on_quit: bool,
553    /// Show desktop notifications (toasts) on recording start/finish/error.
554    /// Persisted as the `notifications_enabled` setting; default on.
555    notifications_enabled: bool,
556    /// The process-manager dialog: whether it's open, its last snapshot, and when
557    /// that snapshot was taken (throttles the per-row `pid_alive`/DB queries).
558    show_processes: bool,
559    processes: Vec<crate::app_core::ProcInfo>,
560    processes_refreshed: Option<std::time::Instant>,
561    /// In-flight background load of the process list (spawned off the UI thread
562    /// to avoid blocking on the store mutex during `list_processes()`).
563    processes_load: Option<std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<Vec<crate::app_core::ProcInfo>>>,
564    /// The issues panel: whether it's open, its last snapshot of recordings
565    /// that still have a `.ts` path, and when that snapshot was taken.
566    show_issues: bool,
567    issues_recs: Vec<crate::models::Recording>,
568    issues_missing: Vec<crate::models::Recording>,
569    /// Failed/aborted/orphaned recordings that have an output file on disk (or no path at all).
570    issues_errors: Vec<crate::models::Recording>,
571    /// Failed/aborted/orphaned recordings whose output path is set but the file is gone from disk.
572    /// Partitioned out of issues_errors at load time; rendered alongside the missing-file section.
573    issues_errors_no_file: Vec<crate::models::Recording>,
574    /// In-flight background load of missing-output-file recordings (spawned off
575    /// the UI thread because `path.exists()` can block on network/slow drives).
576    issues_missing_load: Option<std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<Vec<crate::models::Recording>>>,
577    issues_refreshed: Option<std::time::Instant>,
578    issues_confirm_clear: bool,
579    quitting: bool,
580    /// UI-freeze watchdog heartbeat: stamped each frame so a background thread can
581    /// detect (and surface as a native dialog) a hung UI thread. See [`crate::watchdog`].
582    heartbeat: crate::watchdog::Heartbeat,
583
584    view: View,
585    rows: Vec<MonitorWithChannel>,
586    /// All channel containers (incl. empty ones), for the Streams tree.
587    channels: Vec<Channel>,
588    videos: Vec<Video>,
589    form: Option<MonitorForm>,
590    video_form: VideoForm,
591    /// Per-platform download defaults editable on the Videos tab (persisted JSON).
592    download_defaults: DownloadDefaults,
593    /// Per-platform monitor-creation defaults editable in Settings (persisted JSON).
594    monitor_defaults: MonitorDefaults,
595    /// Shared state of the async "List formats" probe (Videos tab).
596    format_probe: Arc<Mutex<FormatProbe>>,
597    settings: SettingsForm,
598    status: String,
599    /// Monitor id of the currently selected row (target for keyboard shortcuts).
600    selected_monitor: Option<i64>,
601    /// Pending instance-delete confirmation: (monitor id, channel name).
602    confirm_delete: Option<(i64, String)>,
603    /// Pending channel-delete confirmation: (channel id, name).
604    confirm_delete_channel: Option<(i64, String)>,
605    /// Pending schedule-segment-delete confirmation: segment id.
606    confirm_delete_segment: Option<i64>,
607    /// Backing state for the create/rename-channel dialog.
608    channel_form: Option<ChannelForm>,
609    /// Sort + per-column filters for the Streams table.
610    streams_sort: SortState,
611    streams_filters: Vec<String>,
612    /// Expansion state for the Streams history tree (channel id / monitor id /
613    /// stream key), and a lazy cache of recordings per expanded monitor.
614    expanded_channels: HashSet<i64>,
615    expanded_instances: HashSet<i64>,
616    expanded_streams: HashSet<String>,
617    rec_cache: HashMap<i64, Vec<Recording>>,
618    /// Lazy per-recording ad-break detail (cut list), keyed by recording id;
619    /// cleared on reload. Avoids a per-frame DB query for tooltips/the popup.
620    ad_break_cache: HashMap<i64, Vec<AdBreak>>,
621    /// Recording id whose ad-break cut list is shown in a popup (None = closed).
622    ad_popup: Option<i64>,
623    /// Lazy per-recording title/category change log, keyed by recording id;
624    /// cleared on reload. Same caching role as `ad_break_cache`.
625    meta_change_cache: HashMap<i64, Vec<StreamMetaChange>>,
626    /// What the metadata-change popup shows (None = closed): a single take or a
627    /// whole stream's aggregated takes.
628    meta_popup: Option<MetaPopup>,
629    /// Lazy per-monitor upcoming-schedule detail, keyed by monitor id; cleared on
630    /// reload. Backs the Next stream popup.
631    schedule_cache: HashMap<i64, Vec<ScheduleSegment>>,
632    /// Monitor id whose upcoming schedule is shown in a popup (None = closed).
633    schedule_popup: Option<i64>,
634    /// All upcoming scheduled streams (across every monitor), backing the Schedule
635    /// calendar. Loaded lazily on first view + on refresh; see [`Self::spawn_reload_schedule`].
636    schedule_all: Vec<UpcomingStream>,
637    /// Whether [`Self::schedule_all`] has been loaded yet (lazy on first view).
638    schedule_loaded: bool,
639    /// Schedule calendar granularity (month / week / day).
640    schedule_mode: ScheduleMode,
641    /// The focused date the Schedule calendar is centered on; `None` until set to
642    /// today on first view. Month view uses its year+month, week view the week
643    /// containing it, day view the date itself.
644    schedule_anchor: Option<chrono::NaiveDate>,
645    /// Channel ids hidden from the Schedule calendar (sidebar filter). Tracking
646    /// *hidden* (not visible) means newly-added channels default to visible.
647    schedule_hidden: HashSet<i64>,
648    /// Individual segment IDs the user has soft-hidden (not tombstoned). Reset
649    /// on app restart; use Delete for permanent suppression.
650    schedule_hidden_segments: HashSet<i64>,
651    /// When true, soft-hidden segments are shown dimmed instead of filtered out.
652    schedule_show_hidden: bool,
653    /// Whether to flag overlapping streams (time collisions) in the calendar.
654    schedule_collisions: bool,
655    /// The day whose full stream list is shown in a popup (local date; None = closed).
656    schedule_day_popup: Option<chrono::NaiveDate>,
657    /// Whether the "Schedule sources" dialog is open.
658    show_schedule_sources: bool,
659    /// Editable draft of the ordered source list, shown in the dialog. Loaded from
660    /// settings when the dialog opens; saved (+ refresh requested) on every change.
661    schedule_sources_draft: Vec<SourceEntry>,
662    /// The source id selected in the dialog (drives the →/← / ▲/▼ buttons).
663    schedule_sources_selected: Option<String>,
664    /// Editable per-channel schedule-source config shown in the Properties window,
665    /// tagged with its channel id. Loaded when Properties opens; saved on edit.
666    channel_cfg_draft: Option<(i64, crate::schedule_source::ChannelSourceConfig)>,
667    /// Editable per-channel schedule-source *scope* override (custom order +
668    /// title-fill) shown in channel Properties, tagged with its channel id.
669    channel_scope_draft: Option<(i64, crate::schedule_source::SourceScopeConfig)>,
670    /// Editable per-instance (monitor) schedule-source *scope* override shown in
671    /// instance Properties, tagged with its monitor id.
672    instance_scope_draft: Option<(i64, crate::schedule_source::SourceScopeConfig)>,
673    /// Draft for the "Edit schedule item" dialog (None = closed). Saving converts
674    /// the row to a protected `"manual"` source so refreshes don't overwrite it.
675    edit_schedule: Option<EditScheduleDraft>,
676    /// Segment IDs selected in the schedule calendar (Ctrl+click multi-select).
677    schedule_selected: HashSet<i64>,
678    /// Open merge-preview dialog (None = closed).
679    merge_preview: Option<MergePreviewDraft>,
680    /// Pending multi-delete confirmation for schedule segments (None = closed).
681    confirm_delete_segments: Option<Vec<i64>>,
682    /// Computed from `schedule_all`: primary segment_id → merge badge text.
683    /// Built by [`Self::recompute_merge_state`]; drives the 🔀 indicator.
684    schedule_merge_labels: HashMap<i64, String>,
685    /// Computed from `schedule_all`: segment IDs that are auto-merge secondaries
686    /// (hidden in favour of their primary). Built by [`Self::recompute_merge_state`].
687    schedule_auto_secondary: HashSet<i64>,
688    /// User-defined filename template presets loaded from the DB.
689    custom_presets: Vec<(i64, String, String)>,
690    /// Open "Save preset" naming dialog (None = closed).
691    save_preset_dialog: Option<SavePresetDraft>,
692    /// Chat log viewer popup (None = closed).
693    chat_popup: Option<ChatPopup>,
694    /// Platform favicons, uploaded to the GPU on first use (None until then).
695    platform_tex: Option<PlatformTextures>,
696    /// Which monitor's Properties window is open (None = closed).
697    properties_popup: Option<i64>,
698    /// Which channel's Properties window is open (None = closed).
699    channel_properties_popup: Option<i64>,
700    /// The recording whose properties dialog is open (None = closed).
701    rec_props: Option<i64>,
702    /// Draft notes for the open recording properties dialog; synced from the DB
703    /// when the dialog opens and written back on every keystroke.
704    rec_props_notes: String,
705    /// Per-channel cached icon textures loaded from disk for the Properties window.
706    /// A `None` value means the lookup was attempted but no icon file was found.
707    channel_icons: HashMap<i64, Option<egui::TextureHandle>>,
708    /// Pre-scaled (64 px) icon textures for the streams table avatar column.
709    /// Separate from `channel_icons` so the small slot can use a properly
710    /// Lanczos-downscaled thumbnail while Properties loads the full source.
711    channel_icons_small: HashMap<i64, Option<egui::TextureHandle>>,
712    /// Decoded + downscaled chat-emote frames, keyed by absolute image path. Shared
713    /// with background decode tasks (`Arc<Mutex<…>>`). Animated GIF/WebP cycle; the
714    /// frames are downscaled to render size to bound RAM, and the map is LRU-evicted
715    /// against [`EMOTE_BUDGET_BYTES`] + cleared on asset refetch / popup close.
716    emote_anim: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<std::path::PathBuf, crate::emote_anim::EmoteLoad>>>,
717    /// Bumped whenever `emote_anim` is cleared; in-flight decode tasks capture it at
718    /// spawn and skip their insert if it changed, so a decode finishing after a
719    /// popup-close / asset-refetch can't resurrect a stale (leaked) cache entry.
720    emote_epoch: Arc<AtomicU64>,
721    /// Loaded mainpage image assets (icon + banner per platform) for the channel
722    /// Properties thumbnail strip, keyed by channel id. Full-resolution textures
723    /// (so Alt-preview is crisp); loaded on window open, dropped on close/refetch.
724    channel_asset_thumbs: HashMap<i64, Vec<AssetThumb>>,
725    /// Per-provider *viewable* emote counts for the open Properties window, keyed by
726    /// channel id. Cached because the count is derived from the same enumeration the
727    /// viewer uses (one `fs::metadata` per emote) — recomputing it every frame would
728    /// be hundreds of stat calls per repaint. Invalidated wherever `channel_asset_thumbs`
729    /// is (open/rename/refetch/close).
730    channel_emote_counts: HashMap<i64, [(EmoteProvider, usize); 4]>,
731    /// Per-platform asset-status rows for the open Properties window, keyed by channel
732    /// id. Cached for the same reason as `channel_emote_counts`: each row is built from
733    /// blocking filesystem I/O (`read_dir` + per-file `metadata` + full JSON manifest
734    /// parse), and the status grid is rebuilt every frame — so doing the I/O per frame is
735    /// dozens of syscalls per repaint and can freeze the UI thread on slow/AV-scanned
736    /// storage. Invalidated wherever `channel_asset_thumbs` is (open/rename/refetch/close).
737    channel_asset_status: HashMap<i64, Vec<PlatformAssetStatus>>,
738    /// Snapshot of the global schedule-source order for the open Properties window. Taken
739    /// once on open so `scope_override_editor` reads it from memory instead of doing a
740    /// settings DB read (store mutex) every frame.
741    props_source_order: Vec<SourceEntry>,
742    /// In-flight background load of the channel Properties window's per-open data (icon +
743    /// asset-thumbnail decode/upload, per-platform asset enumeration, and the schedule
744    /// -source config/scope/order DB reads). Run OFF the UI thread so a slow disk, an AV
745    /// scan, or the store mutex being held by a background task can't freeze the GUI on
746    /// open — the window shows a "Loading…" placeholder until the bundle lands. `None`
747    /// when no load is running.
748    props_load: Option<PropsLoad>,
749    /// In-flight native file/folder picker (background thread). The OS dialog blocks
750    /// until the user picks or cancels; running it off the UI thread keeps egui alive.
751    /// At most one picker open at a time (a second Browse click replaces any existing).
752    pending_browse: Option<PendingBrowse>,
753    /// In-flight form save (background thread). The INSERT/UPDATE + reload queries can
754    /// block on the store mutex when a detection pass holds it; running off the UI
755    /// thread prevents a visible freeze on "Save".
756    pending_save: Option<PendingSave>,
757    /// In-flight F5 / manual reload (background thread). Same DB queries as
758    /// `pending_save` but no write — avoids blocking the UI thread on the store
759    /// mutex while a schedule-refresh Tokio task holds it.
760    pending_reload: Option<std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<Option<SaveRows>>>,
761    /// Cached YouTube Data API quota for today and the configured daily cutoff.
762    /// Updated by the background reload-rows thread; never read from DB on the
763    /// render thread (which would block if the DB mutex is held elsewhere).
764    yt_quota_today: i64,
765    yt_quota_cutoff: i64,
766    /// Daily search.list query count and its cutoff (separate from unit quota).
767    yt_search_today: i64,
768    yt_search_cutoff: i64,
769    /// Keys of quota warning issues the user has dismissed this session.
770    dismissed_quota_warnings: HashSet<String>,
771    /// In-flight schedule calendar reload (background thread). `all_upcoming_schedule`
772    /// can hold the DB mutex for several seconds when historical rows accumulate;
773    /// running it off the UI thread prevents frame freezes and unblocks the delete action.
774    pending_schedule: Option<std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<Option<Vec<UpcomingStream>>>>,
775    /// Open emote-viewer window (None = closed). Reuses the shared `emote_anim`
776    /// decode cache, so its emotes animate against the same clock as chat replay.
777    emote_viewer: Option<EmoteViewer>,
778    /// Set when this channel's assets are refetched while its emote viewer is open:
779    /// the viewer enumerated its list once on open, so it's now stale. Drives a
780    /// "close and reopen" banner; cleared when the viewer is (re)opened or closed.
781    emote_viewer_stale: bool,
782    /// Open asset change-history popup (None = closed). Holds the channel's
783    /// `asset_changes.jsonl` parsed + formatted once on open (newest first).
784    asset_history: Option<AssetHistoryView>,
785    /// GPU textures for the third-party emote-provider logos (7TV/BTTV), uploaded
786    /// once on first use of the emote launcher buttons.
787    provider_tex: Option<ProviderTextures>,
788    /// Per-channel Twitch broadcaster name colour (from `name_color.txt`, fetched
789    /// via Helix). `None` = looked up but the streamer set no colour / not Twitch.
790    /// Tints the channel name in the Streams list; cleared with `channel_icons`.
791    channel_twitch_colors: HashMap<i64, Option<egui::Color32>>,
792    /// Sort + per-column filters for the Videos table.
793    videos_sort: SortState,
794    videos_filters: Vec<String>,
795    /// Shared state of the interactive "Connect Twitch" device-code flow.
796    twitch_flow: Arc<Mutex<AuthFlow>>,
797    /// Shared state of the interactive "Connect YouTube" (Google) device-code flow.
798    google_flow: Arc<Mutex<AuthFlow>>,
799    /// Open "Import followed/subscriptions" confirmation dialog, if any.
800    import_dialog: Option<ImportDialog>,
801    /// Whether Streams rows show a status background tint (recording / ad / error).
802    /// Toggled from the top bar; persisted under [`K_STATUS_BGCOLOR`]. Keyboard
803    /// row selection is still highlighted regardless.
804    status_bgcolor: bool,
805    /// Whether the per-row Actions column (inline action buttons) is shown in the
806    /// Streams + Videos tables. Off reclaims width; every action is also on the
807    /// row's right-click context menu. Persisted under [`K_SHOW_ACTIONS`].
808    show_actions: bool,
809    /// Whether timestamp columns show a compact short format (e.g. `21/06 14:02`)
810    /// instead of the full datetime. The full value appears in a tooltip. Persisted
811    /// under [`K_SHORT_TIMESTAMPS`].
812    shorten_timestamps: bool,
813    /// Render chat emotes as inline images in the chat replay (off ⇒ show the
814    /// emote code as text). Default on. Persisted under [`K_RENDER_EMOTES`].
815    render_emotes: bool,
816    /// Play animated emotes (off ⇒ static first frame). Default on. Persisted under
817    /// [`K_ANIMATE_EMOTES`].
818    animate_emotes: bool,
819    /// Set to true by the "⇔ Fit columns" button; consumed in `channels_view`
820    /// to call `TableBuilder::reset()` so columns revert to content-fit widths.
821    reset_streams_columns: bool,
822    /// Currently running background tasks (asset fetches, thumbnail downloads).
823    background_tasks: Vec<crate::events::BackgroundTask>,
824    /// Completed/failed background tasks (task, outcome, finished-at unix), newest
825    /// first; capped at 100.
826    finished_tasks: Vec<(crate::events::BackgroundTask, crate::events::TaskOutcome, i64)>,
827    /// Enable/disable state for the periodic jobs (`events::TOGGLEABLE_JOBS`),
828    /// mirrored from settings; edited via the Background "Scheduled" checkboxes.
829    job_toggles: std::collections::HashMap<String, bool>,
830    /// Debug view state — persisted across frames; fields are always present but
831    /// only rendered when `cfg!(debug_assertions)`.
832    debug_monitor_idx: usize,
833    debug_test_title: String,
834    debug_test_game: String,
835    /// Format Designer: an interactive template preview/editor window.
836    format_designer: Option<FormatDesignerState>,
837    /// Pending "Stop recordings & quit" confirmation (triggered by the tray item).
838    confirm_quit_stop: bool,
839    /// Cached (ocr_stats, global_stats) for the Stats view; None = not yet loaded.
840    stats_snapshot: Option<(OcrStats, GlobalStats)>,
841    /// Channel id to scroll into view on the next Streams render, after a save
842    /// adds a new channel. Cleared once consumed. None = no pending scroll.
843    scroll_to_channel: Option<i64>,
844    /// Rename dialog: whether the dialog is open.
845    show_rename_dialog: bool,
846    /// Rename dialog: the recording id being renamed.
847    rename_rec_id: Option<i64>,
848    /// Rename dialog: the current template/stem string the user is editing.
849    rename_draft: String,
850    /// Rename dialog: live-expanded preview of `rename_draft`.
851    rename_preview: String,
852}
853
854impl StreamArchiverApp {
855    pub fn new(
856        core: Arc<AppCore>,
857        tray: TrayIcon,
858        ui_rx: Receiver<UiCommand>,
859        heartbeat: crate::watchdog::Heartbeat,
860    ) -> StreamArchiverApp {
861        let events_rx = core.subscribe();
862        let autostart = AutoStart::new();
863        let autostart_on = autostart.is_enabled();
864        // Detach-on-quit is the default; only `=="1"` opts into stopping downloads.
865        let keep_downloads_on_quit = core
866            .store
867            .get_setting("stop_downloads_on_quit")
868            .ok()
869            .flatten()
870            .as_deref()
871            != Some("1");
872        // Desktop notifications default on; only `=="0"` disables them.
873        let notifications_enabled = core
874            .store
875            .get_setting(crate::notifications::K_NOTIFICATIONS)
876            .ok()
877            .flatten()
878            .as_deref()
879            != Some("0");
880
881        let default_out = core
882            .store
883            .get_setting(K_DEFAULT_OUT)
884            .ok()
885            .flatten()
886            .unwrap_or_else(|| {
887                crate::app_paths::default_output_dir()
888                    .to_string_lossy()
889                    .to_string()
890            });
891
892        let settings = SettingsForm {
893            twitch_client_id: setting_or_empty(&core, K_TWITCH_ID),
894            twitch_client_secret: setting_or_empty(&core, K_TWITCH_SECRET),
895            google_client_id: setting_or_empty(&core, google_oauth::K_CLIENT_ID),
896            google_client_secret: setting_or_empty(&core, google_oauth::K_CLIENT_SECRET),
897            youtube_api_key: setting_or_empty(&core, K_YT_KEY),
898            youtube_api_detect: setting_or_empty(&core, K_YT_API_DETECT) == "1",
899            youtube_api_schedule: setting_or_empty(&core, K_YT_API_SCHEDULE) == "1",
900            youtube_api_quota_cutoff: setting_or_empty(&core, K_YT_API_QUOTA_CUTOFF),
901            youtube_search_quota_cutoff: setting_or_empty(&core, K_YT_SEARCH_QUOTA_CUTOFF),
902            kick_client_id: setting_or_empty(&core, K_KICK_ID),
903            kick_client_secret: setting_or_empty(&core, K_KICK_SECRET),
904            default_output_dir: default_out,
905            max_concurrent_downloads: core
906                .store
907                .get_setting(K_MAX_CONCURRENT)
908                .ok()
909                .flatten()
910                .unwrap_or_else(|| "3".into()),
911            download_auth_method: core
912                .store
913                .get_setting(K_DOWNLOAD_AUTH)
914                .ok()
915                .flatten()
916                .unwrap_or_else(|| "none".into()),
917            cookies_browser: split_browser_profile(&setting_or_empty(&core, K_COOKIES_BROWSER)).0,
918            cookies_profile: split_browser_profile(&setting_or_empty(&core, K_COOKIES_BROWSER)).1,
919            websub_vps_url: setting_or_empty(&core, K_WEBSUB_URL),
920            websub_token: setting_or_empty(&core, K_WEBSUB_TOKEN),
921            websub_poll_secs: core
922                .store
923                .get_setting(K_WEBSUB_POLL)
924                .ok()
925                .flatten()
926                .unwrap_or_else(|| "15".into()),
927            filename_media_info: MediaInfoMode::parse(&setting_or_empty(&core, K_FILENAME_MEDIA)),
928            date_fmt: DateFmt::parse(&setting_or_empty(&core, K_DATE_FORMAT)),
929            short_ts_fmt: {
930                let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_SHORT_TS_FMT);
931                if v.is_empty() { "%d/%m %H:%M".to_string() } else { v }
932            },
933            ytdlp_default_args: setting_or_empty(&core, K_YTDLP_ARGS),
934            ytdlp_binary_path: setting_or_empty(&core, K_YTDLP_BINARY),
935            sabr_binary_path: setting_or_empty(&core, K_SABR_BINARY),
936            // Absent ⇒ enabled by default; an explicit "0" disables it.
937            sabr_enabled: setting_or_empty(&core, K_SABR_ENABLED) != "0",
938            sabr_format: {
939                let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_SABR_FORMAT);
940                if v.is_empty() {
941                    crate::downloader::SABR_DEFAULT_FORMAT.to_string()
942                } else {
943                    v
944                }
945            },
946            sabr_extractor_args: {
947                let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_SABR_EXTRACTOR_ARGS);
948                if v.is_empty() {
949                    crate::downloader::SABR_DEFAULT_EXTRACTOR_ARGS.to_string()
950                } else {
951                    v
952                }
953            },
954            // Absent ⇒ off; an explicit "1" enables it.
955            sabr_deep_rewind: setting_or_empty(&core, K_SABR_DEEP_REWIND) == "1",
956            sabr_raw_args: setting_or_empty(&core, K_SABR_RAW_ARGS),
957            // Absent ⇒ bgutil default; present (even empty) ⇒ honor it verbatim so
958            // the user can disable it and rely on the plugin's auto-detection.
959            sabr_pot_args: match core.store.get_setting(K_SABR_POT_ARGS) {
960                Ok(Some(v)) => v,
961                _ => crate::downloader::SABR_DEFAULT_POT_ARGS.to_string(),
962            },
963            dash_format: {
964                let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_DASH_FORMAT);
965                if v.is_empty() {
966                    crate::downloader::DASH_DEFAULT_FORMAT.to_string()
967                } else {
968                    v
969                }
970            },
971            discord_token: setting_or_empty(&core, K_DISCORD_TOKEN),
972            discord_schedule: setting_or_empty(&core, K_DISCORD_SCHEDULE) == "1",
973            ocr_command: setting_or_empty(&core, K_OCR_COMMAND),
974            ocr_model: setting_or_empty(&core, K_OCR_MODEL),
975            ocr_fallback_model: setting_or_empty(&core, K_OCR_FALLBACK_MODEL),
976            ocr_timezone: setting_or_empty(&core, K_OCR_TIMEZONE),
977            ocr_offset: setting_or_empty(&core, K_OCR_OFFSET),
978            ocr_max_budget: setting_or_empty(&core, K_OCR_MAX_BUDGET),
979            ocr_timeout_secs: setting_or_empty(&core, K_OCR_TIMEOUT_SECS),
980            ocr_effort: setting_or_empty(&core, K_OCR_EFFORT),
981            schedule_title_fill: setting_or_empty(&core, K_SCHEDULE_TITLE_FILL) == "1",
982            youtube_community_max_posts: setting_or_empty(&core, K_YT_COMMUNITY_MAX_POSTS),
983            dialog_icon: setting_or_empty(&core, K_DIALOG_ICON),
984            remux_embed_thumbnail: core.store.get_setting(K_REMUX_EMBED_THUMBNAIL)
985                .ok().flatten().map_or(true, |v| v != "0"),
986            remux_embed_title: setting_or_empty(&core, K_REMUX_EMBED_TITLE) == "1",
987            remux_title_template: {
988                let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_REMUX_TITLE_TEMPLATE);
989                if v.is_empty() { "{title}".into() } else { v }
990            },
991            remux_embed_subs: setting_or_empty(&core, K_REMUX_EMBED_SUBS) == "1",
992            file_split_enabled: setting_or_empty(&core, K_FILE_SPLIT_ENABLED) == "1",
993            file_split_videos: { let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_FILE_SPLIT_VIDEOS); if v.is_empty() { "videos".into() } else { v } },
994            file_split_subs:   { let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_FILE_SPLIT_SUBS);   if v.is_empty() { "subs".into()   } else { v } },
995            file_split_chat:   { let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_FILE_SPLIT_CHAT);   if v.is_empty() { "chat".into()   } else { v } },
996            file_split_thumbs: { let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_FILE_SPLIT_THUMBS); if v.is_empty() { "thumbs".into() } else { v } },
997            file_split_logs:   { let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_FILE_SPLIT_LOGS);   if v.is_empty() { "logs".into()   } else { v } },
998            fetch_thumb_embed: false,
999            maintenance_filename_preset: String::new(),
1000            maintenance_apply_all: false,
1001            media_player_path: {
1002                let v = setting_or_empty(&core, K_MEDIA_PLAYER);
1003                if v.is_empty() { r"C:\Progs\mpv\mpv.exe".into() } else { v }
1004            },
1005        };
1006        // Apply the loaded date format + short-timestamp pattern before the first render.
1007        set_active_date_fmt(settings.date_fmt);
1008        set_short_ts_pattern(&settings.short_ts_fmt);
1009
1010        let twitch_flow = Arc::new(Mutex::new(match oauth::connected_login(&core.store) {
1011            Some(login) => AuthFlow::Connected { login },
1012            None => AuthFlow::Idle,
1013        }));
1014        let google_flow = Arc::new(Mutex::new(if google_oauth::is_connected(&core.store) {
1015            AuthFlow::Connected {
1016                login: google_oauth::connected_identity(&core.store).unwrap_or_default(),
1017            }
1018        } else {
1019            AuthFlow::Idle
1020        }));
1021
1022        // Status row tint defaults on; only an explicit "0" disables it.
1023        let status_bgcolor = core
1024            .store
1025            .get_setting(K_STATUS_BGCOLOR)
1026            .ok()
1027            .flatten()
1028            .map(|v| v != "0")
1029            .unwrap_or(true);
1030        // The Actions column defaults on; only an explicit "0" hides it.
1031        let show_actions = core
1032            .store
1033            .get_setting(K_SHOW_ACTIONS)
1034            .ok()
1035            .flatten()
1036            .map(|v| v != "0")
1037            .unwrap_or(true);
1038        // Short timestamps default off; only an explicit "1" enables.
1039        let shorten_timestamps = core
1040            .store
1041            .get_setting(K_SHORT_TIMESTAMPS)
1042            .ok()
1043            .flatten()
1044            .map(|v| v == "1")
1045            .unwrap_or(false);
1046        set_short_ts(shorten_timestamps);
1047
1048        // Inline chat emotes default on; only an explicit "0" disables.
1049        let render_emotes = core
1050            .store
1051            .get_setting(K_RENDER_EMOTES)
1052            .ok()
1053            .flatten()
1054            .map(|v| v != "0")
1055            .unwrap_or(true);
1056        // Animated emotes default on; only an explicit "0" disables.
1057        let animate_emotes = core
1058            .store
1059            .get_setting(K_ANIMATE_EMOTES)
1060            .ok()
1061            .flatten()
1062            .map(|v| v != "0")
1063            .unwrap_or(true);
1064
1065        let download_defaults = core
1066            .store
1067            .get_setting("download_defaults")
1068            .ok()
1069            .flatten()
1070            .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str::<DownloadDefaults>(&s).ok())
1071            .unwrap_or_else(|| DownloadDefaults::seeded(&settings.default_output_dir));
1072
1073        let monitor_defaults = core
1074            .store
1075            .get_setting(K_MONITOR_DEFAULTS)
1076            .ok()
1077            .flatten()
1078            .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str::<MonitorDefaults>(&s).ok())
1079            .unwrap_or_default();
1080
1081        // Snapshot job enable/disable state before `core` is moved into the struct.
1082        let job_toggles: std::collections::HashMap<String, bool> =
1083            crate::events::TOGGLEABLE_JOBS
1084                .iter()
1085                .map(|(_, key)| (key.to_string(), core.store.job_enabled(key)))
1086                .collect();
1087        // Load user-defined filename presets before `core` is moved.
1088        let initial_custom_presets = core.store.get_filename_presets().unwrap_or_default();
1089
1090        let mut app = StreamArchiverApp {
1091            core,
1092            _tray: tray,
1093            ui_rx,
1094            events_rx,
1095            autostart,
1096            autostart_on,
1097            keep_downloads_on_quit,
1098            notifications_enabled,
1099            show_processes: false,
1100            processes: Vec::new(),
1101            processes_refreshed: None,
1102            processes_load: None,
1103            show_issues: false,
1104            issues_recs: Vec::new(),
1105            issues_missing: Vec::new(),
1106            issues_errors: Vec::new(),
1107            issues_errors_no_file: Vec::new(),
1108            issues_missing_load: None,
1109            issues_refreshed: None,
1110            issues_confirm_clear: false,
1111            quitting: false,
1112            heartbeat,
1113            view: View::Streams,
1114            rows: Vec::new(),
1115            channels: Vec::new(),
1116            videos: Vec::new(),
1117            form: None,
1118            video_form: VideoForm::new(),
1119            download_defaults,
1120            monitor_defaults,
1121            format_probe: Arc::new(Mutex::new(FormatProbe::Idle)),
1122            settings,
1123            status: String::new(),
1124            selected_monitor: None,
1125            confirm_delete: None,
1126            confirm_delete_channel: None,
1127            confirm_delete_segment: None,
1128            channel_form: None,
1129            streams_sort: SortState::default(),
1130            streams_filters: vec![String::new(); STREAM_COLS],
1131            expanded_channels: HashSet::new(),
1132            expanded_instances: HashSet::new(),
1133            expanded_streams: HashSet::new(),
1134            rec_cache: HashMap::new(),
1135            ad_break_cache: HashMap::new(),
1136            ad_popup: None,
1137            meta_change_cache: HashMap::new(),
1138            meta_popup: None,
1139            schedule_cache: HashMap::new(),
1140            schedule_popup: None,
1141            schedule_all: Vec::new(),
1142            schedule_loaded: false,
1143            schedule_mode: ScheduleMode::Month,
1144            schedule_anchor: None,
1145            schedule_hidden: HashSet::new(),
1146            schedule_hidden_segments: HashSet::new(),
1147            schedule_show_hidden: false,
1148            schedule_collisions: true,
1149            schedule_day_popup: None,
1150            show_schedule_sources: false,
1151            schedule_sources_draft: Vec::new(),
1152            schedule_sources_selected: None,
1153            channel_cfg_draft: None,
1154            channel_scope_draft: None,
1155            instance_scope_draft: None,
1156            edit_schedule: None,
1157            schedule_selected: HashSet::new(),
1158            merge_preview: None,
1159            confirm_delete_segments: None,
1160            schedule_merge_labels: HashMap::new(),
1161            schedule_auto_secondary: HashSet::new(),
1162            custom_presets: initial_custom_presets,
1163            save_preset_dialog: None,
1164            chat_popup: None,
1165            platform_tex: None,
1166            properties_popup: None,
1167            channel_properties_popup: None,
1168            rec_props: None,
1169            rec_props_notes: String::new(),
1170            channel_icons: HashMap::new(),
1171            channel_icons_small: HashMap::new(),
1172            emote_anim: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
1173            emote_epoch: Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(0)),
1174            channel_asset_thumbs: HashMap::new(),
1175            channel_emote_counts: HashMap::new(),
1176            channel_asset_status: HashMap::new(),
1177            props_source_order: Vec::new(),
1178            props_load: None,
1179            pending_browse: None,
1180            pending_save: None,
1181            pending_reload: None,
1182            yt_quota_today: 0,
1183            yt_quota_cutoff: 9000,
1184            yt_search_today: 0,
1185            yt_search_cutoff: 90,
1186            dismissed_quota_warnings: HashSet::new(),
1187            pending_schedule: None,
1188            emote_viewer: None,
1189            emote_viewer_stale: false,
1190            asset_history: None,
1191            provider_tex: None,
1192            channel_twitch_colors: HashMap::new(),
1193            videos_sort: SortState::default(),
1194            videos_filters: vec![String::new(); VIDEO_COLS],
1195            twitch_flow,
1196            google_flow,
1197            import_dialog: None,
1198            status_bgcolor,
1199            show_actions,
1200            shorten_timestamps,
1201            render_emotes,
1202            animate_emotes,
1203            reset_streams_columns: false,
1204            background_tasks: Vec::new(),
1205            finished_tasks: Vec::new(),
1206            job_toggles,
1207            format_designer: None,
1208            confirm_quit_stop: false,
1209            debug_monitor_idx: 0,
1210            debug_test_title: "Test Stream Title".into(),
1211            debug_test_game: "Just Chatting".into(),
1212            stats_snapshot: None,
1213            scroll_to_channel: None,
1214            show_rename_dialog: false,
1215            rename_rec_id: None,
1216            rename_draft: String::new(),
1217            rename_preview: String::new(),
1218        };
1219        app.reload_rows();
1220        app.reload_videos();
1221        app.yt_quota_today = app.core.store.get_quota_today("youtube").unwrap_or(0);
1222        app.yt_quota_cutoff = app
1223            .core
1224            .store
1225            .get_setting(K_YT_API_QUOTA_CUTOFF)
1226            .ok()
1227            .flatten()
1228            .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse().ok())
1229            .unwrap_or(9000);
1230        app.yt_search_today = app.core.store.get_quota_today("youtube_search").unwrap_or(0);
1231        app.yt_search_cutoff = app
1232            .core
1233            .store
1234            .get_setting(K_YT_SEARCH_QUOTA_CUTOFF)
1235            .ok()
1236            .flatten()
1237            .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse().ok())
1238            .unwrap_or(90);
1239        app
1240    }
1241
1242    fn reload_rows(&mut self) {
1243        let _t = std::time::Instant::now();
1244        match self.core.store.list_monitors_with_channels() {
1245            Ok(rows) => self.rows = rows,
1246            Err(e) => {
1247                warn!("failed to load monitors: {e:#}");
1248                self.status = format!("Error loading channels: {e}");
1249            }
1250        }
1251        // Merge in each monitor's next upcoming scheduled stream (the row query
1252        // doesn't carry it — it's refreshed on a separate cadence).
1253        if let Ok(next) = self.core.store.next_scheduled_streams(now_unix()) {
1254            let by_mid: HashMap<i64, (i64, String)> =
1255                next.into_iter().map(|(mid, at, title)| (mid, (at, title))).collect();
1256            for row in &mut self.rows {
1257                if let Some((at, title)) = by_mid.get(&row.monitor.id) {
1258                    row.next_stream_at = Some(*at);
1259                    row.next_stream_title = title.clone();
1260                }
1261            }
1262        }
1263        // Load all containers (incl. empty ones) so they show in the tree.
1264        match self.core.store.list_channels() {
1265            Ok(chs) => self.channels = chs,
1266            Err(e) => warn!("failed to load channels: {e:#}"),
1267        }
1268        // Drop expansion state for channels/monitors that no longer exist (avoids
1269        // an unbounded leak and "sticky" expansion if a row id is later reused).
1270        let live_channels: HashSet<i64> = self.channels.iter().map(|c| c.id).collect();
1271        let live_monitors: HashSet<i64> = self.rows.iter().map(|r| r.monitor.id).collect();
1272        // Evict dead monitors from the rec/ad/meta/schedule caches, but KEEP
1273        // entries for live monitors — clearing everything forces a synchronous
1274        // recordings_for_monitor() DB call for every expanded monitor on the very
1275        // next frame, which freezes the UI thread proportionally to the number of
1276        // expanded rows. Stale data (a just-finished recording) is refreshed at
1277        // the specific UiCommand::Reload sites that already know which monitor changed.
1278        self.rec_cache.retain(|k, _| live_monitors.contains(k));
1279        self.ad_break_cache.retain(|k, _| live_monitors.contains(k));
1280        self.meta_change_cache.retain(|k, _| live_monitors.contains(k));
1281        self.schedule_cache.retain(|k, _| live_monitors.contains(k));
1282        self.expanded_channels.retain(|id| live_channels.contains(id));
1283        self.expanded_instances.retain(|id| live_monitors.contains(id));
1284        // Stream keys are "s<mid>:…" / "t<mid>:…"; keep only live monitors'.
1285        self.expanded_streams
1286            .retain(|k| stream_key_monitor(k).is_some_and(|mid| live_monitors.contains(&mid)));
1287        let elapsed_ms = _t.elapsed().as_millis();
1288        if elapsed_ms >= 100 {
1289            warn!(elapsed_ms, rows = self.rows.len(), "reload_rows slow");
1290        } else {
1291            debug!(elapsed_ms, rows = self.rows.len(), "reload_rows");
1292        }
1293    }
1294
1295    fn reload_videos(&mut self) {
1296        match self.core.store.list_videos() {
1297            Ok(v) => self.videos = v,
1298            Err(e) => warn!("failed to load videos: {e:#}"),
1299        }
1300    }
1301
1302    /// Spawn a background thread to reload the Schedule calendar.  The result is
1303    /// installed by [`Self::drain_pending_schedule`] in the next update cycle.
1304    /// Loads 90 days of history plus all future events; the 90-day cutoff keeps
1305    /// the idx_schedule_canceled_start scan bounded.  If a reload is already in
1306    /// flight the new request is dropped — the in-flight one will land shortly.
1307    fn spawn_reload_schedule(&mut self) {
1308        // Always clear the per-monitor popup cache immediately so an edit/delete
1309        // isn't shown stale in the Properties "Upcoming streams" list.
1310        self.schedule_cache.clear();
1311        if self.pending_schedule.is_some() {
1312            return;
1313        }
1314        let after = crate::models::now_unix() - 90 * 86_400;
1315        let store = self.core.store.clone();
1316        let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel();
1317        std::thread::Builder::new()
1318            .name("reload-schedule".into())
1319            .spawn(move || {
1320                let result = (|| -> Option<Vec<UpcomingStream>> {
1321                    let mut v = store.all_upcoming_schedule(after).ok()?;
1322                    // Collapse cross-source duplicates that may have been stored before
1323                    // the per-timestamp eviction was in place. Two rows with the same
1324                    // (monitor_id, start_time) but different sources are the same event;
1325                    // keep the higher-priority source's version.
1326                    let source_priority = |s: &str| -> u8 {
1327                        match s {
1328                            "manual" => 0,
1329                            "platform" => 1,
1330                            "youtube_api" => 2,
1331                            "youtube" => 3,
1332                            "twitch_banner_ocr" | "youtube_community_ocr"
1333                            | "twitter_pinned" | "other_image_ocr" => 4,
1334                            "discord" => 5,
1335                            _ => 6,
1336                        }
1337                    };
1338                    v.sort_by(|a, b| {
1339                        a.monitor_id
1340                            .cmp(&b.monitor_id)
1341                            .then(a.start_time.cmp(&b.start_time))
1342                            .then(source_priority(&a.source).cmp(&source_priority(&b.source)))
1343                    });
1344                    v.dedup_by(|a, b| {
1345                        a.monitor_id == b.monitor_id && a.start_time == b.start_time
1346                    });
1347                    // Restore display order: soonest first, then channel name.
1348                    v.sort_by(|a, b| {
1349                        a.start_time
1350                            .cmp(&b.start_time)
1351                            .then(a.channel_name.to_lowercase().cmp(&b.channel_name.to_lowercase()))
1352                    });
1353                    Some(v)
1354                })();
1355                let _ = tx.send(result);
1356            })
1357            .ok();
1358        self.pending_schedule = Some(rx);
1359    }
1360
1361    /// Install schedule calendar results from an in-flight background reload.
1362    /// Called every frame from the main update loop.
1363    fn drain_pending_schedule(&mut self) {
1364        let recv = match &self.pending_schedule {
1365            Some(rx) => rx.try_recv(),
1366            None => return,
1367        };
1368        match recv {
1369            Ok(Some(v)) => {
1370                self.schedule_all = v;
1371                // Drop hide choices only for channels that no longer EXIST (deleted),
1372                // not ones merely without an upcoming stream right now.
1373                let live: HashSet<i64> = self.channels.iter().map(|c| c.id).collect();
1374                if !live.is_empty() {
1375                    self.schedule_hidden.retain(|id| live.contains(id));
1376                }
1377                self.recompute_merge_state();
1378                // Only latch on success so a transient error retries via lazy-load.
1379                self.schedule_loaded = true;
1380                self.pending_schedule = None;
1381            }
1382            Ok(None) => {
1383                warn!("reload-schedule thread produced no data");
1384                self.status = "Error loading schedule.".into();
1385                self.pending_schedule = None;
1386            }
1387            Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {
1388                warn!("reload-schedule thread disconnected without sending");
1389                self.pending_schedule = None;
1390            }
1391            Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Empty) => {}
1392        }
1393    }
1394
1395    /// Recompute `schedule_merge_labels` and `schedule_auto_secondary` from the
1396    /// current `schedule_all`. Call after any change to `schedule_all` or to
1397    /// `merged_into`/`auto_merge_excluded` on any segment.
1398    fn recompute_merge_state(&mut self) {
1399        self.schedule_merge_labels.clear();
1400        self.schedule_auto_secondary.clear();
1401
1402        // ── Auto-merge: group same-channel overlapping events ──────────────
1403        // Group non-excluded, non-manual-secondary segments by channel_id.
1404        let mut by_channel: HashMap<i64, Vec<usize>> = HashMap::new();
1405        for (i, s) in self.schedule_all.iter().enumerate() {
1406            if s.merged_into.is_some() { continue; } // manual secondary → skip
1407            by_channel.entry(s.channel_id).or_default().push(i);
1408        }
1409        for indices in by_channel.values() {
1410            // schedule_all is already sorted by start_time, so each channel's
1411            // list is time-ordered.
1412            let mut processed: HashSet<usize> = HashSet::new();
1413            for &pi in indices {
1414                if processed.contains(&pi) { continue; }
1415                let ps = &self.schedule_all[pi];
1416                if ps.auto_merge_excluded { continue; }
1417                let p_end = ps.end_time.unwrap_or(ps.start_time + 3600);
1418                let mut group: Vec<usize> = vec![pi];
1419                let mut group_end = p_end;
1420                for &si in indices {
1421                    if si == pi || processed.contains(&si) { continue; }
1422                    let ss = &self.schedule_all[si];
1423                    if ss.auto_merge_excluded { continue; }
1424                    let s_end = ss.end_time.unwrap_or(ss.start_time + 3600);
1425                    if ss.start_time < group_end && s_end > ps.start_time {
1426                        group.push(si);
1427                        group_end = group_end.max(s_end);
1428                    }
1429                }
1430                if group.len() > 1 {
1431                    // Pick primary: highest source priority (YouTube first)
1432                    let primary_pos = group
1433                        .iter()
1434                        .enumerate()
1435                        .min_by_key(|(_, gi)| {
1436                            merge_source_priority(&self.schedule_all[**gi].source)
1437                        })
1438                        .map(|(pos, _)| pos)
1439                        .unwrap_or(0);
1440                    let primary_idx = group[primary_pos];
1441                    let primary_id = self.schedule_all[primary_idx].segment_id;
1442                    // Collect secondary info before mutating self.schedule_merge_labels.
1443                    let auto_sources: Vec<String> = group
1444                        .iter()
1445                        .enumerate()
1446                        .filter(|(pos, _)| *pos != primary_pos)
1447                        .map(|(_, si)| self.schedule_all[*si].source.clone())
1448                        .collect();
1449                    let secondary_sids: Vec<i64> = group
1450                        .iter()
1451                        .enumerate()
1452                        .filter(|(pos, _)| *pos != primary_pos)
1453                        .map(|(_, si)| self.schedule_all[*si].segment_id)
1454                        .collect();
1455                    for idx in &group {
1456                        processed.insert(*idx);
1457                    }
1458                    for sid in secondary_sids {
1459                        self.schedule_auto_secondary.insert(sid);
1460                    }
1461                    let n = auto_sources.len();
1462                    *self.schedule_merge_labels.entry(primary_id).or_default() =
1463                        format!("auto-merged {n} ({})", auto_sources.join(", "));
1464                }
1465            }
1466        }
1467
1468        // ── Manual merge: collect primaries that have manual secondaries ───
1469        for s in &self.schedule_all {
1470            if let Some(primary_id) = s.merged_into {
1471                let src = s.source.clone();
1472                let entry = self.schedule_merge_labels.entry(primary_id).or_default();
1473                if !entry.is_empty() {
1474                    entry.push_str(&format!(", manual: {src}"));
1475                } else {
1476                    *entry = format!("manually merged ({})", src);
1477                }
1478            }
1479        }
1480    }
1481
1482    fn persist_download_defaults(&self) {
1483        match serde_json::to_string(&self.download_defaults) {
1484            Ok(json) => {
1485                let _ = self.core.store.set_setting("download_defaults", &json);
1486            }
1487            Err(e) => warn!("failed to serialize download defaults: {e:#}"),
1488        }
1489    }
1490
1491    fn persist_monitor_defaults(&self) {
1492        match serde_json::to_string(&self.monitor_defaults) {
1493            Ok(json) => {
1494                let _ = self.core.store.set_setting(K_MONITOR_DEFAULTS, &json);
1495            }
1496            Err(e) => warn!("failed to serialize monitor defaults: {e:#}"),
1497        }
1498    }
1499
1500    /// Handle tray commands and bus events; returns true if a repaint is needed.
1501    fn pump_messages(&mut self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
1502        // One-shot startup notice (e.g. detached downloads recovered on launch).
1503        if let Some(msg) = self.core.startup_notice.lock().unwrap().take() {
1504            self.status = msg;
1505        }
1506        while let Ok(cmd) = self.ui_rx.try_recv() {
1507            match cmd {
1508                UiCommand::ShowWindow => {
1509                    ctx.send_viewport_cmd(egui::ViewportCommand::Visible(true));
1510                    ctx.send_viewport_cmd(egui::ViewportCommand::Focus);
1511                }
1512                UiCommand::Quit => {
1513                    self.quitting = true;
1514                    ctx.send_viewport_cmd(egui::ViewportCommand::Close);
1515                }
1516                UiCommand::QuitAndStop => {
1517                    // Show confirmation before stopping active recordings.
1518                    self.confirm_quit_stop = true;
1519                    // Bring the window to the foreground so the dialog is visible.
1520                    ctx.send_viewport_cmd(egui::ViewportCommand::Visible(true));
1521                    ctx.send_viewport_cmd(egui::ViewportCommand::Focus);
1522                }
1523            }
1524        }
1525
1526        let mut dirty = false;
1527        loop {
1528            match self.events_rx.try_recv() {
1529                Ok(crate::events::AppEvent::Error { context, message }) => {
1530                    self.status = format!("{context}: {message}");
1531                    dirty = true;
1532                }
1533                Ok(crate::events::AppEvent::BackgroundTaskStarted(task)) => {
1534                    self.background_tasks.push(task);
1535                    dirty = true;
1536                }
1537                Ok(crate::events::AppEvent::BackgroundTaskProgress { id, progress, info }) => {
1538                    if let Some(task) = self.background_tasks.iter_mut().find(|t| t.id == id) {
1539                        task.progress = progress;
1540                        task.progress_info = Some(info);
1541                    }
1542                    dirty = true;
1543                }
1544                Ok(crate::events::AppEvent::BackgroundTaskFinished { id, outcome }) => {
1545                    if let Some(pos) = self.background_tasks.iter().position(|t| t.id == id) {
1546                        let task = self.background_tasks.remove(pos);
1547                        // A finished asset fetch may have produced a new channel
1548                        // icon or refreshed emote images — drop both caches so they
1549                        // reload from disk (emote files can change at a stable path).
1550                        if task.kind == crate::events::BackgroundTaskKind::AssetFetch {
1551                            // Texture caches are cleared here (in logic(), before any
1552                            // rendering) so new textures are allocated at the top of
1553                            // channels_view() this frame — safe because no paint commands
1554                            // have referenced them yet.
1555                            debug!(
1556                                task_label = task.label.as_str(),
1557                                "AssetFetch complete — clearing icon/emote texture caches"
1558                            );
1559                            self.channel_icons.clear();
