1560                            self.channel_icons_small.clear();
1561                            self.channel_twitch_colors.clear();
1562                            // Banners/icons may have changed — drop the cached
1563                            // Properties thumbnails so they reload from disk.
1564                            self.channel_asset_thumbs.clear();
1565                            // Emote sets may have changed too — drop the cached counts
1566                            // so the launcher buttons re-enumerate.
1567                            self.channel_emote_counts.clear();
1568                            // Asset presence/variants/stamps moved — drop the cached
1569                            // status grid so it re-enumerates from disk on the next frame.
1570                            self.channel_asset_status.clear();
1571                            // Drop decoded emote frames so refreshed images re-decode
1572                            // from disk at their stable paths.
1573                            self.clear_emote_cache();
1574                            // If the emote viewer is open for this channel, its
1575                            // enumerated list was loaded once on open and is now
1576                            // stale — flag it so the window can show a re-open banner.
1577                            if let Some(v) = &self.emote_viewer
1578                                && v.channel_name == task.label
1579                            {
1580                                self.emote_viewer_stale = true;
1581                            }
1582                            // The asset-history view reads the change logs once on
1583                            // open; if it's showing this channel, reload it in place
1584                            // so freshly-recorded changes appear without a reopen.
1585                            if let Some(h) = &mut self.asset_history
1586                                && h.channel_name == task.label
1587                            {
1588                                h.reload();
1589                            }
1590                        }
1591                        self.finished_tasks.insert(0, (task, outcome, now_unix()));
1592                        self.finished_tasks.truncate(100);
1593                    }
1594                    dirty = true;
1595                }
1596                Ok(_) => dirty = true,
1597                Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::TryRecvError::Empty) => break,
1598                Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::TryRecvError::Lagged(_)) => continue,
1599                Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::TryRecvError::Closed) => break,
1600            }
1601        }
1602        if dirty {
1603            self.spawn_pending_reload();
1604            self.reload_videos();
1605            // Keep the Schedule calendar in sync with background schedule fetches
1606            // (which emit a state event) — but only once it has been loaded, so we
1607            // don't pull it in before the user ever opens the tab.
1608            if self.schedule_loaded {
1609                self.spawn_reload_schedule();
1610            }
1611            // Any event that marks the UI dirty may affect recording statuses —
1612            // force a refresh of the issues panel on the next frame it is open.
1613            self.issues_refreshed = None;
1614        }
1615    }
1616
1617    fn save_form(&mut self) {
1618        let Some(form) = self.form.as_ref() else {
1619            return;
1620        };
1621        // Validate before closing the form.
1622        if form.url.trim().is_empty() {
1623            self.status = "An instance URL is required.".into();
1624            return;
1625        }
1626        let new_channel_name: Option<String> = match form.channel_id {
1627            Some(_) => None, // existing container, no create needed
1628            None => {
1629                if form.name.trim().is_empty() {
1630                    self.status = "A channel name is required.".into();
1631                    return;
1632                }
1633                Some(form.name.trim().to_string())
1634            }
1635        };
1636        // Build the monitor value now; channel_id may be 0 when creating a new
1637        // container — the background thread overwrites it with the real id.
1638        let monitor = Monitor {
1639            id: form.monitor_id.unwrap_or(0),
1640            channel_id: form.channel_id.unwrap_or(0),
1641            url: form.url.trim().to_string(),
1642            enabled: form.enabled,
1643            tool: form.tool,
1644            detection_method: form.detection_method,
1645            poll_interval_secs: form.poll_interval_secs.max(5),
1646            quality: form.quality.clone(),
1647            output_dir: form.output_dir.clone(),
1648            filename_template: form.filename_template.clone(),
1649            container: form.container,
1650            capture_from_start: form.capture_from_start,
1651            dual_capture: form.dual_capture,
1652            ad_free: form.ad_free,
1653            auth_kind: form.auth_kind,
1654            auth_value: form.auth_value.clone(),
1655            audio_tracks: form.audio_tracks.trim().to_string(),
1656            subtitle_tracks: form.subtitle_tracks.trim().to_string(),
1657            chat_log: form.chat_log,
1658            fetch_thumbnail: form.fetch_thumbnail,
1659            thumbnail_in_toast: form.thumbnail_in_toast,
1660            fetch_chat_assets: form.fetch_chat_assets,
1661            extra_args: form.extra_args.clone(),
1662            max_concurrent: 1,
1663            last_checked_at: None,
1664            last_state: "idle".into(),
1665        };
1666        let monitor_id = form.monitor_id;
1667
1668        // Close the form immediately so the UI stays responsive while the DB
1669        // work runs. On a background-thread error the status bar shows the error;
1670        // the user can re-open Add/Edit to retry.
1671        self.form = None;
1672        self.status = "Saving…".into();
1673
1674        let store = self.core.store.clone();
1675        let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel();
1676        debug!("spawning save-monitor thread");
1677        std::thread::Builder::new()
1678            .name("save-monitor".into())
1679            .spawn(move || {
1680                let t = std::time::Instant::now();
1681                let result: Result<SaveRows, String> = (|| {
1682                    // Resolve the channel_id, creating a new container if needed.
1683                    let channel_id = match new_channel_name {
1684                        Some(name) => store.create_container(&name).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?,
1685                        None => monitor.channel_id,
1686                    };
1687                    let mut m = monitor;
1688                    m.channel_id = channel_id;
1689                    match monitor_id {
1690                        Some(_) => store.update_monitor(&m).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?,
1691                        None => {
1692                            store.insert_monitor(&m).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
1693                        }
1694                    }
1695                    let rows = store.list_monitors_with_channels().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
1696                    let next_streams =
1697                        store.next_scheduled_streams(now_unix()).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
1698                    let channels = store.list_channels().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
1699                    let yt_quota_today = store.get_quota_today("youtube").unwrap_or(0);
1700                    let yt_quota_cutoff = store
1701                        .get_setting(K_YT_API_QUOTA_CUTOFF)
1702                        .ok()
1703                        .flatten()
1704                        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse().ok())
1705                        .unwrap_or(9000);
1706                    let yt_search_today = store.get_quota_today("youtube_search").unwrap_or(0);
1707                    let yt_search_cutoff = store
1708                        .get_setting(K_YT_SEARCH_QUOTA_CUTOFF)
1709                        .ok()
1710                        .flatten()
1711                        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse().ok())
1712                        .unwrap_or(90);
1713                    Ok(SaveRows { rows, channels, next_streams, yt_quota_today, yt_quota_cutoff, yt_search_today, yt_search_cutoff })
1714                })();
1715                debug!(elapsed_ms = t.elapsed().as_millis(), ok = result.is_ok(), "save-monitor done");
1716                let _ = tx.send(result);
1717            })
1718            .ok();
1719        self.pending_save = Some(PendingSave { rx });
1720    }
1721
1722    /// Poll the in-flight file/folder picker. When the user confirms a selection
1723    /// the `apply` closure runs on the UI thread to install the chosen path.
1724    fn drain_pending_browse(&mut self) {
1725        let recv = match &self.pending_browse {
1726            Some(pb) => pb.rx.try_recv(),
1727            None => return,
1728        };
1729        match recv {
1730            Ok(maybe_path) => {
1731                let pb = self.pending_browse.take().unwrap();
1732                if let Some(path) = maybe_path {
1733                    (pb.apply)(self, path);
1734                }
1735            }
1736            Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {
1737                self.pending_browse = None;
1738            }
1739            Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Empty) => {}
1740        }
1741    }
1742
1743    /// Poll the in-flight save-form thread. Installs the loaded rows on success
1744    /// or shows an error in the status bar on failure.
1745    fn drain_pending_save(&mut self) {
1746        let recv = match &self.pending_save {
1747            Some(ps) => ps.rx.try_recv(),
1748            None => return,
1749        };
1750        match recv {
1751            Ok(Ok(save)) => {
1752                debug!(rows = save.rows.len(), channels = save.channels.len(), "save-monitor result installed");
1753                self.pending_save = None;
1754                self.status = "Saved.".into();
1755                self.install_save_rows(save);
1756            }
1757            Ok(Err(ref e)) => {
1758                warn!(error = %e, "save-monitor thread returned error");
1759                self.pending_save = None;
1760                self.status = format!("Error saving: {e}");
1761            }
1762            Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {
1763                warn!("save-monitor thread disconnected without sending a result");
1764                self.pending_save = None;
1765                self.status = "Save failed (internal error).".into();
1766            }
1767            Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Empty) => {}
1768        }
1769    }
1770
1771    /// Spawn a background thread that reads the current rows/channels from the
1772    /// store without writing anything.  The result is drained by
1773    /// `drain_pending_reload` inside `logic()`.  If a reload is already in
1774    /// flight, the new request is silently dropped (the in-flight one will
1775    /// provide up-to-date data).
1776    fn spawn_pending_reload(&mut self) {
1777        if self.pending_reload.is_some() {
1778            return;
1779        }
1780        let store = self.core.store.clone();
1781        let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel();
1782        debug!("spawning reload-rows thread");
1783        std::thread::Builder::new()
1784            .name("reload-rows".into())
1785            .spawn(move || {
1786                let t = std::time::Instant::now();
1787                let result = (|| -> Option<SaveRows> {
1788                    let rows = store.list_monitors_with_channels().ok()?;
1789                    let next_streams = store.next_scheduled_streams(crate::models::now_unix()).ok()?;
1790                    let channels = store.list_channels().ok()?;
1791                    let yt_quota_today = store.get_quota_today("youtube").unwrap_or(0);
1792                    let yt_quota_cutoff = store
1793                        .get_setting(K_YT_API_QUOTA_CUTOFF)
1794                        .ok()
1795                        .flatten()
1796                        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse().ok())
1797                        .unwrap_or(9000);
1798                    let yt_search_today = store.get_quota_today("youtube_search").unwrap_or(0);
1799                    let yt_search_cutoff = store
1800                        .get_setting(K_YT_SEARCH_QUOTA_CUTOFF)
1801                        .ok()
1802                        .flatten()
1803                        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse().ok())
1804                        .unwrap_or(90);
1805                    Some(SaveRows { rows, channels, next_streams, yt_quota_today, yt_quota_cutoff, yt_search_today, yt_search_cutoff })
1806                })();
1807                debug!(
1808                    elapsed_ms = t.elapsed().as_millis(),
1809                    ok = result.is_some(),
1810                    "reload-rows done"
1811                );
1812                let _ = tx.send(result);
1813            })
1814            .ok();
1815        self.pending_reload = Some(rx);
1816    }
1817
1818    fn drain_pending_reload(&mut self) {
1819        let recv = match &self.pending_reload {
1820            Some(rx) => rx.try_recv(),
1821            None => return,
1822        };
1823        match recv {
1824            Ok(Some(save)) => {
1825                debug!(rows = save.rows.len(), "reload-rows result installed");
1826                self.install_save_rows(save);
1827                self.status = "Refreshed.".into();
1828                self.pending_reload = None;
1829            }
1830            Ok(None) => {
1831                warn!("reload-rows thread produced no data");
1832                self.status = "Refresh failed (DB error).".into();
1833                self.pending_reload = None;
1834            }
1835            Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {
1836                warn!("reload-rows thread disconnected without sending");
1837                self.pending_reload = None;
1838            }
1839            Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Empty) => {}
1840        }
1841    }
1842
1843    /// Install a completed save's rows into the UI, mirroring `reload_rows`.
1844    fn install_save_rows(&mut self, save: SaveRows) {
1845        // Capture existing channel ids before replacing, to detect the new one.
1846        let old_channel_ids: HashSet<i64> = self.channels.iter().map(|c| c.id).collect();
1847        self.rows = save.rows;
1848        let by_mid: HashMap<i64, (i64, String)> = save
1849            .next_streams
1850            .into_iter()
1851            .map(|(mid, at, title)| (mid, (at, title)))
1852            .collect();
1853        for row in &mut self.rows {
1854            if let Some((at, title)) = by_mid.get(&row.monitor.id) {
1855                row.next_stream_at = Some(*at);
1856                row.next_stream_title = title.clone();
1857            }
1858        }
1859        self.channels = save.channels;
1860        self.yt_quota_today = save.yt_quota_today;
1861        self.yt_quota_cutoff = save.yt_quota_cutoff;
1862        self.yt_search_today = save.yt_search_today;
1863        self.yt_search_cutoff = save.yt_search_cutoff;
1864        // Scroll to the newly-added channel on the next render so it's visible
1865        // regardless of where it lands in the alphabetically-sorted list.
1866        if let Some(new_ch) = self.channels.iter().find(|c| !old_channel_ids.contains(&c.id)) {
1867            self.scroll_to_channel = Some(new_ch.id);
1868        }
1869        let live_channels: HashSet<i64> = self.channels.iter().map(|c| c.id).collect();
1870        let live_monitors: HashSet<i64> = self.rows.iter().map(|r| r.monitor.id).collect();
1871        self.rec_cache.retain(|k, _| live_monitors.contains(k));
1872        self.ad_break_cache.retain(|k, _| live_monitors.contains(k));
1873        self.meta_change_cache.retain(|k, _| live_monitors.contains(k));
1874        self.schedule_cache.retain(|k, _| live_monitors.contains(k));
1875        self.expanded_channels.retain(|id| live_channels.contains(id));
1876        self.expanded_instances.retain(|id| live_monitors.contains(id));
1877        self.expanded_streams
1878            .retain(|k| stream_key_monitor(k).is_some_and(|mid| live_monitors.contains(&mid)));
1879    }
1880
1881    fn save_settings(&mut self) {
1882        let s = &self.settings;
1883        // Discord import counts as on only when a token backs the toggle.
1884        let discord_on = s.discord_schedule && !s.discord_token.trim().is_empty();
1885        // Persist the browser + optional profile as one `browser:profile` value.
1886        let cookies_value = compose_browser_profile(&s.cookies_browser, &s.cookies_profile);
1887        let pairs = [
1888            (K_TWITCH_ID, s.twitch_client_id.trim()),
1889            (K_TWITCH_SECRET, s.twitch_client_secret.trim()),
1890            (google_oauth::K_CLIENT_ID, s.google_client_id.trim()),
1891            (google_oauth::K_CLIENT_SECRET, s.google_client_secret.trim()),
1892            (K_YT_KEY, s.youtube_api_key.trim()),
1893            (K_KICK_ID, s.kick_client_id.trim()),
1894            (K_KICK_SECRET, s.kick_client_secret.trim()),
1895            (K_DEFAULT_OUT, s.default_output_dir.trim()),
1896            (K_MAX_CONCURRENT, s.max_concurrent_downloads.trim()),
1897            (K_DOWNLOAD_AUTH, s.download_auth_method.trim()),
1898            (K_COOKIES_BROWSER, cookies_value.as_str()),
1899            (K_WEBSUB_URL, s.websub_vps_url.trim()),
1900            (K_WEBSUB_TOKEN, s.websub_token.trim()),
1901            (K_WEBSUB_POLL, s.websub_poll_secs.trim()),
1902            (K_FILENAME_MEDIA, s.filename_media_info.as_str()),
1903            (K_DATE_FORMAT, s.date_fmt.as_str()),
1904            (K_SHORT_TS_FMT, s.short_ts_fmt.trim()),
1905            (K_YT_API_DETECT, if s.youtube_api_detect { "1" } else { "0" }),
1906            (K_YT_API_SCHEDULE, if s.youtube_api_schedule { "1" } else { "0" }),
1907            (K_YT_API_QUOTA_CUTOFF, s.youtube_api_quota_cutoff.trim()),
1908            (K_YT_SEARCH_QUOTA_CUTOFF, s.youtube_search_quota_cutoff.trim()),
1909            (K_YTDLP_ARGS, s.ytdlp_default_args.trim()),
1910            (K_YTDLP_BINARY, s.ytdlp_binary_path.trim()),
1911            (K_SABR_BINARY, s.sabr_binary_path.trim()),
1912            (K_SABR_ENABLED, if s.sabr_enabled { "1" } else { "0" }),
1913            (K_SABR_FORMAT, s.sabr_format.trim()),
1914            (K_SABR_EXTRACTOR_ARGS, s.sabr_extractor_args.trim()),
1915            (K_SABR_DEEP_REWIND, if s.sabr_deep_rewind { "1" } else { "0" }),
1916            (K_SABR_RAW_ARGS, s.sabr_raw_args.trim()),
1917            (K_SABR_POT_ARGS, s.sabr_pot_args.trim()),
1918            (K_DASH_FORMAT, s.dash_format.trim()),
1919            (K_DISCORD_TOKEN, s.discord_token.trim()),
1920            // Only persist the import as on when a token actually backs it, so the
1921            // consent flag can't be left latched with no token.
1922            (
1923                K_DISCORD_SCHEDULE,
1924                if discord_on { "1" } else { "0" },
1925            ),
1926            (K_OCR_COMMAND, s.ocr_command.trim()),
1927            (K_OCR_MODEL, s.ocr_model.trim()),
1928            (K_OCR_FALLBACK_MODEL, s.ocr_fallback_model.trim()),
1929            (K_OCR_TIMEZONE, s.ocr_timezone.trim()),
1930            (K_OCR_OFFSET, s.ocr_offset.trim()),
1931            (K_OCR_MAX_BUDGET, s.ocr_max_budget.trim()),
1932            (K_OCR_TIMEOUT_SECS, s.ocr_timeout_secs.trim()),
1933            (K_OCR_EFFORT, s.ocr_effort.trim()),
1934            (
1935                K_SCHEDULE_TITLE_FILL,
1936                if s.schedule_title_fill { "1" } else { "0" },
1937            ),
1938            (
1939                K_YT_COMMUNITY_MAX_POSTS,
1940                s.youtube_community_max_posts.trim(),
1941            ),
1942            (K_DIALOG_ICON, s.dialog_icon.trim()),
1943            (K_REMUX_EMBED_THUMBNAIL, if s.remux_embed_thumbnail { "1" } else { "0" }),
1944            (K_REMUX_EMBED_TITLE,     if s.remux_embed_title     { "1" } else { "0" }),
1945            (K_REMUX_TITLE_TEMPLATE, s.remux_title_template.trim()),
1946            (K_REMUX_EMBED_SUBS,      if s.remux_embed_subs      { "1" } else { "0" }),
1947            (K_FILE_SPLIT_ENABLED,  if s.file_split_enabled { "1" } else { "0" }),
1948            (K_FILE_SPLIT_VIDEOS, s.file_split_videos.trim()),
1949            (K_FILE_SPLIT_SUBS,   s.file_split_subs.trim()),
1950            (K_FILE_SPLIT_CHAT,   s.file_split_chat.trim()),
1951            (K_FILE_SPLIT_THUMBS, s.file_split_thumbs.trim()),
1952            (K_FILE_SPLIT_LOGS,   s.file_split_logs.trim()),
1953            (K_MEDIA_PLAYER, s.media_player_path.trim()),
1954        ];
1955        for (k, v) in pairs {
1956            if let Err(e) = self.core.store.set_setting(k, v) {
1957                self.status = format!("Error saving settings: {e}");
1958                return;
1959            }
1960        }
1961        // If Discord import is now off (toggled off or token cleared), purge any
1962        // previously-imported Discord events so they don't linger on the calendar.
1963        if !discord_on {
1964            let _ = self.core.store.clear_schedule_source("discord");
1965            self.spawn_reload_schedule();
1966        }
1967        // Apply the (possibly changed) date format + short-timestamp pattern to the live UI.
1968        set_active_date_fmt(self.settings.date_fmt);
1969        set_short_ts_pattern(&self.settings.short_ts_fmt);
1970        self.persist_monitor_defaults();
1971        self.status = "Settings saved.".into();
1972    }
1973}
1974
1975fn setting_or_empty(core: &AppCore, key: &str) -> String {
1976    core.store
1977        .get_setting(key)
1978        .ok()
1979        .flatten()
1980        .unwrap_or_default()
1981}
1982
1983/// Predefined filename-template presets shown in the preset dropdowns.
1984/// `(display_label, template_string)`
1985const FILENAME_PRESETS: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
1986    ("Name + date",                "{name}_{date}_{time}"),
1987    ("Name + title + date",        "{name}_{title}_{date}_{time}"),
1988    ("Name + date + title",        "{name}_{date}_{time}_{title}"),
1989    ("Name + date + title + game", "{name}_{date}_{time}_{title}_{games}"),
1990    ("Date + name",                "{date}_{time}_{name}"),
1991    ("Date + name + title",        "{date}_{time}_{name}_{title}"),
1992    ("Date + name + title + game", "{date}_{time}_{name}_{title}_{games}"),
1993];
1994
1995/// Render a filename-template preset ComboBox with both built-in and user-defined
1996/// presets. Selecting a preset writes its template into `template`.
1997///
1998/// Returns `(delete_id, open_save)`:
1999/// - `delete_id` — a custom preset the user clicked "×" on (caller should delete + reload)
2000/// - `open_save` — the 💾 button was clicked (caller should open the save-preset dialog)
2001fn filename_preset_combo(
2002    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
2003    id_salt: &str,
2004    template: &mut String,
2005    custom_presets: &[(i64, String, String)],
2006) -> (Option<i64>, bool) {
2007    let current = FILENAME_PRESETS
2008        .iter()
2009        .find(|(_, t)| *t == template.as_str())
2010        .map(|(l, _)| *l)
2011        .or_else(|| {
2012            custom_presets
2013                .iter()
2014                .find(|(_, _, t)| t.as_str() == template.as_str())
2015                .map(|(_, n, _)| n.as_str())
2016        })
2017        .unwrap_or("Manual");
2018    let mut delete_id: Option<i64> = None;
2019    egui::ComboBox::from_id_salt(id_salt)
2020        .selected_text(current)
2021        .width(160.0)
2022        .show_ui(ui, |ui| {
2023            for &(label, tmpl) in FILENAME_PRESETS {
2024                if ui.selectable_label(template.as_str() == tmpl, label).clicked() {
2025                    *template = tmpl.to_string();
2026                }
2027            }
2028            if !custom_presets.is_empty() {
2029                ui.separator();
2030                ui.add(egui::Label::new(egui::RichText::new("My presets").weak().small()));
2031                for (id, name, tmpl) in custom_presets {
2032                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
2033                        if ui
2034                            .selectable_label(template.as_str() == tmpl.as_str(), name.as_str())
2035                            .clicked()
2036                        {
2037                            *template = tmpl.clone();
2038                        }
2039                        if ui
2040                            .small_button("×")
2041                            .on_hover_text("Delete this preset")
2042                            .clicked()
2043                        {
2044                            delete_id = Some(*id);
2045                        }
2046                    });
2047                }
2048            }
2049        });
2050    let open_save = ui
2051        .button("💾")
2052        .on_hover_text("Save current template as a named preset")
2053        .clicked();
2054    (delete_id, open_save)
2055}
2056
2057/// Coarse human duration: `45s` / `5m` / `6h` / `2d`.
2058fn parse_capture_mode(fname: &str) -> Option<String> {
2059    let marker = " (p ";
2060    let start = fname.find(marker)? + marker.len();
2061    let end = fname[start..].find(')')?;
2062    let mode = fname[start..start + end].trim();
2063    if mode.is_empty() { None } else { Some(mode.to_string()) }
2064}
2065
2066fn fmt_duration_secs(secs: i64) -> String {
2067    let s = secs.max(0);
2068    if s < 90 {
2069        format!("{s}s")
2070    } else if s < 90 * 60 {
2071        format!("{}m", (s + 30) / 60)
2072    } else if s < 36 * 3600 {
2073        format!("{}h", (s + 1800) / 3600)
2074    } else {
2075        format!("{}d", (s + 12 * 3600) / 86_400)
2076    }
2077}
2078
2079/// `now` / `in 3m` for a future delta in seconds.
2080fn fmt_relative_future(delta: i64) -> String {
2081    if delta <= 0 {
2082        "now".to_string()
2083    } else {
2084        format!("in {}", fmt_duration_secs(delta))
2085    }
2086}
2087
2088/// Label a take as "SABR"/"DASH" when it's part of a dual capture (two recordings
2089/// sharing a `take_group`). Returns `None` for ordinary single-recording takes.
2090fn dual_take_variant(g: &StreamGroup, t: &Recording) -> Option<&'static str> {
2091    // Only label takes that belong to a multi-recording (dual) capture cluster.
2092    let in_dual = g
2093        .take_groups()
2094        .iter()
2095        .any(|grp| grp.len() >= 2 && grp.iter().any(|r| r.id == t.id));
2096    if !in_dual {
2097        return None;
2098    }
2099    if t.output_path.contains(".dash.") {
2100        Some("DASH")
2101    } else {
2102        Some("SABR")
2103    }
2104}
2105
2106/// Split a stored `--cookies-from-browser` value into `(browser, profile)`.
2107/// `profile` is everything after the first `:` — a profile/session name or an
2108/// absolute path (which may itself contain a `:` drive letter, hence split-once).
2109/// yt-dlp parses the same way. Empty profile when there's no `:`.
2110fn split_browser_profile(raw: &str) -> (String, String) {
2111    match raw.split_once(':') {
2112        Some((b, p)) => (b.trim().to_string(), p.trim().to_string()),
2113        None => (raw.trim().to_string(), String::new()),
2114    }
2115}
2116
2117/// Compose a `--cookies-from-browser` value from a browser + optional profile
2118/// (`firefox` or `firefox:<profile>`). Empty browser → empty (no cookies).
2119fn compose_browser_profile(browser: &str, profile: &str) -> String {
2120    let b = browser.trim();
2121    let p = profile.trim();
2122    if b.is_empty() {
2123        String::new()
2124    } else if p.is_empty() {
2125        b.to_string()
2126    } else {
2127        format!("{b}:{p}")
2128    }
2129}
2130
2131/// User-selectable display format for dates/timestamps (the Settings "Date
2132/// format" control). Read globally via [`active_date_fmt`] so the free-function
2133/// formatters can honor it without threading the setting through every call site.
2134#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default, Debug)]
2135enum DateFmt {
2136    /// ISO 8601-style `2026-06-21` / `2026-06-21 14:02:33` (the default).
2137    #[default]
2138    Iso,
2139    /// ISO without seconds: `2026-06-21 14:02`.
2140    IsoNoSecs,
2141    /// US `06/21/2026` / `06/21/2026 02:02 PM`.
2142    Us,
2143    /// European `21.06.2026` / `21.06.2026 14:02`.
2144    Eu,
2145    /// Compact, year-less `06-21` / `06-21 14:02:33` (narrowest).
2146    Compact,
2147}
2148
2149impl DateFmt {
2150    const ALL: [DateFmt; 5] = [
2151        DateFmt::Iso,
2152        DateFmt::IsoNoSecs,
2153        DateFmt::Us,
2154        DateFmt::Eu,
2155        DateFmt::Compact,
2156    ];
2157
2158    fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
2159        match self {
2160            DateFmt::Iso => "iso",
2161            DateFmt::IsoNoSecs => "iso_no_secs",
2162            DateFmt::Us => "us",
2163            DateFmt::Eu => "eu",
2164            DateFmt::Compact => "compact",
2165        }
2166    }
2167
2168    fn parse(s: &str) -> DateFmt {
2169        match s {
2170            "iso_no_secs" => DateFmt::IsoNoSecs,
2171            "us" => DateFmt::Us,
2172            "eu" => DateFmt::Eu,
2173            "compact" => DateFmt::Compact,
2174            _ => DateFmt::Iso,
2175        }
2176    }
2177
2178    /// chrono pattern for a date-only value.
2179    fn date_pattern(self) -> &'static str {
2180        match self {
2181            DateFmt::Iso | DateFmt::IsoNoSecs => "%Y-%m-%d",
2182            DateFmt::Us => "%m/%d/%Y",
2183            DateFmt::Eu => "%d.%m.%Y",
2184            DateFmt::Compact => "%m-%d",
2185        }
2186    }
2187
2188    /// chrono pattern for a full timestamp.
2189    fn datetime_pattern(self) -> &'static str {
2190        match self {
2191            DateFmt::Iso => "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
2192            DateFmt::IsoNoSecs => "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M",
2193            DateFmt::Us => "%m/%d/%Y %I:%M %p",
2194            DateFmt::Eu => "%d.%m.%Y %H:%M",
2195            DateFmt::Compact => "%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
2196        }
2197    }
2198
2199    /// chrono pattern for a time-only value (12-hour for US, else 24-hour). Used
2200    /// by the Schedule calendar chips, which only have room for the time.
2201    fn time_pattern(self) -> &'static str {
2202        match self {
2203            DateFmt::Us => "%I:%M %p",
2204            _ => "%H:%M",
2205        }
2206    }
2207
2208    fn label(self) -> &'static str {
2209        match self {
2210            DateFmt::Iso => "ISO — 2026-06-21 14:02:33",
2211            DateFmt::IsoNoSecs => "ISO, no seconds — 2026-06-21 14:02",
2212            DateFmt::Us => "US — 06/21/2026 02:02 PM",
2213            DateFmt::Eu => "EU — 21.06.2026 14:02",
2214            DateFmt::Compact => "Compact — 06-21 14:02:33",
2215        }
2216    }
2217}
2218
2219/// The active [`DateFmt`] discriminant (index into [`DateFmt::ALL`]). The UI runs
2220/// single-threaded; this is a cheap shared cell set at startup and on save so the
2221/// formatters below don't need the setting passed in.
2222static ACTIVE_DATE_FMT: std::sync::atomic::AtomicU8 = std::sync::atomic::AtomicU8::new(0);
2223
2224fn active_date_fmt() -> DateFmt {
2225    let i = ACTIVE_DATE_FMT.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed) as usize;
2226    DateFmt::ALL.get(i).copied().unwrap_or(DateFmt::Iso)
2227}
2228
2229fn set_active_date_fmt(f: DateFmt) {
2230    let i = DateFmt::ALL.iter().position(|&x| x == f).unwrap_or(0) as u8;
2231    ACTIVE_DATE_FMT.store(i, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);
2232}
2233
2234/// Whether the "compact timestamps" mode is active. Set at startup and when the
2235/// top-bar toggle changes so formatters don't need the flag threaded through.
2236static SHORT_TS_ENABLED: std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool =
2237    std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool::new(false);
2238
2239/// The compact timestamp pattern (e.g. `"%d/%m %H:%M"`). Protected by a mutex so
2240/// it can be changed at runtime without a full restart.
2241static SHORT_TS_PAT: std::sync::OnceLock<std::sync::Mutex<String>> =
2242    std::sync::OnceLock::new();
2243
2244fn short_ts_on() -> bool {
2245    SHORT_TS_ENABLED.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed)
2246}
2247
2248fn set_short_ts(on: bool) {
2249    SHORT_TS_ENABLED.store(on, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);
2250}
2251
2252fn short_ts_pattern() -> String {
2253    SHORT_TS_PAT
2254        .get_or_init(|| std::sync::Mutex::new("%d/%m %H:%M".to_string()))
2255        .lock()
2256        .unwrap()
2257        .clone()
2258}
2259
2260fn set_short_ts_pattern(pat: &str) {
2261    *SHORT_TS_PAT
2262        .get_or_init(|| std::sync::Mutex::new("%d/%m %H:%M".to_string()))
2263        .lock()
2264        .unwrap() = pat.to_string();
2265}
2266
2267/// Compact variant of [`fmt_datetime_short`] — uses [`short_ts_pattern`] instead of the
2268/// active [`DateFmt`]. Never checks [`short_ts_on`]; call it only when you want compact.
2269fn fmt_datetime_compact(secs: i64) -> String {
2270    if secs <= 0 {
2271        return String::new();
2272    }
2273    let pat = short_ts_pattern();
2274    chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(secs, 0)
2275        .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&chrono::Local).format(&pat).to_string())
2276        .unwrap_or_default()
2277}
2278
2279/// Format a unix timestamp as a local date in the active [`DateFmt`] (empty if
2280/// unset).
2281fn fmt_date(secs: i64) -> String {
2282    if secs <= 0 {
2283        return String::new();
2284    }
2285    chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(secs, 0)
2286        .map(|dt| {
2287            dt.with_timezone(&chrono::Local)
2288                .format(active_date_fmt().date_pattern())
2289                .to_string()
2290        })
2291        .unwrap_or_default()
2292}
2293
2294/// Local timestamp in the active [`DateFmt`] (empty if unset). Used for the
2295/// Polled / Went Live / Started On columns and the history tree.
2296/// A clickable row in the "Schedule sources" dialog's Available column: the
2297/// source label plus a `⚠` badge for risky sources. Returns the click response.
2298fn source_row(ui: &mut egui::Ui, kind: ScheduleSourceKind, selected: bool) -> egui::Response {
2299    let mut text = kind.label().to_string();
2300    if kind.risky() {
2301        text.push_str("  ⚠");
2302    }
2303    ui.selectable_label(selected, text)
2304        .on_hover_text(kind.description())
2305}
2306
2307/// Like [`source_row`] but prefixed with the 1-based priority rank, for the
2308/// Enabled column.
2309fn source_row_ranked(
2310    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
2311    rank: usize,
2312    kind: ScheduleSourceKind,
2313    selected: bool,
2314) -> egui::Response {
2315    let mut text = format!("{rank}.  {}", kind.label());
2316    if kind.risky() {
2317        text.push_str("  ⚠");
2318    }
2319    ui.selectable_label(selected, text)
2320        .on_hover_text(kind.description())
2321}
2322
2323/// Compact, embeddable schedule-source list editor: one row per source in
2324/// priority order with an enable checkbox and ▲/▼ reorder buttons. Used by the
2325/// per-channel / per-instance scope override (the big two-column dialog stays for
2326/// the global order). Returns true if `entries` changed. Unknown ids are skipped
2327/// but kept in place.
2328fn source_list_inline_editor(ui: &mut egui::Ui, entries: &mut [SourceEntry]) -> bool {
2329    let mut changed = false;
2330    let mut move_up: Option<usize> = None;
2331    let mut move_down: Option<usize> = None;
2332    let n = entries.len();
2333    egui::Frame::group(ui.style()).show(ui, |ui| {
2334        for (i, entry) in entries.iter_mut().enumerate() {
2335            let Some(kind) = entry.kind() else { continue };
2336            ui.horizontal(|ui| {
2337                if ui.checkbox(&mut entry.enabled, "").changed() {
2338                    changed = true;
2339                }
2340                if ui
2341                    .add_enabled(i > 0, egui::Button::new("▲").small())
2342                    .on_hover_text("Higher priority")
2343                    .clicked()
2344                {
2345                    move_up = Some(i);
2346                }
2347                if ui
2348                    .add_enabled(i + 1 < n, egui::Button::new("▼").small())
2349                    .on_hover_text("Lower priority")
2350                    .clicked()
2351                {
2352                    move_down = Some(i);
2353                }
2354                let mut label = egui::RichText::new(kind.label());
2355                if !entry.enabled {
2356                    label = label.weak();
2357                }
2358                ui.label(label).on_hover_text(kind.description());
2359                if kind.risky() {
2360                    ui.colored_label(egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xe0, 0x6c, 0x6c), "⚠");
2361                }
2362            });
2363        }
2364    });
2365    if let Some(i) = move_up {
2366        entries.swap(i, i - 1);
2367        changed = true;
2368    }
2369    if let Some(i) = move_down {
2370        entries.swap(i, i + 1);
2371        changed = true;
2372    }
2373    changed
2374}
2375
2376/// Per-channel / per-instance schedule-source scope override editor: an
2377/// Inherit-vs-Custom source-order toggle (with an inline reorderable list when
2378/// Custom) plus a tri-state title-fill override (Inherit / On / Off). `global_order`
2379/// seeds a freshly-switched-on custom list. Returns true if `scope` changed.
2380fn scope_override_editor(
2381    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
2382    scope: &mut crate::schedule_source::SourceScopeConfig,
2383    global_order: &[SourceEntry],
2384) -> bool {
2385    let mut changed = false;
2386
2387    ui.label("Source order");
2388    let custom = scope.order.is_some();
2389    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
2390        if ui
2391            .radio(!custom, "Inherit global")
2392            .on_hover_text("Use the global source order from Settings → Schedule sources.")
2393            .clicked()
2394            && custom
2395        {
2396            scope.order = None;
2397            changed = true;
2398        }
2399        if ui
2400            .radio(custom, "Custom")
2401            .on_hover_text("Override the source order/enabled set just for this scope.")
2402            .clicked()
2403            && !custom
2404        {
2405            // Seed the custom list from the current global order.
2406            scope.order = Some(global_order.to_vec());
2407            changed = true;
2408        }
2409    });
2410    if let Some(order) = scope.order.as_mut() {
2411        if source_list_inline_editor(ui, order) {
2412            changed = true;
2413        }
2414    }
2415
2416    ui.add_space(4.0);
2417    ui.label("Fill blank titles from next source");
2418    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
2419        if ui.radio(scope.title_fill.is_none(), "Inherit").clicked() && scope.title_fill.is_some() {
2420            scope.title_fill = None;
2421            changed = true;
2422        }
2423        if ui.radio(scope.title_fill == Some(true), "On").clicked()
2424            && scope.title_fill != Some(true)
2425        {
2426            scope.title_fill = Some(true);
2427            changed = true;
2428        }
2429        if ui.radio(scope.title_fill == Some(false), "Off").clicked()
2430            && scope.title_fill != Some(false)
2431        {
2432            scope.title_fill = Some(false);
2433            changed = true;
2434        }
2435    });
2436
2437    changed
2438}
2439
2440fn fmt_datetime_short(secs: i64) -> String {
2441    if secs <= 0 {
2442        return String::new();
2443    }
2444    chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(secs, 0)
2445        .map(|dt| {
2446            dt.with_timezone(&chrono::Local)
2447                .format(active_date_fmt().datetime_pattern())
2448                .to_string()
2449        })
2450        .unwrap_or_default()
2451}
2452
2453/// "Polled" cell text: the last-checked timestamp with the poll interval in
2454/// parentheses, e.g. `2026-06-21 14:02:33 (60s)`. When never polled, shows just
2455/// the interval `(60s)` so the configured cadence is still visible.
2456fn fmt_polled(last_checked: Option<i64>, interval_secs: i64) -> String {
2457    let secs = last_checked.unwrap_or(0);
2458    if short_ts_on() {
2459        // Compact: HH:MM only — no date, no interval (full info on hover).
2460        if secs <= 0 {
2461            return String::new();
2462        }
2463        chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(secs, 0)
2464            .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&chrono::Local).format("%H:%M").to_string())
2465            .unwrap_or_default()
2466    } else {
2467        let when = fmt_datetime_short(secs);
2468        if when.is_empty() {
2469            format!("({interval_secs}s)")
2470        } else {
2471            format!("{when} ({interval_secs}s)")
2472        }
2473    }
2474}
2475
2476/// Format a duration in seconds as `HH:MM:SS`.
2477fn fmt_duration(secs: i64) -> String {
2478    let s = secs.max(0);
2479    format!("{:02}:{:02}:{:02}", s / 3600, (s % 3600) / 60, s % 60)
2480}
2481
2482/// Local time-of-day for a unix timestamp in the active [`DateFmt`] (e.g. `14:02`
2483/// or `02:02 PM`). Empty if unset. Used by the Schedule calendar chips.
2484fn fmt_time_short(secs: i64) -> String {
2485    if secs <= 0 {
2486        return String::new();
2487    }
2488    chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(secs, 0)
2489        .map(|dt| {
2490            dt.with_timezone(&chrono::Local)
2491                .format(active_date_fmt().time_pattern())
2492                .to_string()
2493        })
2494        .unwrap_or_default()
2495}
2496
2497/// The local calendar date a unix timestamp falls on (for bucketing schedule
2498/// entries into calendar cells).
2499fn local_date(secs: i64) -> Option<chrono::NaiveDate> {
2500    chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(secs, 0).map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&chrono::Local).date_naive())
2501}
2502
2503/// Split a unix timestamp into local `("YYYY-MM-DD", "HH:MM")` for the
2504/// Edit-schedule dialog fields. Empty pair on an out-of-range timestamp.
2505fn split_local_datetime(unix: i64) -> (String, String) {
2506    match chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(unix, 0) {
2507        Some(dt) => {
2508            let local = dt.with_timezone(&chrono::Local);
2509            (
2510                local.format("%Y-%m-%d").to_string(),
2511                local.format("%H:%M").to_string(),
2512            )
2513        }
2514        None => (String::new(), String::new()),
2515    }
2516}
2517
2518/// Parse local `YYYY-MM-DD` + `HH:MM` (or `HH:MM:SS`) into unix seconds in the
2519/// machine's local timezone. `None` on malformed input or a nonexistent/ambiguous
2520/// local time (a DST gap/overlap), so the Edit dialog can show a validation error.
2521fn parse_local_datetime(date: &str, time: &str) -> Option<i64> {
2522    use chrono::{NaiveDate, NaiveTime, TimeZone};
2523    let d = NaiveDate::parse_from_str(date.trim(), "%Y-%m-%d").ok()?;
2524    let t = NaiveTime::parse_from_str(time.trim(), "%H:%M")
2525        .or_else(|_| NaiveTime::parse_from_str(time.trim(), "%H:%M:%S"))
2526        .ok()?;
2527    chrono::Local
2528        .from_local_datetime(&d.and_time(t))
2529        .single()
2530        .map(|dt| dt.timestamp())
2531}
2532
2533/// Build a filename preview for the Format Designer using real monitor/recording
2534/// data. Media info is synthetic (1920×1080/60fps/h264/aac) since probing requires
2535/// async work the UI thread doesn't do. Extension is NOT included.
2536fn build_preview_filename(
2537    monitor: &MonitorWithChannel,
2538    recording: Option<&Recording>,
2539    template: &str,
2540) -> String {
2541    let ch_name = monitor.channel.name.as_str();
2542    let platform_s = monitor.monitor.platform().as_str().to_string();
2543    let tool_s = monitor.monitor.tool.label().to_string();
2544    let quality_s = monitor.monitor.quality.clone();
2545    let take_s = (monitor.recording_count + 1).to_string();
2546    let mode_s = match monitor.monitor.tool {
2547        Tool::Streamlink => "live".to_string(),
2548        Tool::Ffmpeg => "direct".to_string(),
2549        Tool::YtDlp => {
2550            if monitor.monitor.platform() == Platform::YouTube {
2551                if monitor.monitor.capture_from_start { "sabr".to_string() } else { "dash".to_string() }
2552            } else {
2553                "live".to_string()
2554            }
2555        }
2556    };
2557    let (started_at, went_live, stream_id_s, title_s, games_s) = match recording {
2558        Some(r) => (
2559            r.started_at,
2560            r.went_live_at.unwrap_or(0),
2561            r.stream_id.clone().unwrap_or_default(),
2562            r.title.clone(),
2563            r.category.clone(),
2564        ),
2565        None => (now_unix(), 0i64, String::new(), "Stream Title".to_string(), "Sample Game".to_string()),
2566    };
2567    let vars = crate::downloader::TemplateVars {
2568        name: ch_name,
2569        title: &title_s,
2570        channel: ch_name,
2571        video_id: &stream_id_s,
2572        quality: &quality_s,
2573        resolution: "1920x1080",
2574        height: "1080",
2575        width: "1920",
2576        fps: "60",
2577        vcodec: "h264",
2578        acodec: "aac",
2579        tool: &tool_s,
2580        mode: &mode_s,
2581        platform: &platform_s,
2582        take: &take_s,
2583        games: &games_s,
2584        secs: started_at,
2585        went_live,
2586    };
2587    crate::downloader::preview_filename(template, &vars)
2588}
2589
2590/// Unix seconds at the start (00:00 local) of today. Used to load schedule
2591/// entries from the beginning of today so today's full day shows in the calendar.
2592fn today_start_unix() -> i64 {
2593    use chrono::TimeZone;
2594    let now = chrono::Local::now();
2595    let today = now.date_naive();
2596    let at = |h: u32| {
2597        today
2598            .and_hms_opt(h, 0, 0)
2599            .and_then(|t| chrono::Local.from_local_datetime(&t).earliest())
2600            .map(|dt| dt.timestamp())
2601    };
2602    // Local midnight, falling back to 01:00 when midnight lands in a DST
2603    // spring-forward gap (a handful of zones transition at 00:00), and finally to
2604    // `now` if even that doesn't resolve — always ≤ now so "today" stays inclusive.
2605    at(0).or_else(|| at(1)).unwrap_or_else(|| now.timestamp())
2606}
2607
2608/// When a scheduled stream has no end time (YouTube upcoming streams don't carry
2609/// one), assume this duration when checking for time collisions.
2610const COLLISION_DEFAULT_SECS: i64 = 2 * 3600;
2611
2612/// The effective end time of a stream: its stated end if valid, else 2 hours past start.
2613/// Used by collision detection and the time-grid painter.
2614fn effective_end(s: &UpcomingStream) -> i64 {
2615    s.end_time
2616        .filter(|&e| e > s.start_time)
2617        .unwrap_or(s.start_time + COLLISION_DEFAULT_SECS)
2618}
2619
2620/// Format a time range for display: "HH:MM – HH:MM" when end is valid, else "HH:MM".
2621fn fmt_time_range(start: i64, end: Option<i64>) -> String {
2622    let s = fmt_time_short(start);
2623    match end.filter(|&e| e > start) {
2624        Some(e) => format!("{s} – {}", fmt_time_short(e)),
2625        None => s,
2626    }
2627}
2628
2629/// 16-color palette for calendar event blocks. Indexed by `channel_id % 16` so each
2630/// channel gets a consistent, distinct color across all schedule views.
2631/// Parse a CSS-style hex color string (`"#rrggbb"` or `"rrggbb"`) into an egui
2632/// color. Returns `None` for any other input so callers can fall back gracefully.
2633fn parse_hex_color(s: &str) -> Option<egui::Color32> {
2634    let s = s.trim().trim_start_matches('#');
2635    if s.len() == 6 {
2636        let r = u8::from_str_radix(&s[0..2], 16).ok()?;
2637        let g = u8::from_str_radix(&s[2..4], 16).ok()?;
2638        let b = u8::from_str_radix(&s[4..6], 16).ok()?;
2639        Some(egui::Color32::from_rgb(r, g, b))
2640    } else {
2641        None
2642    }
2643}
2644
2645/// Return the display color for a channel: the custom hex `color` if set and
2646/// valid, otherwise a deterministic palette color keyed on `channel_id`.
2647fn channel_event_color(channel_id: i64, color: &str) -> egui::Color32 {
2648    if !color.is_empty() {
2649        if let Some(c) = parse_hex_color(color) {
2650            return c;
2651        }
2652    }
2653    const PALETTE: &[egui::Color32] = &[
2654        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x42, 0x88, 0xc4), // steel blue
2655        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x9c, 0x27, 0xb0), // purple
2656        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xe9, 0x1e, 0x63), // pink
2657        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xf4, 0x43, 0x36), // red
2658        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xff, 0x98, 0x00), // orange
2659        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xc0, 0x90, 0x00), // amber (darkened for readability)
2660        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x38, 0x8e, 0x3c), // green
2661        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0x83, 0x8f), // cyan (darkened)
2662        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0x79, 0x6b), // teal
2663        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x30, 0x3f, 0x9f), // indigo
2664        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x02, 0x77, 0xbd), // sky blue
2665        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x55, 0x8b, 0x2f), // light green
2666        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x82, 0x77, 0x17), // lime (darkened)
2667        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x6d, 0x40, 0x2f), // brown
2668        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x45, 0x5a, 0x64), // blue-grey
2669        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x75, 0x75, 0x75), // grey
2670    ];
2671    PALETTE[(channel_id.unsigned_abs() as usize) % PALETTE.len()]
2672}
2673
2674/// Source-priority weight for auto-merge primary selection: lower = higher priority.
2675/// YouTube sources beat platform (Twitch) sources because they're manually scheduled
2676/// and have more accurate timing than the Twitch advance schedule.
2677fn merge_source_priority(source: &str) -> u8 {
2678    match source {
2679        "youtube_api" => 0,
2680        s if s.starts_with("youtube") => 1,
2681        "manual" => 2,
2682        "platform" => 4,
2683        _ => 3,
2684    }
2685}
2686
2687/// Indices into `all` whose visible (channel not in `hidden`) time window overlaps
2688/// another visible stream's — i.e. two streams scheduled at the same time. A
2689/// stream with no/invalid end time is treated as [`COLLISION_DEFAULT_SECS`] long.
2690fn schedule_collisions(all: &[UpcomingStream], hidden: &HashSet<i64>) -> HashSet<usize> {
2691    // (original index, start, effective end) for visible streams, sorted by start.
2692    let mut spans: Vec<(usize, i64, i64)> = all
2693        .iter()
2694        .enumerate()
2695        .filter(|(_, s)| !hidden.contains(&s.channel_id))
2696        .map(|(i, s)| (i, s.start_time, effective_end(s)))
2697        .collect();
2698    spans.sort_by_key(|&(_, start, _)| start);
2699    let mut out = HashSet::new();
2700    for a in 0..spans.len() {
2701        let (ia, _, ea) = spans[a];
2702        for &(ib, sb, _) in &spans[a + 1..] {
2703            // Sorted by start, so once a later stream begins at/after `a` ends, no
2704            // remaining stream can overlap `a`.
2705            if sb >= ea {
2706                break;
2707            }
2708            out.insert(ia);
2709            out.insert(ib);
2710        }
2711    }
2712    out
2713}
2714
2715/// Shift a date by `delta` whole months, clamping the day to the target month's
2716/// length (e.g. Jan 31 + 1 month → Feb 28). Used by month-view navigation.
2717fn shift_month(d: chrono::NaiveDate, delta: i32) -> chrono::NaiveDate {
2718    use chrono::Datelike;
2719    let total = d.year() * 12 + (d.month() as i32 - 1) + delta;
2720    let ny = total.div_euclid(12);
2721    let nm = total.rem_euclid(12) as u32 + 1;
2722    // Try the same day, then earlier, until one is valid for the target month.
2723    for day in (1..=d.day()).rev() {
2724        if let Some(nd) = chrono::NaiveDate::from_ymd_opt(ny, nm, day) {
2725            return nd;
2726        }
2727    }
2728    d
2729}
2730
2731/// The Monday of the week containing `d` (weeks start on Monday).
2732fn week_start(d: chrono::NaiveDate) -> chrono::NaiveDate {
2733    use chrono::Datelike;
2734    let lead = d.weekday().num_days_from_monday();
2735    d.checked_sub_days(chrono::Days::new(lead as u64)).unwrap_or(d)
2736}
2737
2738/// Offset a date by `n` days (negative = backwards), saturating on overflow.
2739fn add_days(d: chrono::NaiveDate, n: i64) -> chrono::NaiveDate {
2740    if n >= 0 {
2741        d.checked_add_days(chrono::Days::new(n as u64))
2742    } else {
2743        d.checked_sub_days(chrono::Days::new(n.unsigned_abs()))
2744    }
2745    .unwrap_or(d)
2746}
2747
2748/// Calendar header title, e.g. `June 2026`.
2749fn month_title(y: i32, m: u32) -> String {
2750    const NAMES: [&str; 12] = [
2751        "January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June", "July", "August", "September",
2752        "October", "November", "December",
2753    ];
2754    let name = NAMES.get((m.max(1) - 1) as usize).copied().unwrap_or("");
2755    format!("{name} {y}")
2756}
2757
2758/// Multi-line detail for one upcoming stream — the calendar hover text and the
2759/// "Copy details" payload.
2760/// Like [`schedule_detail_line`] but appends a "Merged: …" line when this event
2761/// is the primary of a merge group.
2762fn schedule_detail_line_merged(s: &UpcomingStream, merge_label: Option<&str>) -> String {
2763    let mut base = schedule_detail_line(s);
2764    if let Some(label) = merge_label {
2765        base.push_str(&format!("\nMerged: {label}"));
2766    }
2767    base
2768}
2769
2770fn schedule_detail_line(s: &UpcomingStream) -> String {
2771    let mut parts = vec![
2772        format!("{}  {}", fmt_datetime_short(s.start_time), s.channel_name),
2773        format!("Platform: {}", s.platform().label()),
2774    ];
2775    if let Some(end) = s.end_time.filter(|&e| e > s.start_time) {
2776        parts.push(format!("Until: {}", fmt_datetime_short(end)));
2777    }
2778    if !s.title.is_empty() {
2779        parts.push(format!("Title: {}", s.title));
2780    }
2781    if !s.category.is_empty() {
2782        parts.push(format!("Category: {}", s.category));
2783    }
2784    if !s.url.is_empty() {
2785        parts.push(s.url.clone());
2786    }
2787    // Always name the source (badge + label) so the hover/copy text says where the
2788    // schedule came from — published platform schedule, an OCR'd image, Discord, or
2789    // a manual correction.
2790    let (badge, label) = source_badge(&s.source);
2791    parts.push(format!("Source: {badge} {label}"));
2792    parts.join("\n")
2793}
2794
2795/// Render the compact source-indicator badge for an upcoming-stream entry next to
2796/// the platform icon (`📡` published schedule · `📺` YouTube · `📷` OCR'd image ·
2797/// `💬` Discord · `✏` manually edited), with a hover naming the source. Shared by
2798/// every calendar render site so the origin is always visible at a glance.
2799fn schedule_source_badge(ui: &mut egui::Ui, source: &str) {
2800    let (icon, label) = source_badge(source);
2801    ui.add(egui::Label::new(egui::RichText::new(icon).small().weak()))
2802        .on_hover_text(format!("Source: {label}"));
2803}
2804
2805/// Right-click menu for an upcoming-stream entry (calendar chip + day popup): copy
2806/// its URL / platform / title / channel / full details, or open it in the browser.
2807/// All actions are immediate (copy/open go straight through the egui context).
2808/// `merge_label` is non-None when this event is the primary of a merge (auto or manual),
2809/// and its value is the human-readable description used in the "Un-merge" button.
2810fn schedule_copy_menu(ui: &mut egui::Ui, s: &UpcomingStream, hidden: bool, merge_label: Option<&str>) {
2811    ui.set_min_width(180.0);
2812    // Un-merge actions (shown at top when this is a merged primary).
2813    if let Some(label) = merge_label {
2814        let is_auto = label.starts_with("auto");
2815        let is_manual = label.starts_with("manual");
2816        if is_auto {
2817            if ui
2818                .button("🔀  Un-merge (auto)")
2819                .on_hover_text(format!("Show merged events separately ({label})"))
2820                .clicked()
2821            {
2822                ui.ctx().data_mut(|d| {
2823                    d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("sched_unmerge_auto"), s.segment_id)
2824                });
2825                ui.close();
2826            }
2827        }
2828        if is_manual {
2829            if ui
2830                .button("🔀  Un-merge (manual)")
2831                .on_hover_text(format!("Remove manual merge ({label})"))
2832                .clicked()
2833            {
2834                ui.ctx().data_mut(|d| {
2835                    d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("sched_unmerge_manual"), s.segment_id)
2836                });
2837                ui.close();
2838            }
2839        }
2840        if is_auto || is_manual {
2841            ui.separator();
2842        }
2843    }
2844    // Hide / show toggle
2845    let hide_label = if hidden { "👁  Show" } else { "🙈  Hide" };
2846    if ui.button(hide_label).clicked() {
2847        ui.ctx()
2848            .data_mut(|d| d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("sched_hide"), s.segment_id));
2849        ui.close();
2850    }
2851    ui.separator();
2852    if ui
2853        .add_enabled(!s.url.is_empty(), egui::Button::new("📋  Copy URL"))
2854        .clicked()
2855    {
2856        ui.ctx().copy_text(s.url.clone());
2857        ui.close();
2858    }
2859    if ui.button("📋  Copy platform").clicked() {
2860        ui.ctx().copy_text(s.platform().label().to_string());
2861        ui.close();
2862    }
2863    if ui
2864        .add_enabled(!s.title.is_empty(), egui::Button::new("📋  Copy title"))
2865        .clicked()
2866    {
2867        ui.ctx().copy_text(s.title.clone());
2868        ui.close();
2869    }
2870    if ui.button("📋  Copy channel").clicked() {
2871        ui.ctx().copy_text(s.channel_name.clone());
2872        ui.close();
2873    }
2874    if ui.button("📋  Copy details").clicked() {
2875        ui.ctx().copy_text(schedule_detail_line(s));
2876        ui.close();
2877    }
2878    ui.separator();
2879    if ui
2880        .add_enabled(!s.url.is_empty(), egui::Button::new("🌐  Open in browser"))
2881        .clicked()
2882    {
2883        ui.ctx().open_url(egui::OpenUrl::new_tab(s.url.clone()));
2884        ui.close();
2885    }
2886    ui.separator();
2887    if ui.button("✏  Edit…").clicked() {
2888        ui.ctx()
2889            .data_mut(|d| d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("sched_edit"), s.segment_id));
2890        ui.close();
2891    }
2892    // "Open source" goes to where the item came from (platform schedule page or
2893    // the source image); disabled for manual edits and Discord events, which have
2894    // no external origin to open.
2895    let open_src_enabled = !s.is_manual()
2896        && ScheduleSourceKind::from_id(&s.source).is_some_and(|k| k.has_open_target());
2897    if ui
2898        .add_enabled(open_src_enabled, egui::Button::new("🔗  Open source"))
2899        .on_hover_text("Open where this schedule item came from (the platform schedule page or the source image).")
2900        .on_disabled_hover_text("No external source to open (manually edited or a Discord event).")
2901        .clicked()
2902    {
2903        ui.ctx()
2904            .data_mut(|d| d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("sched_open_src"), s.segment_id));
2905        ui.close();
2906    }
2907    ui.separator();
2908    if ui.button("📺  Go to channel").clicked() {
2909        ui.ctx()
2910            .data_mut(|d| d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("sched_jump"), s.monitor_id));
2911        ui.close();
2912    }
2913    if ui.button("▶  Start recording").clicked() {
2914        ui.ctx()
2915            .data_mut(|d| d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("sched_start"), s.monitor_id));
2916        ui.close();
2917    }
2918    ui.separator();
2919    if ui
2920        .button(egui::RichText::new("🗑  Delete").color(egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xe0, 0x50, 0x50)))
2921        .on_hover_text("Permanently suppress this event (tombstone — won't reappear on refresh).")
2922        .clicked()
2923    {
2924        ui.ctx()
2925            .data_mut(|d| d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("sched_delete"), s.segment_id));
2926        ui.close();
2927    }
2928}
2929
2930/// One detailed schedule row (⚠ if colliding · time · platform · channel — title
2931/// (category)) with a hover detail and the copy context menu. Shared by the day
2932/// popup and the Day view.
2933fn schedule_detail_row(
2934    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
2935    s: &UpcomingStream,
2936    colliding: bool,
2937    hidden: bool,
2938    ptex: &PlatformTextures,
2939    merge_label: Option<&str>,
2940) {
2941    let color = channel_event_color(s.channel_id, &s.channel_color);
2942    let resp = ui
2943        .horizontal(|ui| {
2944            ui.spacing_mut().item_spacing.x = 5.0;
2945            // 3px colored left stripe
2946            let (stripe_rect, _) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
2947                egui::vec2(3.0, ui.text_style_height(&egui::TextStyle::Body)),
2948                egui::Sense::hover(),
2949            );
2950            ui.painter().rect_filled(stripe_rect, egui::CornerRadius::same(2), color);
2951            if colliding {
2952                ui.colored_label(HL_COLLISION, "⚠");
2953            }
2954            if merge_label.is_some() {
2955                ui.label(egui::RichText::new("🔀").small());
2956            }
2957            ui.label(egui::RichText::new(fmt_time_range(s.start_time, s.end_time)).monospace());
2958            platform_icon(ui, ptex, s.platform());
2959            schedule_source_badge(ui, &s.source);
2960            let mut line = s.channel_name.clone();
2961            if !s.title.is_empty() {
2962                line.push_str(" — ");
2963                line.push_str(&s.title);
2964            }
2965            if !s.category.is_empty() {
2966                line.push_str(&format!("  ({})", s.category));
2967            }
2968            ui.add(egui::Label::new(line).truncate());
2969        })
2970        .response
2971        .interact(egui::Sense::click());
2972    let ml_owned = merge_label.map(str::to_string);
2973    resp.on_hover_text(schedule_detail_line_merged(s, merge_label))
2974        .context_menu(|ui| schedule_copy_menu(ui, s, hidden, ml_owned.as_deref()));
2975}
2976
2977/// Subtle background tint for today's calendar cell (low-alpha accent, so it reads
2978/// on both light and dark themes).
2979const TODAY_BG: egui::Color32 = egui::Color32::from_rgba_premultiplied(0x2c, 0x4a, 0x6e, 0x40);
2980/// Marker color for a stream that overlaps another (a scheduling collision).
2981const HL_COLLISION: egui::Color32 = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xff, 0x8a, 0x5c);
2982
2983// ── Time-grid calendar constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────
2984/// Pixels per hour in the time-grid (Week + Day) views.
2985const SCHED_HOUR_PX: f32 = 60.0;
2986/// Total scrollable height of the 24-hour time grid.
2987const SCHED_TOTAL_H: f32 = 24.0 * SCHED_HOUR_PX;
2988/// Width of the left-side "HH:MM" hour-label column in the time grid.
2989const SCHED_TIME_COL_W: f32 = 44.0;
2990/// Gap between day columns in the time grid.
2991const SCHED_COL_GAP: f32 = 4.0;
2992/// Minimum event block height so zero/short-duration events remain clickable.
2993const SCHED_MIN_BLOCK_H: f32 = 22.0;
2994
2995/// Seconds past local midnight for a unix timestamp (for positioning on the time grid).
2996fn secs_since_midnight(unix: i64) -> f32 {
2997    use chrono::Timelike;
2998    chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(unix, 0)
2999        .map(|dt| {
3000            let local = dt.with_timezone(&chrono::Local);
3001            (local.hour() * 3600 + local.minute() * 60 + local.second()) as f32
3002        })
3003        .unwrap_or(0.0)
3004}
3005
3006/// Assign events to non-overlapping lanes (columns within a day column) so that
3007/// concurrent events are displayed side-by-side. Returns `(stream_idx, lane, total_lanes)`.
3008fn layout_event_lanes(
3009    indices: &[usize],
3010    all: &[UpcomingStream],
3011) -> Vec<(usize, usize, usize)> {
3012    if indices.is_empty() {
3013        return vec![];
3014    }
3015    // lane_end[i] = effective end of the last event assigned to lane i
3016    let mut lane_end: Vec<i64> = Vec::new();
3017    let mut assignments: Vec<(usize, usize)> = Vec::new(); // (stream_idx, lane)
3018
3019    for &idx in indices {
3020        let s = &all[idx];
3021        let end = effective_end(s);
3022        // Find the first lane that is free at s.start_time.
3023        let lane = lane_end
3024            .iter()
3025            .position(|&le| le <= s.start_time)
3026            .unwrap_or_else(|| {
3027                lane_end.push(0);
3028                lane_end.len() - 1
3029            });
3030        lane_end[lane] = end;
3031        assignments.push((idx, lane));
3032    }
3033
3034    let total = lane_end.len().max(1);
3035    assignments.into_iter().map(|(i, l)| (i, l, total)).collect()
3036}
3037
3038/// Draw a 24-hour time-grid for one or more day columns. Called by both the Week
3039/// and Day views. `days` lists the calendar dates; `col_w` is the per-column
3040/// content width (excluding the time label column and gaps).
3041#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
3042fn schedule_time_grid(
3043    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
3044    id: &str,
3045    days: &[chrono::NaiveDate],
3046    col_w: f32,
3047    all: &[UpcomingStream],
3048    by_day: &HashMap<chrono::NaiveDate, Vec<usize>>,
3049    collide: &HashSet<usize>,
3050    open_day: &mut Option<chrono::NaiveDate>,
3051    hidden_segs: &HashSet<i64>,
3052    selected: &HashSet<i64>,
3053    merge_labels: &HashMap<i64, String>,
3054) {
3055    use chrono::Timelike;
3056    // Scroll to show the current local hour, but only the first time the view
3057    // appears so subsequent frames don't fight the user's manual scroll position.
3058    let init_id = egui::Id::new(id).with("scroll_init");
3059    let already_init: bool = ui.ctx().data(|d| d.get_temp(init_id).unwrap_or(false));
3060    let mut scroll = egui::ScrollArea::vertical()
3061        .id_salt(id)
3062        .auto_shrink([false, false]);
3063    if !already_init {
3064        let now_hour = chrono::Local::now().hour() as f32;
3065        let initial_offset = (now_hour * SCHED_HOUR_PX - 120.0).max(0.0);
3066        scroll = scroll.vertical_scroll_offset(initial_offset);
3067        ui.ctx().data_mut(|d| d.insert_temp(init_id, true));
3068    }
3069
3070    let grid_w = SCHED_TIME_COL_W + days.len() as f32 * (col_w + SCHED_COL_GAP);
3071
3072    let mut clicked_day: Option<chrono::NaiveDate> = None;
3073
3074    scroll.show(ui, |ui| {
3075            let (response, painter) = ui.allocate_painter(
3076                egui::vec2(grid_w, SCHED_TOTAL_H),
3077                egui::Sense::hover(),
3078            );
3079            let origin = response.rect.min;
3080
3081            // ── Hour grid lines + labels ──────────────────────────────────
3082            let grid_line_color = egui::Color32::from_white_alpha(18);
3083            let half_line_color = egui::Color32::from_white_alpha(8);
3084            let label_color = ui.visuals().weak_text_color();
3085            let font = egui::FontId::proportional(10.0);
3086
3087            for hour in 0u32..24 {
3088                let y = origin.y + hour as f32 * SCHED_HOUR_PX;
3089                // Hour label
3090                painter.text(
3091                    egui::pos2(origin.x + 2.0, y + 2.0),
3092                    egui::Align2::LEFT_TOP,
3093                    format!("{hour:02}:00"),
3094                    font.clone(),
3095                    label_color,
3096                );
3097                // Full-hour line across all columns
3098                painter.line_segment(
3099                    [
3100                        egui::pos2(origin.x + SCHED_TIME_COL_W, y),
3101                        egui::pos2(origin.x + grid_w, y),
3102                    ],
3103                    egui::Stroke::new(1.0, grid_line_color),
3104                );
3105                // Half-hour line (lighter)
3106                let yh = y + SCHED_HOUR_PX / 2.0;
3107                painter.line_segment(
3108                    [
3109                        egui::pos2(origin.x + SCHED_TIME_COL_W, yh),
3110                        egui::pos2(origin.x + grid_w, yh),
3111                    ],
3112                    egui::Stroke::new(1.0, half_line_color),
3113                );
3114            }
3115
3116            // ── Vertical column dividers ──────────────────────────────────
3117            let divider_color = egui::Color32::from_white_alpha(22);
3118            for col in 0..=days.len() {
3119                let x = origin.x + SCHED_TIME_COL_W + col as f32 * (col_w + SCHED_COL_GAP);
3120                painter.line_segment(
3121                    [egui::pos2(x, origin.y), egui::pos2(x, origin.y + SCHED_TOTAL_H)],
3122                    egui::Stroke::new(1.0, divider_color),
3123                );
3124            }
3125
3126            // ── Current-time indicator (red line) ─────────────────────────
3127            {
3128                let now = chrono::Local::now();
3129                let today = now.date_naive();
3130                let now_secs = (now.hour() * 3600 + now.minute() * 60 + now.second()) as f32;
3131                if let Some(col_idx) = days.iter().position(|&d| d == today) {
3132                    let x_start = origin.x + SCHED_TIME_COL_W + col_idx as f32 * (col_w + SCHED_COL_GAP);
3133                    let x_end = x_start + col_w;
3134                    let y = origin.y + now_secs / 3600.0 * SCHED_HOUR_PX;
3135                    painter.line_segment(
3136                        [egui::pos2(x_start, y), egui::pos2(x_end, y)],
3137                        egui::Stroke::new(2.0, egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xff, 0x44, 0x44)),
3138                    );
3139                    // Small circle at left edge
3140                    painter.circle_filled(
3141                        egui::pos2(x_start, y),
3142                        4.0,
3143                        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xff, 0x44, 0x44),
3144                    );
3145                }
3146            }
3147
3148            // ── Event blocks ──────────────────────────────────────────────
3149            for (col_idx, &day) in days.iter().enumerate() {
3150                let col_x = origin.x + SCHED_TIME_COL_W + col_idx as f32 * (col_w + SCHED_COL_GAP);
3151                let indices = by_day.get(&day).map(Vec::as_slice).unwrap_or(&[]);
3152                let layout = layout_event_lanes(indices, all);
3153
3154                for (stream_idx, lane, total_lanes) in layout {
3155                    let s = &all[stream_idx];
3156                    let start_secs = secs_since_midnight(s.start_time);
3157                    let end_secs = secs_since_midnight(effective_end(s));
3158
3159                    // Clip to day boundaries (midnight transitions handled by bucketing).
3160                    let end_secs = if end_secs <= start_secs {
3161                        // Event ends on next day — clip at midnight.
3162                        SCHED_TOTAL_H / SCHED_HOUR_PX * 3600.0
3163                    } else {
3164                        end_secs
3165                    };
3166                    let duration_secs = (end_secs - start_secs).max(0.0);
3167
3168                    let top = origin.y + start_secs / 3600.0 * SCHED_HOUR_PX;
3169                    let block_h = (duration_secs / 3600.0 * SCHED_HOUR_PX).max(SCHED_MIN_BLOCK_H);
3170                    let lane_w = (col_w - 2.0 * (total_lanes as f32 - 1.0)) / total_lanes as f32;
3171                    let left = col_x + 1.0 + lane as f32 * (lane_w + 2.0);
3172
3173                    let block_rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(
3174                        egui::pos2(left, top),
3175                        egui::vec2(lane_w, block_h),
3176                    );
3177                    // Per-block interactive region: hover coloring, tooltip,
3178                    // left-click (→ day popup), right-click context menu.
3179                    // Must be allocated before painting so hovered() is accurate.
3180                    let is_hidden = hidden_segs.contains(&s.segment_id);
3181                    let evt_id = egui::Id::new("sched_evt").with(day).with(stream_idx);
3182                    let evt_resp = ui.interact(block_rect, evt_id, egui::Sense::click());
3183                    let hovered = evt_resp.hovered();
3184
3185                    let color = channel_event_color(s.channel_id, &s.channel_color);
3186                    let fill = if is_hidden {
3187                        // Soft-hidden: keep hue but drop alpha to ~35% so it reads as "ghost"
3188                        egui::Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied(color.r(), color.g(), color.b(), 85)
3189                    } else if hovered {
3190                        color
3191                    } else {
3192                        egui::Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied(
3193                            color.r(), color.g(), color.b(), 210,
3194                        )
3195                    };
3196                    let rounding = egui::CornerRadius::same(4);
3197                    painter.rect_filled(block_rect, rounding, fill);
3198                    // Slightly darker left edge strip for depth
3199                    painter.rect_filled(
3200                        egui::Rect::from_min_size(block_rect.min, egui::vec2(3.0, block_h)),
3201                        egui::CornerRadius { nw: 4, sw: 4, ne: 0, se: 0 },
3202                        egui::Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied(
3203                            (color.r() as i32 - 30).max(0) as u8,
3204                            (color.g() as i32 - 30).max(0) as u8,
3205                            (color.b() as i32 - 30).max(0) as u8,
3206                            255,
3207                        ),
3208                    );
3209                    if collide.contains(&stream_idx) {
3210                        painter.rect_stroke(
3211                            block_rect,
3212                            rounding,
3213                            egui::Stroke::new(1.5, HL_COLLISION),
3214                            egui::StrokeKind::Inside,
3215                        );
3216                    }
3217                    // Selection highlight: bright white border
3218                    let is_selected = selected.contains(&s.segment_id);
3219                    if is_selected {
3220                        painter.rect_stroke(
3221                            block_rect,
3222                            rounding,
3223                            egui::Stroke::new(2.0, egui::Color32::WHITE),
3224                            egui::StrokeKind::Inside,
3225                        );
3226                    }
3227
3228                    // Merge label for this block (non-empty = primary of a merge)
3229                    let merge_label = merge_labels.get(&s.segment_id).map(String::as_str);
3230
3231                    // Text inside the block — clip to block_rect so text never
3232                    // bleeds into adjacent columns.
3233                    let text_rect = block_rect.shrink2(egui::vec2(5.0, 3.0));
3234                    if text_rect.height() >= 12.0 {
3235                        let text_painter = painter.with_clip_rect(block_rect);
3236                        let name_font = egui::FontId::proportional(11.0);
3237                        let time_font = egui::FontId::proportional(10.0);
3238                        let (name_color, time_color, title_color) = if is_hidden {
3239                            (
3240                                egui::Color32::from_white_alpha(110),
3241                                egui::Color32::from_white_alpha(90),
3242                                egui::Color32::from_white_alpha(70),
3243                            )
3244                        } else {
3245                            (
3246                                egui::Color32::WHITE,
3247                                egui::Color32::from_white_alpha(200),
3248                                egui::Color32::from_white_alpha(180),
3249                            )
3250                        };
3251                        let text_y = text_rect.top();
3252                        let badge = source_badge(&s.source).0;
3253                        // Prepend 🔀 if this block is a merged primary
3254                        let merge_icon = if merge_label.is_some() { "🔀 " } else { "" };
3255                        let name_str = if is_hidden {
3256                            format!("⊘ {}{}{}", merge_icon, badge, s.channel_name)
3257                        } else {
3258                            format!("{}{} {}", merge_icon, badge, s.channel_name)
3259                        };
3260                        text_painter.text(
3261                            egui::pos2(text_rect.left(), text_y),
3262                            egui::Align2::LEFT_TOP,
3263                            name_str,
3264                            name_font,
3265                            name_color,
3266                        );
3267                        if block_h >= 36.0 {
3268                            text_painter.text(
3269                                egui::pos2(text_rect.left(), text_y + 13.0),
3270                                egui::Align2::LEFT_TOP,
3271                                fmt_time_range(s.start_time, s.end_time),
3272                                time_font.clone(),
3273                                time_color,
3274                            );
3275                        }
3276                        if block_h >= 56.0 && !s.title.is_empty() {
3277                            text_painter.text(
3278                                egui::pos2(text_rect.left(), text_y + 26.0),
3279                                egui::Align2::LEFT_TOP,
3280                                &s.title,
3281                                time_font,
3282                                title_color,
3283                            );
3284                        }
3285                    }
3286
3287                    let block_clicked = evt_resp.clicked();
3288                    let ctrl_held = ui.input(|i| i.modifiers.ctrl || i.modifiers.shift);
3289                    let merge_label_owned = merge_label.map(str::to_string);
3290                    evt_resp
3291                        .on_hover_text(schedule_detail_line_merged(s, merge_label))
3292                        .context_menu(|ui| schedule_copy_menu(ui, s, is_hidden, merge_label_owned.as_deref()));
3293                    if block_clicked {
3294                        if ctrl_held {
3295                            // Ctrl+click: toggle in selection without opening day
3296                            ui.ctx().data_mut(|d| {
3297                                d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("sched_sel_toggle"), s.segment_id)
3298                            });
3299                        } else {
3300                            // Plain click: select single event + open day popup
3301                            ui.ctx().data_mut(|d| {
