3302                                d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("sched_sel_single"), s.segment_id)
3303                            });
3304                            clicked_day = Some(day);
3305                        }
3306                    }
3307                }
3308            }
3309        });
3310
3311    if let Some(day) = clicked_day {
3312        *open_day = Some(day);
3313    }
3314}
3315
3316/// Success/affirmative green, shared across the table (recording "completed",
3317/// video "completed", ad-free "Yes").
3318const SUCCESS_GREEN: egui::Color32 = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x57, 0xc7, 0x57);
3319
3320/// Streams-row background tint while an ad is playing (amber) / after an error
3321/// (red). Recording + keyboard-selected rows reuse the theme's selection accent.
3322const HL_AD: egui::Color32 = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x7a, 0x5a, 0x12);
3323const HL_ERROR: egui::Color32 = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x6e, 0x2f, 0x2f);
3324/// Readable red for inline error/validation *text* (the row tint [`HL_ERROR`] is
3325/// too dark to read as a foreground colour).
3326const HL_ERROR_TEXT: egui::Color32 = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xe0, 0x6c, 0x6c);
3327
3328/// Background tint for a Streams row, by state (highest priority first): an ad is
3329/// playing > recording > last poll/recording errored > keyboard-selected.
3330/// `accent` is the theme's selection color (so recording/selected keep the
3331/// existing look). When `status_colors` is off, the status tints (ad / recording
3332/// / error) are suppressed but a keyboard-`selected` row is still highlighted.
3333/// `None` = no tint.
3334fn row_tint(
3335    recording: bool,
3336    ad_running: bool,
3337    errored: bool,
3338    selected: bool,
3339    accent: egui::Color32,
3340    status_colors: bool,
3341) -> Option<egui::Color32> {
3342    if status_colors {
3343        if recording && ad_running {
3344            return Some(HL_AD);
3345        } else if recording {
3346            return Some(accent);
3347        } else if errored {
3348            return Some(HL_ERROR);
3349        }
3350    }
3351    selected.then_some(accent)
3352}
3353
3354/// Background tint for a Videos row, by download status: in-flight = the theme
3355/// accent, failed = the error red. `None` (incl. when `status_colors` is off) =
3356/// no tint. Mirrors [`row_tint`] for the Streams table.
3357fn video_row_tint(status: &str, accent: egui::Color32, status_colors: bool) -> Option<egui::Color32> {
3358    if !status_colors {
3359        return None;
3360    }
3361    match status {
3362        "downloading" | "queued" => Some(accent),
3363        "failed" => Some(HL_ERROR),
3364        _ => None,
3365    }
3366}
3367
3368/// Whether a monitor is in an error/failure state right now. Only `last_state`
3369/// is checked — recording failures are visible via the ⚠ state icon on the
3370/// instance row, and a failed `last_recording_status` should not prevent
3371/// "Clear error" from dismissing the channel-row tint.
3372fn monitor_errored(m: &MonitorWithChannel) -> bool {
3373    matches!(m.monitor.last_state.as_str(), "error" | "failed")
3374}
3375
3376/// Ad-break count for a cell (blank when there are none, so empty rows stay clean).
3377fn fmt_ad_count(n: i64) -> String {
3378    if n > 0 { n.to_string() } else { String::new() }
3379}
3380
3381/// Resolve an instance's ad-free status into a (label, tooltip) for display.
3382/// Manual flag wins; otherwise the auto Twitch-sub result (`Some(true)` = sub'd,
3383/// `Some(false)` = checked & not sub'd, `None` = unknown/not checked). Returns
3384/// `None` when there's nothing to show.
3385fn ad_free_status(manual: bool, sub: Option<bool>) -> Option<(&'static str, &'static str)> {
3386    if manual {
3387        Some((
3388            "Yes",
3389            "Marked ad-free for your account (member/sub/Turbo) — captures won't have \
3390             ad-break hard cuts.",
3391        ))
3392    } else {
3393        match sub {
3394            Some(true) => Some((
3395                "Yes (sub)",
3396                "Your connected Twitch account is subscribed to this channel — \
3397                 subscriber captures have no ad breaks.",
3398            )),
3399            _ => None,
3400        }
3401    }
3402}
3403
3404/// Channel-row ad-free summary (label + numeric sort key) from how many of the
3405/// channel's instances are ad-free.
3406fn ad_free_summary(ad_free_count: usize, total: usize) -> (&'static str, f64) {
3407    if total == 0 || ad_free_count == 0 {
3408        ("", 0.0)
3409    } else if ad_free_count == total {
3410        ("Yes", 2.0)
3411    } else {
3412        ("some", 1.0)
3413    }
3414}
3415
3416/// Human-readable lines describing where ad breaks cause hard cuts in the
3417/// finished file. `at_secs` is already the cut's position in the captured file
3418/// (ad segments are filtered out), so it's shown directly as a seek timestamp.
3419/// `breaks` must be ordered by offset.
3420fn ad_cut_lines(breaks: &[AdBreak]) -> Vec<String> {
3421    breaks
3422        .iter()
3423        .enumerate()
3424        .map(|(i, b)| {
3425            format!(
3426                "#{}  cut at {}  ({}s ad)",
3427                i + 1,
3428                fmt_duration(b.at_secs.max(0)),
3429                b.duration_secs
3430            )
3431        })
3432        .collect()
3433}
3434
3435/// Count string for a Changes cell ("" when zero, so empty cells render nothing).
3436fn fmt_meta_count(n: i64) -> String {
3437    if n > 0 { n.to_string() } else { String::new() }
3438}
3439
3440/// Render a "Next stream" cell: blank when no upcoming stream is known, else the
3441/// scheduled start datetime. When `clickable`, a double-click returns true so the
3442/// caller can open the full-schedule popup; the hover shows the title.
3443fn next_stream_cell(ui: &mut egui::Ui, at: Option<i64>, title: &str, clickable: bool) -> bool {
3444    let Some(at) = at.filter(|&a| a > 0) else {
3445        return false;
3446    };
3447    let compact = short_ts_on();
3448    let display = if compact { fmt_datetime_compact(at) } else { fmt_datetime_short(at) };
3449    let label = if clickable {
3450        egui::Label::new(&display).sense(egui::Sense::click())
3451    } else {
3452        egui::Label::new(&display)
3453    };
3454    let resp = ui.add(label).on_hover_ui(|ui| {
3455        if compact {
3456            ui.label(fmt_datetime_short(at));
3457        }
3458        if title.is_empty() {
3459            ui.label("Next scheduled stream.");
3460        } else {
3461            ui.label(format!("Next: {title}"));
3462        }
3463        if clickable {
3464            ui.label("Double-click for the full upcoming schedule.");
3465        }
3466    });
3467    clickable && resp.double_clicked()
3468}
3469
3470/// One human-readable line per *actual* metadata change (offset + kind +
3471/// `old → new`). The initial value of each field (logged with an empty
3472/// `old_value`) is the starting state, not a change, so it's skipped — it still
3473/// shows as the `old` side of the first real change.
3474fn meta_change_lines(changes: &[StreamMetaChange]) -> Vec<String> {
3475    changes
3476        .iter()
3477        .filter(|c| !c.old_value.is_empty())
3478        .map(|c| {
3479            let at = fmt_duration(c.at_secs.max(0));
3480            let kind = if c.kind == "category" { "Category" } else { "Title" };
3481            let new = if c.new_value.is_empty() {
3482                "(cleared)"
3483            } else {
3484                c.new_value.as_str()
3485            };
3486            format!("{at}  {kind}: {} → {new}", c.old_value)
3487        })
3488        .collect()
3489}
3490
3491/// What the metadata-change popup shows.
3492#[derive(Clone)]
3493enum MetaPopup {
3494    /// A single take's change log (recording id).
3495    Take(i64),
3496    /// A whole stream's takes — `(recording id, started_at)`, oldest-first —
3497    /// aggregated chronologically with the per-take re-baselines omitted.
3498    Stream(Vec<(i64, i64)>),
3499}
3500
3501/// Draft state for the "Edit schedule item" dialog. Times are edited as local
3502/// `YYYY-MM-DD` / `HH:MM` strings; on save they're parsed back to unix seconds and
3503/// written via [`Store::update_schedule_segment_manual`](crate::store::Store::update_schedule_segment_manual),
3504/// which flips the row to the protected `"manual"` source so later automatic
3505/// refreshes don't overwrite the correction.
3506struct EditScheduleDraft {
3507    /// `schedule_segment.id` of the row being edited.
3508    segment_id: i64,
3509    /// For the dialog heading.
3510    channel_name: String,
3511    /// Original source id — shown in the heading so the user sees what they're
3512    /// overriding (e.g. an OCR'd banner).
3513    source: String,
3514    title: String,
3515    category: String,
3516    /// Local `YYYY-MM-DD` / `HH:MM` of the start.
3517    date: String,
3518    time: String,
3519    /// Optional local end — empty strings mean "no end time".
3520    end_date: String,
3521    end_time: String,
3522    /// Validation message shown in red (empty = none).
3523    error: String,
3524}
3525
3526/// Draft state for the "Merge schedule events" preview dialog.
3527struct MergePreviewDraft {
3528    /// Snapshots of the events to merge (2+), sorted highest-priority first.
3529    /// Index 0 is pre-selected as the primary (can be changed by the user).
3530    segments: Vec<UpcomingStream>,
3531    /// Which element of `segments` is chosen as the primary (shown in the calendar).
3532    primary_idx: usize,
3533    /// Validation/error message (empty = none).
3534    error: String,
3535}
3536
3537struct SavePresetDraft {
3538    /// Template string to be saved.
3539    template: String,
3540    /// Name the user has typed for this preset.
3541    name: String,
3542    /// Validation or save error message (empty = none).
3543    error: String,
3544}
3545
3546/// Source platform of a captured chat message (drives username colouring).
3547#[derive(Clone)]
3548enum ChatPlatform {
3549    YouTube,
3550    Twitch,
3551}
3552
3553/// One renderable piece of a chat message body. Built once at parse time;
3554/// [`render_chat_message`] just walks it. `file: None` means "no local image"
3555/// (offline / not downloaded / undecodable / unknown id) → the renderer falls back
3556/// to drawing `fallback_text` (the emoji glyph) if set, else `name` (the emote
3557/// code). A `Text` segment may contain spaces.
3558#[derive(Clone)]
3559enum ChatSegment {
3560    Text(String),
3561    Emote {
3562        name: String,
3563        file: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
3564        /// For emoji: the Unicode glyph to show when there's no image, so it
3565        /// degrades to a (mono) glyph rather than a code. `None` for code emotes.
3566        fallback_text: Option<String>,
3567    },
3568}
3569
3570/// A single parsed chat message (YouTube `.live_chat.json` or Twitch `.chat.jsonl`).
3571#[derive(Clone)]
3572struct ChatMessage {
3573    /// Seconds from stream start (negative = chat arrived before we started recording).
3574    timestamp_secs: f64,
3575    author: String,
3576    /// Verbatim message body with emote codes left inline. KEPT (never replaced by
3577    /// rendered names) so the popup search filter still matches an emote by its
3578    /// code/shortcut even when it renders as an image.
3579    text: String,
3580    /// Render plan: text runs interleaved with emote references. Always built; the
3581    /// "render emotes" toggle is applied at draw time, not here.
3582    segments: Vec<ChatSegment>,
3583    /// Twitch: raw IRC badge segment per entry, e.g. `"subscriber/12"`.
3584    /// YouTube: badge tooltip text, e.g. `"Member"`.
3585    badges: Vec<String>,
3586    /// Explicit hex colour from Twitch USERCOLOR; `None` when unset or YouTube.
3587    color_override: Option<egui::Color32>,
3588    platform: ChatPlatform,
3589}
3590
3591#[derive(Clone)]
3592enum ChatLoadState {
3593    Loading,
3594    Loaded(Vec<ChatMessage>),
3595    NoFile,
3596    Error(String),
3597}
3598
3599struct ChatPopup {
3600    monitor_name: String,
3601    /// Currently-viewed recording (`None` = monitor has no recordings at all).
3602    recording: Option<Recording>,
3603    all_recordings: Vec<Recording>,
3604    load_state: Arc<Mutex<ChatLoadState>>,
3605    search: String,
3606    /// When `true`: show the entire log from the top (no cap, stick-to-bottom off).
3607    /// When `false` (default): show the last 500 msgs and stick to bottom.
3608    full_view: bool,
3609    /// When the popup last triggered a background re-read of the chat file.
3610    /// Used to tail a live recording: the file is re-parsed every few seconds
3611    /// while `recording.ended_at` is `None`.
3612    last_reload: std::time::Instant,
3613    /// Third-party emote code → resolved on-disk image path, built ONCE on
3614    /// popup-open from the channel's BTTV/FFZ/7TV manifests (case-sensitive keys).
3615    /// Empty for YouTube / when chat assets aren't fetched. `Arc` so the
3616    /// background (re)parse tasks share it without rebuilding per tick.
3617    emote_map: Arc<HashMap<String, std::path::PathBuf>>,
3618    /// `…/{channel}/twitch/emotes/twitch/` — Twitch first-party emotes are
3619    /// id-keyed (resolved as `{id}.png` at parse time). `None` for YouTube.
3620    twitch_emote_dir: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
3621    /// True while a background emoji-download pass is running, so the 3s tail-reload
3622    /// doesn't pile up overlapping download passes for the same chat.
3623    loading: Arc<AtomicBool>,
3624}
3625
3626/// Merge a stream's takes into one chronological change list. Each take's offsets
3627/// (`at_secs`, relative to that take's start) are rebased onto the whole stream's
3628/// timeline (`take.started_at - stream_start + at_secs`); the rows are then sorted
3629/// and run through [`meta_change_lines`], which drops each take's initial value
3630/// (empty `old_value`) — so a take re-observing the value the previous take ended
3631/// on adds no duplicate line, while genuine changes are kept.
3632fn aggregate_stream_changes(takes: &[(i64, Vec<StreamMetaChange>)]) -> Vec<StreamMetaChange> {
3633    let stream_start = takes.iter().map(|(s, _)| *s).min().unwrap_or(0);
3634    let mut all: Vec<StreamMetaChange> = Vec::new();
3635    for (started_at, rows) in takes {
3636        for r in rows {
3637            let mut adj = r.clone();
3638            adj.at_secs = (started_at - stream_start) + r.at_secs;
3639            all.push(adj);
3640        }
3641    }
3642    all.sort_by_key(|c| (c.at_secs, c.id));
3643    all
3644}
3645
3646/// Multi-line tooltip body for a Changes cell: a heading plus the change list
3647/// (just the heading when the detail isn't loaded or there are no changes).
3648fn meta_tooltip(count: i64, changes: Option<&Vec<StreamMetaChange>>) -> String {
3649    let mut s = format!("{count} title/category change(s) during this recording.");
3650    if let Some(lines) = changes.map(|c| meta_change_lines(c)).filter(|l| !l.is_empty()) {
3651        s.push('\n');
3652        s.push_str(&lines.join("\n"));
3653    }
3654    s
3655}
3656
3657/// Render one Changes table cell: blank when the count is
3658/// zero, a lazily-built hover list, and (when `clickable`) a double-click to open
3659/// the change-log popup. Returns whether it was double-clicked so the caller can
3660/// open the right popup (a single take, or a whole stream's aggregated takes).
3661fn meta_cell(
3662    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
3663    count: i64,
3664    detail: Option<&Vec<StreamMetaChange>>,
3665    clickable: bool,
3666) -> bool {
3667    let text = fmt_meta_count(count);
3668    if text.is_empty() {
3669        return false;
3670    }
3671    let label = if clickable {
3672        egui::Label::new(text).sense(egui::Sense::click())
3673    } else {
3674        egui::Label::new(text)
3675    };
3676    let resp = ui.add(label).on_hover_ui(|ui| {
3677        ui.label(meta_tooltip(count, detail));
3678    });
3679    clickable && resp.double_clicked()
3680}
3681
3682/// Render the combined Ads column (📢): the ad break count as the cell text, with
3683/// a tooltip showing "Ads: N (total time)" + the per-break cut list if loaded.
3684/// The double-click behaviour mirrors [`combined_ads_cell`].
3685fn combined_ads_cell(
3686    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
3687    count: i64,
3688    secs: i64,
3689    detail: Option<&Vec<AdBreak>>,
3690    clickable_rec: Option<i64>,
3691) -> Option<i64> {
3692    if count == 0 {
3693        return None;
3694    }
3695    let text = fmt_ad_count(count);
3696    let label = if clickable_rec.is_some() {
3697        egui::Label::new(text).sense(egui::Sense::click())
3698    } else {
3699        egui::Label::new(text)
3700    };
3701    let resp = ui.add(label).on_hover_ui(|ui| {
3702        ui.label(format!("Ads: {} ({})", count, fmt_duration(secs)));
3703        if let Some(b) = detail.filter(|b| !b.is_empty()) {
3704            ui.label(ad_cut_lines(b).join("\n"));
3705        }
3706    });
3707    match clickable_rec {
3708        Some(rec) if resp.double_clicked() => Some(rec),
3709        _ => None,
3710    }
3711}
3712
3713/// Render a timestamp cell using the compact format when short-timestamps mode is
3714/// on; falls back to the normal format when off. When compact, the full timestamp
3715/// is shown in a tooltip.
3716fn ts_label(ui: &mut egui::Ui, secs: i64) {
3717    if secs <= 0 {
3718        return;
3719    }
3720    let compact = short_ts_on();
3721    let display = if compact { fmt_datetime_compact(secs) } else { fmt_datetime_short(secs) };
3722    let resp = ui.label(display);
3723    if compact {
3724        resp.on_hover_text(fmt_datetime_short(secs));
3725    }
3726}
3727
3728/// Like [`ts_label`] but appends `~` for approximate times (Went Live column).
3729fn ts_went_live_label(ui: &mut egui::Ui, secs: i64, approx: bool) {
3730    if secs <= 0 {
3731        return;
3732    }
3733    let compact = short_ts_on();
3734    let display = {
3735        let s = if compact { fmt_datetime_compact(secs) } else { fmt_datetime_short(secs) };
3736        if approx { format!("{s}~") } else { s }
3737    };
3738    let resp = ui.label(display);
3739    if compact {
3740        let full = {
3741            let s = fmt_datetime_short(secs);
3742            if approx { format!("{s}~") } else { s }
3743        };
3744        resp.on_hover_text(full);
3745    }
3746}
3747
3748/// Render a current-Title / current-Game cell: blank when empty, otherwise a
3749/// label truncated to the (width-capped) column. egui shows the full text on
3750/// hover automatically when the label is elided (`show_tooltip_when_elided`
3751/// defaults to true), so we add no explicit tooltip — a second one would just
3752/// stack a duplicate.
3753fn meta_value_cell(ui: &mut egui::Ui, value: &str) {
3754    if value.is_empty() {
3755        return;
3756    }
3757    ui.add(egui::Label::new(value).truncate());
3758}
3759
3760/// Parse the monitor id out of a [`StreamGroup`] key (`s<mid>:…` / `t<mid>:…`).
3761fn stream_key_monitor(key: &str) -> Option<i64> {
3762    let rest = key.strip_prefix('s').or_else(|| key.strip_prefix('t'))?;
3763    rest.split(':').next()?.parse().ok()
3764}
3765
3766/// Format a go-live time (`~`-suffixed when only our approximate time is known).
3767fn fmt_went_live(at: Option<i64>, approx: bool) -> String {
3768    match at {
3769        Some(w) => {
3770            let s = fmt_datetime_short(w);
3771            if approx { format!("{s}~") } else { s }
3772        }
3773        None => String::new(),
3774    }
3775}
3776
3777/// Human-readable byte size (B / KB / MB / GB).
3778fn fmt_bytes(bytes: i64) -> String {
3779    let b = bytes.max(0) as f64;
3780    const KB: f64 = 1024.0;
3781    const MB: f64 = KB * 1024.0;
3782    const GB: f64 = MB * 1024.0;
3783    if b >= GB {
3784        format!("{:.2} GB", b / GB)
3785    } else if b >= MB {
3786        format!("{:.1} MB", b / MB)
3787    } else if b >= KB {
3788        format!("{:.0} KB", b / KB)
3789    } else {
3790        format!("{b:.0} B")
3791    }
3792}
3793
3794/// Theme color for a video download status string.
3795fn video_status_color(status: &str) -> egui::Color32 {
3796    use egui::Color32;
3797    match status {
3798        "downloading" => Color32::from_rgb(0x4d, 0x9b, 0xff),
3799        "completed" => SUCCESS_GREEN,
3800        "failed" => Color32::from_rgb(0xe0, 0x6c, 0x6c),
3801        _ => Color32::from_gray(0xa0), // queued / stopped / orphaned
3802    }
3803}
3804
3805/// Human-readable download speed (e.g. `1.2 MB/s`); empty when not downloading.
3806fn fmt_speed(bytes_per_sec: f64) -> String {
3807    if bytes_per_sec <= 0.0 {
3808        return String::new();
3809    }
3810    format!("{}/s", fmt_bytes(bytes_per_sec as i64))
3811}
3812
3813// ─── Sortable + filterable tables ───────────────────────────────────────────
3814//
3815// Both tables share a tiny model: each row is turned into a `Vec<Cell>` in header
3816// order. Videos excludes its trailing Actions column (`VIDEO_COLS` = 9); Streams
3817// keeps a (non-sortable, empty) Actions placeholder slot so the model indices line
3818// up with `STREAM_COLUMNS` (`STREAM_COLS`). The header renders a click-to-sort
3819// title + a per-column filter box; `ordered_rows` filters then sorts and returns
3820// the surviving original-row indices in display order. The data cells themselves
3821// are still drawn by the existing per-row code, indexed by those original indices.
3822// (The optional Actions column is skipped at render time, not in the model.)
3823
3824/// Sortable/filterable Videos columns (Video..File; excludes Actions).
3825const VIDEO_COLS: usize = 9;
3826
3827/// One Streams-table column: header title, hover tooltip, minimum width, and
3828/// whether it takes part in sort/filter (the Actions column doesn't). This list
3829/// is the single source of truth for the `TableBuilder` columns, the header, and
3830/// the per-row sort/filter model, so they can't drift — and an extra cell can't
3831/// be emitted against a missing column width (which panics egui_extras).
3832struct StreamCol {
3833    title: &'static str,
3834    tooltip: &'static str,
3835    min_width: f32,
3836    /// Starting (and clipped) width for content-capped columns whose value can be
3837    /// long (the current Title / Game); the cell truncates to it and shows the
3838    /// full text on hover. `0.0` = auto-size to content (the default).
3839    initial: f32,
3840    sortable: bool,
3841}
3842
3843/// The Streams columns in display order: Actions and the platform-icon column sit
3844/// just left of Name; the current Game/Title sit just right of State. Widths are
3845/// floors — `Column::auto` shrinks tight columns to their content — except the
3846/// `initial`-width columns, which start narrow and truncate (full value on hover).
3847/// Column order: Auto · Actions · Plat · Name · Tool · Detection · Polled · State ·
3848/// Next stream · Game · Title · ✏ (Changes) · 📢 (Ads) · Went Live · Started On ·
3849/// Lost time · Duration · Ad-free · Added.
3850const STREAM_COLUMNS: [StreamCol; 19] = [
3851    StreamCol { title: "Auto",       tooltip: "Enable/disable monitoring. The channel checkbox and each instance checkbox are independent.", min_width: 36.0,  initial: 0.0,   sortable: true },
3852    StreamCol { title: "Actions",    tooltip: "Per-row actions: start/stop recording, edit, add instance, open folder, delete.",            min_width: 126.0, initial: 0.0,   sortable: false },
3853    StreamCol { title: "Plat",       tooltip: "Source platform (icon): Twitch, YouTube, Kick, or a generic URL. A channel shows every platform among its instances.", min_width: 52.0, initial: 0.0, sortable: true },
3854    StreamCol { title: "Name",       tooltip: "Channel (container) name. Expand it to see its instances and recording history.",            min_width: 130.0, initial: 0.0,   sortable: true },
3855    StreamCol { title: "Tool",  tooltip: "Capture tool: SL = streamlink, yt-dlp, ff = ffmpeg. Hover a row for the full name.", min_width: 36.0, initial: 0.0, sortable: true },
3856    StreamCol { title: "⇄",    tooltip: "Detection method — how liveness is detected: ↺ = API poll, ⚡ = push event, ⌁ = scrape, ◉ = probe, C = CLI. Hover a row for the full method.", min_width: 24.0, initial: 0.0, sortable: true },
3857    StreamCol { title: "Polled", tooltip: "When this instance was last checked. Compact mode shows HH:MM only; hover for the full timestamp.", min_width: 64.0, initial: 0.0, sortable: true },
3858    StreamCol { title: "●",    tooltip: "Current monitor state. ⏺ = recording, ● = live (not recording), ○ = idle, ⚠ = failed, ⚡ = aborted.", min_width: 26.0, initial: 0.0, sortable: true },
3859    StreamCol { title: "Next stream",tooltip: "Next scheduled stream (Twitch schedule / YouTube upcoming). Hover for its title; double-click for the full schedule.", min_width: 96.0, initial: 0.0, sortable: true },
3860    StreamCol { title: "Game",       tooltip: "Current game / category of the most recent recording. Truncated — hover for the full name.", min_width: 60.0,  initial: 96.0,  sortable: true },
3861    StreamCol { title: "Title",      tooltip: "Current stream title of the most recent recording. Truncated — hover for the full title.",   min_width: 80.0,  initial: 170.0, sortable: true },
3862    StreamCol { title: "✏",          tooltip: "Title / game-category changes logged during the recording. Hover or double-click for the log.", min_width: 24.0, initial: 0.0, sortable: true },
3863    StreamCol { title: "📢",         tooltip: "Ad breaks detected (Twitch + streamlink); each is a hard cut. Hover for count + total time; double-click for the cut list.", min_width: 24.0, initial: 0.0, sortable: true },
3864    StreamCol { title: "Went Live",  tooltip: "When the stream went live on the platform (a trailing \"~\" means it's our approximate time).", min_width: 96.0, initial: 0.0,  sortable: true },
3865    StreamCol { title: "Started On", tooltip: "When recording started.",                                                                    min_width: 92.0,  initial: 0.0,   sortable: true },
3866    StreamCol { title: "Lost time",  tooltip: "How much of the start was missed. Drops to 0 once a from-start capture catches up to the live edge.", min_width: 52.0, initial: 0.0, sortable: true },
3867    StreamCol { title: "Duration",   tooltip: "How long we've recorded (ticks while live).",                                               min_width: 56.0,  initial: 0.0,   sortable: true },
3868    StreamCol { title: "Ad-free",    tooltip: "Marked or auto-detected ad-free (sub / Turbo / Premium) — captures have no ad-break cuts.", min_width: 54.0,  initial: 0.0,   sortable: true },
3869    StreamCol { title: "Added",      tooltip: "When the channel was added.",                                                               min_width: 84.0,  initial: 0.0,   sortable: true },
3870];
3871
3872/// Total Streams columns (19 total, including the non-sortable Actions slot).
3873const STREAM_COLS: usize = STREAM_COLUMNS.len(); // = 19
3874
3875/// Index of the optional Actions column in [`STREAM_COLUMNS`]. When the Actions
3876/// column is hidden, this column is skipped in the builder, header, and every row
3877/// renderer (kept in the sort/filter model — it's a non-sortable, empty slot).
3878const STREAM_ACTIONS_COL: usize = 1;
3879
3880/// Which column a table is sorted by and in what direction. `col == None` keeps
3881/// the natural (database) order.
3882#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
3883struct SortState {
3884    col: Option<usize>,
3885    ascending: bool,
3886}
3887
3888/// A cell's sort key: numeric columns sort numerically, text columns sort
3889/// case-insensitively. (Filtering always uses the cell's displayed `text`.)
3890enum SortKey {
3891    Num(f64),
3892    Text(String),
3893}
3894
3895/// A precomputed cell: `text` is what's shown/filtered (case-insensitive
3896/// substring), `key` is what's sorted.
3897struct Cell {
3898    text: String,
3899    key: SortKey,
3900}
3901
3902impl Cell {
3903    /// A text cell — filter and sort both use the string.
3904    fn text(s: impl Into<String>) -> Cell {
3905        let s = s.into();
3906        Cell {
3907            key: SortKey::Text(s.clone()),
3908            text: s,
3909        }
3910    }
3911    /// A numeric cell — sorts by `n`, filters/shows `display`.
3912    fn num(n: f64, display: impl Into<String>) -> Cell {
3913        Cell {
3914            text: display.into(),
3915            key: SortKey::Num(n),
3916        }
3917    }
3918}
3919
3920fn cmp_sort_key(a: &SortKey, b: &SortKey) -> std::cmp::Ordering {
3921    use std::cmp::Ordering;
3922    match (a, b) {
3923        (SortKey::Num(x), SortKey::Num(y)) => x.partial_cmp(y).unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal),
3924        (SortKey::Text(x), SortKey::Text(y)) => x.to_lowercase().cmp(&y.to_lowercase()),
3925        _ => Ordering::Equal,
3926    }
3927}
3928
3929/// Filter then sort `rows`, returning surviving original indices in display
3930/// order. `filters[c]` is a case-insensitive substring filter for column `c`.
3931fn ordered_rows(rows: &[Vec<Cell>], sort: &SortState, filters: &[String]) -> Vec<usize> {
3932    let mut idx: Vec<usize> = (0..rows.len())
3933        .filter(|&i| {
3934            filters.iter().enumerate().all(|(c, f)| {
3935                let f = f.trim().to_lowercase();
3936                f.is_empty()
3937                    || rows[i]
3938                        .get(c)
3939                        .map(|cell| cell.text.to_lowercase().contains(&f))
3940                        .unwrap_or(true)
3941            })
3942        })
3943        .collect();
3944    if let Some(c) = sort.col {
3945        // `sort_by` is stable, so equal keys keep their natural order.
3946        idx.sort_by(|&a, &b| {
3947            let o = match (rows[a].get(c), rows[b].get(c)) {
3948                (Some(x), Some(y)) => cmp_sort_key(&x.key, &y.key),
3949                _ => std::cmp::Ordering::Equal,
3950            };
3951            if sort.ascending { o } else { o.reverse() }
3952        });
3953    }
3954    idx
3955}
3956
3957/// Render one sortable + optionally filterable header cell for column `idx`: a
3958/// click-to-sort title (with ▲/▼ when active) above a filter box.
3959fn sort_filter_header(
3960    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
3961    idx: usize,
3962    title: &str,
3963    tooltip: &str,
3964    filterable: bool,
3965    sort: &mut SortState,
3966    filter: &mut String,
3967) {
3968    ui.vertical(|ui| {
3969        let active = sort.col == Some(idx);
3970        let arrow = if active {
3971            if sort.ascending { " ▲" } else { " ▼" }
3972        } else {
3973            ""
3974        };
3975        let hover = if tooltip.is_empty() {
3976            "Click to sort (click again to reverse)".to_string()
3977        } else {
3978            format!("{tooltip}\n\n(click to sort; click again to reverse)")
3979        };
3980        let resp = ui
3981            .add(egui::Button::new(egui::RichText::new(format!("{title}{arrow}")).strong()).frame(false))
3982            .on_hover_text(hover);
3983        if resp.clicked() {
3984            if active {
3985                sort.ascending = !sort.ascending;
3986            } else {
3987                *sort = SortState {
3988                    col: Some(idx),
3989                    ascending: true,
3990                };
3991            }
3992        }
3993        if filterable {
3994            ui.add(
3995                egui::TextEdit::singleline(filter)
3996                    .hint_text("filter")
3997                    .desired_width(f32::INFINITY),
3998            );
3999        }
4000    });
4001}
4002
4003/// Sort/filter cells for one video row, in Videos-table column order:
4004/// Video, Channel, Platform, Tool, Status, Speed, Size, Added, File.
4005fn video_cells(
4006    v: &Video,
4007    speed: &std::collections::HashMap<i64, f64>,
4008) -> Vec<Cell> {
4009    let label = if v.title.trim().is_empty() {
4010        v.url.clone()
4011    } else {
4012        v.title.clone()
4013    };
4014    // Speed is only meaningful while actively downloading.
4015    let spd = if v.status == "downloading" {
4016        speed.get(&v.id).copied().unwrap_or(0.0)
4017    } else {
4018        0.0
4019    };
4020    vec![
4021        Cell::text(label),
4022        Cell::text(v.channel.clone()),
4023        Cell::text(v.platform.label()),
4024        Cell::text(v.tool.label()),
4025        Cell::text(v.status.clone()),
4026        Cell::num(spd, fmt_speed(spd)),
4027        Cell::num(
4028            v.bytes as f64,
4029            if v.bytes > 0 { fmt_bytes(v.bytes) } else { String::new() },
4030        ),
4031        Cell::num(v.created_at as f64, fmt_date(v.created_at)),
4032        Cell::text(v.output_path.clone()),
4033    ]
4034}
4035
4036/// Columns derived from a monitor's latest recording.
4037struct RecordingCells {
4038    /// True while the take is still in progress (status == "recording").
4039    active: bool,
4040    /// When *we* started recording (formatted for filter/sort; render via [`ts_label`]).
4041    started_on: String,
4042    /// Raw unix seconds for "Started On" — used by [`ts_label`] for compact tooltips.
4043    started_secs: i64,
4044    /// How long we've recorded (ticks while active; final length otherwise).
4045    duration: String,
4046    /// When the stream went live on the platform (`~`-prefixed if approximate, formatted).
4047    went_live: String,
4048    /// Raw unix seconds for "Went Live" — used by [`ts_went_live_label`].
4049    went_live_secs: i64,
4050    /// True when the went-live timestamp is our approximation, not the platform's.
4051    went_live_approx: bool,
4052    /// How much of the beginning we missed.
4053    lost: String,
4054}
4055
4056fn recording_cells(row: &MonitorWithChannel, now: i64) -> RecordingCells {
4057    let active = row.last_recording_status.as_deref() == Some("recording");
4058    let started = row.last_recording_started;
4059    let started_secs = started.unwrap_or(0);
4060    let dur = match (started, row.last_recording_ended, active) {
4061        (Some(s), _, true) => Some(now - s),
4062        (Some(s), Some(e), false) => Some(e - s),
4063        _ => None,
4064    };
4065    let went_live_secs = row.last_recording_went_live.unwrap_or(0);
4066    let went_live_approx = row.last_recording_went_live_approx;
4067    let went_live = match row.last_recording_went_live {
4068        Some(w) => {
4069            let s = fmt_datetime_short(w);
4070            if went_live_approx {
4071                format!("{s}~")
4072            } else {
4073                s
4074            }
4075        }
4076        None => String::new(),
4077    };
4078    // Prefer the resolved lost time (0 once a from-start capture caught up, or
4079    // the exact residual) when known; else fall back to started - went_live.
4080    let lost = match row.last_recording_lost_secs {
4081        Some(s) => fmt_duration(s.max(0)),
4082        None => match (started, row.last_recording_went_live) {
4083            (Some(s), Some(w)) => fmt_duration((s - w).max(0)),
4084            _ => String::new(),
4085        },
4086    };
4087    RecordingCells {
4088        active,
4089        started_on: started.map(fmt_datetime_short).unwrap_or_default(),
4090        started_secs,
4091        duration: dur.map(fmt_duration).unwrap_or_default(),
4092        went_live,
4093        went_live_secs,
4094        went_live_approx,
4095        lost,
4096    }
4097}
4098
4099/// Theme color for a recording / stream status string.
4100/// Short abbreviation for the Tool column — narrower than the full label.
4101fn short_tool_label(tool: crate::models::Tool) -> &'static str {
4102    match tool {
4103        crate::models::Tool::Streamlink => "SL",
4104        crate::models::Tool::YtDlp => "yt-dlp",
4105        crate::models::Tool::Ffmpeg => "ff",
4106    }
4107}
4108
4109/// Icon for the Detection column — one or two Unicode chars that convey the
4110/// detection mechanism. Tooltip shows the full label + explanation.
4111fn detection_icon(m: crate::models::DetectionMethod) -> &'static str {
4112    use crate::models::DetectionMethod::*;
4113    match m {
4114        TwitchApi | YouTubeApi | KickApi => "↺",  // API polling
4115        Scrape => "⌁",                            // page scrape
4116        CliSelfPoll => "C",                       // CLI retry loop
4117        GenericProbe => "◉",                      // HTTP probe
4118        EventSub => "⚡",                          // pure push event
4119        EventSubHelix => "⚡↺",                   // push + poll fallback
4120        WebSub | WebSubOnly => "⚡",             // WebSub push
4121    }
4122}
4123
4124/// Icon + color for the State column. Returns `(icon, text_color)`.
4125fn state_icon(state: &str) -> (&'static str, egui::Color32) {
4126    use egui::Color32;
4127    match state {
4128        "recording" => ("⏺", Color32::from_rgb(0x4d, 0x9b, 0xff)), // blue
4129        "live" => ("●", SUCCESS_GREEN),                              // green (live not yet recording)
4130        "failed" => ("⚠", HL_ERROR_TEXT),                           // red
4131        "stopped" => ("⏹", Color32::from_gray(0xa0)),               // gray
4132        "aborted" => ("⚡", Color32::from_rgb(0xe0, 0xa8, 0x50)),   // amber
4133        "ended" => ("✔", Color32::from_gray(0xa0)),                  // gray
4134        "completed" => ("✔", SUCCESS_GREEN),                         // green
4135        _ => ("○", Color32::from_gray(0x70)),                        // idle/unknown — dim
4136    }
4137}
4138
4139/// Render the Streams-tree Name cell: indent by `depth`, a clickable ▶/▼ when
4140/// `has_children`, then `label`. Returns true if the disclosure was clicked.
4141fn tree_name(
4142    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
4143    depth: usize,
4144    has_children: bool,
4145    expanded: bool,
4146    label: impl Into<egui::WidgetText>,
4147) -> bool {
4148    let mut clicked = false;
4149    ui.add_space(depth as f32 * 16.0);
4150    if has_children {
4151        let tri = if expanded { "▼" } else { "▶" };
4152        if ui
4153            .add(egui::Button::new(tri).small().frame(false))
4154            .on_hover_text("Expand / collapse")
4155            .clicked()
4156        {
4157            clicked = true;
4158        }
4159    } else {
4160        ui.add_space(16.0); // align with rows that have a triangle
4161    }
4162    ui.label(label);
4163    clicked
4164}
4165
4166/// Compact, readable form of an instance's source URL for the Name cell (drops
4167/// the scheme and a leading `www.`).
4168fn instance_label(url: &str) -> String {
4169    let s = url.trim();
4170    let s = s
4171        .strip_prefix("https://")
4172        .or_else(|| s.strip_prefix("http://"))
4173        .unwrap_or(s);
4174    let s = s.strip_prefix("www.").unwrap_or(s);
4175    let s = s.trim_end_matches('/');
4176    if s.is_empty() { "(no URL)".to_string() } else { s.to_string() }
4177}
4178
4179/// The platform shared by all of a channel's instances, or `None` if they differ
4180/// (or there are none) — drives the container row's badge.
4181fn channel_platform(monitors: &[&MonitorWithChannel]) -> Option<Platform> {
4182    let mut it = monitors.iter().map(|m| m.monitor.platform());
4183    let first = it.next()?;
4184    if it.all(|p| p == first) { Some(first) } else { None }
4185}
4186
4187/// The channel's most-recent-activity instance, which drives the container row's
4188/// time + ad columns. `None` only for an empty container. Shared by the
4189/// sort/filter model and the row render so they can't drift.
4190fn channel_primary<'a>(monitors: &[&'a MonitorWithChannel]) -> Option<&'a MonitorWithChannel> {
4191    monitors
4192        .iter()
4193        .copied()
4194        .max_by_key(|m| m.last_recording_started.unwrap_or(0))
4195}
4196
4197/// How many of the channel's instances are ad-free (manual flag or detected sub).
4198fn channel_ad_free_count(monitors: &[&MonitorWithChannel]) -> usize {
4199    monitors
4200        .iter()
4201        .filter(|m| ad_free_status(m.monitor.ad_free, m.ad_free_sub).is_some())
4202        .count()
4203}
4204
4205/// Sort/filter cells for a channel container's top-level row (matches the table
4206/// columns). `channel` is the container; `monitors` are its instances (possibly
4207/// none, for an empty container).
4208fn channel_cells(channel: &Channel, monitors: &[&MonitorWithChannel], now: i64) -> Vec<Cell> {
4209    if monitors.is_empty() {
4210        // Empty container: just the name + "added"; everything else blank. Index
4211        // order matches STREAM_COLUMNS: On=0, Name=3, Added=last.
4212        let mut cells: Vec<Cell> = (0..STREAM_COLS).map(|_| Cell::text(String::new())).collect();
4213        cells[0] = Cell::num(0.0, "off");
4214        cells[3] = Cell::text(channel.name.clone());
4215        cells[STREAM_COLS - 1] = Cell::num(channel.created_at as f64, fmt_date(channel.created_at));
4216        return cells;
4217    }
4218    let any_recording = monitors
4219        .iter()
4220        .any(|m| m.last_recording_status.as_deref() == Some("recording"));
4221    // The most recent recording across instances drives the time columns.
4222    let primary = channel_primary(monitors).unwrap_or(monitors[0]);
4223    let rec = recording_cells(primary, now);
4224    let ninst = monitors.len();
4225    let tool = ninst.to_string();
4226    let last = monitors
4227        .iter()
4228        .filter_map(|m| m.monitor.last_checked_at)
4229        .max()
4230        .unwrap_or(0);
4231    // In STREAM_COLUMNS order: Auto, Actions(empty), Plat, Name, Tool, Detection,
4232    // Polled, State, Next stream, Game, Title, ✏ (Changes), 📢 (Ads), Went Live,
4233    // Started On, Lost, Duration, Ad-free, Added.
4234    vec![
4235        Cell::num(
4236            if channel.enabled { 1.0 } else { 0.0 },
4237            if channel.enabled { "on" } else { "off" },
4238        ),
4239        Cell::text(String::new()), // actions (not sortable/filterable)
4240        Cell::text(
4241            channel_platform(monitors)
4242                .map(|p| p.label().to_string())
4243                .unwrap_or_else(|| "mixed".into()),
4244        ),
4245        Cell::text(channel.name.clone()),
4246        Cell::text(tool),
4247        Cell::text(String::new()), // detection
4248        Cell::num(last as f64, fmt_datetime_short(last)), // polled (datetime only)
4249        Cell::text(if any_recording { "recording".to_string() } else { String::new() }),
4250        {
4251            // Sort/show the channel's SOONEST upcoming stream across its instances.
4252            let next_at = monitors.iter().filter_map(|m| m.next_stream_at).min();
4253            Cell::num(
4254                next_at.unwrap_or(0) as f64,
4255                next_at.map(fmt_datetime_short).unwrap_or_default(),
4256            )
4257        },
4258        Cell::text(if rec.active { primary.last_recording_category.clone() } else { String::new() }),
4259        Cell::text(if rec.active { primary.last_recording_title.clone() } else { String::new() }),
4260        // ✏ Changes (index 11)
4261        Cell::num(
4262            primary.last_recording_meta_changes as f64,
4263            fmt_meta_count(primary.last_recording_meta_changes),
4264        ),
4265        // 📢 Ads combined (index 12) — sort by count; ad time surfaced via tooltip
4266        Cell::num(
4267            primary.last_recording_ad_count as f64,
4268            fmt_ad_count(primary.last_recording_ad_count),
4269        ),
4270        // Went Live (index 13)
4271        Cell::num(
4272            primary.last_recording_went_live.unwrap_or(0) as f64,
4273            rec.went_live.clone(),
4274        ),
4275        // Started On (index 14)
4276        Cell::num(
4277            primary.last_recording_started.unwrap_or(0) as f64,
4278            rec.started_on.clone(),
4279        ),
4280        Cell::num(0.0, rec.lost.clone()),
4281        Cell::num(0.0, rec.duration.clone()),
4282        {
4283            let (label, key) =
4284                ad_free_summary(channel_ad_free_count(monitors), monitors.len());
4285            Cell::num(key, label)
4286        },
4287        Cell::num(channel.created_at as f64, fmt_date(channel.created_at)),
4288    ]
4289}
4290
4291/// Self-mutating actions collected while rendering a capture-instance row.
4292#[derive(Default)]
4293struct RowActions {
4294    start: Option<i64>,                 // monitor id
4295    stop: Option<i64>,                  // monitor id
4296    stop_chat: Option<i64>,             // monitor id
4297    view_chat: Option<i64>,             // monitor id
4298    edit: Option<i64>,                  // monitor id
4299    add_instance: Option<i64>,          // channel id
4300    delete: Option<(i64, String)>,      // (monitor id, channel name)
4301    toggle_enabled: Option<(i64, bool)>,
4302    select: Option<i64>,                // monitor id
4303    open_schedule: Option<i64>,         // monitor id (open its Next stream popup)
4304    properties: Option<i64>,            // monitor id
4305    reorganize_monitor: Option<i64>,    // monitor id
4306    reorganize_channel: Option<i64>,    // channel id
4307    /// Target to open in the configured media player (set by "Stream in player").
4308    stream_in_player: Option<StreamTarget>,
4309    /// Monitor id to open a live stream in the player without recording (set by "Play new instance").
4310    play_new_instance: Option<i64>,
4311}
4312
4313/// Render one capture-instance (monitor) row across all columns, with the Name
4314/// column carrying the tree disclosure. Returns true if the disclosure (the
4315/// row's stream history) was toggled. Self-mutating picks land in `a`.
4316#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
4317fn render_instance_row(
4318    tr: &mut egui_extras::TableRow<'_, '_>,
4319    row: &MonitorWithChannel,
4320    ptex: &PlatformTextures,
4321    now: i64,
4322    recording: bool,
4323    chat_active: bool,
4324    highlight: bool,
4325    depth: usize,
4326    has_history: bool,
4327    expanded: bool,
4328    show_actions: bool,
4329    needs_remux_count: usize,
4330    stream_target: Option<&StreamTarget>,
4331    media_player: &str,
4332    a: &mut RowActions,
4333) -> bool {
4334    let m = &row.monitor;
4335    let rec = recording_cells(row, now);
4336    // The caller set the row's tint color (recording/ad/error/selected); paint it.
4337    tr.set_selected(highlight);
4338
4339    // Right-click context menu (shared with the inline action buttons).
4340    let add_menu = |ui: &mut egui::Ui, a: &mut RowActions| {
4341        ui.set_min_width(180.0);
4342        if recording {
4343            if ui.button("⏹  Stop recording").clicked() {
4344                a.stop = Some(m.id);
4345                ui.close();
4346            }
4347        } else if ui.button("▶  Start recording").clicked() {
4348            a.start = Some(m.id);
4349            ui.close();
4350        }
4351        if chat_active {
4352            if ui.button("💬  Stop chat download").clicked() {
4353                a.stop_chat = Some(m.id);
4354                ui.close();
4355            }
4356        }
4357        if m.chat_log {
4358            if ui.button("💬  View chat").clicked() {
4359                a.view_chat = Some(m.id);
4360                ui.close();
4361            }
4362        }
4363        ui.separator();
4364        if ui.button("🔗  Open channel URL").clicked() {
4365            ui.ctx().open_url(egui::OpenUrl::new_tab(row.monitor.url.clone()));
4366            ui.close();
4367        }
4368        let folder_exists = std::path::Path::new(&m.output_dir).is_dir();
4369        if ui
4370            .add_enabled(folder_exists, egui::Button::new("📂  Open output folder"))
4371            .clicked()
4372        {
4373            crate::platform::open_path(std::path::Path::new(&m.output_dir));
4374            ui.close();
4375        }
4376        {
4377            let ok = !media_player.is_empty()
4378                && stream_target.map(|t| playable_with(t, media_player)).unwrap_or(false);
4379            if ui
4380                .add_enabled(ok, egui::Button::new("⏵  Stream in player"))
4381                .on_hover_text(if recording {
4382                    "Open live capture in the configured media player"
4383                } else {
4384                    "Open most recent recording in the configured media player"
4385                })
4386                .on_disabled_hover_text(if media_player.is_empty() {
4387                    "Set a media player in Settings → Defaults first"
4388                } else if stream_target.is_some() {
4389                    "In-progress SABR capture needs mpv (separate audio/video files)"
4390                } else {
4391                    "No playable capture file found"
4392                })
4393                .clicked()
4394            {
4395                a.stream_in_player = stream_target.cloned();
4396                ui.close();
4397            }
4398        }
4399        if ui
4400            .add_enabled(!media_player.is_empty(), egui::Button::new("▷  Play new instance"))
4401            .on_hover_text("Tune into the stream at the live edge in the media player (does not record)")
4402            .on_disabled_hover_text("Set a media player in Settings → Defaults first")
4403            .clicked()
4404        {
4405            a.play_new_instance = Some(m.id);
4406            ui.close();
4407        }
4408        if ui.button("📋  Copy URL").clicked() {
4409            ui.ctx().copy_text(row.monitor.url.clone());
4410            ui.close();
4411        }
4412        ui.separator();
4413        if ui.button("✏  Edit instance…").clicked() {
4414            a.edit = Some(m.id);
4415            ui.close();
4416        }
4417        if ui.button("➕  Add instance to channel").clicked() {
4418            a.add_instance = Some(row.channel.id);
4419            ui.close();
4420        }
4421        let toggle_label = if m.enabled { "⏸  Disable" } else { "✔  Enable" };
4422        if ui.button(toggle_label).clicked() {
4423            a.toggle_enabled = Some((m.id, !m.enabled));
4424            ui.close();
4425        }
4426        ui.separator();
4427        if ui.button("📁  Re-organize recordings").on_hover_text("Move all recordings for this monitor into/out of subdirectories.").clicked() {
4428            a.reorganize_monitor = Some(m.id);
4429            ui.close();
4430        }
4431        ui.separator();
4432        if ui.button("🗑  Delete").clicked() {
4433            a.delete = Some((m.id, row.channel.name.clone()));
4434            ui.close();
4435        }
4436        ui.separator();
4437        if ui.button("ℹ  Properties").clicked() {
4438            a.properties = Some(m.id);
4439            ui.close();
4440        }
4441    };
4442
4443    let mut disclosure_clicked = false;
4444    // Column order: On · Actions · Platform · Name · Tool · Detection · Polled ·
4445    // State · Next stream · Game · Title · ✏ (Changes) · 📢 (Ads) · Went Live ·
4446    // Started On · Lost · Duration · Ad-free · Added.
4447    tr.col(|ui| {
4448        let mut on = m.enabled;
4449        let cb = ui.checkbox(&mut on, "");
4450        if cb.changed() {
4451            a.toggle_enabled = Some((m.id, on));
4452        }
4453        cb.context_menu(|ui| add_menu(ui, a));
4454    });
4455    if show_actions {
4456        tr.col(|ui| {
4457            ui.push_id(m.id, |ui| {
4458                let mut btns: Vec<egui::Response> = Vec::with_capacity(6);
4459                if recording {
4460                    let b = ui.small_button("⏹").on_hover_text("Stop / abort recording");
4461                    if b.clicked() {
4462                        a.stop = Some(m.id);
4463                    }
4464                    btns.push(b);
4465                } else {
4466                    let b = ui
4467                        .small_button("▶")
4468                        .on_hover_text("Start recording now (checks if live)");
4469                    if b.clicked() {
4470                        a.start = Some(m.id);
4471                    }
4472                    btns.push(b);
4473                }
4474                {
4475                    let player_ok = !media_player.is_empty()
4476                        && stream_target.map(|t| playable_with(t, media_player)).unwrap_or(false);
4477                    let b = ui
4478                        .add_enabled(player_ok, egui::Button::new("⏵").small())
4479                        .on_hover_text(if recording {
4480                            "Stream in player"
4481                        } else {
4482                            "Open in player"
4483                        })
4484                        .on_disabled_hover_text(if media_player.is_empty() {
4485                            "Set a media player in Settings → Defaults first"
4486                        } else if stream_target.is_some() {
4487                            "In-progress SABR capture needs mpv (separate audio/video files)"
4488                        } else {
4489                            "No playable capture file found"
4490                        });
4491                    if b.clicked() {
4492                        a.stream_in_player = stream_target.cloned();
4493                    }
4494                    btns.push(b);
4495                }
4496                {
4497                    let b = ui
4498                        .add_enabled(!media_player.is_empty(), egui::Button::new("▷").small())
4499                        .on_hover_text("Play new instance in media player at the live edge (does not record)")
4500                        .on_disabled_hover_text("Set a media player in Settings → Defaults first");
4501                    if b.clicked() {
4502                        a.play_new_instance = Some(m.id);
4503                    }
4504                    btns.push(b);
4505                }
4506                let b = ui.small_button("✏").on_hover_text("Edit");
4507                if b.clicked() {
4508                    a.edit = Some(m.id);
4509                }
4510                btns.push(b);
4511                let b = ui.small_button("➕").on_hover_text("Add another tool instance");
4512                if b.clicked() {
4513                    a.add_instance = Some(row.channel.id);
4514                }
4515                btns.push(b);
4516                let b = ui.small_button("🗑").on_hover_text("Delete this instance");
4517                if b.clicked() {
4518                    a.delete = Some((m.id, row.channel.name.clone()));
4519                }
4520                btns.push(b);
4521                for b in &btns {
4522                    b.context_menu(|ui| add_menu(ui, a));
4523                }
4524            });
4525        });
4526    }
4527    tr.col(|ui| {
4528        platform_icon(ui, ptex, m.platform()).on_hover_text(m.platform().label());
4529    });
4530    tr.col(|ui| {
4531        let (_, name_color) = state_icon(&m.last_state);
4532        disclosure_clicked = tree_name(
4533            ui,
4534            depth,
4535            has_history,
4536            expanded,
4537            egui::RichText::new(instance_label(&row.monitor.url)).color(name_color),
4538        );
4539        ui.response().on_hover_text(&row.monitor.url);
4540    });
4541    tr.col(|ui| {
4542        ui.label(short_tool_label(m.tool)).on_hover_text(m.tool.tooltip());
4543    });
4544    tr.col(|ui| {
4545        ui.label(detection_icon(m.detection_method)).on_hover_text(format!(
4546            "{}\n\n{}",
4547            m.detection_method.label(),
4548            m.detection_method.tooltip()
4549        ));
4550    });
4551    tr.col(|ui| {
4552        ui.label(fmt_polled(m.last_checked_at, m.poll_interval_secs))
4553            .on_hover_text(format!(
4554                "Last checked {} · polled every {}s",
4555                if m.last_checked_at.unwrap_or(0) > 0 {
4556                    fmt_datetime_short(m.last_checked_at.unwrap_or(0))
4557                } else {
4558                    "never".to_string()
4559                },
4560                m.poll_interval_secs,
4561            ));
4562    });
4563    tr.col(|ui| {
4564        ui.horizontal(|ui| {
4565            let (icon, color) = state_icon(&m.last_state);
4566            let resp = ui.colored_label(color, icon);
4567            let is_failed = m.last_state == "failed"
4568                || row.last_recording_status.as_deref() == Some("failed");
4569            if is_failed {
4570                resp.on_hover_text(fail_hover(&row.last_recording_log));
4571            } else {
4572                resp.on_hover_text(&m.last_state);
4573            }
4574            if chat_active {
4575                ui.colored_label(
4576                    egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x4a, 0xc2, 0xff),
4577                    egui::RichText::new("💬").small(),
4578                )
4579                .on_hover_text(
4580                    "Live-chat download is still running.\n\
4581                     Right-click → Stop chat download to abort it.",
4582                );
4583            }
4584            if needs_remux_count > 0 {
4585                let lbl = if needs_remux_count == 1 {
4586                    "⚠ needs remux".to_string()
4587                } else {
4588                    format!("⚠ {} need remux", needs_remux_count)
4589                };
4590                let tip = if needs_remux_count == 1 {
4591                    "1 recording is stuck as .ts — expand and right-click the take to re-remux.".to_string()
4592                } else {
4593                    format!("{} recordings are stuck as .ts — expand and right-click each take to re-remux.", needs_remux_count)
4594                };
4595                ui.colored_label(egui::Color32::from_rgb(220, 140, 30), lbl)
4596                    .on_hover_text(tip);
4597            }
4598        });
4599    });
4600    tr.col(|ui| {
4601        if next_stream_cell(ui, row.next_stream_at, &row.next_stream_title, true) {
4602            a.open_schedule = Some(m.id);
4603        }
4604    });
4605    tr.col(|ui| {
4606        if rec.active {
4607            meta_value_cell(ui, &row.last_recording_category);
4608        }
4609    });
4610    tr.col(|ui| {
4611        if rec.active {
4612            meta_value_cell(ui, &row.last_recording_title);
4613        }
4614    });
4615    // ✏ Changes (index 11)
4616    tr.col(|ui| {
4617        meta_cell(ui, row.last_recording_meta_changes, None, false);
4618    });
4619    // 📢 Ads combined (index 12)
4620    let (ad_c, ad_s) = (row.last_recording_ad_count, row.last_recording_ad_secs);
4621    tr.col(|ui| {
4622        combined_ads_cell(ui, ad_c, ad_s, None, None);
4623    });
4624    // Went Live (index 13)
4625    tr.col(|ui| {
4626        ts_went_live_label(ui, rec.went_live_secs, rec.went_live_approx);
4627    });
4628    // Started On (index 14)
4629    tr.col(|ui| {
4630        ts_label(ui, rec.started_secs);
4631    });
4632    tr.col(|ui| {
4633        let resp = ui.label(&rec.lost);
4634        if m.capture_from_start {
4635            resp.on_hover_text(
4636                "How much of the beginning we missed. Capturing from start, so this drops \
4637                 to 0 once the capture catches up to the live edge; until then it's an \
4638                 estimate (the gap before recording began).",
4639            );
4640        }
4641    });
4642    tr.col(|ui| {
4643        ui.label(&rec.duration);
4644    });
4645    tr.col(|ui| {
4646        if let Some((label, hover)) = ad_free_status(m.ad_free, row.ad_free_sub) {
4647            ui.colored_label(SUCCESS_GREEN, label).on_hover_text(hover);
4648        }
4649    });
4650    tr.col(|ui| {
4651        ui.label(fmt_date(row.channel.created_at));
4652    });
4653
4654    let row_resp = tr.response();
4655    if row_resp.clicked() || row_resp.secondary_clicked() {
4656        a.select = Some(m.id);
4657    }
4658    row_resp.context_menu(|ui| add_menu(ui, a));
4659    disclosure_clicked
4660}
4661
4662/// Draw a small colored brand badge for the platform.
4663fn platform_badge(ui: &mut egui::Ui, platform: Platform) -> egui::Response {
4664    use egui::{Color32, RichText};
4665    let (label, bg, fg) = match platform {
4666        Platform::Twitch => ("T", Color32::from_rgb(0x91, 0x46, 0xFF), Color32::WHITE),
4667        Platform::YouTube => ("▶", Color32::from_rgb(0xFF, 0x00, 0x00), Color32::WHITE),
4668        Platform::Kick => ("K", Color32::from_rgb(0x53, 0xFC, 0x18), Color32::BLACK),
4669        Platform::Generic => ("●", Color32::from_gray(0x80), Color32::WHITE),
4670    };
4671    ui.label(
4672        RichText::new(format!(" {label} "))
4673            .monospace()
4674            .strong()
4675            .color(fg)
4676            .background_color(bg),
4677    )
4678}
4679
4680/// Platform favicons, decoded to raw 32×32 RGBA at build time (see build.rs) and
4681/// embedded here so no image decoder ships in the binary.
4682const ICON_SRC: usize = 32;
4683/// On-screen icon size (favicons are designed for small sizes).
4684const ICON_PX: f32 = 16.0;
4685static TWITCH_ICON: &[u8] = include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/platform_twitch.rgba"));
4686static YOUTUBE_ICON: &[u8] = include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/platform_youtube.rgba"));
4687static KICK_ICON: &[u8] = include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/platform_kick.rgba"));
4688
4689/// GPU textures for the platform favicons, uploaded once and cheaply cloned
4690/// (each `TextureHandle` is reference-counted).
4691#[derive(Clone)]
4692struct PlatformTextures {
4693    twitch: egui::TextureHandle,
4694    youtube: egui::TextureHandle,
4695    kick: egui::TextureHandle,
4696}
4697
4698impl PlatformTextures {
4699    fn load(ctx: &egui::Context) -> PlatformTextures {
4700        let mk = |name: &str, rgba: &[u8]| {
4701            let image = egui::ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied([ICON_SRC, ICON_SRC], rgba);
4702            ctx.load_texture(format!("platform_{name}"), image, egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR)
4703        };
4704        PlatformTextures {
4705            twitch: mk("twitch", TWITCH_ICON),
4706            youtube: mk("youtube", YOUTUBE_ICON),
4707            kick: mk("kick", KICK_ICON),
4708        }
4709    }
4710
4711    /// The favicon for a platform, or `None` for `Generic` (no favicon → badge).
4712    fn get(&self, p: Platform) -> Option<&egui::TextureHandle> {
4713        match p {
4714            Platform::Twitch => Some(&self.twitch),
4715            Platform::YouTube => Some(&self.youtube),
4716            Platform::Kick => Some(&self.kick),
4717            Platform::Generic => None,
4718        }
4719    }
4720}
4721
4722/// Third-party emote-provider brand logos, rasterized from their SVGs to 64×64
4723/// straight-alpha RGBA at build time (see `decode_provider_logos` in build.rs) and
4724/// embedded so no SVG decoder ships in the binary. Used for the emote-viewer
4725/// launcher buttons (7TV blue, BTTV red). FFZ has no embedded logo (text button).
4726const LOGO_SRC: usize = 64;
4727static LOGO_7TV: &[u8] = include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/logo_7tv.rgba"));
4728static LOGO_BTTV: &[u8] = include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/logo_bttv.rgba"));
4729
4730/// GPU textures for the emote-provider logos, uploaded once and cheaply cloned.
4731#[derive(Clone)]
4732struct ProviderTextures {
4733    seventv: egui::TextureHandle,
4734    bttv: egui::TextureHandle,
4735}
4736
4737impl ProviderTextures {
4738    fn load(ctx: &egui::Context) -> ProviderTextures {
4739        let mk = |name: &str, rgba: &[u8]| {
4740            let image = egui::ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied([LOGO_SRC, LOGO_SRC], rgba);
4741            ctx.load_texture(format!("emote_logo_{name}"), image, egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR)
4742        };
4743        ProviderTextures {
4744            seventv: mk("7tv", LOGO_7TV),
4745            bttv: mk("bttv", LOGO_BTTV),
4746        }
4747    }
4748}
4749
4750/// Which emote provider the emote viewer is showing. Twitch is first-party
4751/// (directory-listed, opaque ids, no codes); the rest read their JSON manifests.
4752#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
4753enum EmoteProvider {
4754    Twitch,
4755    SevenTv,
4756    Bttv,
4757    Ffz,
4758}
4759
4760impl EmoteProvider {
4761    fn label(self) -> &'static str {
4762        match self {
4763            EmoteProvider::Twitch => "Twitch",
4764            EmoteProvider::SevenTv => "7TV",
4765            EmoteProvider::Bttv => "BetterTTV",
4766            EmoteProvider::Ffz => "FrankerFaceZ",
4767        }
4768    }
4769    fn manifest(self) -> Option<&'static str> {
4770        match self {
4771            EmoteProvider::Twitch => Some("twitch.json"),
4772            EmoteProvider::SevenTv => Some("7tv.json"),
4773            EmoteProvider::Bttv => Some("bttv.json"),
4774            EmoteProvider::Ffz => Some("ffz.json"),
4775        }
4776    }
4777}
4778
4779/// Open emote-viewer window: shows one provider's emotes for one channel. The emote
4780/// list is enumerated once on open (a dir-list / manifest parse) and split into
4781/// still-present (`active`) and gone-from-cache (`deprecated`) so the window can
4782/// repaint — e.g. for animation — without re-touching the disk each frame.
4783struct EmoteViewer {
4784    channel_name: String,
4785    provider: EmoteProvider,
4786    active: Vec<ViewerEmote>,
4787    deprecated: Vec<ViewerEmote>,
4788    emote_properties: Option<ViewerEmote>,
4789}
4790
4791impl EmoteViewer {
4792    fn new(channel_name: String, provider: EmoteProvider) -> EmoteViewer {
4793        let (active, deprecated): (Vec<ViewerEmote>, Vec<ViewerEmote>) =
4794            enumerate_provider_emotes(&channel_name, provider)
4795                .into_iter()
4796                .partition(|e| e.exists);
4797        EmoteViewer { channel_name, provider, active, deprecated, emote_properties: None }
4798    }
4799}
4800
4801/// Open asset change-history popup: a channel's recorded asset changes (added /
4802/// removed emotes, icon / banner / name-colour replacements) across all its
4803/// platforms, read from each platform's `asset_changes.jsonl` and formatted once
4804/// on open into display lines (newest first). Mirrors [`EmoteViewer`]'s
4805/// load-once-on-open pattern so the popup never touches the disk per frame.
4806struct AssetHistoryView {
4807    channel_name: String,
4808    /// Platforms this channel runs on (non-`Generic`), retained so the view can be
4809    /// reloaded in place when a background asset refetch lands while it's open.
4810    platforms: Vec<Platform>,
4811    lines: Vec<String>,
4812}
4813
4814impl AssetHistoryView {
4815    fn new(channel_name: String, platforms: &[Platform]) -> AssetHistoryView {
4816        let lines = load_asset_history_lines(&channel_name, platforms);
4817        AssetHistoryView { channel_name, platforms: platforms.to_vec(), lines }
4818    }
4819
4820    /// Re-read the change logs from disk (newest first), keeping the window open.
4821    /// Called when an asset refetch for this channel completes so a live history
4822    /// view reflects the just-recorded changes without a manual reopen.
4823    fn reload(&mut self) {
4824        self.lines = load_asset_history_lines(&self.channel_name, &self.platforms);
4825    }
4826}
4827
4828/// Short display label for an emote-provider manifest stem as stored in
4829/// [`crate::assets::AssetChange::provider`]. Falls back to the raw stem.
4830fn provider_label_from_id(id: &str) -> &str {
4831    match id {
4832        "7tv" => "7TV",
4833        "bttv" => "BTTV",
4834        "ffz" => "FFZ",
4835        other => other,
4836    }
4837}
4838
4839/// One display line for a recorded asset change, e.g.
4840/// `2026-06-29 14:30  Twitch · 7TV emote +PogU`, `… · Twitch icon replaced`, or
4841/// `… · Twitch name colour #9146FF → #00FF00`.
4842fn fmt_asset_change_line(platform: Platform, c: &crate::assets::AssetChange) -> String {
4843    let when = fmt_datetime_short(c.at);
4844    let what = match c.kind.as_str() {
4845        "emote" => {
4846            let prov = provider_label_from_id(&c.provider);
4847            // U+2212 MINUS SIGN for removals to line up visually with '+'.
4848            let sign = if c.action == "removed" { "−" } else { "+" };
4849            format!("{prov} emote {sign}{}", c.name)
4850        }
4851        "icon" => "icon replaced".to_string(),
4852        "banner" => "banner replaced".to_string(),
4853        "name_color" => match c.action.as_str() {
4854            "added" => format!("name colour set {}", c.new),
4855            "removed" => format!("name colour cleared (was {})", c.old),
4856            _ => format!("name colour {} → {}", c.old, c.new),
4857        },
4858        other => format!("{other} {}", c.action),
4859    };
4860    format!("{when}  {} · {what}", platform.label())
4861}
4862
4863/// Load and format a channel's recorded asset changes across all its (non-generic)
4864/// platforms, newest first. Empty when nothing has been recorded yet.
4865fn load_asset_history_lines(name: &str, platforms: &[Platform]) -> Vec<String> {
4866    let mut all: Vec<(Platform, crate::assets::AssetChange)> = Vec::new();
4867    for &p in platforms.iter().filter(|p| **p != Platform::Generic) {
4868        let dir = channel_asset_dir(name, p);
4869        for c in crate::assets::read_asset_changes(&dir) {
4870            all.push((p, c));
4871        }
4872    }
4873    // Newest first. `sort_by_key` is stable, so changes sharing a timestamp keep
4874    // their per-platform append order.
4875    all.sort_by_key(|c| std::cmp::Reverse(c.1.at));
4876    all.iter().map(|(p, c)| fmt_asset_change_line(*p, c)).collect()
4877}
4878
4879/// A loaded mainpage image asset (icon/banner) shown as a thumbnail in the channel
4880/// Properties window. Holds the full-resolution texture so the global Alt-hover
4881/// preview shows it at original size; the strip itself draws it small.
4882#[derive(Clone)]
4883struct AssetThumb {
4884    /// Human label, e.g. "Twitch icon" / "YouTube banner".
4885    label: String,
4886    /// Absolute path on disk (opened on click).
4887    path: std::path::PathBuf,
4888    tex: egui::TextureHandle,
4889    dims: (u32, u32),
4890}
4891
4892/// One row of the Properties window's per-platform asset-status grid. Every field is
4893/// derived from blocking filesystem I/O (`read_dir` for presence/variants, a recursive
4894/// depth-2 badge-dir count, a `read_dir` of the Twitch emote dir, a full JSON parse of
4895/// each third-party manifest, and a `read_to_string` of the fetch stamp). The grid is
4896/// rebuilt every frame the window is open, so these rows are computed once on open and
4897/// cached (see `channel_asset_status`) — doing the I/O per frame is dozens of syscalls
4898/// per repaint and can stall the UI thread for >10s on slow or AV-scanned storage.
4899#[derive(Clone)]
4900struct PlatformAssetStatus {
4901    platform: Platform,
4902    icon_present: bool,
4903    icon_variants: usize,
4904    banner_present: bool,
4905    banner_variants: usize,
4906    badges: usize,
4907    emotes: usize,
4908    stamp: String,
4909}
4910
4911/// Handle to the background thread loading a channel Properties window's per-open data.
4912/// Polled each frame the window is open until the [`PropsLoaded`] bundle arrives. See
4913/// the `props_load` field for why this work is off the UI thread.
4914struct PropsLoad {
4915    /// The channel being loaded; lets us ignore a bundle that arrives after the user
4916    /// switched the window to a different channel.
4917    channel_id: i64,
4918    rx: std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<PropsLoaded>,
4919}
4920
4921/// The fully-loaded per-open Properties data, produced on a background thread and
4922/// installed into the per-channel caches on the UI thread. Every field is the result of
4923/// blocking work (disk reads + image decode/upload, asset-dir enumeration, store-mutex
4924/// DB reads) that previously ran inline on the UI thread and could freeze the GUI.
4925struct PropsLoaded {
4926    channel_id: i64,
4927    /// `None` = no icon file found (a successful "no icon" result, not a failure).
4928    icon: Option<egui::TextureHandle>,
4929    thumbs: Vec<AssetThumb>,
4930    emote_counts: [(EmoteProvider, usize); 4],
4931    asset_status: Vec<PlatformAssetStatus>,
4932    cfg: crate::schedule_source::ChannelSourceConfig,
4933    source_order: Vec<SourceEntry>,
4934    scope: crate::schedule_source::SourceScopeConfig,
4935}
4936
4937/// In-flight native file/folder picker spawned on a background thread so the UI
4938/// thread is never blocked by the OS dialog. Polled each frame via `try_recv`.
4939struct PendingBrowse {
4940    rx: std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<Option<String>>,
4941    /// Called on the UI thread once the picker returns a path. Receives `&mut App`
4942    /// and the selected path; skipped when the user cancels (dialog returns `None`).
4943    apply: Box<dyn FnOnce(&mut StreamArchiverApp, String)>,
4944}
4945
4946/// Loaded rows returned by a background save-form thread; installed by
4947/// `drain_pending_save` once the thread completes.
4948struct SaveRows {
4949    rows: Vec<MonitorWithChannel>,
4950    channels: Vec<Channel>,
4951    next_streams: Vec<(i64, i64, String)>,
4952    yt_quota_today: i64,
4953    yt_quota_cutoff: i64,
4954    yt_search_today: i64,
4955    yt_search_cutoff: i64,
4956}
4957
4958/// In-flight form-save spawned on a background thread. The thread holds the store
4959/// mutex while doing the INSERT/UPDATE + reload queries, keeping the UI thread free.
4960struct PendingSave {
4961    rx: std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<Result<SaveRows, String>>,
4962}
4963
4964/// Build the Properties window's per-platform asset-status rows. Runs the blocking
4965/// filesystem I/O once on open (the result is cached in `channel_asset_status`); see
4966/// [`PlatformAssetStatus`] for why this must not run per frame. Logic mirrors what the
4967/// status grid used to compute inline: icon/banner presence + archived-variant counts,
4968/// a depth-2 badge-dir count, the Twitch emote-dir file count plus each third-party
4969/// manifest length, and the last-fetched stamp.
4970fn build_platform_asset_status(name: &str, platforms: &[Platform]) -> Vec<PlatformAssetStatus> {
4971    platforms
4972        .iter()
4973        .map(|&p| {
4974            let pdir = channel_asset_dir(name, p);
4975            let mut emotes = prop_count_dir_files(&pdir.join("emotes").join("twitch"));
4976            for src in &["bttv", "ffz", "7tv"] {
4977                emotes += prop_read_manifest_count(&pdir.join("emotes").join(format!("{src}.json")));
4978            }
4979            PlatformAssetStatus {
4980                platform: p,
4981                icon_present: prop_find_first(&pdir, "icon.").is_some(),
4982                icon_variants: prop_variant_count(&pdir, "icon"),
4983                banner_present: prop_find_first(&pdir, "banner.").is_some(),
4984                banner_variants: prop_variant_count(&pdir, "banner"),
4985                badges: prop_count_nested_dirs(&pdir.join("badges"), 2),
4986                emotes,
4987                stamp: fmt_asset_stamp(&pdir),
4988            }
4989        })
4990        .collect()
4991}
4992
4993/// One emote row for the emote viewer: a code resolved to its on-disk image, plus
4994/// whether that image still exists (absent ⇒ shown in the "Deprecated" section).
4995#[derive(Clone)]
4996struct ViewerEmote {
4997    name: String,
4998    id: String,
4999    ext: String,
5000    path: std::path::PathBuf,
5001    exists: bool,
5002}
5003
5004/// Draw one platform's icon (its favicon, or the colored badge for Generic),
5005/// returning the response so callers can attach the platform name on hover.
5006fn platform_icon(ui: &mut egui::Ui, ptex: &PlatformTextures, platform: Platform) -> egui::Response {
5007    match ptex.get(platform) {
5008        Some(handle) => {
5009            let tex = egui::load::SizedTexture::new(handle.id(), egui::vec2(ICON_PX, ICON_PX));
5010            ui.image(tex)
5011        }
5012        None => platform_badge(ui, platform),
5013    }
5014}
5015
5016/// Draw the platform icon(s) for a cell: one per distinct platform, side by side
5017/// (a channel may span several). Each shows the platform name on hover.
5018fn platform_icons(ui: &mut egui::Ui, ptex: &PlatformTextures, platforms: &[Platform]) {
5019    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
5020        ui.spacing_mut().item_spacing.x = 2.0;
5021        for &p in platforms {
5022            platform_icon(ui, ptex, p).on_hover_text(p.label());
5023        }
5024    });
5025}
5026
5027/// Distinct platforms among a channel's instances, in first-seen order.
5028fn channel_platforms(monitors: &[&MonitorWithChannel]) -> Vec<Platform> {
5029    let mut out: Vec<Platform> = Vec::new();
5030    for m in monitors {
5031        let p = m.monitor.platform();
5032        if !out.contains(&p) {
5033            out.push(p);
5034        }
5035    }
5036    out
5037}
5038
5039/// Tooltip for a failed row: the captured reason (last stderr line), if any.
5040fn fail_hover(log: &str) -> String {
5041    match log.lines().map(str::trim).rev().find(|l| !l.is_empty()) {
5042        Some(reason) => format!("Failed: {reason}"),
5043        None => "Failed (no captured output).".to_string(),
5044    }
5045}
5046
5047/// Spawn a native folder picker on a background thread. The picker blocks until
5048/// the user chooses or cancels; keeping it off the UI thread lets egui keep
5049/// painting (and the watchdog heartbeat keep beating). Returns a [`PendingBrowse`]
5050/// that the caller stores in `app.pending_browse`; the `apply` closure is called
5051/// on the UI thread once the user confirms a selection.
5052fn spawn_browse_folder(
5053    current: &str,
5054    apply: impl FnOnce(&mut StreamArchiverApp, String) + 'static,
5055) -> PendingBrowse {
5056    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel();
5057    let current = current.to_string();
5058    std::thread::Builder::new()
5059        .name("browse-folder".into())
5060        .spawn(move || {
5061            let mut dialog = rfd::FileDialog::new();
5062            if !current.trim().is_empty() && std::path::Path::new(&current).exists() {
5063                dialog = dialog.set_directory(&current);
5064            }
5065            let _ = tx.send(dialog.pick_folder().map(|p| p.to_string_lossy().to_string()));
5066        })
5067        .ok();
5068    PendingBrowse { rx, apply: Box::new(apply) }
5069}
5070
5071/// Same as [`spawn_browse_folder`] but opens a file picker instead.
5072fn spawn_browse_file(
5073    current: &str,
5074    apply: impl FnOnce(&mut StreamArchiverApp, String) + 'static,
5075) -> PendingBrowse {
5076    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel();
5077    let current = current.to_string();
5078    std::thread::Builder::new()
5079        .name("browse-file".into())
5080        .spawn(move || {
5081            let mut dialog = rfd::FileDialog::new();
5082            if let Some(parent) = std::path::Path::new(&current).parent() {
5083                if parent.is_dir() {
5084                    dialog = dialog.set_directory(parent);
5085                }
5086            }
5087            let _ = tx.send(dialog.pick_file().map(|p| p.to_string_lossy().to_string()));
5088        })
5089        .ok();
5090    PendingBrowse { rx, apply: Box::new(apply) }
5091}
5092
5093impl eframe::App for StreamArchiverApp {
5094    /// Non-drawing logic. eframe also calls this while the window is hidden when
