11010            if let Some(r) = self.rows.iter().find(|r| r.monitor.id == mid) {
11011                self.form = Some(MonitorForm::from_existing(r));
11012            }
11013        }
11014        if let Some(mid) = acts.properties {
11015            self.properties_popup = Some(mid);
11016            // Invalidate the full-size icon cache so the Properties window reloads it
11017            // (assets may have been fetched since last open). We do NOT invalidate
11018            // channel_icons_small here: the small avatar in the streams table is still
11019            // referenced in this frame's paint commands, and dropping it now would free
11020            // the texture from the shared painter before the main viewport paints,
11021            // causing "Failed to find texture" warnings. The small icon refreshes
11022            // automatically on the next AssetFetch completion (cleared in logic()).
11023            if let Some(r) = self.rows.iter().find(|r| r.monitor.id == mid) {
11024                self.channel_icons.remove(&r.channel.id);
11025                self.channel_twitch_colors.remove(&r.channel.id);
11026            }
11027        }
11028        if let Some(cid) = acts.add_instance {
11029            // Look up the container in `channels` (not `rows`) so this also works
11030            // for an empty container that has no instances yet.
11031            if let Some(c) = self.channels.iter().find(|c| c.id == cid) {
11032                self.form = Some(MonitorForm::add_instance(
11033                    c,
11034                    &self.monitor_defaults,
11035                    &self.settings.default_output_dir,
11036                ));
11037            }
11038        }
11039        if let Some(id) = acts.select {
11040            self.selected_monitor = Some(id);
11041        }
11042        if let Some((id, on)) = acts.toggle_enabled {
11043            if let Err(e) = self.core.store.set_monitor_enabled(id, on) {
11044                self.status = format!("Error: {e}");
11045            }
11046            self.reload_rows();
11047        }
11048        if let Some((id, name)) = acts.delete {
11049            self.confirm_delete = Some((id, name));
11050        }
11051        if let Some((cid, on)) = toggle_channel_enabled {
11052            if let Err(e) = self.core.store.set_channel_enabled(cid, on) {
11053                self.status = format!("Error: {e}");
11054            }
11055            self.reload_rows();
11056        }
11057        if let Some(cid) = rename_channel {
11058            if let Some(c) = self.channels.iter().find(|c| c.id == cid) {
11059                self.channel_form = Some(ChannelForm {
11060                    id: Some(cid),
11061                    name: c.name.clone(),
11062                    color: c.color.clone(),
11063                });
11064            }
11065        }
11066        if let Some((cid, name)) = delete_channel {
11067            self.confirm_delete_channel = Some((cid, name));
11068        }
11069        if let Some(cid) = clear_channel_err {
11070            if let Err(e) = self.core.store.clear_channel_errors(cid) {
11071                self.status = format!("Error: {e}");
11072            } else {
11073                self.reload_rows();
11074            }
11075        }
11076        if let Some(cid) = open_channel_props {
11077            self.channel_properties_popup = Some(cid);
11078            // Same reasoning as acts.properties above: only drop the full-size icon
11079            // (used inside the child viewport) — not the small avatar (still painted
11080            // by the main viewport this frame).
11081            self.channel_icons.remove(&cid);
11082            self.channel_twitch_colors.remove(&cid);
11083            self.channel_asset_thumbs.remove(&cid);
11084            self.channel_emote_counts.remove(&cid);
11085            self.channel_asset_status.remove(&cid);
11086        }
11087        if let Some(id) = acts.start {
11088            self.core.manual(ManualCommand::Start { id, notify_offline: true });
11089            self.status = "Checking channel… will record if live.".into();
11090        }
11091        if let Some(id) = acts.stop {
11092            self.core.manual(ManualCommand::Stop(id));
11093            self.status = "Stopping recording…".into();
11094        }
11095        if let Some(id) = acts.stop_chat {
11096            self.core.manual(ManualCommand::StopChat(id));
11097            self.status = "Stopping chat download…".into();
11098        }
11099        if let Some(mid) = acts.reorganize_monitor {
11100            self.core.manual(ManualCommand::ReorganizeMonitor(mid));
11101            self.status = "Re-organizing monitor recordings…".into();
11102        }
11103        if let Some(cid) = acts.reorganize_channel {
11104            self.core.manual(ManualCommand::ReorganizeChannel(cid));
11105            self.status = "Re-organizing channel recordings…".into();
11106        }
11107        if let Some(mid) = acts.view_chat {
11108            self.open_chat_popup(mid, ui.ctx());
11109        }
11110        if let Some(p) = open_path {
11111            crate::platform::open_path(&p);
11112        }
11113        if let Some(target) = open_in_player.or_else(|| acts.stream_in_player.take()) {
11114            let player = self.settings.media_player_path.trim().to_string();
11115            if !player.is_empty() {
11116                let _ = build_player_command(&player, &target).spawn();
11117            } else if let StreamTarget::Finished(p) | StreamTarget::Growing(p) = &target {
11118                // SplitAv is unreachable here: its buttons gate on a player.
11119                crate::platform::open_path(p);
11120            }
11121        }
11122        if let Some(mid) = play_new_instance_mid.or(acts.play_new_instance.take()) {
11123            let player = self.settings.media_player_path.trim().to_string();
11124            if !player.is_empty()
11125                && let Some(row) = self.rows.iter().find(|r| r.monitor.id == mid)
11126                && let Some(msg) =
11127                    spawn_play_new_instance(row, &player, &self.settings, &self.core.store)
11128            {
11129                self.status = msg;
11130            }
11131        }
11132        if let Some(t) = copy_text {
11133            ui.ctx().copy_text(t);
11134        }
11135        if let Some(rid) = delete_recording {
11136            if let Err(e) = self.core.store.delete_recording(rid) {
11137                self.status = format!("Error: {e}");
11138            }
11139            // The take (and its cascaded ad breaks / meta changes) is gone; close
11140            // any popup that referenced it (a take popup for it, or a stream popup
11141            // that included it).
11142            if self.ad_popup == Some(rid) {
11143                self.ad_popup = None;
11144            }
11145            let meta_refs_rid = match &self.meta_popup {
11146                Some(MetaPopup::Take(id)) => *id == rid,
11147                Some(MetaPopup::Stream(takes)) => takes.iter().any(|(id, _)| *id == rid),
11148                None => false,
11149            };
11150            if meta_refs_rid {
11151                self.meta_popup = None;
11152            }
11153            if self.rec_props == Some(rid) {
11154                self.rec_props = None;
11155            }
11156            self.reload_rows();
11157        }
11158        if let Some(rid) = open_recording_props {
11159            // Seed the notes draft from the cached recording (already loaded).
11160            if let Some(notes) = self
11161                .rec_cache
11162                .values()
11163                .flat_map(|v| v.iter())
11164                .find(|r| r.id == rid)
11165                .map(|r| r.notes.clone())
11166            {
11167                self.rec_props_notes = notes;
11168            }
11169            self.rec_props = Some(rid);
11170        }
11171    }
11172
11173    /// Kick off the Twitch device-code flow on the async runtime, updating the
11174    /// shared `twitch_flow` state as it progresses and waking the UI.
11175    fn start_twitch_connect(&mut self, ctx: egui::Context) {
11176        let client_id = self.settings.twitch_client_id.trim().to_string();
11177        if client_id.is_empty() {
11178            self.status = "Enter and save a Twitch Client ID first.".into();
11179            return;
11180        }
11181        // Persist the Client ID so the flow + later refresh can read it.
11182        let _ = self.core.store.set_setting(K_TWITCH_ID, &client_id);
11183
11184        let flow = self.twitch_flow.clone();
11185        let store = self.core.store.clone();
11186        *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Pending {
11187            user_code: String::new(),
11188            url: String::new(),
11189        };
11190        self.core.rt.spawn(async move {
11191            let http = match reqwest::Client::builder()
11192                .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(20))
11193                .build()
11194            {
11195                Ok(c) => c,
11196                Err(e) => {
11197                    *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Failed {
11198                        message: e.to_string(),
11199                    };
11200                    ctx.request_repaint();
11201                    return;
11202                }
11203            };
11204            let dc = match oauth::start_device(&http, &client_id).await {
11205                Ok(dc) => dc,
11206                Err(e) => {
11207                    *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Failed {
11208                        message: e.to_string(),
11209                    };
11210                    ctx.request_repaint();
11211                    return;
11212                }
11213            };
11214            *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Pending {
11215                user_code: dc.user_code.clone(),
11216                url: dc.verification_uri.clone(),
11217            };
11218            ctx.request_repaint();
11219            match oauth::poll_token(&http, &client_id, &dc).await {
11220                Ok(tokens) => match oauth::fetch_user(&http, &client_id, &tokens.access).await {
11221                    Ok((login, user_id)) => {
11222                        let _ = oauth::store_tokens(&store, &tokens, &login);
11223                        let _ = store.set_setting(oauth::K_USER_ID, &user_id);
11224                        *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Connected { login };
11225                    }
11226                    // Authorized, but the account lookup failed (after retries). Keep
11227                    // the valid tokens — detection only needs the token — but leave
11228                    // the user id unset, so sub-based ad-free detection stays off
11229                    // until a reconnect (rather than discarding the connection).
11230                    Err(e) => {
11231                        let _ = oauth::store_tokens(&store, &tokens, "");
11232                        warn!("Twitch connected, but Get Users failed: {e}");
11233                        *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Connected {
11234                            login: String::new(),
11235                        };
11236                    }
11237                },
11238                Err(e) => {
11239                    *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Failed {
11240                        message: e.to_string(),
11241                    }
11242                }
11243            }
11244            ctx.request_repaint();
11245        });
11246    }
11247
11248    /// Kick off the Google device-code flow (for YouTube subscriptions import),
11249    /// updating the shared `google_flow` state as it progresses.
11250    fn start_google_connect(&mut self, ctx: egui::Context) {
11251        let client_id = self.settings.google_client_id.trim().to_string();
11252        let client_secret = self.settings.google_client_secret.trim().to_string();
11253        if client_id.is_empty() || client_secret.is_empty() {
11254            self.status = "Enter and save a Google Client ID and Secret first.".into();
11255            return;
11256        }
11257        let _ = self.core.store.set_setting(google_oauth::K_CLIENT_ID, &client_id);
11258        let _ = self
11259            .core
11260            .store
11261            .set_setting(google_oauth::K_CLIENT_SECRET, &client_secret);
11262
11263        let flow = self.google_flow.clone();
11264        let store = self.core.store.clone();
11265        *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Pending {
11266            user_code: String::new(),
11267            url: String::new(),
11268        };
11269        self.core.rt.spawn(async move {
11270            let http = match reqwest::Client::builder()
11271                .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(20))
11272                .build()
11273            {
11274                Ok(c) => c,
11275                Err(e) => {
11276                    *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Failed { message: e.to_string() };
11277                    ctx.request_repaint();
11278                    return;
11279                }
11280            };
11281            let dc = match google_oauth::start_device(&http, &client_id).await {
11282                Ok(dc) => dc,
11283                Err(e) => {
11284                    *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Failed { message: e.to_string() };
11285                    ctx.request_repaint();
11286                    return;
11287                }
11288            };
11289            *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Pending {
11290                user_code: dc.user_code.clone(),
11291                url: dc.verification_uri.clone(),
11292            };
11293            ctx.request_repaint();
11294            match google_oauth::poll_token(&http, &client_id, &client_secret, &dc).await {
11295                Ok(tokens) => {
11296                    let _ = google_oauth::store_tokens(&store, &tokens);
11297                    let identity = google_oauth::fetch_identity(&http, &tokens.access)
11298                        .await
11299                        .unwrap_or_default();
11300                    let _ = store.set_setting(google_oauth::K_IDENTITY, &identity);
11301                    *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Connected { login: identity };
11302                }
11303                Err(e) => {
11304                    *flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Failed { message: e.to_string() };
11305                }
11306            }
11307            ctx.request_repaint();
11308        });
11309    }
11310
11311    /// Open the import dialog for `platform` and kick off the background fetch of
11312    /// the user's followed channels / subscriptions.
11313    fn open_import(&mut self, platform: Platform, ctx: egui::Context) {
11314        let load = Arc::new(Mutex::new(ImportLoadState::Loading));
11315        let load2 = load.clone();
11316        let store = self.core.store.clone();
11317        self.core.rt.spawn(async move {
11318            let result = match reqwest::Client::builder()
11319                .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30))
11320                .build()
11321            {
11322                Err(e) => ImportLoadState::Error(e.to_string()),
11323                Ok(http) => {
11324                    let fetched = match platform {
11325                        Platform::Twitch => imports::twitch_followed(&http, &store).await,
11326                        Platform::YouTube => imports::youtube_subscriptions(&http, &store).await,
11327                        _ => Err(anyhow::anyhow!("unsupported platform")),
11328                    };
11329                    match fetched {
11330                        Ok(c) => ImportLoadState::Loaded(c),
11331                        Err(e) => ImportLoadState::Error(e.to_string()),
11332                    }
11333                }
11334            };
11335            *load2.lock().unwrap() = result;
11336            ctx.request_repaint();
11337        });
11338        let title = match platform {
11339            Platform::Twitch => "Import followed Twitch channels",
11340            Platform::YouTube => "Import YouTube subscriptions",
11341            _ => "Import channels",
11342        }
11343        .to_string();
11344        self.import_dialog = Some(ImportDialog {
11345            title,
11346            load,
11347            rows: Vec::new(),
11348            loaded: false,
11349            search: String::new(),
11350            status: String::new(),
11351        });
11352    }
11353
11354    #[allow(deprecated)]
11355    fn import_window(&mut self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
11356        if self.import_dialog.is_none() {
11357            return;
11358        }
11359        // Promote a completed background fetch into editable rows once — this needs
11360        // `self.rows` to mark channels already added, so it happens before the
11361        // viewport closure borrows the dialog.
11362        let promote = {
11363            let d = self.import_dialog.as_ref().unwrap();
11364            !d.loaded && matches!(&*d.load.lock().unwrap(), ImportLoadState::Loaded(_))
11365        };
11366        if promote {
11367            // Confident dedup: per-platform identity (Twitch login / YouTube UC id).
11368            let existing_ids: HashSet<(Platform, String)> = self
11369                .rows
11370                .iter()
11371                .map(|r| (r.monitor.platform(), monitor_import_identity(&r.monitor.url)))
11372                .collect();
11373            // Fuzzy dedup: existing container names (catches a channel added under a
11374            // URL form whose identity can't be matched, e.g. a YouTube @handle).
11375            let existing_names: HashSet<String> =
11376                self.rows.iter().map(|r| r.channel.name.to_lowercase()).collect();
11377            let d = self.import_dialog.as_mut().unwrap();
11378            // Take the guard once (a second .lock() on the same thread would deadlock).
11379            let cands = {
11380                let mut g = d.load.lock().unwrap();
11381                match std::mem::replace(&mut *g, ImportLoadState::Loading) {
11382                    ImportLoadState::Loaded(c) => c,
11383                    other => {
11384                        *g = other;
11385                        Vec::new()
11386                    }
11387                }
11388            };
11389            d.rows = cands
11390                .into_iter()
11391                .map(|c| {
11392                    let already = existing_ids.contains(&(c.platform, c.identity.clone()));
11393                    let maybe_dup = !already && existing_names.contains(&c.name.to_lowercase());
11394                    ImportRow {
11395                        cand: c,
11396                        selected: !already && !maybe_dup,
11397                        auto: false,
11398                        disabled: false,
11399                        already,
11400                        maybe_dup,
11401                    }
11402                })
11403                .collect();
11404            d.loaded = true;
11405        }
11406
11407        let Some(dialog) = &mut self.import_dialog else {
11408            return;
11409        };
11410        let mut open = true;
11411        let mut do_import = false;
11412        let mut do_close = false;
11413
11414        ctx.show_viewport_immediate(
11415            egui::ViewportId::from_hash_of("import_vp"),
11416            egui::ViewportBuilder::default()
11417                .with_title(dialog.title.clone())
11418                .with_inner_size([620.0, 560.0]),
11419            |ctx, _class| {
11420                if ctx.input(|i| i.viewport().close_requested()) {
11421                    open = false;
11422                }
11423                egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
11424                    if !dialog.loaded {
11425                        match &*dialog.load.lock().unwrap() {
11426                            ImportLoadState::Loading => {
11427                                ui.horizontal(|ui| {
11428                                    ui.spinner();
11429                                    ui.label("Loading…");
11430                                });
11431                                ctx.request_repaint();
11432                            }
11433                            ImportLoadState::Error(e) => {
11434                                ui.add_space(8.0);
11435                                ui.colored_label(
11436                                    egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xE0, 0x6C, 0x6C),
11437                                    format!("Couldn't load: {e}"),
11438                                );
11439                            }
11440                            ImportLoadState::Loaded(_) => {
11441                                ctx.request_repaint(); // will promote next frame
11442                            }
11443                        }
11444                        ui.add_space(8.0);
11445                        if ui.button("Close").clicked() {
11446                            do_close = true;
11447                        }
11448                        return;
11449                    }
11450
11451                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
11452                        ui.label(format!("{} channels found.", dialog.rows.len()));
11453                        ui.with_layout(
11454                            egui::Layout::right_to_left(egui::Align::Center),
11455                            |ui| {
11456                                ui.add(
11457                                    egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut dialog.search)
11458                                        .hint_text("Filter…")
11459                                        .desired_width(160.0),
11460                                );
11461                                ui.label("🔍");
11462                            },
11463                        );
11464                    });
11465                    ui.label(
11466                        egui::RichText::new(
11467                            "Tick the channels to import. \"Auto\" lets the scheduler \
11468                             auto-record (off = monitor only); \"Disabled\" imports the \
11469                             channel turned off. Already-added channels are greyed out.",
11470                        )
11471                        .small()
11472                        .weak(),
11473                    );
11474                    ui.separator();
11475
11476                    let q = dialog.search.to_lowercase();
11477                    let visible: Vec<usize> = (0..dialog.rows.len())
11478                        .filter(|&i| import_row_matches(&dialog.rows[i], &q))
11479                        .collect();
11480                    let selectable: Vec<usize> =
11481                        visible.iter().copied().filter(|&i| !dialog.rows[i].already).collect();
11482
11483                    // Master controls.
11484                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
11485                        let mut all = !selectable.is_empty()
11486                            && selectable.iter().all(|&i| dialog.rows[i].selected);
11487                        if ui
11488                            .checkbox(&mut all, "All")
11489                            .on_hover_text("Select/deselect every (not-already-added) channel")
11490                            .changed()
11491                        {
11492                            for &i in &selectable {
11493                                dialog.rows[i].selected = all;
11494                            }
11495                        }
11496                        ui.separator();
11497                        if ui.small_button("Auto: all").clicked() {
11498                            for &i in &selectable {
11499                                if dialog.rows[i].selected {
11500                                    dialog.rows[i].auto = true;
11501                                }
11502                            }
11503                        }
11504                        if ui.small_button("Auto: none").clicked() {
11505                            for &i in &selectable {
11506                                dialog.rows[i].auto = false;
11507                            }
11508                        }
11509                    });
11510                    ui.add_space(4.0);
11511
11512                    egui::ScrollArea::vertical()
11513                        .auto_shrink([false, false])
11514                        .max_height(380.0)
11515                        .show(ui, |ui| {
11516                            egui::Grid::new("import_grid")
11517                                .num_columns(6)
11518                                .striped(true)
11519                                .spacing([10.0, 4.0])
11520                                .show(ui, |ui| {
11521                                    ui.strong("Import");
11522                                    ui.strong("Auto");
11523                                    ui.strong("Disabled");
11524                                    ui.strong("Channel");
11525                                    ui.strong("ID");
11526                                    ui.strong("Info");
11527                                    ui.end_row();
11528                                    for &i in &visible {
11529                                        let row = &mut dialog.rows[i];
11530                                        let on = row.selected && !row.already;
11531                                        ui.add_enabled(
11532                                            !row.already,
11533                                            egui::Checkbox::new(&mut row.selected, ""),
11534                                        );
11535                                        ui.add_enabled(
11536                                            on,
11537                                            egui::Checkbox::new(&mut row.auto, ""),
11538                                        );
11539                                        ui.add_enabled(
11540                                            on,
11541                                            egui::Checkbox::new(&mut row.disabled, ""),
11542                                        );
11543                                        if row.already {
11544                                            ui.weak(format!("{} (added)", row.cand.name));
11545                                        } else if row.maybe_dup {
11546                                            ui.horizontal(|ui| {
11547                                                ui.label(&row.cand.name);
11548                                                ui.weak("(maybe added)").on_hover_text(
11549                                                    "A channel with this name is already in your \
11550                                                     list — tick to import anyway.",
11551                                                );
11552                                            });
11553                                        } else {
11554                                            ui.label(&row.cand.name);
11555                                        }
11556                                        ui.add(
11557                                            egui::Label::new(
11558                                                egui::RichText::new(&row.cand.id).monospace().small(),
11559                                            )
11560                                            .truncate(),
11561                                        );
11562                                        ui.weak(&row.cand.detail);
11563                                        ui.end_row();
11564                                    }
11565                                });
11566                        });
11567
11568                    ui.separator();
11569                    let n = dialog
11570                        .rows
11571                        .iter()
11572                        .filter(|r| r.selected && !r.already)
11573                        .count();
11574                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
11575                        if ui
11576                            .add_enabled(
11577                                n > 0,
11578                                egui::Button::new(format!("Import {n} selected")),
11579                            )
11580                            .clicked()
11581                        {
11582                            do_import = true;
11583                        }
11584                        if ui.button("Cancel").clicked() {
11585                            do_close = true;
11586                        }
11587                        if !dialog.status.is_empty() {
11588                            ui.label(&dialog.status);
11589                        }
11590                    });
11591                });
11592            },
11593        );
11594
11595        // Collect the chosen rows before the dialog borrow ends (the create + reload
11596        // below need `&mut self`).
11597        let to_create: Vec<(String, String, bool, bool)> = if do_import {
11598            dialog
11599                .rows
11600                .iter()
11601                .filter(|r| r.selected && !r.already)
11602                .map(|r| (r.cand.name.clone(), r.cand.url.clone(), r.auto, r.disabled))
11603                .collect()
11604        } else {
11605            Vec::new()
11606        };
11607        let close = do_close || !open;
11608
11609        if do_import {
11610            let out = self.settings.default_output_dir.clone();
11611            let mut ok = 0usize;
11612            let mut failed = 0usize;
11613            let mut last_err: Option<String> = None;
11614            // Continue past a per-row failure so one bad channel can't drop the rest
11615            // of the batch.
11616            for (name, url, auto, disabled) in &to_create {
11617                match imports::create_monitor(
11618                    &self.core.store,
11619                    &self.monitor_defaults,
11620                    &out,
11621                    name,
11622                    url,
11623                    *auto,
11624                    !*disabled,
11625                ) {
11626                    Ok(_) => ok += 1,
11627                    Err(e) => {
11628                        failed += 1;
11629                        last_err = Some(e.to_string());
11630                    }
11631                }
11632            }
11633            self.reload_rows();
11634            self.status = if failed == 0 {
11635                format!("Imported {ok} channel(s).")
11636            } else {
11637                format!(
11638                    "Imported {ok} channel(s); {failed} failed{}.",
11639                    last_err.map(|e| format!(" (last: {e})")).unwrap_or_default()
11640                )
11641            };
11642            self.import_dialog = None;
11643        } else if close {
11644            self.import_dialog = None;
11645        }
11646    }
11647
11648    fn background_view(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui) {
11649        use egui_extras::{Column, TableBuilder};
11650        // Both elapsed-time and next-run-countdown labels update every second —
11651        // request a repaint so they tick continuously without needing mouse input.
11652        ui.ctx().request_repaint_after(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1));
11653        let now = now_unix();
11654        // Next-run estimates, plus the editable enable/disable state for each job.
11655        let reg = self.core.jobs.lock().unwrap().clone();
11656        let mut toggles: Vec<(&'static str, &'static str, bool)> = crate::events::TOGGLEABLE_JOBS
11657            .iter()
11658            .map(|(name, key)| (*name, *key, self.job_toggles.get(*key).copied().unwrap_or(true)))
11659            .collect();
11660        let before: Vec<bool> = toggles.iter().map(|t| t.2).collect();
11661
11662        egui::ScrollArea::vertical().show(ui, |ui| {
11663            ui.add_space(8.0);
11664
11665            // ── Scheduled (periodic jobs) ────────────────────────────────
11666            ui.strong("Scheduled");
11667            ui.label(
11668                egui::RichText::new(
11669                    "Recurring background jobs. Untick to disable — turning off Live poll \
11670                     pauses all detection/recording.",
11671                )
11672                .small()
11673                .weak(),
11674            );
11675            ui.add_space(4.0);
11676            egui::Grid::new("bg_scheduled_grid")
11677                .num_columns(4)
11678                .striped(true)
11679                .spacing([16.0, 6.0])
11680                .show(ui, |ui| {
11681                    ui.strong("On");
11682                    ui.strong("Job");
11683                    ui.strong("Every");
11684                    ui.strong("Next run");
11685                    ui.end_row();
11686                    for (name, _key, en) in toggles.iter_mut() {
11687                        ui.checkbox(en, "");
11688                        ui.label(*name);
11689                        let r = reg.iter().find(|j| j.name == *name);
11690                        ui.label(
11691                            r.map(|j| fmt_duration_secs(j.interval_secs))
11692                                .unwrap_or_else(|| "—".into()),
11693                        );
11694                        if !*en {
11695                            ui.weak("disabled");
11696                        } else {
11697                            ui.label(
11698                                r.map(|j| fmt_relative_future(j.next_run_at - now))
11699                                    .unwrap_or_else(|| "pending".into()),
11700                            );
11701                        }
11702                        ui.end_row();
11703                    }
11704                });
11705
11706            ui.add_space(12.0);
11707
11708            // ── Active tasks ─────────────────────────────────────────────
11709            ui.strong("Active");
11710            ui.add_space(4.0);
11711
11712            if self.background_tasks.is_empty() {
11713                ui.weak("No tasks running.");
11714            } else {
11715                ui.push_id("bg_active", |ui| {
11716                    TableBuilder::new(ui)
11717                        .striped(true)
11718                        .cell_layout(egui::Layout::left_to_right(egui::Align::Center))
11719                        .column(Column::auto())                  // Channel
11720                        .column(Column::auto())                  // Task
11721                        .column(Column::remainder().clip(true))  // Detail
11722                        .column(Column::auto())                  // Elapsed
11723                        .header(20.0, |mut h| {
11724                            h.col(|ui| { ui.strong("Channel / Label"); });
11725                            h.col(|ui| { ui.strong("Task"); });
11726                            h.col(|ui| { ui.strong("Detail"); });
11727                            h.col(|ui| { ui.strong("Elapsed"); });
11728                        })
11729                        .body(|mut body| {
11730                            for task in &self.background_tasks {
11731                                body.row(20.0, |mut row| {
11732                                    row.col(|ui| { ui.label(&task.label); });
11733                                    row.col(|ui| { ui.label(task.kind.label()); });
11734                                    row.col(|ui| {
11735                                        // Show live ffmpeg stats when available; fall back to static detail.
11736                                        let text = task.progress_info.as_deref().unwrap_or(&task.detail);
11737                                        if let Some(p) = task.progress {
11738                                            ui.add(egui::ProgressBar::new(p).show_percentage().desired_width(90.0));
11739                                            ui.label(text);
11740                                        } else {
11741                                            ui.label(text);
11742                                        }
11743                                    });
11744                                    row.col(|ui| {
11745                                        ui.label(format!(
11746                                            "⏳ {}",
11747                                            fmt_duration_secs(now - task.started_at)
11748                                        ));
11749                                    });
11750                                });
11751                            }
11752                        });
11753                });
11754            }
11755
11756            ui.add_space(12.0);
11757
11758            // ── Recent completed / failed ────────────────────────────────
11759            ui.strong("Recent");
11760            ui.add_space(4.0);
11761
11762            if self.finished_tasks.is_empty() {
11763                ui.weak("No completed tasks yet.");
11764            } else {
11765                ui.push_id("bg_recent", |ui| {
11766                    TableBuilder::new(ui)
11767                        .striped(true)
11768                        .cell_layout(egui::Layout::left_to_right(egui::Align::Center))
11769                        .column(Column::auto())
11770                        .column(Column::auto())
11771                        .column(Column::remainder().clip(true))
11772                        .column(Column::auto())
11773                        .header(20.0, |mut h| {
11774                            h.col(|ui| { ui.strong("Channel / Label"); });
11775                            h.col(|ui| { ui.strong("Task"); });
11776                            h.col(|ui| { ui.strong("Detail"); });
11777                            h.col(|ui| { ui.strong("Outcome"); });
11778                        })
11779                        .body(|mut body| {
11780                            for (task, outcome, finished_at) in &self.finished_tasks {
11781                                let dur = fmt_duration_secs(finished_at - task.started_at);
11782                                body.row(20.0, |mut row| {
11783                                    row.col(|ui| { ui.label(&task.label); });
11784                                    row.col(|ui| { ui.label(task.kind.label()); });
11785                                    row.col(|ui| { ui.label(&task.detail); });
11786                                    row.col(|ui| {
11787                                        match outcome {
11788                                            crate::events::TaskOutcome::Completed => {
11789                                                ui.label(format!("✔ OK ({dur})"));
11790                                            }
11791                                            crate::events::TaskOutcome::CompletedWithNote(note) => {
11792                                                // "0 events" is a soft-warn (OCR ran but found
11793                                                // nothing); anything else is a normal success.
11794                                                let zero = note.starts_with("0 ");
11795                                                let text = format!("{} ({dur})", note);
11796                                                if zero {
11797                                                    ui.colored_label(
11798                                                        egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 160, 50),
11799                                                        format!("⚠ {text}"),
11800                                                    );
11801                                                } else {
11802                                                    ui.colored_label(
11803                                                        egui::Color32::from_rgb(80, 200, 120),
11804                                                        format!("✔ {text}"),
11805                                                    );
11806                                                }
11807                                            }
11808                                            crate::events::TaskOutcome::Failed(e) => {
11809                                                ui.colored_label(
11810                                                    egui::Color32::from_rgb(220, 80, 80),
11811                                                    format!("✘ {e}"),
11812                                                );
11813                                            }
11814                                        }
11815                                    });
11816                                });
11817                            }
11818                        });
11819                });
11820            }
11821
11822            ui.add_space(8.0);
11823        });
11824
11825        // Persist any toggle changes (after the closure releases its borrows).
11826        for ((_, key, en), was) in toggles.iter().zip(before.iter()) {
11827            if en != was {
11828                self.job_toggles.insert((*key).to_string(), *en);
11829                let _ = self.core.store.set_setting(key, if *en { "1" } else { "0" });
11830            }
11831        }
11832    }
11833
11834    fn settings_view(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui) {
11835        egui::ScrollArea::vertical().show(ui, |ui| {
11836            ui.add_space(8.0);
11837            ui.heading("Detection credentials (optional)");
11838            ui.label("Used only by monitors set to an API detection method.");
11839            egui::Grid::new("creds_grid")
11840                .num_columns(2)
11841                .spacing([12.0, 8.0])
11842                .show(ui, |ui| {
11843                    ui.label("Twitch Client ID");
11844                    ui.text_edit_singleline(&mut self.settings.twitch_client_id);
11845                    ui.end_row();
11846                    ui.label("Twitch Client Secret");
11847                    ui.add(
11848                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.twitch_client_secret)
11849                            .password(true),
11850                    );
11851                    ui.end_row();
11852                    ui.label("YouTube API Key");
11853                    ui.add(
11854                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.youtube_api_key)
11855                            .password(true),
11856                    );
11857                    ui.end_row();
11858                    ui.label("Kick Client ID");
11859                    ui.text_edit_singleline(&mut self.settings.kick_client_id);
11860                    ui.end_row();
11861                    ui.label("Kick Client Secret");
11862                    ui.add(
11863                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.kick_client_secret)
11864                            .password(true),
11865                    );
11866                    ui.end_row();
11867                });
11868
11869            ui.add_space(12.0);
11870            ui.heading("YouTube Data API usage");
11871            let key_set = !self.settings.youtube_api_key.trim().is_empty();
11872            ui.label(
11873                "By default these YouTube features scrape public pages (free, but can break \
11874                 when YouTube changes them). With the YouTube API Key set above you can use the \
11875                 Data API instead for more reliable results — but each call spends quota (the \
11876                 free daily quota is ~10,000 units).",
11877            );
11878            if !key_set {
11879                ui.colored_label(
11880                    egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xe0, 0xb0, 0x6c),
11881                    "⚠ Set a YouTube API Key above to enable these.",
11882                );
11883            }
11884            ui.add_enabled_ui(key_set, |ui| {
11885                ui.checkbox(
11886                    &mut self.settings.youtube_api_detect,
11887                    "Live detection (instead of scraping /live)",
11888                )
11889                .on_hover_text(
11890                    "Use search.list for liveness on YouTube monitors whose detection method is \
11891                     'Scrape'. ~100 quota units per check — with a long poll interval. (Monitors \
11892                     already set to the 'YouTube Data API' method use it regardless.)",
11893                );
11894                ui.checkbox(
11895                    &mut self.settings.youtube_api_schedule,
11896                    "Upcoming schedule — exact times via videos.list",
11897                )
11898                .on_hover_text(
11899                    "Scraping /streams parses human-readable text so times are approximate. \
11900                     With this enabled, scheduled stream video IDs are collected during scraping \
11901                     and batched into a single videos.list call (~1 quota unit for ALL channels \
11902                     combined) to get exact scheduled start times from the API.",
11903                );
11904                if self.settings.youtube_api_schedule {
11905                    if ui
11906                        .button("Re-fetch missing video IDs")
11907                        .on_hover_text(
11908                            "Re-scrape YouTube channels whose schedule entries are missing video \
11909                             IDs (needed for exact times). Only fetches channels with gaps — \
11910                             others keep their cached schedules.",
11911                        )
11912                        .clicked()
11913                    {
11914                        self.core.request_yt_video_id_refetch();
11915                    }
11916                }
11917                ui.add_space(4.0);
11918                ui.horizontal(|ui| {
11919                    ui.label("Daily quota limit (units)");
11920                    ui.add(
11921                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.youtube_api_quota_cutoff)
11922                            .desired_width(80.0)
11923                            .hint_text("9000"),
11924                    )
11925                    .on_hover_text(
11926                        "Stop making YouTube Data API calls today once this many units are spent. \
11927                         The free tier allows 10,000 units/day; leaving a buffer prevents outages \
11928                         from unexpected bursts. Default: 9000.",
11929                    );
11930                });
11931                ui.horizontal(|ui| {
11932                    ui.label("Search query warning cutoff");
11933                    ui.add(
11934                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.youtube_search_quota_cutoff)
11935                            .desired_width(80.0)
11936                            .hint_text("90"),
11937                    )
11938                    .on_hover_text(
11939                        "Show a warning in Issues when today's search.list call count reaches this \
11940                         value. The free tier allows 100 search queries/day. Default: 90.",
11941                    );
11942                });
11943            });
11944
11945            ui.add_space(12.0);
11946            ui.heading("Discord schedule import");
11947            ui.label(
11948                "Import upcoming streams from Discord scheduled events in the servers you're in. \
11949                 Events whose location/description contains a monitored channel's stream URL are \
11950                 attached to it — useful for streamers who post their schedule on Discord but \
11951                 don't publish a Twitch/YouTube one.",
11952            );
11953            ui.colored_label(
11954                egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xe0, 0x6c, 0x6c),
11955                "⚠ This uses your personal Discord token. Automating a user token is against \
11956                 Discord's Terms of Service and could get your account banned — use at your own risk.",
11957            );
11958            ui.horizontal(|ui| {
11959                ui.label("Discord user token");
11960                ui.add(
11961                    egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.discord_token)
11962                        .password(true)
11963                        .desired_width(280.0),
11964                );
11965            });
11966            let token_set = !self.settings.discord_token.trim().is_empty();
11967            if !token_set {
11968                ui.colored_label(
11969                    egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xe0, 0xb0, 0x6c),
11970                    "⚠ Paste your Discord token above to enable import.",
11971                );
11972            }
11973            ui.add_enabled_ui(token_set, |ui| {
11974                ui.checkbox(
11975                    &mut self.settings.discord_schedule,
11976                    "Import schedules from Discord events",
11977                )
11978                .on_hover_text(
11979                    "Sweeps your Discord servers a few hours apart (and on a manual reload), \
11980                     matching scheduled events to your monitors by stream URL. Discord events are \
11981                     only used for channels without a published Twitch/YouTube schedule.",
11982                );
11983            });
11984
11985            ui.add_space(12.0);
11986            ui.heading("Schedule sources");
11987            ui.label(
11988                "Schedules are fetched from several sources, tried in priority order per channel \
11989                 until one resolves. Some sources read the week off an image (a Twitch offline \
11990                 banner, a YouTube community post, a pinned tweet) via OCR — done by shelling out \
11991                 to an LLM CLI (the `claude` CLI by default; no API key needed).",
11992            );
11993            if ui
11994                .button("Configure source order…")
11995                .on_hover_text(
11996                    "Choose which sources to use and their priority. The first source that \
11997                     resolves an actual schedule for a channel wins.",
11998                )
11999                .clicked()
12000            {
12001                self.open_schedule_sources();
12002            }
12003
12004            ui.add_space(6.0);
12005            ui.checkbox(
12006                &mut self.settings.schedule_title_fill,
12007                "Go to next schedule source when no title found",
12008            )
12009            .on_hover_text(
12010                "After a source resolves a schedule, if any of its events have a time but no \
12011                 title, keep querying the lower-priority sources to borrow titles (matched to \
12012                 the nearest event within ±2h). Useful when a Twitch schedule publishes times \
12013                 but no titles and a banner / community-post OCR source has them. Override per \
12014                 channel or per instance in Properties.",
12015            );
12016            ui.horizontal(|ui| {
12017                ui.label("YouTube community post backlog");
12018                ui.add(
12019                    egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.youtube_community_max_posts)
12020                        .hint_text("5")
12021                        .desired_width(60.0),
12022                )
12023                .on_hover_text(
12024                    "How many recent YouTube community posts to scan for a schedule image \
12025                     (some channels post the week several posts back). Empty = 5. Clamped to \
12026                     1–20. Override per channel in Properties.",
12027                );
12028            });
12029
12030            ui.add_space(6.0);
12031            ui.label("Image OCR (for banner / community / tweet sources)");
12032            egui::Grid::new("ocr_grid")
12033                .num_columns(2)
12034                .spacing([12.0, 8.0])
12035                .show(ui, |ui| {
12036                    ui.label("OCR CLI command");
12037                    ui.add(
12038                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.ocr_command)
12039                            .hint_text("claude")
12040                            .desired_width(220.0),
12041                    )
12042                    .on_hover_text(
12043                        "Executable to shell out to for image OCR. Must be on PATH (or an absolute \
12044                         path) and accept `--model <m> --add-dir <dir> -p <prompt>`. Default: claude.",
12045                    );
12046                    ui.end_row();
12047                    ui.label("Model");
12048                    ui.add(
12049                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.ocr_model)
12050                            .hint_text("haiku")
12051                            .desired_width(220.0),
12052                    )
12053                    .on_hover_text("Primary model passed to the CLI. Default: haiku.");
12054                    ui.end_row();
12055                    ui.label("Fallback model");
12056                    ui.add(
12057                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.ocr_fallback_model)
12058                            .hint_text("sonnet")
12059                            .desired_width(220.0),
12060                    )
12061                    .on_hover_text(
12062                        "Stronger model retried once if the primary returns invalid JSON. \
12063                         Default: sonnet.",
12064                    );
12065                    ui.end_row();
12066                    ui.label("Primary timezone");
12067                    ui.add(
12068                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.ocr_timezone)
12069                            .hint_text("(machine local)")
12070                            .desired_width(220.0),
12071                    )
12072                    .on_hover_text(
12073                        "The primary IANA timezone a schedule's day/date headers are written in \
12074                         (e.g. America/Los_Angeles). When an image lists several timezones for one \
12075                         stream, this anchors the date and is preferred for the conversion. Leave \
12076                         empty to use the machine's local timezone. Override per channel in \
12077                         Properties.",
12078                    );
12079                    ui.end_row();
12080                    ui.label("UTC offset");
12081                    ui.add(
12082                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.ocr_offset)
12083                            .hint_text("(machine local)")
12084                            .desired_width(220.0),
12085                    )
12086                    .on_hover_text(
12087                        "UTC offset matching the timezone/season, e.g. +02:00. Leave empty to use \
12088                         the machine's current local offset.",
12089                    );
12090                    ui.end_row();
12091                    ui.label("Max budget (USD/call)");
12092                    ui.add(
12093                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.ocr_max_budget)
12094                            .hint_text("(no limit)")
12095                            .desired_width(120.0),
12096                    )
12097                    .on_hover_text(
12098                        "Hard cost cap per claude CLI call via --max-budget-usd (e.g. 0.05). \
12099                         The call is aborted and counted as a failure if the budget is hit. \
12100                         Leave empty for no cap.",
12101                    );
12102                    ui.end_row();
12103                    ui.label("Timeout (seconds)");
12104                    ui.add(
12105                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.ocr_timeout_secs)
12106                            .hint_text("150")
12107                            .desired_width(80.0),
12108                    )
12109                    .on_hover_text(
12110                        "Maximum seconds to wait for one claude CLI call before killing it and \
12111                         counting it as a failure. Default: 150 s.",
12112                    );
12113                    ui.end_row();
12114                    ui.label("Effort level");
12115                    egui::ComboBox::from_id_salt("ocr_effort_combo")
12116                        .selected_text(if self.settings.ocr_effort.is_empty() {
12117                            "default"
12118                        } else {
12119                            &self.settings.ocr_effort
12120                        })
12121                        .width(120.0)
12122                        .show_ui(ui, |ui| {
12123                            for level in &["", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh", "max"] {
12124                                let label = if level.is_empty() { "default" } else { level };
12125                                ui.selectable_value(
12126                                    &mut self.settings.ocr_effort,
12127                                    level.to_string(),
12128                                    label,
12129                                );
12130                            }
12131                        })
12132                        .response
12133                        .on_hover_text(
12134                            "Effort level passed as --effort to the claude CLI. Lower effort = \
12135                             fewer tokens and lower cost, but may miss details. 'default' omits \
12136                             the flag entirely (claude chooses). 'low' is recommended for simple \
12137                             banner OCR.",
12138                        );
12139                    ui.end_row();
12140                });
12141
12142            ui.add_space(12.0);
12143            ui.heading("Twitch account (OAuth)");
12144            ui.label("Connect to use a user token for detection (Client Secret then optional).");
12145            let flow = self.twitch_flow.lock().unwrap().clone();
12146            match flow {
12147                AuthFlow::Connected { login } => {
12148                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
12149                        if login.is_empty() {
12150                            ui.label("✅ Connected");
12151                        } else {
12152                            ui.label(format!("✅ Connected as {login}"));
12153                        }
12154                        if ui.button("Disconnect").clicked() {
12155                            let _ = oauth::disconnect(&self.core.store);
12156                            *self.twitch_flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Idle;
12157                            // disconnect() clears the cached ad-free (sub) results;
12158                            // reload so the Streams column drops the stale badges now.
12159                            self.reload_rows();
12160                        }
12161                    });
12162                    ui.small(
12163                        "Tip: if you connected before the Ad-free / Import features, reconnect to \
12164                         grant the subscriptions + follows scopes.",
12165                    );
12166                    if ui
12167                        .button("📥 Import followed channels")
12168                        .on_hover_text(
12169                            "Add the channels this Twitch account follows as new streams \
12170                             (Auto off by default). Needs the 'follows' scope — reconnect if it \
12171                             was granted before this feature.",
12172                        )
12173                        .clicked()
12174                    {
12175                        self.open_import(Platform::Twitch, ui.ctx().clone());
12176                    }
12177                }
12178                AuthFlow::Pending { user_code, url } => {
12179                    ui.label("Authorize in your browser, then wait:");
12180                    if url.is_empty() {
12181                        ui.label("Requesting code…");
12182                    } else {
12183                        ui.hyperlink(&url);
12184                        ui.label(format!("Enter code: {user_code}"));
12185                    }
12186                }
12187                AuthFlow::Failed { message } => {
12188                    ui.colored_label(egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xE0, 0x6C, 0x6C), &message);
12189                    if ui.button("🔗 Connect Twitch").clicked() {
12190                        self.start_twitch_connect(ui.ctx().clone());
12191                    }
12192                }
12193                AuthFlow::Idle => {
12194                    if ui.button("🔗 Connect Twitch").clicked() {
12195                        self.start_twitch_connect(ui.ctx().clone());
12196                    }
12197                }
12198            }
12199
12200            ui.add_space(12.0);
12201            ui.heading("YouTube account (Google OAuth)");
12202            ui.label(
12203                "Connect a Google account to import your YouTube subscriptions. Needs a Google \
12204                 Cloud OAuth client of type \"TV and Limited Input devices\" with the YouTube \
12205                 Data API enabled.",
12206            );
12207            egui::Grid::new("google_creds_grid")
12208                .num_columns(2)
12209                .spacing([12.0, 8.0])
12210                .show(ui, |ui| {
12211                    ui.label("Google Client ID");
12212                    ui.add(
12213                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.google_client_id)
12214                            .desired_width(320.0)
12215                            .hint_text("xxxxx.apps.googleusercontent.com"),
12216                    );
12217                    ui.end_row();
12218                    ui.label("Google Client Secret");
12219                    ui.add(
12220                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.google_client_secret)
12221                            .password(true),
12222                    );
12223                    ui.end_row();
12224                });
12225            let gflow = self.google_flow.lock().unwrap().clone();
12226            match gflow {
12227                AuthFlow::Connected { login } => {
12228                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
12229                        if login.is_empty() {
12230                            ui.label("✅ Connected");
12231                        } else {
12232                            ui.label(format!("✅ Connected as {login}"));
12233                        }
12234                        if ui.button("Disconnect").clicked() {
12235                            let _ = google_oauth::disconnect(&self.core.store);
12236                            *self.google_flow.lock().unwrap() = AuthFlow::Idle;
12237                        }
12238                    });
12239                    if ui
12240                        .button("📥 Import subscriptions")
12241                        .on_hover_text(
12242                            "Add the channels this YouTube account subscribes to as new streams \
12243                             (Auto off by default).",
12244                        )
12245                        .clicked()
12246                    {
12247                        self.open_import(Platform::YouTube, ui.ctx().clone());
12248                    }
12249                }
12250                AuthFlow::Pending { user_code, url } => {
12251                    ui.label("Authorize in your browser, then wait:");
12252                    if url.is_empty() {
12253                        ui.label("Requesting code…");
12254                    } else {
12255                        ui.hyperlink(&url);
12256                        ui.label(format!("Enter code: {user_code}"));
12257                    }
12258                }
12259                AuthFlow::Failed { message } => {
12260                    ui.colored_label(egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xE0, 0x6C, 0x6C), &message);
12261                    if ui.button("🔗 Connect YouTube").clicked() {
12262                        self.start_google_connect(ui.ctx().clone());
12263                    }
12264                }
12265                AuthFlow::Idle => {
12266                    if ui.button("🔗 Connect YouTube").clicked() {
12267                        self.start_google_connect(ui.ctx().clone());
12268                    }
12269                }
12270            }
12271
12272            ui.add_space(12.0);
12273            ui.heading("YouTube WebSub (push via VPS)");
12274            ui.label(
12275                "Optional. Point at a running yt-websub relay to get near-instant \
12276                 go-live triggers for YouTube channels set to the WebSub method.",
12277            );
12278            egui::Grid::new("websub_grid")
12279                .num_columns(2)
12280                .spacing([12.0, 8.0])
12281                .show(ui, |ui| {
12282                    ui.label("VPS base URL");
12283                    ui.add(
12284                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.websub_vps_url)
12285                            .desired_width(320.0)
12286                            .hint_text("https://hooks.example.com"),
12287                    )
12288                    .on_hover_text("The yt-websub server's HTTPS base URL (no trailing /api).");
12289                    ui.end_row();
12290                    ui.label("Bearer token");
12291                    ui.add(
12292                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.websub_token).password(true),
12293                    )
12294                    .on_hover_text("YTWEBSUB_BEARER_TOKEN configured on the VPS.");
12295                    ui.end_row();
12296                    ui.label("Poll interval (s)");
12297                    ui.add(egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.websub_poll_secs))
12298                        .on_hover_text("How often to pull new events from the VPS (min 5).");
12299                    ui.end_row();
12300                });
12301
12302            ui.add_space(12.0);
12303            ui.heading("Defaults");
12304            egui::Grid::new("defaults_grid")
12305                .num_columns(2)
12306                .spacing([12.0, 8.0])
12307                .show(ui, |ui| {
12308                    ui.label("Default output folder");
12309                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
12310                        ui.text_edit_singleline(&mut self.settings.default_output_dir);
12311                        if ui.button("Browse…").clicked() {
12312                            self.pending_browse = Some(spawn_browse_folder(
12313                                &self.settings.default_output_dir,
12314                                |app, p| app.settings.default_output_dir = p,
12315                            ));
12316                        }
12317                    });
12318                    ui.end_row();
12319                    ui.label("Media player path").on_hover_text(
12320                        "Path to the media player used by \"Stream in player\" on recording rows. \
12321                         Passed the file path as the only argument (e.g. mpv.exe, vlc.exe). \
12322                         With mpv, in-progress recordings open with live-view flags that follow \
12323                         the growing file, and in-progress SABR captures (separate audio/video \
12324                         files) are playable too — other players only open finished or \
12325                         single-file captures.",
12326                    );
12327                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
12328                        ui.add(
12329                            egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.media_player_path)
12330                                .hint_text(r"C:\Progs\mpv\mpv.exe")
12331                                .desired_width(360.0),
12332                        );
12333                        if ui.button("Browse…").clicked() {
12334                            self.pending_browse = Some(spawn_browse_file(
12335                                &self.settings.media_player_path,
12336                                |app, p| app.settings.media_player_path = p,
12337                            ));
12338                        }
12339                    });
12340                    ui.end_row();
12341                    ui.label("Max concurrent downloads");
12342                    ui.text_edit_singleline(&mut self.settings.max_concurrent_downloads);
12343                    ui.end_row();
12344                    ui.label("Filename media info")
12345                        .on_hover_text(
12346                            "How the {resolution}/{height}/{width}/{fps}/{vcodec} filename \
12347                             variables get their values. Only applies when the filename \
12348                             template uses one of them.",
12349                        );
12350                    let mode = &mut self.settings.filename_media_info;
12351                    egui::ComboBox::from_id_salt("media_info_cb")
12352                        .selected_text(mode.label())
12353                        .show_ui(ui, |ui| {
12354                            for m in MediaInfoMode::ALL {
12355                                ui.selectable_value(mode, m, m.label())
12356                                    .on_hover_text(m.tooltip());
12357                            }
12358                        });
12359                    ui.end_row();
12360
12361                    ui.label("Date format").on_hover_text(
12362                        "How dates and timestamps are shown throughout the app \
12363                         (the Polled / Went Live / Started On / Added columns, the \
12364                         history tree, etc.). Applies on Save.",
12365                    );
12366                    let df = &mut self.settings.date_fmt;
12367                    egui::ComboBox::from_id_salt("date_fmt_cb")
12368                        .selected_text(df.label())
12369                        .show_ui(ui, |ui| {
12370                            for f in DateFmt::ALL {
12371                                ui.selectable_value(df, f, f.label());
12372                            }
12373                        });
12374                    ui.end_row();
12375
12376                    ui.label("Short timestamp format").on_hover_text(
12377                        "chrono pattern used when \"Short timestamps\" is on (top bar). \
12378                         Default: %d/%m %H:%M  (day/month + 24h time). \
12379                         Applies on Save.",
12380                    );
12381                    ui.text_edit_singleline(&mut self.settings.short_ts_fmt);
12382                    ui.end_row();
12383                });
12384
12385            ui.add_space(12.0);
12386            ui.heading("Display");
12387            if ui
12388                .checkbox(&mut self.show_actions, "Show Actions column")
12389                .on_hover_text(
12390                    "Show the per-row Actions buttons column in the Streams and Videos \
12391                     tables. Turn it off to reclaim width — every action is also on each \
12392                     row's right-click context menu. Applies immediately.",
12393                )
12394                .changed()
12395            {
12396                let _ = self.core.store.set_setting(
12397                    K_SHOW_ACTIONS,
12398                    if self.show_actions { "1" } else { "0" },
12399                );
12400            }
12401            if ui
12402                .checkbox(&mut self.render_emotes, "Render emotes in chat")
12403                .on_hover_text(
12404                    "Show Twitch / BTTV / FFZ / 7TV emotes (and color emoji) as inline \
12405                     images in the chat replay. Off shows the emote code as plain text. \
12406                     Requires \"Fetch chat assets\" so the images are on disk. Applies \
12407                     immediately.",
12408                )
12409                .changed()
12410            {
12411                let _ = self.core.store.set_setting(
12412                    K_RENDER_EMOTES,
12413                    if self.render_emotes { "1" } else { "0" },
12414                );
12415            }
12416            if ui
12417                .add_enabled(
12418                    self.render_emotes,
12419                    egui::Checkbox::new(&mut self.animate_emotes, "Animate emotes"),
12420                )
12421                .on_hover_text(
12422                    "Play animated GIF / WebP emotes (Twitch, BTTV/FFZ, 7TV) and animated \
12423                     emoji. Off shows a static first frame — turn it off if a busy channel's \
12424                     animations use too much memory or CPU. Applies immediately.",
12425                )
12426                .changed()
12427            {
12428                let _ = self.core.store.set_setting(
12429                    K_ANIMATE_EMOTES,
12430                    if self.animate_emotes { "1" } else { "0" },
12431                );
12432            }
12433            ui.label(
12434                egui::RichText::new(
12435                    "Color emoji use Twemoji (© Twitter/jdecked, CC-BY 4.0), downloaded on \
12436                     demand and cached for offline replay.",
12437                )
12438                .small()
12439                .weak(),
12440            );
12441
12442            ui.add_space(12.0);
12443            ui.heading("Download authentication");
12444            ui.label("Default for capturing sub-only / members-only / ad-reduced streams. Per-channel settings override this.");
12445            egui::Grid::new("auth_grid")
12446                .num_columns(2)
12447                .spacing([12.0, 8.0])
12448                .show(ui, |ui| {
12449                    ui.label("Method");
12450                    let mut cookies = self.settings.download_auth_method == "cookies";
12451                    egui::ComboBox::from_id_salt("dl_auth_cb")
12452                        .selected_text(if cookies { "Browser cookies" } else { "None" })
12453                        .show_ui(ui, |ui| {
12454                            ui.selectable_value(&mut cookies, false, "None");
12455                            ui.selectable_value(&mut cookies, true, "Browser cookies");
12456                        });
12457                    self.settings.download_auth_method =
12458                        if cookies { "cookies".into() } else { "none".into() };
12459                    ui.end_row();
12460
12461                    if cookies {
12462                        ui.label("Browser");
12463                        egui::ComboBox::from_id_salt("cookies_browser_cb")
12464                            .selected_text(if self.settings.cookies_browser.is_empty() {
12465                                "(choose)"
12466                            } else {
12467                                &self.settings.cookies_browser
12468                            })
12469                            .show_ui(ui, |ui| {
12470                                for b in COOKIE_BROWSERS {
12471                                    ui.selectable_value(
12472                                        &mut self.settings.cookies_browser,
12473                                        b.to_string(),
12474                                        b,
12475                                    );
12476                                }
12477                            });
12478                        ui.end_row();
12479
12480                        ui.label("Profile / session");
12481                        ui.add(
12482                            egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.cookies_profile)
12483                                .hint_text("optional — e.g. dmrf6eed.YouTube"),
12484                        )
12485                        .on_hover_text(
12486                            "Which browser profile/session to read cookies from. Blank = the \
12487                             browser's default (most-recently-used) profile — which is why a \
12488                             dedicated login can be missed. For Firefox, use the profile folder \
12489                             name (the directory under …/Mozilla/Firefox/Profiles, e.g. \
12490                             dmrf6eed.YouTube) or an absolute path to it; find it at about:profiles.",
12491                        );
12492                        ui.end_row();
12493                    }
12494                });
12495
12496            ui.add_space(12.0);
12497            ui.heading("yt-dlp default arguments");
12498            ui.label("Prepended to every yt-dlp invocation. Per-channel extra args are appended after and override these.");
12499            egui::Grid::new("ytdlp_args_grid")
12500                .num_columns(2)
12501                .spacing([12.0, 8.0])
12502                .show(ui, |ui| {
12503                    ui.label("Extra args");
12504                    ui.add(
12505                        egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.ytdlp_default_args)
12506                            .hint_text("e.g. --js-runtimes node --cookies-from-browser firefox:dmrf6eed.YouTube")
12507                            .desired_width(f32::INFINITY),
12508                    )
12509                    .on_hover_text(
12510                        "Shell-style space-separated arguments. Quoted strings are supported \
12511                         (e.g. \"value with spaces\"). Applied to all yt-dlp monitors; \
12512                         useful for --js-runtimes node, --cookies-from-browser, \
12513                         --concurrent-fragments, --throttled-rate, etc.",
12514                    );
12515                    ui.end_row();
12516                });
12517
12518            ui.add_space(12.0);
12519            ui.heading("YouTube SABR (live-from-start)");
12520            ui.label(
12521                "YouTube live capture-from-start needs the SABR protocol, which only the \
12522                 yt-dlp dev build provides. Point to that binary below; it is used ONLY for \
12523                 YouTube monitors with Capture-from-start. Chat, assets, VODs, and every other \
12524                 capture keep using the system yt-dlp.",
12525            );
12526            egui::Grid::new("sabr_grid")
12527                .num_columns(2)
12528                .spacing([12.0, 8.0])
12529                .show(ui, |ui| {
12530                    ui.label("System yt-dlp path");
12531                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
12532                        ui.add(
12533                            egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.settings.ytdlp_binary_path)
12534                                .hint_text("(empty = yt-dlp on PATH)")
12535                                .desired_width(360.0),
12536                        );
12537                        if ui.button("Browse…").clicked() {
