15425            self.properties_popup = None;
15426            self.instance_scope_draft = None;
15427        }
15428    }
15429
15430    /// Drive the off-UI-thread load of the channel Properties window's per-open data,
15431    /// drawing a "Loading…" placeholder until it lands. Returns `true` once the bundle has
15432    /// been installed into the per-channel caches (the caller then falls through and draws
15433    /// the real window *this* frame, no flicker); `false` while still loading (the caller
15434    /// returns — the placeholder is on screen). All the blocking work (disk reads, image
15435    /// decode/upload, asset-dir enumeration, store-mutex DB reads) happens on the spawned
15436    /// thread, so the UI thread can't freeze on a slow disk / AV scan / contended store.
15437    #[must_use]
15438    #[allow(deprecated)] // CentralPanel::show in an immediate viewport (matches the real window)
15439    fn drive_props_load(
15440        &mut self,
15441        cid: i64,
15442        ch: &Channel,
15443        platforms: &[Platform],
15444        asset_platforms: &[Platform],
15445        ctx: &egui::Context,
15446    ) -> bool {
15447        // Ensure a load for THIS channel is in flight (replace a stale one left over from
15448        // a different channel if the user switched the open window).
15449        let need_spawn = !matches!(&self.props_load, Some(pl) if pl.channel_id == cid);
15450        if need_spawn {
15451            let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel();
15452            let ch_bg = ch.clone();
15453            let platforms_bg = platforms.to_vec();
15454            let asset_platforms_bg = asset_platforms.to_vec();
15455            let ctx_bg = ctx.clone();
15456            let store = self.core.store.clone();
15457            debug!(channel_id = cid, "spawning props-load thread");
15458            let spawned = std::thread::Builder::new()
15459                .name("props-load".into())
15460                .spawn(move || {
15461                    let t = std::time::Instant::now();
15462                    // None of this touches `self`. egui's `load_texture` (inside
15463                    // `resolve_channel_icon` / `load_channel_asset_thumbs`) is thread-safe:
15464                    // it queues a `TexturesDelta` the paint thread uploads later.
15465                    let id = ch_bg.id;
15466                    let loaded = PropsLoaded {
15467                        channel_id: id,
15468                        icon: resolve_channel_icon(&ch_bg, &platforms_bg, &ctx_bg),
15469                        thumbs: load_channel_asset_thumbs(&ch_bg, &platforms_bg, &ctx_bg),
15470                        emote_counts: emote_provider_counts(&ch_bg.name),
15471                        asset_status: build_platform_asset_status(&ch_bg.name, &asset_platforms_bg),
15472                        cfg: load_channel_cfg(&store, id),
15473                        source_order: load_source_order(&store),
15474                        scope: load_channel_scope(&store, id),
15475                    };
15476                    debug!(elapsed_ms = t.elapsed().as_millis(), channel_id = id, "props-load done");
15477                    // Receiver gone (window closed) → the send is simply dropped.
15478                    let _ = tx.send(loaded);
15479                    // Wake the UI thread to install the bundle even if otherwise idle.
15480                    ctx_bg.request_repaint();
15481                });
15482            match spawned {
15483                Ok(_) => self.props_load = Some(PropsLoad { channel_id: cid, rx }),
15484                Err(e) => {
15485                    // Spawn failed (extremely unlikely). Fall back to a synchronous load so
15486                    // the window still opens rather than spinning forever.
15487                    warn!("props-load thread spawn failed ({e}); loading on the UI thread");
15488                    let id = ch.id;
15489                    let loaded = PropsLoaded {
15490                        channel_id: id,
15491                        icon: resolve_channel_icon(ch, platforms, ctx),
15492                        thumbs: load_channel_asset_thumbs(ch, platforms, ctx),
15493                        emote_counts: emote_provider_counts(&ch.name),
15494                        asset_status: build_platform_asset_status(&ch.name, asset_platforms),
15495                        cfg: load_channel_cfg(&self.core.store, id),
15496                        source_order: load_source_order(&self.core.store),
15497                        scope: load_channel_scope(&self.core.store, id),
15498                    };
15499                    self.install_props_loaded(loaded);
15500                    return true;
15501                }
15502            }
15503        }
15504
15505        // Poll the in-flight load without blocking. Extract the owned `try_recv` result
15506        // first so the `&self.props_load` borrow ends before we mutate `self`.
15507        let polled = self
15508            .props_load
15509            .as_ref()
15510            .filter(|pl| pl.channel_id == cid)
15511            .map(|pl| pl.rx.try_recv());
15512        match polled {
15513            Some(Ok(loaded)) => {
15514                self.install_props_loaded(loaded);
15515                self.props_load = None;
15516                return true; // caller draws the real window this frame
15517            }
15518            Some(Err(std::sync::mpsc::TryRecvError::Disconnected)) => {
15519                // Loader vanished without sending (it never panics in practice — every
15520                // step degrades to a default). Drop the handle so the next frame respawns;
15521                // the placeholder stays up meanwhile.
15522                self.props_load = None;
15523            }
15524            // `Some(Err(Empty))` (still loading) or `None` (no load for this channel) →
15525            // fall through to the placeholder.
15526            _ => {}
15527        }
15528
15529        // Still loading: draw the placeholder. Same viewport id as the real window so it
15530        // morphs in place when the bundle lands.
15531        self.heartbeat.set_context(format!("Properties: {}", ch.name));
15532        self.heartbeat.set_activity(crate::watchdog::Activity::Properties);
15533        let mut open = true;
15534        ctx.show_viewport_immediate(
15535            egui::ViewportId::from_hash_of("channel_props_vp"),
15536            egui::ViewportBuilder::default()
15537                .with_title(format!("Properties — {}", ch.name))
15538                .with_inner_size([480.0, 600.0]),
15539            |ctx, _class| {
15540                if ctx.input(|i| i.viewport().close_requested()) {
15541                    open = false;
15542                }
15543                egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
15544                    ui.vertical_centered(|ui| {
15545                        ui.add_space(220.0);
15546                        ui.spinner();
15547                        ui.add_space(8.0);
15548                        ui.label(format!("Loading {} assets…", ch.name));
15549                    });
15550                });
15551            },
15552        );
15553        if !open {
15554            // Closed mid-load: forget the popup and the in-flight load (a bundle that
15555            // still arrives is dropped because the receiver is gone).
15556            self.channel_properties_popup = None;
15557            self.props_load = None;
15558        }
15559        // Keep frames coming so we poll the loader (the spinner also animates).
15560        ctx.request_repaint();
15561        false
15562    }
15563
15564    /// Install a finished [`PropsLoaded`] bundle into the per-channel Properties caches —
15565    /// but only if the window is still showing that channel (the user may have closed it or
15566    /// switched away while the background load ran). In-progress config/scope edits are
15567    /// preserved: the drafts are only seeded when they don't already belong to this channel.
15568    fn install_props_loaded(&mut self, loaded: PropsLoaded) {
15569        if self.channel_properties_popup != Some(loaded.channel_id) {
15570            return;
15571        }
15572        let id = loaded.channel_id;
15573        self.channel_icons.insert(id, loaded.icon);
15574        self.channel_asset_thumbs.insert(id, loaded.thumbs);
15575        self.channel_emote_counts.insert(id, loaded.emote_counts);
15576        self.channel_asset_status.insert(id, loaded.asset_status);
15577        self.props_source_order = loaded.source_order;
15578        if self.channel_cfg_draft.as_ref().map(|(i, _)| *i) != Some(id) {
15579            self.channel_cfg_draft = Some((id, loaded.cfg));
15580        }
15581        if self.channel_scope_draft.as_ref().map(|(i, _)| *i) != Some(id) {
15582            self.channel_scope_draft = Some((id, loaded.scope));
15583        }
15584    }
15585
15586    /// Channel properties window — header + channel info, assets, and schedule-source config.
15587    #[allow(deprecated)]
15588    fn channel_properties_window(&mut self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
15589        let Some(cid) = self.channel_properties_popup else { return };
15590        let ch = match self.channels.iter().find(|c| c.id == cid).cloned() {
15591            Some(c) => c,
15592            None => {
15593                self.channel_properties_popup = None;
15594                return;
15595            }
15596        };
15597        // Watchdog: the synchronous first-open work below (icon/thumbnail image decode +
15598        // GPU upload and the per-platform asset enumeration) is the part that can block
15599        // the UI thread for seconds. Stamp it distinctly from the window paint so a freeze
15600        // dialog says whether we stalled *loading assets* or *inside the window*. Reset to
15601        // `Properties` right before the sub-window is created (below).
15602        self.heartbeat.set_context(format!("Properties: {}", ch.name));
15603        self.heartbeat.set_activity(crate::watchdog::Activity::PropertiesLoad);
15604        // First monitor of this channel — used for asset refetch.
15605        let first_mon = self.rows.iter().find(|r| r.channel.id == cid).map(|r| r.monitor.clone());
15606
15607        let platforms: Vec<Platform> = {
15608            let mons: Vec<&MonitorWithChannel> =
15609                self.rows.iter().filter(|r| r.channel.id == cid).collect();
15610            channel_platforms(&mons)
15611        };
15612        let asset_platforms: Vec<Platform> =
15613            platforms.iter().copied().filter(|p| *p != Platform::Generic).collect();
15614
15615        // First-open — and every reopen, since these per-open caches are dropped on
15616        // close — asset load runs OFF the UI thread: icon/thumbnail image decode + GPU
15617        // upload, the per-platform asset enumeration, and the schedule-source
15618        // config/scope/order DB reads. Those reads take the single store mutex, which a
15619        // background task (e.g. the asset-refresh loop's heavy `list_monitors_with_channels`
15620        // query) can hold long enough to freeze the GUI here. Until the bundle lands we
15621        // show a "Loading…" placeholder and stay responsive. Gated on `channel_asset_status`
15622        // because it is the cache dropped on every close (and on rename/refetch), so each
15623        // open re-loads through this off-thread path rather than blocking the UI thread.
15624        if !self.channel_asset_status.contains_key(&ch.id)
15625            && !self.drive_props_load(cid, &ch, &platforms, &asset_platforms, ctx)
15626        {
15627            return; // still loading — placeholder is on screen
15628        }
15629
15630        let icon_tex = self
15631            .channel_icons
15632            .entry(ch.id)
15633            .or_insert_with(|| resolve_channel_icon(&ch, &platforms, ctx))
15634            .clone();
15635
15636        // Mainpage asset thumbnails (icon + banner per platform). Loaded full-res
15637        // once per open; the clone is cheap (Arc-backed texture handles).
15638        let thumbs = self
15639            .channel_asset_thumbs
15640            .entry(ch.id)
15641            .or_insert_with(|| load_channel_asset_thumbs(&ch, &platforms, ctx))
15642            .clone();
15643        // Viewable emote counts per provider — enumerated once per open (one stat per
15644        // emote) and cached, since this runs every frame the window is up.
15645        let emote_counts = *self
15646            .channel_emote_counts
15647            .entry(ch.id)
15648            .or_insert_with(|| emote_provider_counts(&ch.name));
15649        // Per-platform asset-status rows — built once per open from blocking filesystem
15650        // I/O (read_dir + per-file metadata + full JSON manifest parse) and cached, so the
15651        // status grid (rebuilt every frame the window is open) reads from memory instead of
15652        // re-running dozens of syscalls per repaint (which can stall the UI thread on slow
15653        // or AV-scanned storage). The clone is cheap (a handful of small rows).
15654        let asset_status = self
15655            .channel_asset_status
15656            .entry(ch.id)
15657            .or_insert_with(|| build_platform_asset_status(&ch.name, &asset_platforms))
15658            .clone();
15659
15660        let mut pref_change: Option<Option<Platform>> = None;
15661        let mut open_emote_viewer: Option<EmoteProvider> = None;
15662        let mut open_asset_history = false;
15663
15664        if self.channel_cfg_draft.as_ref().map(|(id, _)| *id) != Some(ch.id) {
15665            self.channel_cfg_draft = Some((ch.id, load_channel_cfg(&self.core.store, ch.id)));
15666            // Snapshot the global source order once per open (read once here, not via a
15667            // settings DB read every frame inside `scope_override_editor`).
15668            self.props_source_order = load_source_order(&self.core.store);
15669        }
15670        if self.channel_scope_draft.as_ref().map(|(id, _)| *id) != Some(ch.id) {
15671            self.channel_scope_draft =
15672                Some((ch.id, load_channel_scope(&self.core.store, ch.id)));
15673        }
15674        let global_order = self.props_source_order.clone();
15675        let mut cfg_dirty = false;
15676        let mut scope_dirty = false;
15677
15678        // The first-open asset loads are done; everything from here is the sub-window
15679        // build + paint. Stamp that distinctly so a freeze here is attributed to the
15680        // window itself rather than to asset loading.
15681        self.heartbeat.set_activity(crate::watchdog::Activity::Properties);
15682        // context already set during PropertiesLoad above; refresh in case we skipped load
15683
15684        let mut open = true;
15685        ctx.show_viewport_immediate(
15686            egui::ViewportId::from_hash_of("channel_props_vp"),
15687            egui::ViewportBuilder::default()
15688                .with_title(format!("Properties — {}", ch.name))
15689                .with_inner_size([480.0, 600.0]),
15690            |ctx, _class| {
15691                if ctx.input(|i| i.viewport().close_requested()) {
15692                    open = false;
15693                }
15694                egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
15695                // ── Header ──────────────────────────────────────────────
15696                ui.horizontal(|ui| {
15697                    if let Some(tex) = &icon_tex {
15698                        let resp = ui.add(
15699                            egui::Image::from_texture(tex)
15700                                .max_size(egui::vec2(96.0, 96.0))
15701                                .corner_radius(egui::CornerRadius::same(8)),
15702                        );
15703                        queue_alt_image_preview(ui.ctx(), &resp, tex);
15704                    } else {
15705                        let (rect, _) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
15706                            egui::vec2(96.0, 96.0),
15707                            egui::Sense::hover(),
15708                        );
15709                        ui.painter().rect_filled(
15710                            rect,
15711                            8.0,
15712                            ui.visuals().weak_text_color(),
15713                        );
15714                    }
15715                    ui.add_space(8.0);
15716                    ui.vertical(|ui| {
15717                        ui.add_space(4.0);
15718                        ui.heading(&ch.name);
15719                        ui.horizontal(|ui| {
15720                            let ptex = self
15721                                .platform_tex
15722                                .get_or_insert_with(|| PlatformTextures::load(ui.ctx()));
15723                            for &p in &platforms {
15724                                if let Some(t) = ptex.get(p) {
15725                                    ui.add(
15726                                        egui::Image::from_texture(t)
15727                                            .max_size(egui::vec2(14.0, 14.0)),
15728                                    );
15729                                }
15730                            }
15731                            let names: Vec<&str> =
15732                                platforms.iter().map(|p| p.label()).collect();
15733                            ui.label(if names.is_empty() {
15734                                "—".to_string()
15735                            } else {
15736                                names.join(" · ")
15737                            });
15738                        });
15739                    });
15740                });
15741
15742                ui.separator();
15743
15744                // ── Mainpage asset thumbnails ────────────────────────────
15745                // A visual overview of every original icon/banner this channel
15746                // has across its platforms. Hover for size, Alt to preview at
15747                // full resolution, click to open the file.
15748                if !thumbs.is_empty() {
15749                    ui.add_space(2.0);
15750                    ui.strong("Assets");
15751                    ui.add_space(3.0);
15752                    const THUMB_H: f32 = 56.0;
15753                    ui.horizontal_wrapped(|ui| {
15754                        for t in &thumbs {
15755                            let (w, h) = t.dims;
15756                            let aspect = if h > 0 { w as f32 / h as f32 } else { 1.0 };
15757                            // Clamp very wide banners so one asset can't dominate.
15758                            let width = (THUMB_H * aspect).min(THUMB_H * 4.0);
15759                            let resp = ui
15760                                .add(
15761                                    egui::Image::from_texture(&t.tex)
15762                                        .fit_to_exact_size(egui::vec2(width, THUMB_H))
15763                                        .corner_radius(egui::CornerRadius::same(4))
15764                                        .sense(egui::Sense::click()),
15765                                )
15766                                .on_hover_text(format!(
15767                                    "{} — {w}×{h} px\nAlt: preview full size · click: open file",
15768                                    t.label,
15769                                ));
15770                            queue_alt_image_preview(ui.ctx(), &resp, &t.tex);
15771                            if resp.clicked() {
15772                                crate::platform::open_path(&t.path);
15773                            }
15774                        }
15775                    });
15776                    ui.separator();
15777                }
15778
15779                // ── Channel ─────────────────────────────────────────────
15780                ui.strong("Channel");
15781                egui::Grid::new("props_ch")
15782                    .num_columns(2)
15783                    .spacing([12.0, 4.0])
15784                    .show(ui, |ui| {
15785                        ui.label("DB channel ID");
15786                        ui.label(ch.id.to_string());
15787                        ui.end_row();
15788
15789                        ui.label("URL");
15790                        if ui.link(&ch.url).clicked() {
15791                            ui.ctx().open_url(egui::OpenUrl::new_tab(ch.url.clone()));
15792                        }
15793                        ui.end_row();
15794
15795                        if ch.platform == Platform::YouTube {
15796                            let yt_id = extract_yt_channel_id(&ch.url)
15797                                .unwrap_or_else(|| "— (handle URL, ID not in URL)".into());
15798                            ui.label("Channel ID");
15799                            ui.horizontal(|ui| {
15800                                ui.label(&yt_id);
15801                                if !yt_id.starts_with('—')
15802                                    && ui.small_button("⧉").on_hover_text("Copy").clicked()
15803                                {
15804                                    ui.ctx().copy_text(yt_id.clone());
15805                                }
15806                            });
15807                            ui.end_row();
15808                        }
15809                    });
15810
15811                // ── Assets ───────────────────────────────────────────────
15812                {
15813                    ui.add_space(6.0);
15814                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
15815                        ui.strong("Channel assets");
15816                        if let Some(m) = &first_mon {
15817                            if ui
15818                                .button("⟳ Refetch")
15819                                .on_hover_text(format!(
15820                                    "Fetch icon / banner / badges / emotes for this channel's \
15821                                     platform ({}) now — ignores the 24h cache.",
15822                                    m.platform().label(),
15823                                ))
15824                                .clicked()
15825                            {
15826                                self.core.manual(ManualCommand::RefetchAssets(m.id));
15827                                self.channel_icons.remove(&ch.id);
15828                                // channel_icons_small is NOT cleared here: this callback
15829                                // runs inside a child viewport whose paint_and_update_textures
15830                                // would free the texture from the shared painter before the
15831                                // main viewport paints the streams table, causing "Failed to
15832                                // find texture" warnings. The small icon reloads on the next
15833                                // AssetFetch completion (logic() clears it before any rendering).
15834                                self.channel_twitch_colors.remove(&ch.id);
15835                                self.channel_asset_thumbs.remove(&ch.id);
15836                                self.channel_emote_counts.remove(&ch.id);
15837                                self.channel_asset_status.remove(&ch.id);
15838                                self.status = format!("Refetching assets for {}…", ch.name);
15839                            }
15840                        }
15841                        if ui
15842                            .button("📂")
15843                            .on_hover_text(
15844                                "Open the channel's asset folder (per-platform icons, banners, \
15845                                 and the history/ archive of older versions).",
15846                            )
15847                            .clicked()
15848                        {
15849                            let root = crate::app_paths::asset_cache_dir()
15850                                .join("channel_assets")
15851                                .join(crate::downloader::sanitize_filename(&ch.name));
15852                            crate::platform::open_path(&root);
15853                        }
15854                        if ui
15855                            .button("🕑 History")
15856                            .on_hover_text(
15857                                "Show this channel's recorded asset changes over time \
15858                                 (added / removed emotes, icon / banner / name-colour \
15859                                 replacements) across all its platforms.",
15860                            )
15861                            .clicked()
15862                        {
15863                            open_asset_history = true;
15864                        }
15865                    });
15866
15867                    // Icon source picker.
15868                    ui.add_space(4.0);
15869                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
15870                        ui.label("Icon source:");
15871                        let cur = ch.preferred_platform;
15872                        egui::ComboBox::from_id_salt("pref_plat_cb")
15873                            .selected_text(match cur {
15874                                Some(p) => p.label(),
15875                                None => "Auto (first available)",
15876                            })
15877                            .show_ui(ui, |ui| {
15878                                if ui
15879                                    .selectable_label(cur.is_none(), "Auto (first available)")
15880                                    .clicked()
15881                                {
15882                                    pref_change = Some(None);
15883                                }
15884                                for &p in &asset_platforms {
15885                                    if ui
15886                                        .selectable_label(cur == Some(p), p.label())
15887                                        .clicked()
15888                                    {
15889                                        pref_change = Some(Some(p));
15890                                    }
15891                                }
15892                            })
15893                            .response
15894                            .on_hover_text(
15895                                "Which platform's profile pic represents this channel. \
15896                                 Auto uses the first platform that has a fetched icon.",
15897                            );
15898                    });
15899
15900                    // Per-platform asset status.
15901                    ui.add_space(4.0);
15902                    egui::Grid::new("props_assets")
15903                        .num_columns(6)
15904                        .spacing([12.0, 4.0])
15905                        .striped(true)
15906                        .show(ui, |ui| {
15907                            ui.strong("Platform");
15908                            ui.strong("Icon");
15909                            ui.strong("Banner");
15910                            ui.strong("Badges");
15911                            ui.strong("Emotes");
15912                            ui.strong("Updated");
15913                            ui.end_row();
15914                            // Reads from the per-open `asset_status` cache — NO filesystem
15915                            // I/O here (it would otherwise be dozens of syscalls per frame;
15916                            // see `PlatformAssetStatus`).
15917                            for st in &asset_status {
15918                                ui.horizontal(|ui| {
15919                                    let ptex = self.platform_tex.get_or_insert_with(|| {
15920                                        PlatformTextures::load(ui.ctx())
15921                                    });
15922                                    if let Some(t) = ptex.get(st.platform) {
15923                                        ui.add(
15924                                            egui::Image::from_texture(t)
15925                                                .max_size(egui::vec2(13.0, 13.0)),
15926                                        );
15927                                    }
15928                                    ui.label(st.platform.label());
15929                                });
15930                                asset_status_cell(ui, st.icon_present, st.icon_variants);
15931                                asset_status_cell(ui, st.banner_present, st.banner_variants);
15932                                ui.label(if st.badges > 0 {
15933                                    st.badges.to_string()
15934                                } else {
15935                                    "—".into()
15936                                });
15937                                ui.label(if st.emotes > 0 {
15938                                    st.emotes.to_string()
15939                                } else {
15940                                    "—".into()
15941                                });
15942                                ui.label(&st.stamp);
15943                                ui.end_row();
15944                            }
15945                        });
15946
15947                    // Emote viewers: one launcher per provider that has emotes.
15948                    // Twitch (first-party) uses a generic emote glyph; the third
15949                    // parties use their brand logo (7TV/BTTV) or a text badge (FFZ).
15950                    if emote_counts.iter().any(|(_, n)| *n > 0) {
15951                        ui.add_space(6.0);
15952                        ui.horizontal(|ui| {
15953                            ui.strong("View emotes:");
15954                            let ptex = self
15955                                .provider_tex
15956                                .get_or_insert_with(|| ProviderTextures::load(ui.ctx()))
15957                                .clone();
15958                            for (provider, count) in emote_counts {
15959                                if count == 0 {
15960                                    continue;
15961                                }
15962                                let resp = match provider {
15963                                    EmoteProvider::SevenTv => ui.add(egui::ImageButton::new(
15964                                        egui::Image::from_texture(&ptex.seventv)
15965                                            .fit_to_exact_size(egui::vec2(18.0, 18.0)),
15966                                    )),
15967                                    EmoteProvider::Bttv => ui.add(egui::ImageButton::new(
15968                                        egui::Image::from_texture(&ptex.bttv)
15969                                            .fit_to_exact_size(egui::vec2(18.0, 18.0)),
15970                                    )),
15971                                    EmoteProvider::Twitch => ui.button("😀"),
15972                                    EmoteProvider::Ffz => ui.button("FFZ"),
15973                                };
15974                                if resp
15975                                    .on_hover_text(format!(
15976                                        "View {} {} emote{} for this channel",
15977                                        count,
15978                                        provider.label(),
15979                                        if count == 1 { "" } else { "s" },
15980                                    ))
15981                                    .clicked()
15982                                {
15983                                    open_emote_viewer = Some(provider);
15984                                }
15985                            }
15986                        });
15987                    }
15988                }
15989
15990                // ── Schedule sources (per-channel) ───────────────────────
15991                if let Some((_, cfg)) = self.channel_cfg_draft.as_mut() {
15992                    ui.add_space(6.0);
15993                    ui.strong("Schedule sources (this channel)");
15994                    ui.label(
15995                        egui::RichText::new(
15996                            "Used by the image/scrape sources you enable in Settings → Schedule \
15997                             sources. Changes apply on the next schedule refresh (or click ⟳ in \
15998                             the Schedule tab).",
15999                        )
16000                        .small()
16001                        .weak(),
16002                    );
16003                    egui::Grid::new("props_sched_src")
16004                        .num_columns(2)
16005                        .spacing([12.0, 4.0])
16006                        .show(ui, |ui| {
16007                            ui.label("Twitter/X handle");
16008                            if ui
16009                                .add(
16010                                    egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut cfg.twitter_handle)
16011                                        .hint_text("without @")
16012                                        .desired_width(240.0),
16013                                )
16014                                .on_hover_text(
16015                                    "Used by the 'Twitter/X pinned' source to read the schedule \
16016                                     off the pinned tweet's image.",
16017                                )
16018                                .changed()
16019                            {
16020                                cfg_dirty = true;
16021                            }
16022                            ui.end_row();
16023
16024                            ui.label("Other image (path/URL)");
16025                            if ui
16026                                .add(
16027                                    egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut cfg.other_image)
16028                                        .hint_text("local path or https://…")
16029                                        .desired_width(240.0),
16030                                )
16031                                .on_hover_text(
16032                                    "Used by the 'Other image (OCR)' source — point it at any \
16033                                     schedule image (a saved screenshot or a direct image URL).",
16034                                )
16035                                .changed()
16036                            {
16037                                cfg_dirty = true;
16038                            }
16039                            ui.end_row();
16040
16041                            ui.label("OCR model override");
16042                            if ui
16043                                .add(
16044                                    egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut cfg.ocr_model)
16045                                        .hint_text("(global default)")
16046                                        .desired_width(240.0),
16047                                )
16048                                .changed()
16049                            {
16050                                cfg_dirty = true;
16051                            }
16052                            ui.end_row();
16053
16054                            ui.label("OCR primary timezone");
16055                            if ui
16056                                .add(
16057                                    egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut cfg.ocr_timezone)
16058                                        .hint_text("(global default)")
16059                                        .desired_width(240.0),
16060                                )
16061                                .on_hover_text(
16062                                    "Primary IANA timezone this channel's schedule images are \
16063                                     written in (e.g. America/Los_Angeles). Anchors the date when \
16064                                     an image lists multiple timezones for one stream.",
16065                                )
16066                                .changed()
16067                            {
16068                                cfg_dirty = true;
16069                            }
16070                            ui.end_row();
16071
16072                            ui.label("OCR UTC offset override");
16073                            if ui
16074                                .add(
16075                                    egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut cfg.ocr_offset)
16076                                        .hint_text("(global default, e.g. +02:00)")
16077                                        .desired_width(240.0),
16078                                )
16079                                .changed()
16080                            {
16081                                cfg_dirty = true;
16082                            }
16083                            ui.end_row();
16084
16085                            ui.label("YouTube community backlog");
16086                            if ui
16087                                .add(
16088                                    egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut cfg.max_community_posts)
16089                                        .hint_text("(global default)")
16090                                        .desired_width(80.0),
16091                                )
16092                                .on_hover_text(
16093                                    "How many recent YouTube community posts to scan for this \
16094                                     channel's schedule image. Empty = use the global setting. \
16095                                     Clamped to 1–20.",
16096                                )
16097                                .changed()
16098                            {
16099                                cfg_dirty = true;
16100                            }
16101                            ui.end_row();
16102                        });
16103
16104                    // Per-channel source-order + title-fill override.
16105                    ui.add_space(6.0);
16106                    if let Some((_, scope)) = self.channel_scope_draft.as_mut() {
16107                        if scope_override_editor(ui, scope, &global_order) {
16108                            scope_dirty = true;
16109                        }
16110                    }
16111                }
16112
16113                // Apply an icon-source change picked in the combo above.
16114                if let Some(newp) = pref_change {
16115                    if let Err(e) =
16116                        self.core.store.set_channel_preferred_platform(ch.id, newp)
16117                    {
16118                        self.status = format!("Error: {e}");
16119                    } else {
16120                        self.channel_icons.remove(&ch.id);
16121                        // channel_icons_small intentionally omitted — same viewport-race
16122                        // reason as the Refetch button above. Refreshes on next AssetFetch.
16123                        self.reload_rows();
16124                    }
16125                }
16126                });
16127                draw_alt_image_preview(ctx);
16128            },
16129        );
16130
16131        // A launcher button was clicked above — open the emote viewer for it.
16132        if let Some(provider) = open_emote_viewer {
16133            self.emote_viewer = Some(EmoteViewer::new(ch.name.clone(), provider));
16134            // Fresh enumeration — clear any stale flag left from a prior session.
16135            self.emote_viewer_stale = false;
16136        }
16137        // The "🕑 History" button was clicked — load and open the asset-history popup.
16138        if open_asset_history {
16139            self.asset_history = Some(AssetHistoryView::new(ch.name.clone(), &asset_platforms));
16140        }
16141
16142        if cfg_dirty {
16143            if let Some((cid, cfg)) = &self.channel_cfg_draft {
16144                if let Err(e) = save_channel_cfg(&self.core.store, *cid, cfg) {
16145                    self.status = format!("Error saving channel config: {e}");
16146                }
16147            }
16148        }
16149        if scope_dirty {
16150            if let Some((cid, scope)) = &self.channel_scope_draft {
16151                if let Err(e) = save_channel_scope(&self.core.store, *cid, scope) {
16152                    self.status = format!("Error saving channel sources: {e}");
16153                } else {
16154                    self.core.request_schedule_refresh();
16155                }
16156            }
16157        }
16158
16159        if !open {
16160            self.channel_properties_popup = None;
16161            self.channel_cfg_draft = None;
16162            self.channel_scope_draft = None;
16163            // Free the full-resolution thumbnail textures (only this window uses
16164            // them); they reload from disk on the next open.
16165            self.channel_asset_thumbs.remove(&cid);
16166            self.channel_emote_counts.remove(&cid);
16167            self.channel_asset_status.remove(&cid);
16168        }
16169    }
16170
16171    /// Emote-viewer window — every emote one provider has for one channel, drawn as a
16172    /// grid of images with their codes. Emotes whose image file is gone (the manifest
16173    /// still lists them, but the file was removed upstream) appear separately under
16174    /// "Deprecated". Reuses the shared `emote_anim` decode cache, so emotes animate
16175    /// against the same clock as chat replay.
16176    #[allow(deprecated)]
16177    fn emote_viewer_window(&mut self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
16178        if self.emote_viewer.is_none() {
16179            return;
16180        }
16181        // Watchdog: name this phase (REPRO 2 — emote viewer grid left open).
16182        self.heartbeat.set_activity(crate::watchdog::Activity::EmoteViewerGrid);
16183        // Render toggles + shared cache copied out before borrowing the viewer, so
16184        // the closure never has to touch `self`.
16185        let anim_cache = self.emote_anim.clone();
16186        let animate_emotes = self.animate_emotes;
16187        let now = ctx.input(|i| i.time);
16188        let mut decode_misses: Vec<std::path::PathBuf> = Vec::new();
16189        // Were this channel's assets refetched while the window stayed open? The
16190        // lists below were enumerated once on open, so they no longer reflect disk.
16191        let stale = self.emote_viewer_stale;
16192
16193        // Extract everything we need from the viewer up front, so the borrow ends
16194        // before we need to mutably borrow self later (for NLL borrow checker).
16195        let viewer = self.emote_viewer.as_ref().expect("checked Some above");
16196        let provider = viewer.provider;
16197        let channel_name = viewer.channel_name.clone();
16198        let active = viewer.active.clone();
16199        let deprecated = viewer.deprecated.clone();
16200        let current_properties = viewer.emote_properties.clone();
16201        // viewer borrow ends here; all derived values are owned or Copy
16202
16203        let mut open = true;
16204        let mut pending_properties: Option<ViewerEmote> = None;
16205        let mut clear_properties = false;
16206
16207        ctx.show_viewport_immediate(
16208            egui::ViewportId::from_hash_of("emote_viewer_vp"),
16209            egui::ViewportBuilder::default()
16210                .with_title(format!("{} emotes — {}", provider.label(), channel_name))
16211                .with_inner_size([560.0, 600.0]),
16212            |ctx, _class| {
16213                if ctx.input(|i| i.viewport().close_requested()) {
16214                    open = false;
16215                }
16216                egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
16217                    if stale {
16218                        // Assets were refetched behind this window; the lists are a
16219                        // snapshot from when it opened. Amber, matching other
16220                        // "outdated info" hints elsewhere in the UI.
16221                        ui.colored_label(
16222                            egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xe0, 0xb0, 0x6c),
16223                            "⚠ Assets were refetched while this was open — close and \
16224                             reopen to see the latest.",
16225                        );
16226                        ui.separator();
16227                    }
16228                    ui.horizontal(|ui| {
16229                        ui.heading(provider.label());
16230                        // Active tally, plus the deprecated count when any — so the two
16231                        // reconcile with the launcher button (which counts the universe).
16232                        let mut tally = format!(
16233                            "· {} emote{}",
16234                            active.len(),
16235                            if active.len() == 1 { "" } else { "s" },
16236                        );
16237                        if !deprecated.is_empty() {
16238                            tally.push_str(&format!(" · {} deprecated", deprecated.len()));
16239                        }
16240                        ui.label(egui::RichText::new(tally).weak());
16241                    });
16242                    ui.separator();
16243                    egui::ScrollArea::vertical().auto_shrink([false, false]).show(ui, |ui| {
16244                        // Only claim "no emotes" when there is genuinely nothing to show.
16245                        // If `active` is empty but `deprecated` is not, the Deprecated
16246                        // section below carries the explanation — saying "no emotes
16247                        // available" above a populated list would contradict itself.
16248                        if active.is_empty() && deprecated.is_empty() {
16249                            ui.add_space(8.0);
16250                            ui.weak("No emotes available for this provider.");
16251                        } else if !active.is_empty() {
16252                            emote_viewer_grid(
16253                                ui,
16254                                &active,
16255                                &anim_cache,
16256                                animate_emotes,
16257                                now,
16258                                &mut decode_misses,
16259                                ctx,
16260                                false,
16261                                provider,
16262                                &mut pending_properties,
16263                            );
16264                        }
16265                        // The "Deprecated" section appears only when the manifest
16266                        // still references codes whose image is gone from the cache.
16267                        if !deprecated.is_empty() {
16268                            ui.add_space(12.0);
16269                            ui.separator();
16270                            ui.strong("Deprecated (no longer available)");
16271                            ui.label(
16272                                egui::RichText::new(
16273                                    "Still listed in this channel's emote manifest, but the image \
16274                                     is gone from the cache (removed upstream). Refetch assets to \
16275                                     prune them.",
16276                                )
16277                                .small()
16278                                .weak(),
16279                            );
16280                            ui.add_space(6.0);
16281                            emote_viewer_grid(
16282                                ui,
16283                                &deprecated,
16284                                &anim_cache,
16285                                animate_emotes,
16286                                now,
16287                                &mut decode_misses,
16288                                ctx,
16289                                true,
16290                                provider,
16291                                &mut pending_properties,
16292                            );
16293                        }
16294                    });
16295                });
16296
16297                // Properties window (floats above the central panel)
16298                if let Some(ep) = &current_properties {
16299                    let url = emote_cdn_url(provider, &ep.id, &ep.ext);
16300                    let size_bytes = std::fs::metadata(&ep.path).map(|m| m.len()).unwrap_or(0);
16301                    let mut prop_open = true;
16302                    egui::Window::new("Emote Properties")
16303                        .collapsible(false)
16304                        .resizable(false)
16305                        .open(&mut prop_open)
16306                        .show(ctx, |ui| {
16307                            egui::Grid::new("ep_grid")
16308                                .num_columns(2)
16309                                .striped(true)
16310                                .show(ui, |ui| {
16311                                    ui.label("Name:");
16312                                    ui.label(&ep.name);
16313                                    ui.end_row();
16314                                    ui.label("ID:");
16315                                    ui.label(&ep.id);
16316                                    ui.end_row();
16317                                    ui.label("Provider:");
16318                                    ui.label(provider.label());
16319                                    ui.end_row();
16320                                    ui.label("Extension:");
16321                                    ui.label(&ep.ext);
16322                                    ui.end_row();
16323                                    ui.label("File:");
16324                                    ui.label(ep.path.to_string_lossy());
16325                                    ui.end_row();
16326                                    ui.label("Size:");
16327                                    ui.label(fmt_bytes(size_bytes as i64));
16328                                    ui.end_row();
16329                                    ui.label("URL:");
16330                                    ui.label(&url);
16331                                    ui.end_row();
16332                                });
16333                            ui.add_space(4.0);
16334                            ui.horizontal(|ui| {
16335                                if ui.button("Copy URL").clicked() {
16336                                    ui.ctx().copy_text(url.clone());
16337                                }
16338                                if ui.button("Open File").clicked() {
16339                                    open_path(&ep.path);
16340                                }
16341                                if ui.button("Open Folder").clicked() {
16342                                    if let Some(dir) = ep.path.parent() {
16343                                        open_path(dir);
16344                                    }
16345                                }
16346                            });
16347                        });
16348                    if !prop_open {
16349                        clear_properties = true;
16350                    }
16351                }
16352
16353                draw_alt_image_preview(ctx);
16354            },
16355        );
16356
16357        // Apply context-menu / properties-window state changes collected during render.
16358        if let Some(ep) = pending_properties {
16359            if let Some(viewer) = &mut self.emote_viewer {
16360                viewer.emote_properties = Some(ep);
16361            }
16362        } else if clear_properties {
16363            if let Some(viewer) = &mut self.emote_viewer {
16364                viewer.emote_properties = None;
16365            }
16366        }
16367
16368        self.pump_emote_decodes(decode_misses, now, ctx);
16369
16370        if !open {
16371            self.emote_viewer = None;
16372            // Reset the stale flag so a later re-open starts fresh (not pre-flagged).
16373            self.emote_viewer_stale = false;
16374            // Free decoded emote frames now the window is closed (mirrors the chat
16375            // popup). If the chat replay is still open it re-decodes its visible
16376            // emotes next frame — cheap and bounded.
16377            self.clear_emote_cache();
16378        }
16379    }
16380
16381    /// Asset change-history popup: the recorded add/remove of emotes plus
16382    /// icon / banner / name-colour replacements for one channel, newest first,
16383    /// across all its platforms. Lines are built once on open (see
16384    /// [`AssetHistoryView::new`]); this just renders the snapshot. Mirrors
16385    /// [`Self::meta_popup_window`].
16386    #[allow(deprecated)]
16387    fn asset_history_window(&mut self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
16388        let Some(view) = self.asset_history.as_ref() else {
16389            return;
16390        };
16391        let mut open = true;
16392        ctx.show_viewport_immediate(
16393            egui::ViewportId::from_hash_of("asset_history_vp"),
16394            egui::ViewportBuilder::default()
16395                .with_title(format!("Asset history — {}", view.channel_name))
16396                .with_inner_size([560.0, 440.0]),
16397            |ctx, _class| {
16398                if ctx.input(|i| i.viewport().close_requested()) {
16399                    open = false;
16400                }
16401                egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
16402                    if view.lines.is_empty() {
16403                        ui.label(
16404                            "No asset changes recorded yet. The first asset fetch is the \
16405                             baseline; changes appear here after a later refetch alters the \
16406                             channel's emotes, icon, banner, or name colour.",
16407                        );
16408                        return;
16409                    }
16410                    ui.label(format!(
16411                        "{} recorded change(s), newest first. Removed emotes are kept here \
16412                         even after they vanish from the channel's manifest.",
16413                        view.lines.len(),
16414                    ));
16415                    ui.add_space(6.0);
16416                    egui::ScrollArea::vertical().auto_shrink([false, false]).show(ui, |ui| {
16417                        for line in &view.lines {
16418                            ui.label(egui::RichText::new(line).monospace());
16419                        }
16420                    });
16421                    ui.add_space(6.0);
16422                    if ui.button("📋  Copy").clicked() {
16423                        ui.ctx().copy_text(view.lines.join("\n"));
16424                    }
16425                });
16426            },
16427        );
16428        if !open {
16429            self.asset_history = None;
16430        }
16431    }
16432}
16433
16434// ── Properties window helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────
16435
16436/// Per-platform channel asset directory: `…/channel_assets/{name}/{platform}/`.
16437/// Thin alias for the shared definition in [`crate::assets::channel_asset_dir`].
16438fn channel_asset_dir(name: &str, platform: Platform) -> std::path::PathBuf {
16439    crate::assets::channel_asset_dir(name, platform)
16440}
16441
16442/// Stable per-platform identity of a monitor's source URL, used to detect whether
16443/// an import candidate is already added (matches [`ImportCandidate::identity`]).
16444fn monitor_import_identity(url: &str) -> String {
16445    match Platform::detect(url) {
16446        Platform::Twitch => crate::detectors::twitch_login(url).unwrap_or_default().to_lowercase(),
16447        Platform::Kick => crate::detectors::kick_slug(url).unwrap_or_default().to_lowercase(),
16448        Platform::YouTube => yt_channel_id(url).unwrap_or_else(|| url.to_lowercase()),
16449        Platform::Generic => url.to_lowercase(),
16450    }
16451}
16452
16453/// Extract a lowercased YouTube `UC…` channel id from a `/channel/UC…` URL.
16454fn yt_channel_id(url: &str) -> Option<String> {
16455    let lower = url.to_lowercase();
16456    let idx = lower.find("/channel/")?;
16457    let rest = &lower[idx + "/channel/".len()..];
16458    let id = rest.split(['/', '?', '#']).next()?;
16459    (!id.is_empty()).then(|| id.to_string())
16460}
16461
16462/// Whether an import row matches the dialog's (already-lowercased) filter query.
16463fn import_row_matches(row: &ImportRow, q: &str) -> bool {
16464    q.is_empty()
16465        || row.cand.name.to_lowercase().contains(q)
16466        || row.cand.identity.contains(q)
16467        || row.cand.id.to_lowercase().contains(q)
16468}
16469
16470/// The Twitch broadcaster's chosen chat name colour for `name`, if the asset fetch
16471/// cached one (`…/{name}/twitch/name_color.txt`, e.g. `#9146FF`). `None` when the
16472/// streamer set no colour or assets haven't been fetched.
16473fn load_twitch_name_color(name: &str) -> Option<egui::Color32> {
16474    let path = channel_asset_dir(name, Platform::Twitch).join("name_color.txt");
16475    let s = std::fs::read_to_string(path).ok()?;
16476    parse_chat_hex_color(s.trim())
16477}
16478
16479/// Build a Twitch channel's third-party emote lookup: case-sensitive emote code →
16480/// resolved on-disk image path. Reads the per-channel BTTV/FFZ/7TV manifests once
16481/// (called on popup-open, not per message) and resolves each entry to its file in
16482/// the per-channel or shared-global cache, keeping only those that exist.
16483///
16484/// Precedence on a code defined by multiple providers: **7TV > BTTV > FFZ** — we
16485/// insert in that order with `or_insert`, so the first (highest-priority) provider
16486/// to define a code wins and later duplicates don't clobber it.
16487fn build_emote_map(name: &str) -> HashMap<String, std::path::PathBuf> {
16488    use crate::assets::EmoteManifestEntry;
16489    let emotes_dir = channel_asset_dir(name, Platform::Twitch).join("emotes");
16490    let plat = crate::app_paths::platform_assets_dir();
16491    let mut map: HashMap<String, std::path::PathBuf> = HashMap::new();
16492
16493    let load = |file: &str| -> Vec<EmoteManifestEntry> {
16494        std::fs::read_to_string(emotes_dir.join(file))
16495            .ok()
16496            .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str(&s).ok())
16497            .unwrap_or_default()
16498    };
16499    let mut insert = |entries: Vec<EmoteManifestEntry>, resolve: &dyn Fn(&EmoteManifestEntry) -> std::path::PathBuf| {
16500        for e in entries {
16501            // Skip empty/whitespace-only codes (old name-less manifests, or odd
16502            // provider data) — they could never match a chat token anyway.
16503            if e.name.trim().is_empty() {
16504                continue;
16505            }
16506            let path = resolve(&e);
16507            if path.exists() {
16508                map.entry(e.name).or_insert(path);
16509            }
16510        }
16511    };
16512
16513    // 7TV: always in the shared global cache, `{id}.webp`.
16514    insert(load("7tv.json"), &|e| {
16515        plat.join("7tv").join("emotes").join(format!("{}.{}", e.id, e.ext))
16516    });
16517    // BTTV: per-channel for channel emotes, shared global for shared emotes.
16518    let bttv_channel = emotes_dir.join("bttv");
16519    let bttv_shared = plat.join("bttv").join("emotes");
16520    insert(load("bttv.json"), &|e| {
16521        let base = if e.shared { &bttv_shared } else { &bttv_channel };
16522        base.join(format!("{}.{}", e.id, e.ext))
16523    });
16524    // FFZ: always in the shared global cache.
16525    insert(load("ffz.json"), &|e| {
16526        plat.join("ffz").join("emotes").join(format!("{}.{}", e.id, e.ext))
16527    });
16528    map
16529}
16530
16531/// Decode encoded image `bytes` to RGBA8 under hard resource limits. A corrupt or
16532/// hostile image (absurd dimensions / a decompression bomb) would otherwise tie up the
16533/// UI thread for many seconds inside a synchronous decode — exactly the kind of single
16534/// blocking op that trips the freeze watchdog — or balloon into a multi-GB allocation
16535/// that, under the release `panic = "abort"` profile, kills the process outright.
16536///
16537/// Bounds: 16384×16384 px (the usual `GL_MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE`; anything larger could not be
16538/// uploaded as a texture anyway) and a 1 GiB peak decode allocation. A limit breach or a
16539/// decode error returns `None`, which every caller already treats as "no usable image".
16540fn decode_rgba_bounded(bytes: &[u8]) -> Option<image::RgbaImage> {
16541    let mut reader = image::ImageReader::new(std::io::Cursor::new(bytes))
16542        .with_guessed_format()
16543        .ok()?;
16544    // `Limits` is `#[non_exhaustive]`, so build via `default()` and set fields.
16545    let mut limits = image::Limits::default();
16546    limits.max_image_width = Some(16_384);
16547    limits.max_image_height = Some(16_384);
16548    limits.max_alloc = Some(1 << 30); // 1 GiB
16549    reader.limits(limits);
16550    Some(reader.decode().ok()?.to_rgba8())
16551}
16552
16553/// Decode an image file into an egui texture, returning the texture and its pixel
16554/// dimensions. `key` must be unique per logical image so textures never collide.
16555/// Returns `None` when the file is missing or undecodable.
16556fn load_image_texture(
16557    path: &std::path::Path,
16558    ctx: &egui::Context,
16559    key: &str,
16560) -> Option<(egui::TextureHandle, (u32, u32))> {
16561    let bytes = std::fs::read(path).ok()?;
16562    let img = decode_rgba_bounded(&bytes)?;
16563    let (w, h) = (img.width(), img.height());
16564    let color_image =
16565        egui::ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied([w as usize, h as usize], &img.into_raw());
16566    let tex = ctx.load_texture(format!("asset_{key}"), color_image, egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR);
16567    Some((tex, (w, h)))
16568}
16569
16570/// Load a channel's mainpage image assets (the `icon.*` and `banner.*` of each
16571/// platform — never emotes or badges) into textures for the Properties thumbnail
16572/// strip, in a stable platform/asset order. Skips assets that are absent.
16573fn load_channel_asset_thumbs(
16574    channel: &Channel,
16575    platforms: &[Platform],
16576    ctx: &egui::Context,
16577) -> Vec<AssetThumb> {
16578    let mut out: Vec<AssetThumb> = Vec::new();
16579    for &p in platforms.iter().filter(|p| **p != Platform::Generic) {
16580        let dir = channel_asset_dir(&channel.name, p);
16581        for (prefix, kind) in [("icon.", "icon"), ("banner.", "banner")] {
16582            let Some(path) = prop_find_first(&dir, prefix) else { continue };
16583            let key = format!("thumb_{}_{}_{kind}", channel.id, p.as_str());
16584            if let Some((tex, dims)) = load_image_texture(&path, ctx, &key) {
16585                out.push(AssetThumb {
16586                    label: format!("{} {kind}", p.label()),
16587                    path,
16588                    tex,
16589                    dims,
16590                });
16591            }
16592        }
16593    }
16594    out
16595}
16596
16597/// Count the emotes per provider the viewer would list for a channel — its whole
16598/// *universe* of named entries (active + deprecated), derived from the very same
16599/// [`enumerate_provider_emotes`] the viewer enumerates. Using that one source means the
16600/// launcher button's count can never drift from what the viewer shows: an empty-code
16601/// manifest entry is excluded here exactly as the viewer excludes it, while a
16602/// missing/0-byte image still counts (it appears in the viewer's "Deprecated" section).
16603/// Counting the universe — not just the active ones — keeps the button (and thus the
16604/// Deprecated section) reachable even for a provider whose emotes were all removed
16605/// upstream. Order: Twitch, 7TV, BTTV, FFZ — the order the launcher buttons are drawn
16606/// in. This stats one file per emote, so callers cache the result rather than recompute
16607/// it per frame.
16608fn emote_provider_counts(name: &str) -> [(EmoteProvider, usize); 4] {
16609    [
16610        EmoteProvider::Twitch,
16611        EmoteProvider::SevenTv,
16612        EmoteProvider::Bttv,
16613        EmoteProvider::Ffz,
16614    ]
16615    .map(|p| (p, enumerate_provider_emotes(name, p).len()))
16616}
16617
16618/// Enumerate a provider's emotes for a channel, resolved to on-disk image paths.
16619/// Third parties read their manifest and resolve into the per-channel/shared caches
16620/// exactly like [`build_emote_map`]; an entry whose image is gone is kept but marked
16621/// `exists = false` (the viewer lists those under "Deprecated"). Twitch is
16622/// directory-listed by opaque id (no codes, so never deprecated). Sorted by code.
16623fn enumerate_provider_emotes(name: &str, provider: EmoteProvider) -> Vec<ViewerEmote> {
16624    use crate::assets::{EmoteManifestEntry, sanitize_emote_name};
16625    let emotes_dir = channel_asset_dir(name, Platform::Twitch).join("emotes");
16626    let plat = crate::app_paths::platform_assets_dir();
16627
16628    // Resolve an emote's on-disk path: try the new `{id}_{name}.{ext}` pattern
16629    // first (written by updated fetchers), fall back to old `{id}.{ext}` for
16630    // files downloaded before this change.
16631    let resolve_path = |base: std::path::PathBuf, e: &EmoteManifestEntry| -> std::path::PathBuf {
16632        let new_name = format!("{}_{}.{}", e.id, sanitize_emote_name(&e.name), e.ext);
16633        let new_path = base.join(&new_name);
16634        if new_path.exists() {
16635            new_path
16636        } else {
16637            base.join(format!("{}.{}", e.id, e.ext))
16638        }
16639    };
16640
16641    // For Twitch: use the manifest when available (written after a refetch under
16642    // the new code). Fall back to directory listing for channels not yet refetched
16643    // so existing `{id}.{ext}` files still appear, shown with the numeric id as name.
16644    if provider == EmoteProvider::Twitch {
16645        let manifest_path = emotes_dir.join("twitch.json");
16646        if manifest_path.exists() {
16647            let entries: Vec<EmoteManifestEntry> =
16648                std::fs::read_to_string(&manifest_path)
16649                    .ok()
16650                    .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str(&s).ok())
16651                    .unwrap_or_default();
16652            let twitch_dir = emotes_dir.join("twitch");
16653            let mut out: Vec<ViewerEmote> = entries
16654                .into_iter()
16655                .filter(|e| !e.name.trim().is_empty())
16656                .map(|e| {
16657                    let path = resolve_path(twitch_dir.clone(), &e);
16658                    let exists = std::fs::metadata(&path).map(|m| m.len() > 0).unwrap_or(false);
16659                    ViewerEmote { name: e.name, id: e.id, ext: e.ext, path, exists }
16660                })
16661                .collect();
16662            out.sort_by_key(|e| e.name.to_lowercase());
16663            return out;
16664        }
16665        // No manifest yet — dir-list fallback (shows numeric id as name)
16666        let mut out: Vec<ViewerEmote> = std::fs::read_dir(emotes_dir.join("twitch"))
16667            .into_iter()
16668            .flatten()
16669            .flatten()
16670            .map(|e| e.path())
16671            .filter(|p| p.is_file())
16672            .map(|p| {
16673                let stem = p
16674                    .file_stem()
16675                    .and_then(|s| s.to_str())
16676                    .unwrap_or_default()
16677                    .to_string();
16678                let ext = p
16679                    .extension()
16680                    .and_then(|s| s.to_str())
16681                    .unwrap_or_default()
16682                    .to_string();
16683                // In the old layout the stem is the numeric id
16684                ViewerEmote { name: stem.clone(), id: stem, ext, path: p, exists: true }
16685            })
16686            .collect();
16687        out.sort_by(|a, b| a.name.cmp(&b.name));
16688        return out;
16689    }
16690
16691    let Some(manifest_file) = provider.manifest() else { return Vec::new() };
16692    let entries: Vec<EmoteManifestEntry> =
16693        std::fs::read_to_string(emotes_dir.join(manifest_file))
16694            .ok()
16695            .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str(&s).ok())
16696            .unwrap_or_default();
16697
16698    let resolve = |e: &EmoteManifestEntry| -> std::path::PathBuf {
16699        match provider {
16700            EmoteProvider::SevenTv => {
16701                resolve_path(plat.join("7tv").join("emotes"), e)
16702            }
16703            EmoteProvider::Ffz => {
16704                resolve_path(plat.join("ffz").join("emotes"), e)
16705            }
16706            EmoteProvider::Bttv => {
16707                let base = if e.shared {
16708                    plat.join("bttv").join("emotes")
16709                } else {
16710                    emotes_dir.join("bttv")
16711                };
16712                resolve_path(base, e)
16713            }
16714            EmoteProvider::Twitch => unreachable!("Twitch handled above"),
16715        }
16716    };
16717
16718    let mut out: Vec<ViewerEmote> = entries
16719        .into_iter()
16720        .filter(|e| !e.name.trim().is_empty())
16721        .map(|e| {
16722            let path = resolve(&e);
16723            // Mirror `assets::asset_present`: a 0-byte file is treated as absent.
16724            let exists = std::fs::metadata(&path).map(|m| m.len() > 0).unwrap_or(false);
16725            ViewerEmote { name: e.name, id: e.id, ext: e.ext, path, exists }
16726        })
16727        .collect();
16728    out.sort_by_key(|e| e.name.to_lowercase());
16729    out
16730}
16731
16732/// Truncate a label to at most `max` characters, appending `…` when shortened.
16733/// Char-aware so it never splits a multi-byte UTF-8 emote code mid-codepoint.
16734fn truncate_label(s: &str, max: usize) -> String {
16735    if s.chars().count() <= max {
16736        return s.to_string();
16737    }
16738    let head: String = s.chars().take(max.saturating_sub(1)).collect();
16739    format!("{head}…")
16740}
16741
16742/// Twitch `/me` actions arrive over IRC wrapped as `\x01ACTION <body>\x01` (CTCP).
16743/// The `emotes` tag's offsets index `<body>`, and the wrapper is protocol noise, so
16744/// it must be unwrapped before slicing / searching / display (otherwise emote
16745/// offsets are shifted and the raw control chars show in the replay). Returns the
16746/// inner body when both the prefix and the trailing `\x01` are present, else the
16747/// input unchanged. The raw `.chat.jsonl` keeps the wrapper for archival fidelity.
16748fn strip_ctcp_action(text: &str) -> &str {
16749    text.strip_prefix("\u{1}ACTION ")
16750        .and_then(|s| s.strip_suffix('\u{1}'))
16751        .unwrap_or(text)
16752}
16753
16754/// Slice `text` into [`ChatSegment`]s for a Twitch message: first-party emotes are
16755/// placed by the IRC `emotes` tag (id + inclusive **code-point** ranges), then any
16756/// remaining plain-text gaps are word-matched against the third-party `emote_map`.
16757///
16758/// Offsets are Unicode code points (Rust `char` index), not bytes and not UTF-16.
16759/// Every slice goes through `text.get(..)`, and any malformed/overlapping/out-of-
16760/// range tag aborts first-party substitution for the whole message (→ one Text
16761/// segment, still word-matched), so this never panics on hostile input.
16762fn build_twitch_segments(
16763    text: &str,
16764    emotes_tag: &str,
16765    emote_map: &HashMap<String, std::path::PathBuf>,
16766    twitch_dir: Option<&Path>,
16767) -> Vec<ChatSegment> {
16768    let spans = parse_first_party_spans(text, emotes_tag);
16769    if spans.is_empty() {
16770        return word_match_segments(text, emote_map);
16771    }
16772    // Emit text gaps (word-matched) and first-party emote images in order.
16773    let mut out: Vec<ChatSegment> = Vec::new();
16774    let mut cursor = 0usize;
16775    for (b0, b1, id) in spans {
16776        if b0 > cursor {
16777            if let Some(gap) = text.get(cursor..b0) {
16778                out.extend(word_match_segments(gap, emote_map));
16779            }
16780        }
16781        let name = text.get(b0..b1).unwrap_or("").to_string();
16782        // First-party files are `{id}.png` (static) or `{id}.gif` (animated — we
16783        // render its first frame). Probe both so animated channel emotes show too.
16784        let file = twitch_dir.and_then(|d| find_emote_file(d, &id));
16785        out.push(ChatSegment::Emote { name, file, fallback_text: None });
16786        cursor = b1;
16787    }
16788    if cursor < text.len() {
16789        if let Some(rest) = text.get(cursor..) {
16790            out.extend(word_match_segments(rest, emote_map));
16791        }
16792    }
16793    out
16794}
16795
16796/// Parse the IRC `emotes` tag into a sorted list of `(byte_start, byte_end, id)`
16797/// spans over `text`, converting inclusive code-point offsets to validated byte
16798/// ranges in ONE walk. Returns empty when the tag is empty OR anything is
16799/// malformed/overlapping/out-of-range (caller then renders the text verbatim).
16800fn parse_first_party_spans(text: &str, emotes_tag: &str) -> Vec<(usize, usize, String)> {
16801    if emotes_tag.is_empty() {
16802        return Vec::new();
16803    }
16804    // Collect (cp_start, cp_end_inclusive, id), dropping any malformed entry.
16805    let mut ranges: Vec<(usize, usize, String)> = Vec::new();
16806    for group in emotes_tag.split('/') {
16807        let Some((id, positions)) = group.split_once(':') else {
16808            return Vec::new();
16809        };
16810        if id.is_empty() {
16811            return Vec::new();
16812        }
16813        for pair in positions.split(',') {
16814            let Some((s, e)) = pair.split_once('-') else {
16815                return Vec::new();
16816            };
16817            let (Ok(s), Ok(e)) = (s.parse::<usize>(), e.parse::<usize>()) else {
16818                return Vec::new();
16819            };
16820            if e < s {
16821                return Vec::new();
16822            }
16823            ranges.push((s, e, id.to_string()));
16824        }
16825    }
16826    ranges.sort_by_key(|r| r.0);
16827    // Resolve code-point offsets → byte offsets in one pass. b0 = byte index of the
16828    // first char with cp_idx >= start; b1 = first char with cp_idx >= end+1 (or
16829    // text.len() when the span reaches end-of-string).
16830    let total_cps = text.chars().count();
16831    let mut spans: Vec<(usize, usize, String)> = Vec::with_capacity(ranges.len());
16832    let mut cursor_cp = 0usize; // overlap guard: next allowed start (code points)
16833    for (s, e, id) in ranges {
16834        if e >= total_cps || s < cursor_cp {
16835            // Out of range, or overlaps/touches a previous span → bail entirely.
16836            return Vec::new();
16837        }
16838        let mut b0: Option<usize> = None;
16839        let mut b1: Option<usize> = None;
16840        for (cp_idx, (byte_idx, _ch)) in text.char_indices().enumerate() {
16841            if b0.is_none() && cp_idx >= s {
16842                b0 = Some(byte_idx);
16843            }
16844            if cp_idx >= e + 1 {
16845                b1 = Some(byte_idx);
16846                break;
16847            }
16848        }
16849        let b0 = match b0 {
16850            Some(b) => b,
16851            None => return Vec::new(),
16852        };
16853        let b1 = b1.unwrap_or(text.len()); // span ended at end-of-string
16854        if b1 <= b0 || text.get(b0..b1).is_none() {
16855            return Vec::new();
16856        }
16857        spans.push((b0, b1, id));
16858        cursor_cp = e + 1;
16859    }
16860    spans
16861}
16862
16863/// Split `text` on Unicode whitespace, emitting an `Emote` segment for each
16864/// whitespace-delimited token that exactly (case-sensitively) matches the
16865/// third-party `emote_map`, and `Text` for everything else (whitespace runs and
16866/// non-matching words), preserving the original spacing. Used both for whole
16867/// messages without first-party emotes and for the text gaps between them.
16868fn word_match_segments(
16869    text: &str,
16870    emote_map: &HashMap<String, std::path::PathBuf>,
16871) -> Vec<ChatSegment> {
16872    if text.is_empty() {
16873        return Vec::new();
16874    }
16875    if emote_map.is_empty() {
16876        return vec![ChatSegment::Text(text.to_string())];
16877    }
16878    let mut out: Vec<ChatSegment> = Vec::new();
16879    let mut pending = String::new(); // accumulates Text (whitespace + non-emote words)
16880    // Walk maximal non-whitespace runs (candidate emote codes) and the whitespace
16881    // between them, so tabs / NBSP / multiple spaces are preserved verbatim.
16882    let mut rest = text;
16883    while !rest.is_empty() {
16884        // Leading whitespace run.
16885        let ws_end = rest
16886            .char_indices()
16887            .find(|(_, c)| !c.is_whitespace())
16888            .map(|(i, _)| i)
16889            .unwrap_or(rest.len());
16890        if ws_end > 0 {
16891            pending.push_str(&rest[..ws_end]);
16892            rest = &rest[ws_end..];
16893            continue;
16894        }
16895        // Non-whitespace token run.
16896        let tok_end = rest
16897            .char_indices()
16898            .find(|(_, c)| c.is_whitespace())
16899            .map(|(i, _)| i)
16900            .unwrap_or(rest.len());
16901        let token = &rest[..tok_end];
16902        if let Some(path) = emote_map.get(token) {
16903            if !pending.is_empty() {
16904                out.push(ChatSegment::Text(std::mem::take(&mut pending)));
16905            }
16906            out.push(ChatSegment::Emote {
16907                name: token.to_string(),
16908                file: Some(path.clone()),
16909                fallback_text: None,
16910            });
16911        } else {
16912            pending.push_str(token);
16913        }
16914        rest = &rest[tok_end..];
16915    }
16916    if !pending.is_empty() {
16917        out.push(ChatSegment::Text(pending));
16918    }
16919    out
16920}
16921
16922/// Resolve a container's avatar — the chosen-platform profile pic. An explicit
16923/// `preferred_platform` wins when it's one of the container's instance platforms
16924/// (showing a placeholder until that platform's icon is fetched); otherwise auto:
16925/// the first instance-platform (first-seen order) whose icon loads. `None` when
16926/// no platform has a fetched icon yet.
16927fn resolve_channel_icon(
16928    channel: &Channel,
16929    platforms: &[Platform],
16930    ctx: &egui::Context,
