16931) -> Option<egui::TextureHandle> {
16932    let key = channel.id.to_string();
16933    let load =
16934        |p: Platform| load_channel_icon(&channel_asset_dir(&channel.name, p), ctx, &key);
16935    if let Some(p) = channel.preferred_platform {
16936        if platforms.contains(&p) {
16937            return load(p);
16938        }
16939    }
16940    platforms.iter().copied().find_map(load).or_else(|| {
16941        // Legacy fallback (auto mode only): assets fetched before per-platform
16942        // namespacing lived in the flat channel_assets/{name}/ dir. Show that
16943        // icon until a per-platform refetch repopulates the namespaced dir, so an
16944        // existing container's avatar doesn't go blank on upgrade.
16945        let flat = crate::app_paths::asset_cache_dir()
16946            .join("channel_assets")
16947            .join(crate::downloader::sanitize_filename(&channel.name));
16948        load_channel_icon(&flat, ctx, &key)
16949    })
16950}
16951
16952/// Count archived historical variants of a singular asset (`history/{stem}_*`).
16953/// These are the older profile pics / banners kept when the channel changed them.
16954fn prop_variant_count(asset_dir: &std::path::Path, stem: &str) -> usize {
16955    let prefix = format!("{stem}_");
16956    std::fs::read_dir(asset_dir.join("history"))
16957        .map(|rd| {
16958            rd.flatten()
16959                .filter(|e| e.file_name().to_string_lossy().starts_with(&prefix))
16960                .count()
16961        })
16962        .unwrap_or(0)
16963}
16964
16965/// One asset-status cell: `—` (absent), `✔` (present), or `✔ +N` when `N` older
16966/// versions are archived. Hovering a `+N` cell explains the kept history.
16967fn asset_status_cell(ui: &mut egui::Ui, present: bool, variants: usize) {
16968    let text = if !present {
16969        "—".to_string()
16970    } else if variants > 0 {
16971        format!("✔ +{variants}")
16972    } else {
16973        "✔".to_string()
16974    };
16975    let resp = ui.label(text);
16976    if variants > 0 {
16977        resp.on_hover_text(format!(
16978            "{variants} older version(s) archived under history/ — kept, never overwritten."
16979        ));
16980    }
16981}
16982
16983/// Short "last fetched" label for an asset dir's `.assets_fetched_at` stamp.
16984/// Returns `"never"` when the stamp is missing/unparseable.
16985fn fmt_asset_stamp(asset_dir: &std::path::Path) -> String {
16986    std::fs::read_to_string(asset_dir.join(".assets_fetched_at"))
16987        .ok()
16988        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<i64>().ok())
16989        .and_then(|t| chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(t, 0))
16990        .map(|dt| {
16991            dt.with_timezone(&chrono::Local)
16992                .format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
16993                .to_string()
16994        })
16995        .unwrap_or_else(|| "never".into())
16996}
16997
16998/// Load a channel icon from `asset_dir/icon.*` into an egui texture.
16999/// Returns `None` when no icon file is found or decoding fails.
17000fn load_channel_icon(
17001    asset_dir: &std::path::Path,
17002    ctx: &egui::Context,
17003    key: &str,
17004) -> Option<egui::TextureHandle> {
17005    let entry = prop_find_first(asset_dir, "icon.")?;
17006    let bytes = std::fs::read(&entry).ok()?;
17007    let img = decode_rgba_bounded(&bytes)?;
17008    let size = [img.width() as usize, img.height() as usize];
17009    let color_image =
17010        egui::ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied(size, &img.into_raw());
17011    Some(ctx.load_texture(
17012        format!("chan_icon_{key}"),
17013        color_image,
17014        egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
17015    ))
17016}
17017
17018/// Like [`load_channel_icon`] but loads the pre-scaled 64 px thumbnail
17019/// (`icon_64.png`) generated by [`crate::assets::ensure_scaled_icon`].
17020/// Used for the small avatar slots in the streams table where uploading a full
17021/// 300 px source and letting the GPU scale it to 18 px gives poor quality.
17022fn load_channel_icon_small(
17023    asset_dir: &std::path::Path,
17024    ctx: &egui::Context,
17025    key: &str,
17026) -> Option<egui::TextureHandle> {
17027    let path = crate::assets::ensure_scaled_icon(asset_dir, 64)?;
17028    let bytes = std::fs::read(&path).ok()?;
17029    let img = decode_rgba_bounded(&bytes)?;
17030    let size = [img.width() as usize, img.height() as usize];
17031    let color_image = egui::ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied(size, &img.into_raw());
17032    Some(ctx.load_texture(
17033        format!("chan_icon_small_{key}"),
17034        color_image,
17035        egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
17036    ))
17037}
17038
17039/// Like [`resolve_channel_icon`] but uses the 64 px pre-scaled thumbnail for
17040/// the streams-table avatar slot.
17041fn resolve_channel_icon_small(
17042    channel: &Channel,
17043    platforms: &[Platform],
17044    ctx: &egui::Context,
17045) -> Option<egui::TextureHandle> {
17046    let key = channel.id.to_string();
17047    let load = |p: Platform| {
17048        load_channel_icon_small(&channel_asset_dir(&channel.name, p), ctx, &key)
17049    };
17050    if let Some(p) = channel.preferred_platform {
17051        if platforms.contains(&p) {
17052            return load(p);
17053        }
17054    }
17055    platforms.iter().copied().find_map(load).or_else(|| {
17056        let flat = crate::app_paths::asset_cache_dir()
17057            .join("channel_assets")
17058            .join(crate::downloader::sanitize_filename(&channel.name));
17059        load_channel_icon_small(&flat, ctx, &key)
17060    })
17061}
17062
17063// ---------- Alt-hover full-resolution image preview ----------
17064
17065/// When the user holds Alt while hovering `resp`, queue `tex` for a
17066/// full-resolution floating preview shown by [`draw_alt_image_preview`].
17067/// Call immediately after `ui.add(egui::Image::from_texture(...))`.
17068fn queue_alt_image_preview(ctx: &egui::Context, resp: &egui::Response, tex: &egui::TextureHandle) {
17069    if resp.hovered() && ctx.input(|i| i.modifiers.alt) {
17070        // Wrapped in Option so remove_temp (which needs T: Default) can use None as sentinel.
17071        ctx.data_mut(|d| {
17072            d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("alt_img_preview"), Some(tex.clone()))
17073        });
17074    }
17075}
17076
17077/// Draw the Alt-hover image preview queued this frame by [`queue_alt_image_preview`].
17078/// Call once at the end of each viewport's rendering pass so it floats on top.
17079/// Destructively consumes the queued texture, so the overlay vanishes the frame
17080/// the user stops hovering or releases Alt — no explicit clear needed.
17081fn draw_alt_image_preview(ctx: &egui::Context) {
17082    // remove_temp::<Option<T>> works because Option implements Default (= None).
17083    let Some(Some(tex)) = ctx.data_mut(|d| {
17084        d.remove_temp::<Option<egui::TextureHandle>>(egui::Id::new("alt_img_preview"))
17085    }) else {
17086        return;
17087    };
17088    let Some(pos) = ctx.input(|i| i.pointer.hover_pos()) else {
17089        return;
17090    };
17091
17092    let tex_size = tex.size_vec2();
17093    let screen = ctx.viewport_rect();
17094    // Cap at 85% of the viewport so large banners don't overflow off-screen.
17095    let max_size = screen.size() * 0.85;
17096    let scale = (max_size.x / tex_size.x.max(1.0))
17097        .min(max_size.y / tex_size.y.max(1.0))
17098        .min(1.0);
17099    let preview_size = tex_size * scale;
17100
17101    // Prefer bottom-right of the cursor; flip to the other side when that
17102    // would go off-screen.
17103    const GAP: f32 = 14.0;
17104    let mut tl = pos + egui::vec2(GAP, GAP);
17105    if tl.x + preview_size.x > screen.right() - GAP {
17106        tl.x = (pos.x - preview_size.x - GAP).max(screen.left() + GAP);
17107    }
17108    if tl.y + preview_size.y > screen.bottom() - GAP {
17109        tl.y = (pos.y - preview_size.y - GAP).max(screen.top() + GAP);
17110    }
17111
17112    egui::Area::new(egui::Id::new("alt_img_preview_area"))
17113        .fixed_pos(tl)
17114        .order(egui::Order::Tooltip)
17115        .interactable(false)
17116        .show(ctx, |ui| {
17117            egui::Frame::new()
17118                .fill(ui.visuals().window_fill)
17119                .stroke(ui.visuals().window_stroke)
17120                .inner_margin(egui::Margin::same(6))
17121                .corner_radius(egui::CornerRadius::same(6))
17122                .show(ui, |ui| {
17123                    ui.add(egui::Image::from_texture(&tex).fit_to_exact_size(preview_size));
17124                    ui.label(
17125                        egui::RichText::new(format!(
17126                            "{} × {} px",
17127                            tex_size.x as u32,
17128                            tex_size.y as u32
17129                        ))
17130                        .small()
17131                        .weak(),
17132                    );
17133                });
17134        });
17135}
17136
17137/// Try to extract a YouTube UC… channel ID from a channel URL.
17138fn extract_yt_channel_id(url: &str) -> Option<String> {
17139    // Matches "/channel/UCxxxxxxxxx" style URLs.
17140    let idx = url.find("/channel/")?;
17141    let after = &url[idx + "/channel/".len()..];
17142    let id: String = after.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_alphanumeric() || *c == '-' || *c == '_').collect();
17143    if id.starts_with("UC") && id.len() > 10 {
17144        Some(id)
17145    } else {
17146        None
17147    }
17148}
17149
17150/// Find the first entry in `dir` whose filename starts with `prefix`.
17151fn prop_find_first(dir: &std::path::Path, prefix: &str) -> Option<std::path::PathBuf> {
17152    std::fs::read_dir(dir)
17153        .ok()?
17154        .flatten()
17155        .find(|e| e.file_name().to_string_lossy().starts_with(prefix))
17156        .map(|e| e.path())
17157}
17158
17159/// Count files (non-recursive) in `dir`.
17160fn prop_count_dir_files(dir: &std::path::Path) -> usize {
17161    std::fs::read_dir(dir)
17162        .into_iter()
17163        .flatten()
17164        .flatten()
17165        .filter(|e| e.path().is_file())
17166        .count()
17167}
17168
17169/// Count directories at exactly `depth` levels below `root`.
17170fn prop_count_nested_dirs(root: &std::path::Path, depth: usize) -> usize {
17171    if depth == 0 || !root.is_dir() {
17172        return 0;
17173    }
17174    std::fs::read_dir(root)
17175        .into_iter()
17176        .flatten()
17177        .flatten()
17178        .map(|e| {
17179            if depth == 1 {
17180                if e.path().is_dir() { 1 } else { 0 }
17181            } else {
17182                prop_count_nested_dirs(&e.path(), depth - 1)
17183            }
17184        })
17185        .sum()
17186}
17187
17188/// "yes" / "no" display for boolean fields.
17189fn prop_bool(v: bool) -> &'static str {
17190    if v { "yes" } else { "no" }
17191}
17192
17193/// Truncate a long path string keeping its tail (for compact display).
17194fn prop_truncate_path(p: &str, max_chars: usize) -> String {
17195    if p.len() <= max_chars {
17196        p.to_string()
17197    } else {
17198        format!("…{}", &p[p.len() - max_chars..])
17199    }
17200}
17201
17202/// Read the length of a JSON-array manifest file (BTTV/FFZ/7TV emote manifests).
17203/// Returns 0 if the file is absent or unparseable.
17204fn prop_read_manifest_count(path: &std::path::Path) -> usize {
17205    std::fs::read_to_string(path)
17206        .ok()
17207        .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&s).ok())
17208        .and_then(|v| v.as_array().map(|a| a.len()))
17209        .unwrap_or(0)
17210}
17211
17212// ── Chat viewer helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
17213
17214/// Derive the chat sidecar path from a recording's output path.
17215/// Locate a recording's chat sidecar. yt-dlp's `live_chat` writer **appends** to the
17216/// `-o` value (keeping the video extension), so the YouTube sidecar is
17217/// `<output_path>.live_chat.json` (e.g. `clip.mkv.live_chat.json`) — not a simple
17218/// extension swap. The Twitch native logger instead **replaces** the extension
17219/// (`clip.chat.jsonl`). We try both forms, plus the legacy pre-`.cache` YouTube name
17220/// (`clip.ts.live_chat.json`).
17221fn chat_file_for_recording(rec: &Recording) -> Option<std::path::PathBuf> {
17222    let base = Path::new(&rec.output_path);
17223    let candidates = [
17224        // YouTube (yt-dlp append form): `<output_path>.live_chat.json`.
17225        std::path::PathBuf::from(format!("{}.live_chat.json", rec.output_path)),
17226        // Twitch native logger (extension replace): `<stem>.chat.jsonl`.
17227        base.with_extension("chat.jsonl"),
17228        // Extension-replace live_chat form, just in case.
17229        base.with_extension("live_chat.json"),
17230        // Legacy pre-`.cache` YouTube name: `<stem>.ts.live_chat.json`.
17231        base.with_extension("ts.live_chat.json"),
17232    ];
17233    candidates.into_iter().find(|p| p.exists())
17234}
17235
17236fn fmt_recording_label(rec: &Recording) -> String {
17237    let dt = chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(rec.started_at, 0)
17238        .map(|dt| dt.format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M").to_string())
17239        .unwrap_or_else(|| rec.started_at.to_string());
17240    format!("{dt} ({})", rec.status)
17241}
17242
17243fn fmt_chat_ts(secs: f64) -> String {
17244    if secs < 0.0 {
17245        return format!("-{}", fmt_chat_ts(-secs));
17246    }
17247    let s = secs as u64;
17248    format!("[{:02}:{:02}:{:02}]", s / 3600, (s % 3600) / 60, s % 60)
17249}
17250
17251/// Soft cap on decoded emote-frame GPU memory; the cache is LRU-evicted past this.
17252const EMOTE_BUDGET_BYTES: usize = 192 * 1024 * 1024;
17253
17254#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
17255fn render_chat_message(
17256    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
17257    msg: &ChatMessage,
17258    cache: &Mutex<HashMap<std::path::PathBuf, crate::emote_anim::EmoteLoad>>,
17259    render_emotes: bool,
17260    animate: bool,
17261    now: f64,
17262    misses: &mut Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
17263    ctx: &egui::Context,
17264) {
17265    ui.horizontal_wrapped(|ui| {
17266        ui.spacing_mut().item_spacing.x = 3.0;
17267        // Timestamp — muted monospace
17268        ui.label(
17269            egui::RichText::new(fmt_chat_ts(msg.timestamp_secs))
17270                .monospace()
17271                .small()
17272                .color(ui.visuals().weak_text_color()),
17273        );
17274        // Badges
17275        for badge in &msg.badges {
17276            let (sym, color) = badge_display(badge, &msg.platform);
17277            ui.label(egui::RichText::new(sym).small().color(color));
17278        }
17279        // Username — bold, platform/user colour, adjusted for contrast on the
17280        // chat panel's background so dark colours stay legible.
17281        let name_color = chat_username_color(msg, ui.visuals().panel_fill);
17282        ui.label(
17283            egui::RichText::new(format!("{}:", msg.author))
17284                .strong()
17285                .color(name_color),
17286        );
17287        // Message body — text runs and (when enabled & on disk) inline emote images.
17288        let emote_h = (ui.text_style_height(&egui::TextStyle::Body) * 1.5).min(28.0);
17289        for seg in &msg.segments {
17290            match seg {
17291                ChatSegment::Text(t) => {
17292                    // One label per run: egui wraps a multi-word galley at word
17293                    // boundaries inside horizontal_wrapped while preserving the run's
17294                    // internal/leading/trailing whitespace verbatim.
17295                    ui.label(t);
17296                }
17297                ChatSegment::Emote { name, file, fallback_text } => {
17298                    let drawn = render_emotes
17299                        && file.as_ref().is_some_and(|f| {
17300                            match draw_cached_emote(ui, cache, f, animate, emote_h, now, misses, ctx)
17301                            {
17302                                Some(resp) => {
17303                                    resp.on_hover_text(name);
17304                                    true
17305                                }
17306                                None => false,
17307                            }
17308                        });
17309                    if !drawn {
17310                        // No image (off / loading / not on disk / undecodable): show
17311                        // the emoji glyph if we have one, else the emote code.
17312                        ui.label(fallback_text.as_deref().unwrap_or(name));
17313                    }
17314                }
17315            }
17316        }
17317    });
17318}
17319
17320/// CDN URL for an emote given provider, id, and extension.
17321fn emote_cdn_url(provider: EmoteProvider, id: &str, ext: &str) -> String {
17322    match provider {
17323        EmoteProvider::Twitch => {
17324            if ext == "gif" {
17325                format!("https://static-cdn.jtvnw.net/emoticons/v2/{id}/animated/dark/3.0")
17326            } else {
17327                format!("https://static-cdn.jtvnw.net/emoticons/v2/{id}/static/dark/3.0")
17328            }
17329        }
17330        EmoteProvider::SevenTv => format!("https://cdn.7tv.app/emote/{id}/4x.{ext}"),
17331        EmoteProvider::Bttv => format!("https://cdn.betterttv.net/emote/{id}/3x.{ext}"),
17332        EmoteProvider::Ffz => format!("https://cdn.frankerfacez.com/emoticon/{id}/4"),
17333    }
17334}
17335
17336/// Open a path (file or directory) with the default associated application.
17337fn open_path(path: &std::path::Path) {
17338    let _ = std::process::Command::new("explorer").arg(path).spawn();
17339}
17340
17341/// What "Stream in player" should open for a recording, and how.
17342#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
17343enum StreamTarget {
17344    /// A completed output file — open plainly (works with any player).
17345    Finished(std::path::PathBuf),
17346    /// A single still-growing capture file (`.ts` / `.mkv` / `.mkv.mp4` /
17347    /// `.dash.ts`). mpv follows growth via `appending://`; other players open
17348    /// it plainly (playable, but they stop at the current end).
17349    Growing(std::path::PathBuf),
17350    /// Mid-SABR capture: separate still-growing per-format files (video +
17351    /// audio), largest first. Playable only in mpv: the largest (video) file
17352    /// opens as `appending://` main file, the rest attach via
17353    /// `--audio-file=appending://…`. (An `edl://` merge also loads both
17354    /// tracks but keeps zero seconds of demuxer readahead and bakes the
17355    /// total duration at open — video freezes and growth is not followed.)
17356    SplitAv(Vec<std::path::PathBuf>),
17357}
17358
17359/// SABR growing per-format files only need moderately-sized init data before
17360/// they're openable; also filters out the tiny `.state` sidecars (~50 B).
17361const SPLIT_AV_MIN_BYTES: u64 = 64 * 1024;
17362
17363/// True current size of a possibly-being-written file, or `None` if it can't
17364/// be opened / isn't a file. The size that `fs::metadata` / `read_dir`
17365/// metadata report comes from the DIRECTORY ENTRY, which NTFS only updates
17366/// lazily while another process holds the file open for writing — a capture
17367/// started seconds ago reads as 0 bytes there even with megabytes written
17368/// (verified against a live download: dir entry 0, handle size 5 MB).
17369/// Opening the file queries the handle, which is always current.
17370fn live_file_len(p: &std::path::Path) -> Option<u64> {
17371    let md = std::fs::File::open(p).ok()?.metadata().ok()?;
17372    md.is_file().then(|| md.len())
17373}
17374
17375/// Find what an active recording's in-progress capture can be played from, by
17376/// probing `.cache\` next to its final output path.
17377///
17378/// 1. The single-file captures streamlink/yt-dlp produce (`{stem}.ts`,
17379///    `{stem}.mkv`, `{stem}.mkv.mp4`) → [`StreamTarget::Growing`]. This also
17380///    covers the DASH companion (its own output path is `{stem}.dash.mkv`, so
17381///    its capture probe hits `{stem}.dash.ts`) and the brief post-merge window
17382///    of a SABR capture.
17383/// 2. SABR mid-download: the per-format growing files. Naming drifts between
17384///    dev-build versions — both `{stem}.mkv.f140.mp4[.sq0.part]` and
17385///    `{stem}.f303.mkv[.sq0.part]` orderings are seen in the wild — so this
17386///    scans for `{stem}.`-prefixed names containing an `f<digits>` dot-segment
17387///    with a media extension or an in-flight `.sq<N>.part` suffix. During the
17388///    active download the growing media file IS the `.sq<N>.part` file; the
17389///    bare-named twin only appears once the stream ends and the merge starts.
17390///    Two or more formats → [`StreamTarget::SplitAv`]; one → `Growing`.
17391fn stream_target_for_active(output_path: &str) -> Option<StreamTarget> {
17392    let final_path = std::path::Path::new(output_path);
17393    let stem = final_path.file_stem()?.to_string_lossy().into_owned();
17394    let cache_dir = final_path.parent()?.join(".cache");
17395    for ext in [".ts", ".mkv", ".mkv.mp4"] {
17396        let candidate = cache_dir.join(format!("{stem}{ext}"));
17397        if live_file_len(&candidate).unwrap_or(0) > 0 {
17398            return Some(StreamTarget::Growing(candidate));
17399        }
17400    }
17401    // SABR split scan: group candidates by format id, keep the best file per
17402    // format (bare beats .part; higher .sq<N> beats lower — a resume starts a
17403    // new sequence file and the highest is the one currently growing).
17404    // (format_id, sequence: None = bare/merge-phase file) → (path, size)
17405    let mut best: std::collections::HashMap<u64, (Option<u64>, std::path::PathBuf, u64)> =
17406        std::collections::HashMap::new();
17407    let prefix = format!("{stem}.");
17408    for entry in std::fs::read_dir(&cache_dir).ok()?.flatten() {
17409        let name = entry.file_name().to_string_lossy().into_owned();
17410        let Some(rest) = name.strip_prefix(&prefix) else { continue };
17411        if rest.ends_with(".state") || rest.ends_with(".log") || rest.ends_with(".ytdl") {
17412            continue;
17413        }
17414        if rest.contains(".temp.") {
17415            continue; // in-flight ffmpeg merge output
17416        }
17417        let segs: Vec<&str> = rest.split('.').collect();
17418        let parse_fmt = |s: &str| -> Option<u64> {
17419            let d = s.strip_prefix('f')?;
17420            if d.is_empty() || !d.bytes().all(|b| b.is_ascii_digit()) {
17421                return None;
17422            }
17423            d.parse().ok()
17424        };
17425        let Some(fpos) = segs.iter().position(|s| parse_fmt(s).is_some()) else { continue };
17426        // Everything before the f<id> segment must be container decoration
17427        // (variant A has the template ext there: `{stem}.mkv.f140.mp4…`),
17428        // never arbitrary title text — otherwise a sibling recording whose
17429        // stem merely extends this stem after a dot ("Chan" vs
17430        // "Chan. Part 2.f303….part" → rest " Part 2.f303….part") leaks in.
17431        if !segs[..fpos].iter().all(|s| matches!(*s, "mkv" | "mp4" | "webm" | "m4a" | "ts")) {
17432            continue;
17433        }
17434        let format_id = parse_fmt(segs[fpos]).unwrap_or(0);
17435        // Growing in-flight file `….sq<N>.part`, or a bare media file
17436        // (merge phase / finished-writing).
17437        let seq: Option<u64> = match segs.as_slice() {
17438            [.., sq, "part"] => match sq.strip_prefix("sq").and_then(|d| d.parse().ok()) {
17439                Some(n) => Some(n),
17440                None => continue, // other .part files aren't playable media
17441            },
17442            [.., ext] if matches!(*ext, "mp4" | "m4a" | "webm" | "mkv") => None,
17443            _ => continue,
17444        };
17445        let Some(len) = live_file_len(&entry.path()) else { continue };
17446        if len < SPLIT_AV_MIN_BYTES {
17447            continue;
17448        }
17449        let candidate = (seq, entry.path(), len);
17450        match best.entry(format_id) {
17451            std::collections::hash_map::Entry::Vacant(v) => {
17452                v.insert(candidate);
17453            }
17454            std::collections::hash_map::Entry::Occupied(mut o) => {
17455                // Bare (None) outranks any .part; among .parts, higher sq wins.
17456                let better = match (&o.get().0, &candidate.0) {
17457                    (Some(_), None) => true,
17458                    (Some(cur), Some(new)) => new > cur,
17459                    _ => false,
17460                };
17461                if better {
17462                    o.insert(candidate);
17463                }
17464            }
17465        }
17466    }
17467    let mut parts: Vec<(std::path::PathBuf, u64)> =
17468        best.into_values().map(|(_, p, len)| (p, len)).collect();
17469    match parts.len() {
17470        0 => None,
17471        1 => Some(StreamTarget::Growing(parts.remove(0).0)),
17472        _ => {
17473            parts.sort_by_key(|p| std::cmp::Reverse(p.1)); // video (largest) first
17474            Some(StreamTarget::SplitAv(parts.into_iter().map(|(p, _)| p).collect()))
17475        }
17476    }
17477}
17478
17479/// True when the configured player binary is mpv (or an mpv front-end like
17480/// mpv.net) — the only player that supports `appending://` and `edl://`.
17481fn player_is_mpv(player_path: &str) -> bool {
17482    std::path::Path::new(player_path)
17483        .file_stem()
17484        .map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().to_ascii_lowercase().starts_with("mpv"))
17485        .unwrap_or(false)
17486}
17487
17488/// Whether the configured player can play this target. Split SABR captures
17489/// need mpv; everything else is a plain file any player opens.
17490fn playable_with(t: &StreamTarget, player: &str) -> bool {
17491    match t {
17492        StreamTarget::Finished(_) | StreamTarget::Growing(_) => true,
17493        StreamTarget::SplitAv(_) => player_is_mpv(player),
17494    }
17495}
17496
17497/// Flags for watching a still-growing capture in mpv: don't quit on a
17498/// momentary EOF/stall, and allow seeking within what's been read.
17499const MPV_LIVE_FLAGS: &[&str] = &["--keep-open=yes", "--cache=yes", "--force-seekable=yes"];
17500
17501/// Forward-slash form of a path — the safe spelling inside `appending://` /
17502/// `edl://` URL arguments on Windows.
17503fn fwd_slashes(p: &std::path::Path) -> String {
17504    p.to_string_lossy().replace('\\', "/")
17505}
17506
17507/// Build the player invocation for a stream target. mpv gets live-view flags
17508/// and `appending://` URLs for growing files; other players get plain paths.
17509fn build_player_command(player: &str, t: &StreamTarget) -> std::process::Command {
17510    let mut cmd = std::process::Command::new(player);
17511    let mpv = player_is_mpv(player);
17512    match t {
17513        StreamTarget::Finished(p) => {
17514            cmd.arg(p);
17515        }
17516        StreamTarget::Growing(p) => {
17517            if mpv {
17518                cmd.args(MPV_LIVE_FLAGS);
17519                cmd.arg(format!("appending://{}", fwd_slashes(p)));
17520            } else {
17521                cmd.arg(p);
17522            }
17523        }
17524        StreamTarget::SplitAv(parts) => {
17525            // Gated to mpv by playable_with; build the mpv form regardless.
17526            // Largest file (video) is the main file; the rest join as
17527            // external audio tracks. Each source is its own appending://
17528            // demuxer, so readahead and growth-following work normally
17529            // (unlike an edl:// merge, which starves the video stream).
17530            cmd.args(MPV_LIVE_FLAGS);
17531            let mut parts = parts.iter();
17532            if let Some(main) = parts.next() {
17533                cmd.arg(format!("appending://{}", fwd_slashes(main)));
17534            }
17535            for p in parts {
17536                cmd.arg(format!("--audio-file=appending://{}", fwd_slashes(p)));
17537            }
17538        }
17539    }
17540    cmd
17541}
17542
17543/// Log and spawn a player/downloader command built for "play new instance",
17544/// returning a status-bar error message on spawn failure.
17545fn spawn_logged(mut cmd: std::process::Command, what: &str) -> Option<String> {
17546    let line = format!(
17547        "{} {}",
17548        cmd.get_program().to_string_lossy(),
17549        cmd.get_args().map(|a| a.to_string_lossy()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(" ")
17550    );
17551    tracing::info!(%line, "play-new-instance: spawning {what}");
17552    match cmd.spawn() {
17553        Ok(_) => None,
17554        Err(e) => {
17555            warn!(%line, "play-new-instance: failed to spawn {what}: {e}");
17556            Some(format!("Failed to launch {what}: {e}"))
17557        }
17558    }
17559}
17560
17561/// Root for throwaway live-edge preview downloads (see [`spawn_live_preview`]).
17562fn preview_root() -> std::path::PathBuf {
17563    std::env::temp_dir().join("streamarchiver-preview")
17564}
17565
17566/// Best-effort sweep of preview dirs older than a day (leftovers from previews
17567/// orphaned by an app exit — their downloader dies when the stream ends, but
17568/// the files linger until this runs on the next preview).
17569fn sweep_stale_previews() {
17570    let cutoff = std::time::SystemTime::now() - std::time::Duration::from_secs(24 * 3600);
17571    let Ok(rd) = std::fs::read_dir(preview_root()) else { return };
17572    for entry in rd.flatten() {
17573        if entry.metadata().and_then(|m| m.modified()).map(|t| t < cutoff).unwrap_or(false) {
17574            let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(entry.path());
17575        }
17576    }
17577}
17578
17579/// Spawn a throwaway live-edge download into a temp dir and open its growing
17580/// capture in the player once it buffers — "tune in now" for YouTube streams.
17581///
17582/// This is the only viable live-edge path for SABR-only streams: they can't be
17583/// piped to stdout (yt-dlp PR #13515), stock yt-dlp sees no formats for a
17584/// player's URL handler, and seeking to the end of the main recording's
17585/// growing cue-less MKV means a multi-GB linear scan. A fresh live-edge
17586/// download's files BEGIN at the edge, so the player just plays from 0.
17587///
17588/// A watcher thread polls the temp dir, launches the player when a playable
17589/// target appears, waits for the player to exit, then kills the downloader
17590/// tree and deletes the temp dir. If the app exits first the downloader is
17591/// orphaned but self-limiting (it dies when the stream ends); leftovers are
17592/// swept by [`sweep_stale_previews`].
17593fn spawn_live_preview(
17594    row: &crate::models::MonitorWithChannel,
17595    player: &str,
17596    settings: &SettingsForm,
17597    store: &crate::store::Store,
17598) -> Option<String> {
17599    use crate::downloader::{load_ytdlp_bins, resolve_auth, sabr_preview_args, split_args, youtube_live_url, AuthSource};
17600    use crate::models::Platform;
17601
17602    let m = &row.monitor;
17603    sweep_stale_previews();
17604
17605    let bins = load_ytdlp_bins(store);
17606    let use_sabr = m.platform() == Platform::YouTube && bins.sabr.usable();
17607    if use_sabr && !player_is_mpv(player) {
17608        return Some("Live-edge preview of SABR streams requires mpv as the media player".into());
17609    }
17610
17611    let tmp = preview_root().join(format!("{}-{}", m.id, crate::models::now_unix()));
17612    let cache = tmp.join(".cache");
17613    if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(&cache) {
17614        return Some(format!("Failed to create preview dir: {e}"));
17615    }
17616
17617    // The settings form splits browser and profile; downloads need the
17618    // composed "browser:profile" form (a bare "firefox" would hit the
17619    // default profile, not the one holding the YouTube login).
17620    let cookies = compose_browser_profile(&settings.cookies_browser, &settings.cookies_profile);
17621    let auth = resolve_auth(row, &settings.download_auth_method, &cookies);
17622    let extra = split_args(&m.extra_args);
17623    let global_args = split_args(&settings.ytdlp_default_args);
17624    // Downloader writes into <tmp>\.cache\preview.*, matching the app's capture
17625    // convention so stream_target_for_active(<tmp>\preview.mkv) finds it.
17626    let probe_path = tmp.join("preview.mkv");
17627    let (program, args) = if use_sabr {
17628        (
17629            bins.sabr.binary.clone(),
17630            sabr_preview_args(&cache.join("preview.mkv"), &auth, &global_args, &bins.sabr, &extra, &m.url),
17631        )
17632    } else {
17633        let mut args = vec![
17634            "--no-part".to_string(),
17635            "--hls-use-mpegts".into(),
17636            "-o".into(),
17637            cache.join("preview.ts").to_string_lossy().into_owned(),
17638            "--no-live-from-start".into(),
17639        ];
17640        match &auth {
17641            AuthSource::CookiesBrowser(b) => {
17642                args.push("--cookies-from-browser".into());
17643                args.push(b.clone());
17644            }
17645            AuthSource::CookiesFile(p) => {
17646                args.push("--cookies".into());
17647                args.push(p.clone());
17648            }
17649            _ => {}
17650        }
17651        args.extend(global_args);
17652        if m.platform() == Platform::YouTube && !bins.sabr.pot_args.is_empty() {
17653            args.push("--extractor-args".into());
17654            args.push(bins.sabr.pot_args.clone());
17655        }
17656        args.extend(extra);
17657        args.push(if m.platform() == Platform::YouTube {
17658            youtube_live_url(&m.url)
17659        } else {
17660            m.url.clone()
17661        });
17662        (bins.system_program(), args)
17663    };
17664
17665    let log_path = tmp.join("preview.log");
17666    let (log_out, log_err) = match std::fs::File::create(&log_path)
17667        .and_then(|f| Ok((f.try_clone()?, f)))
17668    {
17669        Ok(pair) => pair,
17670        Err(e) => {
17671            let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&tmp);
17672            return Some(format!("Failed to create preview log: {e}"));
17673        }
17674    };
17675    let line = format!("{program} {}", args.join(" "));
17676    tracing::info!(%line, "live-preview: spawning downloader");
17677    let mut dl = match std::process::Command::new(&program)
17678        .args(&args)
17679        .stdout(log_out)
17680        .stderr(log_err)
17681        .spawn()
17682    {
17683        Ok(c) => c,
17684        Err(e) => {
17685            let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&tmp);
17686            warn!(%line, "live-preview: failed to spawn downloader: {e}");
17687            return Some(format!("Failed to launch downloader for live preview: {e}"));
17688        }
17689    };
17690
17691    let msg = format!(
17692        "Starting live-edge preview of {} — the player opens once the stream buffers (~10-30 s)",
17693        row.channel.name
17694    );
17695    let player = player.to_string();
17696    let channel = row.channel.name.clone();
17697    std::thread::spawn(move || {
17698        let cleanup = |dl: &mut std::process::Child, tmp: &std::path::Path| {
17699            let pid = dl.id().to_string();
17700            let _ = std::process::Command::new("taskkill")
17701                .args(["/T", "/F", "/PID", &pid])
17702                .stdout(std::process::Stdio::null())
17703                .stderr(std::process::Stdio::null())
17704                .status();
17705            let _ = dl.kill(); // fallback; no-op if taskkill got it
17706            let _ = dl.wait();
17707            for _ in 0..10 {
17708                if std::fs::remove_dir_all(tmp).is_ok() {
17709                    break;
17710                }
17711                std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(500));
17712            }
17713        };
17714        // Lossy read: yt-dlp's console output isn't guaranteed UTF-8.
17715        let log_tail = |tmp: &std::path::Path| -> String {
17716            let bytes = std::fs::read(tmp.join("preview.log")).unwrap_or_default();
17717            let s = String::from_utf8_lossy(&bytes);
17718            let cut = s.char_indices().rev().nth(599).map(|(i, _)| i).unwrap_or(0);
17719            s[cut..].to_string()
17720        };
17721        // What the downloader has produced so far (true handle sizes — the
17722        // dir-entry sizes are stale for open files), for timeout diagnostics.
17723        let cache_listing = |tmp: &std::path::Path| -> String {
17724            let Ok(rd) = std::fs::read_dir(tmp.join(".cache")) else { return String::new() };
17725            rd.flatten()
17726                .map(|e| {
17727                    let len = live_file_len(&e.path()).unwrap_or(0);
17728                    format!("{} ({len} B)", e.file_name().to_string_lossy())
17729                })
17730                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
17731                .join(", ")
17732        };
17733
17734        // Wait for a playable target: SABR needs both A/V parts (SplitAv), but
17735        // settle for a single growing file if no second part shows up shortly.
17736        let mut growing_since: Option<std::time::Instant> = None;
17737        let mut target: Option<StreamTarget> = None;
17738        let probe = probe_path.to_string_lossy().into_owned();
17739        for _ in 0..240 {
17740            if let Ok(Some(status)) = dl.try_wait() {
17741                warn!(
17742                    %channel,
17743                    %status,
17744                    tail = %log_tail(&tmp),
17745                    "live-preview: downloader exited before producing a playable stream"
17746                );
17747                cleanup(&mut dl, &tmp);
17748                return;
17749            }
17750            match stream_target_for_active(&probe).filter(|t| playable_with(t, &player)) {
17751                Some(t @ StreamTarget::SplitAv(_)) => {
17752                    target = Some(t);
17753                    break;
17754                }
17755                Some(t) => {
17756                    let since = *growing_since.get_or_insert_with(std::time::Instant::now);
17757                    if !use_sabr || since.elapsed() >= std::time::Duration::from_secs(4) {
17758                        target = Some(t);
17759                        break;
17760                    }
17761                }
17762                None => growing_since = None,
17763            }
17764            std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(500));
17765        }
17766        let Some(target) = target else {
17767            warn!(
17768                %channel,
17769                cache = %cache_listing(&tmp),
17770                tail = %log_tail(&tmp),
17771                "live-preview: no playable stream within 2 minutes"
17772            );
17773            cleanup(&mut dl, &tmp);
17774            return;
17775        };
17776        tracing::info!(%channel, ?target, "live-preview: buffered, launching player");
17777        // Split SABR previews are served through a generated live HLS playlist
17778        // — the only transport that follows the growing files at the live edge
17779        // indefinitely (appending:// latches EOF after one lost race against
17780        // the segment cadence). Non-ISOBMFF variants and single files fall
17781        // back to direct appending:// playback.
17782        let launched: Option<(std::process::Child, Option<crate::hls_preview::HlsPreview>)> =
17783            (|| {
17784                if let StreamTarget::SplitAv(parts) = &target
17785                    && parts.len() >= 2
17786                    && let Some(mut hp) =
17787                        crate::hls_preview::HlsPreview::open(&parts[0], &parts[1], &tmp)
17788                {
17789                    // Playlists need ≥2 coalesced segments per track
17790                    // (~10 s of media) before there's anything to play.
17791                    let mut ready = hp.tick(false);
17792                    for _ in 0..60 {
17793                        if ready {
17794                            break;
17795                        }
17796                        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1));
17797                        ready = hp.tick(false);
17798                    }
17799                    if ready {
17800                        tracing::info!(%channel, "live-preview: serving live HLS playlist");
17801                        let mut cmd = std::process::Command::new(&player);
17802                        cmd.args(MPV_LIVE_FLAGS);
17803                        // Segments end in ".part", which lavf's HLS demuxer
17804                        // blocks by default for local files.
17805                        cmd.arg("--demuxer-lavf-o=allowed_extensions=ALL");
17806                        cmd.arg(fwd_slashes(&hp.master_path()));
17807                        match cmd.spawn() {
17808                            Ok(p) => return Some((p, Some(hp))),
17809                            Err(e) => {
17810                                warn!(%channel, "live-preview: failed to launch player: {e}");
17811                                return None;
17812                            }
17813                        }
17814                    }
17815                    warn!(%channel, "live-preview: HLS playlists not ready in time, falling back to appending://");
17816                }
17817                match build_player_command(&player, &target).spawn() {
17818                    Ok(p) => Some((p, None)),
17819                    Err(e) => {
17820                        warn!(%channel, "live-preview: failed to launch player: {e}");
17821                        None
17822                    }
17823                }
17824            })();
17825        match launched {
17826            Some((mut p, Some(mut hp))) => {
17827                // Keep the playlists fresh while the player runs. When the
17828                // preview download ends (stream over / killed), write final
17829                // playlists with ENDLIST so the player finishes cleanly
17830                // instead of polling forever.
17831                let mut dl_ended = false;
17832                loop {
17833                    if !matches!(p.try_wait(), Ok(None)) {
17834                        break;
17835                    }
17836                    if !dl_ended && !matches!(dl.try_wait(), Ok(None)) {
17837                        dl_ended = true;
17838                        hp.tick(true);
17839                    } else if !dl_ended {
17840                        hp.tick(false);
17841                    }
17842                    std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(2));
17843                }
17844                tracing::info!(%channel, "live-preview: player closed, stopping preview download");
17845            }
17846            Some((mut p, None)) => {
17847                let _ = p.wait();
17848                tracing::info!(%channel, "live-preview: player closed, stopping preview download");
17849            }
17850            None => {}
17851        }
17852        cleanup(&mut dl, &tmp);
17853    });
17854
17855    Some(msg)
17856}
17857
17858/// Spawn a "play new instance" command — tunes into the stream at the LIVE
17859/// EDGE in the configured media player, without recording. (⏵ "Stream in
17860/// player" is the from-start counterpart: it opens the in-progress capture.)
17861/// Returns a status-bar message to show the user, or `None`.
17862///
17863/// - Streamlink: `--player <path>` routes output to the player (live edge).
17864/// - yt-dlp + YouTube: throwaway live-edge preview download, see
17865///   [`spawn_live_preview`] — SABR-only streams can't be piped or URL-played.
17866/// - yt-dlp + other platforms (Kick): pipes `-o -` stdout to the player's
17867///   stdin (from the live edge — from-start capture can't pipe).
17868/// - ffmpeg source: passes the URL directly.
17869fn spawn_play_new_instance(
17870    row: &crate::models::MonitorWithChannel,
17871    player: &str,
17872    settings: &SettingsForm,
17873    store: &crate::store::Store,
17874) -> Option<String> {
17875    use crate::downloader::{
17876        push_track_args, resolve_auth, resolved_quality, split_args, AuthSource,
17877    };
17878    use crate::models::{Platform, Tool};
17879
17880    let m = &row.monitor;
17881    // The settings form splits browser and profile; downloads need the
17882    // composed "browser:profile" form (a bare "firefox" would hit the
17883    // default profile, not the one holding the YouTube login).
17884    let cookies = compose_browser_profile(&settings.cookies_browser, &settings.cookies_profile);
17885    let auth = resolve_auth(row, &settings.download_auth_method, &cookies);
17886    let extra: Vec<String> = split_args(&m.extra_args);
17887
17888    match m.tool {
17889        Tool::Streamlink => {
17890            let mut args: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
17891            if m.platform() == Platform::Twitch {
17892                args.push("--twitch-supported-codecs=h264,h265,av1".into());
17893                if let AuthSource::Token(ref t) = auth {
17894                    args.push(format!("--twitch-api-header=Authorization=OAuth {t}"));
17895                }
17896            }
17897            // No --hls-live-restart even for from-start monitors: ▷ means
17898            // "tune in at the live edge"; ⏵ covers from-start viewing.
17899            args.push("--retry-streams".into());
17900            args.push("3".into());
17901            args.push("--retry-max".into());
17902            args.push("5".into());
17903            push_track_args(&mut args, Tool::Streamlink, &m.audio_tracks, &m.subtitle_tracks, false);
17904            args.extend(extra);
17905            args.push("--player".into());
17906            args.push(player.to_string());
17907            args.push(m.url.clone());
17908            args.push(resolved_quality(&m.quality));
17909            let mut cmd = std::process::Command::new("streamlink");
17910            cmd.args(&args);
17911            spawn_logged(cmd, "streamlink")
17912        }
17913        Tool::YtDlp if m.platform() == Platform::YouTube => {
17914            spawn_live_preview(row, player, settings, store)
17915        }
17916        Tool::YtDlp => {
17917            let ytdlp_bin = if settings.ytdlp_binary_path.trim().is_empty() {
17918                "yt-dlp".to_string()
17919            } else {
17920                settings.ytdlp_binary_path.trim().to_string()
17921            };
17922            let global_args = split_args(&settings.ytdlp_default_args);
17923            let mut args = vec![
17924                "--no-part".to_string(),
17925                "--hls-use-mpegts".into(),
17926                "-o".into(),
17927                "-".into(),
17928                // From-start needs fragment merging, which can't pipe — a new
17929                // player instance always starts at the live edge.
17930                "--no-live-from-start".into(),
17931            ];
17932            match &auth {
17933                AuthSource::CookiesBrowser(b) => {
17934                    args.push("--cookies-from-browser".into());
17935                    args.push(b.clone());
17936                }
17937                AuthSource::CookiesFile(p) => {
17938                    args.push("--cookies".into());
17939                    args.push(p.clone());
17940                }
17941                _ => {}
17942            }
17943            args.extend(global_args);
17944            push_track_args(&mut args, Tool::YtDlp, &m.audio_tracks, &m.subtitle_tracks, false);
17945            args.extend(extra);
17946            args.push(m.url.clone());
17947            use std::process::Stdio;
17948            let line = format!("{ytdlp_bin} {}", args.join(" "));
17949            tracing::info!(%line, "play-new-instance: spawning yt-dlp pipe");
17950            match std::process::Command::new(&ytdlp_bin)
17951                .args(&args)
17952                .stdout(Stdio::piped())
17953                .stderr(Stdio::null())
17954                .spawn()
17955            {
17956                Ok(mut child) => {
17957                    let pipe = child.stdout.take()?;
17958                    let mut cmd = std::process::Command::new(player);
17959                    cmd.arg("-").stdin(Stdio::from(pipe));
17960                    spawn_logged(cmd, "media player")
17961                }
17962                Err(e) => {
17963                    warn!(%line, "play-new-instance: failed to spawn yt-dlp: {e}");
17964                    Some(format!("Failed to launch yt-dlp: {e}"))
17965                }
17966            }
17967        }
17968        Tool::Ffmpeg => {
17969            let mut cmd = std::process::Command::new(player);
17970            cmd.arg(&m.url);
17971            spawn_logged(cmd, "media player")
17972        }
17973    }
17974}
17975
17976/// Copy an image file's raw bytes to the Windows clipboard under the `PNG` format.
17977/// Most apps (Discord, browsers, image editors) accept `CF_PNG` for paste.
17978fn copy_emote_image_to_clipboard(path: &std::path::Path) {
17979    use windows::Win32::System::DataExchange::{
17980        CloseClipboard, EmptyClipboard, OpenClipboard, RegisterClipboardFormatW, SetClipboardData,
17981    };
17982    use windows::Win32::System::Memory::{GlobalAlloc, GlobalLock, GlobalUnlock, GMEM_MOVEABLE};
17983
17984    let Ok(bytes) = std::fs::read(path) else { return };
17985
17986    let fmt_name: Vec<u16> = "PNG\0".encode_utf16().collect();
17987    let fmt = unsafe { RegisterClipboardFormatW(windows::core::PCWSTR(fmt_name.as_ptr())) };
17988    if fmt == 0 {
17989        return;
17990    }
17991
17992    unsafe {
17993        let Ok(hmem) = GlobalAlloc(GMEM_MOVEABLE, bytes.len()) else { return };
17994        let ptr = GlobalLock(hmem);
17995        if ptr.is_null() {
17996            return;
17997        }
17998        std::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(bytes.as_ptr(), ptr as *mut u8, bytes.len());
17999        let _ = GlobalUnlock(hmem);
18000
18001        if OpenClipboard(None).is_ok() {
18002            let _ = EmptyClipboard();
18003            // SetClipboardData takes ownership of hmem on success; do not free it.
18004            let _ = SetClipboardData(
18005                fmt,
18006                Some(windows::Win32::Foundation::HANDLE(hmem.0 as *mut std::ffi::c_void)),
18007            );
18008            let _ = CloseClipboard();
18009        }
18010    }
18011}
18012
18013/// Lay out a provider's emotes as a wrapping grid of fixed-width cells: the emote
18014/// image above its code. `deprecated` cells skip the image entirely (the file is
18015/// gone) — they show a 🚫 placeholder and strike through the code. Loading cells
18016/// show a `…` until the off-thread decode lands.
18017#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
18018fn emote_viewer_grid(
18019    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
18020    emotes: &[ViewerEmote],
18021    cache: &Mutex<HashMap<std::path::PathBuf, crate::emote_anim::EmoteLoad>>,
18022    animate: bool,
18023    now: f64,
18024    misses: &mut Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
18025    ctx: &egui::Context,
18026    deprecated: bool,
18027    provider: EmoteProvider,
18028    pending_properties: &mut Option<ViewerEmote>,
18029) {
18030    const CELL_W: f32 = 92.0;
18031    const IMG_H: f32 = 44.0;
18032    ui.horizontal_wrapped(|ui| {
18033        for e in emotes {
18034            let cell = ui.allocate_ui(egui::vec2(CELL_W, IMG_H + 22.0), |ui| {
18035                // Virtualize: only decode/upload/draw emotes whose cell is on screen.
18036                // `draw_cached_emote` stamps `last_drawn = now` on every Ready entry it
18037                // touches, which pins it against `evict_emote_cache` (it keeps anything
18038                // with `last_drawn >= now`). Drawing every emote each frame would pin the
18039                // entire provider — hundreds of animated emotes — past EMOTE_BUDGET_BYTES
18040                // and the LRU could never reclaim it. Off-screen cells reserve the same
18041                // band height (so wrap points / scroll extent stay put) but skip the cache
18042                // entirely, letting scrolled-away emotes age out and be evicted.
18043                let visible = ui.is_rect_visible(ui.max_rect());
18044                ui.vertical_centered(|ui| {
18045                    let img_resp = if deprecated {
18046                        ui.add_space((IMG_H - 18.0) / 2.0);
18047                        ui.label(egui::RichText::new("🚫").size(18.0).weak());
18048                        ui.add_space((IMG_H - 18.0) / 2.0);
18049                        None
18050                    } else if !visible {
18051                        ui.add_space(IMG_H);
18052                        None
18053                    } else {
18054                        let r = draw_cached_emote(ui, cache, &e.path, animate, IMG_H, now, misses, ctx);
18055                        if r.is_none() {
18056                            ui.add_space(IMG_H / 2.0 - 6.0);
18057                            ui.weak("…");
18058                            ui.add_space(IMG_H / 2.0 - 6.0);
18059                        }
18060                        r
18061                    };
18062
18063                    // Alt-hover: show enlarged image + emote info as a tooltip.
18064                    // on_hover_ui_at_pointer takes self; clone the response so
18065                    // img_resp stays usable for the label below.
18066                    if let Some(resp) = img_resp.clone() {
18067                        if resp.hovered() && ctx.input(|i| i.modifiers.alt) {
18068                            let (epath, ename, eid, eext) = (
18069                                e.path.clone(),
18070                                e.name.clone(),
18071                                e.id.clone(),
18072                                e.ext.clone(),
18073                            );
18074                            resp.on_hover_ui_at_pointer(|ui| {
18075                                ui.set_max_width(280.0);
18076                                // Render cached texture at 3-4× cell size.
18077                                // The cache caps decode at 56 px so no re-upload.
18078                                draw_cached_emote(
18079                                    ui, cache, &epath, false, 160.0, now,
18080                                    &mut Vec::new(), ctx,
18081                                );
18082                                ui.separator();
18083                                let url = emote_cdn_url(provider, &eid, &eext);
18084                                egui::Grid::new(
18085                                    egui::Id::new("alt_emote_tip").with(&eid),
18086                                )
18087                                .num_columns(2)
18088                                .show(ui, |ui| {
18089                                    ui.label("Name:");
18090                                    ui.label(&ename);
18091                                    ui.end_row();
18092                                    ui.label("ID:");
18093                                    ui.label(&eid);
18094                                    ui.end_row();
18095                                    ui.label("URL:");
18096                                    ui.label(&url);
18097                                    ui.end_row();
18098                                });
18099                            });
18100                        }
18101                    }
18102
18103                    let mut rt = egui::RichText::new(truncate_label(&e.name, 12)).small();
18104                    if deprecated {
18105                        rt = rt.strikethrough().weak();
18106                    }
18107                    ui.label(rt).on_hover_text(&e.name);
18108                });
18109            });
18110
18111            // Right-click context menu on the entire cell.
18112            // allocate_ui returns Sense::hover(), which makes secondary_clicked()
18113            // always false and context_menu never fires. Re-interact with Sense::click()
18114            // on the same rect so the right-click is detected properly.
18115            let ctx_resp = ui.interact(
18116                cell.response.rect,
18117                egui::Id::new("emote_ctx").with(&e.id),
18118                egui::Sense::click(),
18119            );
18120            ctx_resp.context_menu(|ui| {
18121                if ui.button("Copy Image").clicked() {
18122                    copy_emote_image_to_clipboard(&e.path);
18123                    ui.close();
18124                }
18125                if ui.button("Open File").clicked() {
18126                    open_path(&e.path);
18127                    ui.close();
18128                }
18129                if ui.button("Open Folder").clicked() {
18130                    if let Some(dir) = e.path.parent() {
18131                        open_path(dir);
18132                    }
18133                    ui.close();
18134                }
18135                if ui.button("Copy URL").clicked() {
18136                    ui.ctx().copy_text(emote_cdn_url(provider, &e.id, &e.ext));
18137                    ui.close();
18138                }
18139                ui.separator();
18140                if ui.button("Properties").clicked() {
18141                    *pending_properties = Some(ViewerEmote {
18142                        name: e.name.clone(),
18143                        id: e.id.clone(),
18144                        ext: e.ext.clone(),
18145                        path: e.path.clone(),
18146                        exists: e.exists,
18147                    });
18148                    ui.close();
18149                }
18150            });
18151        }
18152    });
18153}
18154
18155/// Draw an emote from the decode cache. Returns the image `Response` when drawn, or
18156/// `None` (caller shows the text fallback) when the emote is still loading / failed.
18157/// Promotes a freshly-decoded entry to GPU textures (UI-thread upload), advances
18158/// the animation against the global clock `now`, and records `last_drawn` for LRU.
18159#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
18160fn draw_cached_emote(
18161    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
18162    cache: &Mutex<HashMap<std::path::PathBuf, crate::emote_anim::EmoteLoad>>,
18163    path: &Path,
18164    animate: bool,
18165    emote_h: f32,
18166    now: f64,
18167    misses: &mut Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
18168    ctx: &egui::Context,
18169) -> Option<egui::Response> {
18170    use crate::emote_anim::EmoteLoad;
18171    let mut g = cache.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
18172    // Promote Decoded → Ready by uploading the frames to GPU textures here (must be
18173    // on the UI thread / with a live `ctx`).
18174    if matches!(g.get(path), Some(EmoteLoad::Decoded(..))) {
18175        if let Some(EmoteLoad::Decoded(imgs, delays)) = g.remove(path) {
18176            let anim = crate::emote_anim::upload(imgs, delays, ctx, &path.to_string_lossy());
18177            g.insert(path.to_path_buf(), EmoteLoad::Ready(anim));
18178        }
18179    }
18180    match g.get_mut(path) {
18181        None => {
18182            g.insert(path.to_path_buf(), EmoteLoad::Loading);
18183            misses.push(path.to_path_buf());
18184            None
18185        }
18186        Some(EmoteLoad::Loading) | Some(EmoteLoad::Failed) | Some(EmoteLoad::Decoded(..)) => None,
18187        Some(EmoteLoad::Ready(anim)) => {
18188            anim.last_drawn = now;
18189            let s = anim.size();
18190            // Height ≤ emote_h, width capped at 112, aspect preserved. Never upscale
18191            // (`.min(1.0)`) — a small emote keeps its native size, matching the prior
18192            // loader behaviour. `s` is already downscaled to ≤56px at decode time.
18193            let scale = (emote_h / s.y.max(1.0)).min(112.0 / s.x.max(1.0)).min(1.0);
18194            let size = egui::vec2(s.x * scale, s.y * scale);
18195            if animate && anim.is_animated() {
18196                let (tex, remaining) = anim.frame_at(now);
18197                let resp = ui.add(egui::Image::from_texture(tex).fit_to_exact_size(size));
18198                // Only schedule the next frame for emotes actually on screen, so a
18199                // scrolled-away animation doesn't keep waking the UI.
18200                if ui.is_rect_visible(resp.rect) {
18201                    ctx.request_repaint_after(std::time::Duration::from_secs_f32(
18202                        remaining.min(1.0),
18203                    ));
18204                }
18205                Some(resp)
18206            } else {
18207                let (tex, _) = anim.frame_at(0.0);
18208                Some(ui.add(egui::Image::from_texture(tex).fit_to_exact_size(size)))
18209            }
18210        }
18211    }
18212}
18213
18214/// Evict the least-recently-drawn ready emotes once the decoded-frame cache exceeds
18215/// [`EMOTE_BUDGET_BYTES`]. Emotes drawn this frame (`last_drawn == now`) are kept.
18216fn evict_emote_cache(
18217    cache: &Mutex<HashMap<std::path::PathBuf, crate::emote_anim::EmoteLoad>>,
18218    now: f64,
18219) {
18220    use crate::emote_anim::EmoteLoad;
18221    let mut g = cache.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
18222    let total: usize = g
18223        .values()
18224        .map(|v| if let EmoteLoad::Ready(a) = v { a.bytes } else { 0 })
18225        .sum();
18226    if total <= EMOTE_BUDGET_BYTES {
18227        return;
18228    }
18229    let mut ready: Vec<(std::path::PathBuf, f64, usize)> = g
18230        .iter()
18231        .filter_map(|(k, v)| match v {
18232            EmoteLoad::Ready(a) => Some((k.clone(), a.last_drawn, a.bytes)),
18233            _ => None,
18234        })
18235        .collect();
18236    ready.sort_by(|a, b| a.1.partial_cmp(&b.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));
18237    let mut cur = total;
18238    for (k, last_drawn, bytes) in ready {
18239        if cur <= EMOTE_BUDGET_BYTES {
18240            break;
18241        }
18242        if last_drawn >= now {
18243            continue; // visible this frame — keep
18244        }
18245        g.remove(&k);
18246        cur -= bytes;
18247    }
18248}
18249
18250fn badge_display(badge: &str, platform: &ChatPlatform) -> (&'static str, egui::Color32) {
18251    match platform {
18252        ChatPlatform::Twitch => {
18253            let name = badge.split('/').next().unwrap_or(badge);
18254            match name {
18255                "broadcaster" => ("📡", egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xe9, 0x1e, 0x63)),
18256                "moderator" | "mod" => ("⚔", egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0xad, 0x03)),
18257                "subscriber" => ("★", egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x96, 0x4b, 0xff)),
18258                "bits" => ("💎", egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0xc7, 0xac)),
18259                "premium" => ("👑", egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xff, 0xd7, 0x00)),
18260                "partner" => ("✓", egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x97, 0x45, 0xff)),
18261                _ => ("•", egui::Color32::GRAY),
18262            }
18263        }
18264        ChatPlatform::YouTube => {
18265            let lower = badge.to_lowercase();
18266            if lower.contains("member") {
18267                ("⭐", egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xff, 0xd7, 0x00))
18268            } else if lower.contains("moderator") {
18269                ("⚔", egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0xad, 0x03))
18270            } else if lower.contains("verified") || lower.contains("owner") {
18271                ("✓", egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x4a, 0xc2, 0xff))
18272            } else {
18273                ("•", egui::Color32::GRAY)
18274            }
18275        }
18276    }
18277}
18278
18279/// The display colour for a chat author's name, adjusted to stay legible on the
18280/// chat panel's background `bg`. The base colour mirrors each platform: a Twitch
18281/// user's chosen USERCOLOR (or their deterministic default from Twitch's 15-colour
18282/// palette), and YouTube's role-based name colours (mod/member/owner/regular).
18283fn chat_username_color(msg: &ChatMessage, bg: egui::Color32) -> egui::Color32 {
18284    let base = match (msg.color_override, &msg.platform) {
18285        // Twitch USERCOLOR (IRC `color` tag), used as-is by both platforms when set.
18286        (Some(c), _) => c,
18287        (None, ChatPlatform::Twitch) => twitch_username_color(&msg.author),
18288        (None, ChatPlatform::YouTube) => youtube_username_color(&msg.badges),
18289    };
18290    readable_color(base, bg)
18291}
18292
18293/// Twitch's 15 default name colours, assigned to users who never picked one.
18294/// Twitch keys this off the name (first + last char), so the same user is always
18295/// the same colour — we reproduce that exactly for ASCII names.
18296fn twitch_username_color(name: &str) -> egui::Color32 {
18297    const DEFAULTS: [egui::Color32; 15] = [
18298        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xFF, 0x00, 0x00), // Red
18299        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0x00, 0xFF), // Blue
18300        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0x80, 0x00), // Green
18301        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xB2, 0x22, 0x22), // FireBrick
18302        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xFF, 0x7F, 0x50), // Coral
18303        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x9A, 0xCD, 0x32), // YellowGreen
18304        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xFF, 0x45, 0x00), // OrangeRed
18305        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x2E, 0x8B, 0x57), // SeaGreen
18306        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xDA, 0xA5, 0x20), // GoldenRod
18307        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xD2, 0x69, 0x1E), // Chocolate
18308        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x5F, 0x9E, 0xA0), // CadetBlue
18309        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x1E, 0x90, 0xFF), // DodgerBlue
18310        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xFF, 0x69, 0xB4), // HotPink
18311        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x8A, 0x2B, 0xE2), // BlueViolet
18312        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0xFF, 0x7F), // SpringGreen
18313    ];
18314    let b = name.as_bytes();
18315    if b.is_empty() {
18316        return egui::Color32::GRAY;
18317    }
18318    let n = (b[0] as usize + b[b.len() - 1] as usize) % DEFAULTS.len();
18319    DEFAULTS[n]
18320}
18321
18322/// YouTube live-chat name colours by role (derived from the author's badges):
18323/// moderator blue, member green, owner gold, and a neutral grey for everyone else
18324/// (YouTube doesn't per-user colour regular names). Readability is applied later.
18325fn youtube_username_color(badges: &[String]) -> egui::Color32 {
18326    let has = |needle: &str| badges.iter().any(|b| b.to_lowercase().contains(needle));
18327    if has("owner") {
18328        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xFF, 0xD6, 0x00) // channel owner — gold
18329    } else if has("moderator") {
18330        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x5E, 0x84, 0xF1) // YouTube moderator blue
18331    } else if has("member") {
18332        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x2B, 0xA6, 0x40) // YouTube member green
18333    } else {
18334        egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xB0, 0xB0, 0xB0) // regular — neutral grey
18335    }
18336}
18337
18338/// WCAG relative luminance of a colour (sRGB → linear, then the standard weights).
18339fn relative_luminance(c: egui::Color32) -> f32 {
18340    let lin = |v: u8| {
18341        let s = v as f32 / 255.0;
18342        if s <= 0.03928 {
18343            s / 12.92
18344        } else {
18345            ((s + 0.055) / 1.055).powf(2.4)
18346        }
18347    };
18348    0.2126 * lin(c.r()) + 0.7152 * lin(c.g()) + 0.0722 * lin(c.b())
18349}
18350
18351/// WCAG contrast ratio between two colours (1.0 = identical, 21.0 = black/white).
18352fn contrast_ratio(a: egui::Color32, b: egui::Color32) -> f32 {
18353    let (la, lb) = (relative_luminance(a), relative_luminance(b));
18354    let (hi, lo) = if la >= lb { (la, lb) } else { (lb, la) };
18355    (hi + 0.05) / (lo + 0.05)
18356}
18357
18358/// Nudge `fg`'s lightness away from the background (lighter on a dark bg, darker on
18359/// a light bg) until it clears a contrast floor, preserving hue — the way Twitch
18360/// lightens dark name colours in dark mode so e.g. pure blue stays legible. Returns
18361/// `fg` unchanged when it's already comfortable.
18362fn readable_color(fg: egui::Color32, bg: egui::Color32) -> egui::Color32 {
18363    // Slightly under WCAG AA (4.5): names are bold, and staying closer keeps the
18364    // colour vivid rather than washing it toward white/black.
18365    const TARGET: f32 = 4.0;
18366    if contrast_ratio(fg, bg) >= TARGET {
18367        return fg;
18368    }
18369    // Push toward whichever extreme can actually out-contrast the background, not a
18370    // flat luminance midpoint — for a mid-tone background, lightening toward white
18371    // may never reach the target while darkening toward black does (and vice-versa).
18372    let lighten = contrast_ratio(egui::Color32::WHITE, bg) >= contrast_ratio(egui::Color32::BLACK, bg);
18373    let (h, s, mut l) = rgb_to_hsl(fg);
18374    let mut out = fg;
18375    for _ in 0..50 {
18376        l = if lighten { (l + 0.02).min(1.0) } else { (l - 0.02).max(0.0) };
18377        out = hsl_to_rgb(h, s, l);
18378        if contrast_ratio(out, bg) >= TARGET {
18379            return out;
18380        }
18381        if l <= 0.0 || l >= 1.0 {
18382            break; // can't push further; return the best we reached
18383        }
18384    }
18385    out
18386}
18387
18388/// sRGB → HSL (hue degrees 0–360, saturation/lightness 0–1).
18389fn rgb_to_hsl(c: egui::Color32) -> (f32, f32, f32) {
18390    let (r, g, b) = (
18391        c.r() as f32 / 255.0,
18392        c.g() as f32 / 255.0,
18393        c.b() as f32 / 255.0,
18394    );
18395    let max = r.max(g).max(b);
18396    let min = r.min(g).min(b);
18397    let l = (max + min) / 2.0;
18398    let d = max - min;
18399    if d < 1e-6 {
18400        return (0.0, 0.0, l); // achromatic (grey)
18401    }
18402    let s = d / (1.0 - (2.0 * l - 1.0).abs());
18403    let h = if max == r {
18404        ((g - b) / d).rem_euclid(6.0)
18405    } else if max == g {
18406        (b - r) / d + 2.0
18407    } else {
18408        (r - g) / d + 4.0
18409    };
18410    ((h * 60.0).rem_euclid(360.0), s, l)
18411}
18412
18413/// HSL → sRGB (inverse of [`rgb_to_hsl`]).
18414fn hsl_to_rgb(h: f32, s: f32, l: f32) -> egui::Color32 {
18415    let c = (1.0 - (2.0 * l - 1.0).abs()) * s;
18416    let hp = h / 60.0;
18417    let x = c * (1.0 - (hp.rem_euclid(2.0) - 1.0).abs());
18418    let (r1, g1, b1) = match hp as i32 {
18419        0 => (c, x, 0.0),
18420        1 => (x, c, 0.0),
18421        2 => (0.0, c, x),
18422        3 => (0.0, x, c),
18423        4 => (x, 0.0, c),
18424        _ => (c, 0.0, x),
18425    };
18426    let m = l - c / 2.0;
18427    let to = |v: f32| (((v + m) * 255.0).round()).clamp(0.0, 255.0) as u8;
18428    egui::Color32::from_rgb(to(r1), to(g1), to(b1))
18429}
18430
18431fn parse_chat_hex_color(s: &str) -> Option<egui::Color32> {
18432    let s = s.strip_prefix('#')?;
18433    if s.len() != 6 {
18434        return None;
18435    }
18436    let r = u8::from_str_radix(&s[0..2], 16).ok()?;
18437    let g = u8::from_str_radix(&s[2..4], 16).ok()?;
18438    let b = u8::from_str_radix(&s[4..6], 16).ok()?;
18439    Some(egui::Color32::from_rgb(r, g, b))
18440}
18441
18442/// First existing emote image for `{stem}` in `dir`, trying the formats Twitch
18443/// uses (static `.png`, animated `.gif`) plus `.webp`. `None` when none exist.
18444fn find_emote_file(dir: &Path, stem: &str) -> Option<std::path::PathBuf> {
18445    ["png", "gif", "webp"]
18446        .iter()
18447        .map(|ext| dir.join(format!("{stem}.{ext}")))
18448        .find(|p| p.exists())
18449}
18450
18451/// An emoji image not yet on disk that the renderer would otherwise show as a
18452/// glyph. Collected during parse; the popup tries each `url` in order (Twemoji's
18453/// FE0F naming is irregular) and writes the first that succeeds to `dest`.
18454#[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
18455struct EmojiFetch {
18456    dest: std::path::PathBuf,
18457    urls: Vec<String>,
18458}
18459
18460/// Result of parsing a chat file: the messages plus the emoji images to fetch.
18461struct ParsedChat {
18462    messages: Vec<ChatMessage>,
18463    fetches: Vec<EmojiFetch>,
18464}
18465
18466/// Split a text run into [`ChatSegment`]s, turning each Unicode-emoji cluster into
18467/// an `Emote` that resolves to a cached Twemoji image (with the glyph as fallback),
18468/// and recording any not-yet-downloaded image in `fetches`. Plain text passes
18469/// through unchanged (fast path).
18470fn emoji_split(text: &str, fetches: &mut Vec<EmojiFetch>) -> Vec<ChatSegment> {
18471    let runs = crate::emoji::segment(text);
18472    if runs.iter().all(|(_, is_emoji)| !is_emoji) {
18473        return vec![ChatSegment::Text(text.to_string())];
18474    }
18475    let emoji_dir = emoji_cache_dir();
18476    let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(runs.len());
18477    for (slice, is_emoji) in runs {
18478        if is_emoji {
18479            let key = crate::emoji::cache_key(slice);
18480            let dest = emoji_dir.join(format!("{key}.png"));
18481            let file = dest.exists().then(|| dest.clone());
18482            // Skip re-fetching emoji we've already failed to download (a `.404`
18483            // marker), so a liberal false-positive / missing asset isn't re-requested
18484            // on every live tail-reload.
18485            if file.is_none() && !emoji_dir.join(format!("{key}.404")).exists() {
18486                fetches.push(EmojiFetch {
18487                    dest,
18488                    urls: crate::emoji::twemoji_url_candidates(slice),
18489                });
18490            }
18491            out.push(ChatSegment::Emote {
18492                name: slice.to_string(),
18493                file,
18494                fallback_text: Some(slice.to_string()),
18495            });
18496        } else if !slice.is_empty() {
18497            out.push(ChatSegment::Text(slice.to_string()));
18498        }
18499    }
18500    out
18501}
18502
18503/// The shared emoji image cache directory (`asset-cache/emotes/emoji/`).
18504fn emoji_cache_dir() -> std::path::PathBuf {
18505    crate::app_paths::asset_cache_dir()
18506        .join("emotes")
18507        .join("emoji")
18508}
18509
18510/// Expand the `Text` segments of an already-built segment list, splitting out any
18511/// Unicode emoji into image segments. Emote segments are left untouched.
18512fn expand_emoji(segments: Vec<ChatSegment>, fetches: &mut Vec<EmojiFetch>) -> Vec<ChatSegment> {
18513    let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(segments.len());
18514    for seg in segments {
18515        match seg {
18516            ChatSegment::Text(t) => out.extend(emoji_split(&t, fetches)),
18517            other => out.push(other),
18518        }
18519    }
18520    out
18521}
18522
18523/// File extension to use for a downloaded image, from the URL path (png/gif/webp),
18524/// defaulting to `png`.
18525fn url_ext(url: &str) -> &str {
18526    url.split(['?', '#'])
18527        .next()
18528        .and_then(|p| p.rsplit('.').next())
18529        .filter(|e| matches!(*e, "png" | "gif" | "webp" | "jpg" | "jpeg"))
18530        .unwrap_or("png")
18531}
18532
18533fn parse_chat_file(
18534    path: &Path,
18535    start_unix_secs: i64,
18536    emote_map: &HashMap<String, std::path::PathBuf>,
18537    twitch_dir: Option<&Path>,
18538) -> anyhow::Result<ParsedChat> {
18539    let s = path.to_string_lossy();
18540    let mut fetches: Vec<EmojiFetch> = Vec::new();
18541    let messages = if s.ends_with("live_chat.json") {
18542        parse_yt_live_chat(path, &mut fetches)?
18543    } else {
18544        parse_twitch_chat(path, start_unix_secs, emote_map, twitch_dir, &mut fetches)?
18545    };
18546    // De-duplicate so the same emoji isn't downloaded once per occurrence.
18547    fetches.sort_by(|a, b| a.dest.cmp(&b.dest));
18548    fetches.dedup();
18549    Ok(ParsedChat { messages, fetches })
18550}
18551
18552/// Parse a chat file off the UI thread, mapping both join and parse errors to a
18553/// string for [`ChatLoadState::Error`].
18554async fn parse_chat_blocking(
18555    path: std::path::PathBuf,
18556    start_ts: i64,
18557    emote_map: Arc<HashMap<String, std::path::PathBuf>>,
18558    twitch_dir: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
18559) -> Result<ParsedChat, String> {
18560    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
18561        parse_chat_file(&path, start_ts, &emote_map, twitch_dir.as_deref())
18562    })
18563    .await
18564    .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?
18565    .map_err(|e| e.to_string())
18566}
18567
18568/// Download missing emoji/emoji-emote images (sequential, best-effort; 404s for a
18569/// liberally-detected non-emoji just leave the glyph). Capped so a pathological
18570/// message can't trigger thousands of requests.
18571async fn download_emoji_images(fetches: &[EmojiFetch]) {
18572    let Ok(client) = reqwest::Client::builder()
18573        .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(10))
18574        .build()
18575    else {
18576        return;
18577    };
18578    for f in fetches.iter().take(300) {
18579        if f.dest.exists() {
18580            continue;
18581        }
18582        let mut got = false;
18583        let mut network_error = false; // a transient failure → don't negative-cache
18584        for url in &f.urls {
18585            match client.get(url).send().await {
18586                Ok(resp) if resp.status().is_success() => {
18587                    if let Ok(bytes) = resp.bytes().await {
18588                        if let Some(parent) = f.dest.parent() {
18589                            let _ = tokio::fs::create_dir_all(parent).await;
18590                        }
18591                        if tokio::fs::write(&f.dest, &bytes).await.is_ok() {
18592                            got = true;
18593                            break;
18594                        }
18595                    }
18596                }
18597                // 404 = this candidate name doesn't exist → try the next candidate.
18598                Ok(resp) if resp.status() == reqwest::StatusCode::NOT_FOUND => {}
18599                // Any other HTTP status or a transport error is transient-ish.
18600                Ok(_) | Err(_) => network_error = true,
18601            }
18602        }
18603        // Negative-cache ONLY a definitive miss (every candidate 404'd, no network
18604        // error), so a transient offline failure can't permanently block a real
18605        // emoji. `dest` is `{key}.png` → marker `{key}.404`.
18606        if !got && !network_error {
18607            let marker = f.dest.with_extension("404");
18608            if let Some(parent) = marker.parent() {
18609                let _ = tokio::fs::create_dir_all(parent).await;
18610            }
18611            let _ = tokio::fs::write(&marker, b"").await;
18612        }
18613    }
18614}
18615
18616/// Load a chat file into `state`: parse + show immediately (emoji may still be
18617/// glyphs), then — when `fetch_emoji` — download any missing emoji images and
