18618/// re-parse once so they upgrade to colour images. Runs entirely off the UI thread.
18619async fn load_chat(
18620    state: Arc<Mutex<ChatLoadState>>,
18621    loading: Arc<AtomicBool>,
18622    path: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
18623    start_ts: i64,
18624    emote_map: Arc<HashMap<String, std::path::PathBuf>>,
18625    twitch_dir: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
18626    fetch_emoji: bool,
18627    ctx: egui::Context,
18628) {
18629    let Some(path) = path else {
18630        *state.lock().unwrap() = ChatLoadState::NoFile;
18631        ctx.request_repaint();
18632        return;
18633    };
18634    let fetches = match parse_chat_blocking(
18635        path.clone(),
18636        start_ts,
18637        emote_map.clone(),
18638        twitch_dir.clone(),
18639    )
18640    .await
18641    {
18642        Ok(parsed) => {
18643            let fetches = parsed.fetches;
18644            *state.lock().unwrap() = ChatLoadState::Loaded(parsed.messages);
18645            ctx.request_repaint();
18646            fetches
18647        }
18648        Err(e) => {
18649            *state.lock().unwrap() = ChatLoadState::Error(e);
18650            ctx.request_repaint();
18651            return;
18652        }
18653    };
18654    // Messages are already shown; only one download+upgrade pass runs at a time
18655    // (a concurrent tail-reload still shows new messages via the parse above, it
18656    // just skips kicking off a second overlapping download).
18657    if fetch_emoji && !fetches.is_empty() && !loading.swap(true, Ordering::SeqCst) {
18658        download_emoji_images(&fetches).await;
18659        loading.store(false, Ordering::SeqCst);
18660        if let Ok(parsed) = parse_chat_blocking(path, start_ts, emote_map, twitch_dir).await {
18661            *state.lock().unwrap() = ChatLoadState::Loaded(parsed.messages);
18662            ctx.request_repaint();
18663        }
18664    }
18665}
18666
18667fn parse_yt_live_chat(
18668    path: &Path,
18669    fetches: &mut Vec<EmojiFetch>,
18670) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<ChatMessage>> {
18671    let mut out = Vec::new();
18672    let reader = BufReader::new(File::open(path)?);
18673    for line in reader.lines() {
18674        let line = line?;
18675        if line.is_empty() {
18676            continue;
18677        }
18678        let v: serde_json::Value = match serde_json::from_str(&line) {
18679            Ok(v) => v,
18680            Err(_) => continue,
18681        };
18682        if let Some(replay) = v.get("replayChatItemAction") {
18683            // VOD replay format: replayChatItemAction.{videoOffsetTimeMsec, actions[]}
18684            let offset_ms = replay
18685                .get("videoOffsetTimeMsec")
18686                .and_then(|x| x.as_str().and_then(|s| s.parse::<i64>().ok()).or_else(|| x.as_i64()));
18687            if let Some(actions) = replay.get("actions").and_then(|a| a.as_array()) {
18688                for action in actions {
18689                    if let Some(msg) = yt_action_to_msg(action, offset_ms, fetches) {
18690                        out.push(msg);
18691                    }
18692                }
18693            }
18694        } else if let Some(msg) = yt_action_to_msg(&v, None, fetches) {
18695            // Live format: addChatItemAction directly at the top level of each line.
18696            out.push(msg);
18697        }
18698    }
18699    Ok(out)
18700}
18701
18702fn yt_action_to_msg(
18703    action: &serde_json::Value,
18704    offset_ms: Option<i64>,
18705    fetches: &mut Vec<EmojiFetch>,
18706) -> Option<ChatMessage> {
18707    let r = action.pointer("/addChatItemAction/item/liveChatTextMessageRenderer")?;
18708    let ts_secs = if let Some(ms) = offset_ms {
18709        ms as f64 / 1000.0
18710    } else {
18711        r["timestampUsec"]
18712            .as_str()
18713            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<f64>().ok())
18714            .unwrap_or(0.0)
18715            / 1_000_000.0
18716    };
18717    let author = r
18718        .pointer("/authorName/simpleText")
18719        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
18720        .unwrap_or("")
18721        .to_string();
18722    // YouTube pre-tokenizes the body as `message.runs[]`: text runs are literal,
18723    // emoji runs carry either a standard unicode char (`emojiId`) or a custom
18724    // channel emoji (image-only). Build the display `segments` and the verbatim
18725    // search `text` in one pass.
18726    let mut text = String::new();
18727    let mut segments: Vec<ChatSegment> = Vec::new();
18728    if let Some(runs) = r["message"]["runs"].as_array() {
18729        for run in runs {
18730            if let Some(t) = run["text"].as_str() {
18731                text.push_str(t);
18732                // Text runs can themselves contain literal unicode emoji.
18733                segments.extend(emoji_split(t, fetches));
18734            } else if let Some(emoji) = run.get("emoji") {
18735                let shortcut = emoji["shortcuts"]
18736                    .as_array()
18737                    .and_then(|s| s.first())
18738                    .and_then(|e| e.as_str());
18739                let emoji_id = emoji["emojiId"].as_str();
18740                let label = shortcut.or(emoji_id).unwrap_or("[emoji]");
18741                text.push_str(label);
18742                if emoji["isCustomEmoji"].as_bool() == Some(true) {
18743                    // Custom channel emoji: image-only. Download YouTube's own PNG
18744                    // (largest thumbnail) into the cache; until present, fall back to
18745                    // the shortcut text.
18746                    let url = emoji
18747                        .pointer("/image/thumbnails")
18748                        .and_then(|t| t.as_array())
18749                        .and_then(|a| a.last())
18750                        .and_then(|t| t["url"].as_str());
18751                    let file = emoji_id.zip(url).and_then(|(id, url)| {
18752                        let dest = crate::app_paths::asset_cache_dir()
18753                            .join("emotes")
18754                            .join("youtube")
18755                            .join(format!(
18756                                "{}.{}",
18757                                crate::downloader::sanitize_filename(id),
18758                                url_ext(url)
18759                            ));
18760                        if dest.exists() {
18761                            Some(dest)
18762                        } else {
18763                            fetches.push(EmojiFetch { dest, urls: vec![url.to_string()] });
18764                            None
18765                        }
18766                    });
18767                    segments.push(ChatSegment::Emote {
18768                        name: label.to_string(),
18769                        file,
18770                        fallback_text: None,
18771                    });
18772                } else {
18773                    // Standard unicode emoji: `emojiId` is the actual char(s) → route
18774                    // through the shared Twemoji emoji pipeline for colour.
18775                    let glyph = emoji_id.or(shortcut).unwrap_or("[emoji]");
18776                    segments.extend(emoji_split(glyph, fetches));
18777                }
18778            }
18779        }
18780    }
18781    let badges: Vec<String> = r["authorBadges"]
18782        .as_array()
18783        .map(|bs| {
18784            bs.iter()
18785                .filter_map(|b| {
18786                    b.pointer("/liveChatAuthorBadgeRenderer/tooltip")
18787                        .and_then(|t| t.as_str())
18788                        .map(|t| t.split('(').next().unwrap_or(t).trim().to_string())
18789                })
18790                .collect()
18791        })
18792        .unwrap_or_default();
18793    Some(ChatMessage {
18794        timestamp_secs: ts_secs,
18795        author,
18796        text,
18797        segments,
18798        badges,
18799        color_override: None,
18800        platform: ChatPlatform::YouTube,
18801    })
18802}
18803
18804fn parse_twitch_chat(
18805    path: &Path,
18806    start_unix_secs: i64,
18807    emote_map: &HashMap<String, std::path::PathBuf>,
18808    twitch_dir: Option<&Path>,
18809    fetches: &mut Vec<EmojiFetch>,
18810) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<ChatMessage>> {
18811    let start_ms = start_unix_secs as f64 * 1000.0;
18812    let mut out = Vec::new();
18813    let reader = BufReader::new(File::open(path)?);
18814    for line in reader.lines() {
18815        let line = line?;
18816        if line.is_empty() {
18817            continue;
18818        }
18819        let v: serde_json::Value = match serde_json::from_str(&line) {
18820            Ok(v) => v,
18821            Err(_) => continue,
18822        };
18823        let ts_ms = v["ts"].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
18824        let author = v["name"]
18825            .as_str()
18826            .or_else(|| v["login"].as_str())
18827            .unwrap_or("")
18828            .to_string();
18829        // Unwrap `/me` CTCP actions so the emote offsets (which index the inner
18830        // body) align and the raw control chars don't show in the replay/search.
18831        let text = strip_ctcp_action(v["text"].as_str().unwrap_or("")).to_string();
18832        let color_override = v["color"].as_str().and_then(parse_chat_hex_color);
18833        // Split raw badge tag "subscriber/12,moderator/1" into one entry per badge.
18834        let badges = v["badges"]
18835            .as_str()
18836            .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
18837            .map(|s| s.split(',').map(str::to_string).collect::<Vec<_>>())
18838            .unwrap_or_default();
18839        // `emotes` tag is absent on pre-feature logs → empty → first-party emotes
18840        // simply don't render (third-party word-matching still applies).
18841        let emotes_tag = v["emotes"].as_str().unwrap_or("");
18842        let segments = build_twitch_segments(&text, emotes_tag, emote_map, twitch_dir);
18843        // Split literal unicode emoji out of the text segments into colour images.
18844        let segments = expand_emoji(segments, fetches);
18845        out.push(ChatMessage {
18846            timestamp_secs: (ts_ms - start_ms) / 1000.0,
18847            author,
18848            text,
18849            segments,
18850            badges,
18851            color_override,
18852            platform: ChatPlatform::Twitch,
18853        });
18854    }
18855    Ok(out)
18856}
18857
18858#[cfg(test)]
18859mod tests {
18860    use super::{
18861        ChatSegment, DateFmt, StreamMetaChange, StreamTarget, active_date_fmt,
18862        aggregate_stream_changes, build_twitch_segments, chat_file_for_recording,
18863        compose_browser_profile, contrast_ratio, fmt_polled, hsl_to_rgb, meta_change_lines,
18864        monitor_import_identity, parse_first_party_spans, playable_with, player_is_mpv,
18865        readable_color, rgb_to_hsl, set_active_date_fmt, split_browser_profile,
18866        stream_target_for_active, strip_ctcp_action, twitch_username_color, word_match_segments,
18867        yt_channel_id,
18868    };
18869    use eframe::egui;
18870    use std::collections::HashMap;
18871    use std::path::PathBuf;
18872
18873    // ----- first-party emote offset parsing (IRC `emotes` tag) -----
18874
18875    #[test]
18876    fn first_party_spans_ascii_sorted_byte_ranges() {
18877        // "Kappa Keepo Kappa": cp ranges 0-4 / 6-10 / 12-16 == byte ranges (ASCII).
18878        let text = "Kappa Keepo Kappa";
18879        let spans = parse_first_party_spans(text, "25:0-4,12-16/1902:6-10");
18880        assert_eq!(
18881            spans,
18882            vec![
18883                (0, 5, "25".to_string()),
18884                (6, 11, "1902".to_string()),
18885                (12, 17, "25".to_string()),
18886            ]
18887        );
18888        for (b0, b1, _) in &spans {
18889            assert!(text.get(*b0..*b1).is_some());
18890        }
18891    }
18892
18893    #[test]
18894    fn first_party_offsets_are_code_points_not_utf16_or_bytes() {
18895        // A leading astral emoji (😀 = 1 code point, 2 UTF-16 units, 4 bytes) before
18896        // the emote. Twitch counts code points, so "Kappa" is cp 2..=6.
18897        let text = "😀 Kappa";
18898        let spans = parse_first_party_spans(text, "25:2-6");
18899        assert_eq!(spans.len(), 1);
18900        let (b0, b1, id) = &spans[0];
18901        assert_eq!(id, "25");
18902        assert_eq!(text.get(*b0..*b1), Some("Kappa")); // not "appa"/"aKapp"/garbage
18903    }
18904
18905    #[test]
18906    fn first_party_trailing_emote_reaches_end_of_string() {
18907        // Emote as the final token: end+1 is one-past-end → b1 must be text.len().
18908        let text = "gg Kappa";
18909        let spans = parse_first_party_spans(text, "25:3-7");
18910        assert_eq!(spans, vec![(3, 8, "25".to_string())]);
18911        assert_eq!(text.get(3..8), Some("Kappa"));
18912    }
18913
18914    #[test]
18915    fn first_party_bails_on_overlap_reversed_or_oob() {
18916        // Overlapping spans → abort first-party entirely (empty).
18917        assert!(parse_first_party_spans("abcde", "25:0-2/1902:1-4").is_empty());
18918        // Reversed (end < start) → empty.
18919        assert!(parse_first_party_spans("abcde", "25:5-3").is_empty());
18920        // Out of range (end >= code-point count) → empty.
18921        assert!(parse_first_party_spans("hi", "25:0-5").is_empty());
18922        // Malformed → empty.
18923        assert!(parse_first_party_spans("hi", "garbage").is_empty());
18924        // Empty tag → empty.
18925        assert!(parse_first_party_spans("hi", "").is_empty());
18926    }
18927
18928    // ----- third-party word matching -----
18929
18930    fn emote(name: &str, file: &Option<PathBuf>) -> ChatSegment {
18931        ChatSegment::Emote { name: name.into(), file: file.clone(), fallback_text: None }
18932    }
18933
18934    #[test]
18935    fn word_match_is_case_sensitive_and_whole_token() {
18936        let mut map = HashMap::new();
18937        let p = PathBuf::from("/x/poggers.webp");
18938        map.insert("POGGERS".to_string(), p.clone());
18939        let segs = word_match_segments("hi POGGERS poggers POGGERSx", &map);
18940        // Only the exact, whole-token "POGGERS" matches; "poggers"/"POGGERSx" stay text.
18941        let emotes: Vec<_> = segs
18942            .iter()
18943            .filter(|s| matches!(s, ChatSegment::Emote { .. }))
18944            .collect();
18945        assert_eq!(emotes.len(), 1);
18946        assert!(matches!(&emotes[0], ChatSegment::Emote { name, .. } if name == "POGGERS"));
18947    }
18948
18949    #[test]
18950    fn word_match_preserves_spacing_and_tabs() {
18951        let mut map = HashMap::new();
18952        map.insert("Kappa".to_string(), PathBuf::from("/x/k.png"));
18953        // Tab-separated tokens still match (Unicode-whitespace tokenization).
18954        let segs = word_match_segments("a\tKappa\tb", &map);
18955        // Reconstructing all text + emote names must round-trip the original.
18956        let mut rebuilt = String::new();
18957        for s in &segs {
18958            match s {
18959                ChatSegment::Text(t) => rebuilt.push_str(t),
18960                ChatSegment::Emote { name, .. } => rebuilt.push_str(name),
18961            }
18962        }
18963        assert_eq!(rebuilt, "a\tKappa\tb");
18964        assert!(segs.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, ChatSegment::Emote { name, .. } if name == "Kappa")));
18965    }
18966
18967    #[test]
18968    fn empty_map_yields_single_text_segment() {
18969        let map: HashMap<String, PathBuf> = HashMap::new();
18970        let segs = word_match_segments("hello world", &map);
18971        assert_eq!(segs.len(), 1);
18972        assert!(matches!(&segs[0], ChatSegment::Text(t) if t == "hello world"));
18973    }
18974
18975    #[test]
18976    fn strips_ctcp_action_wrapper() {
18977        // `/me` actions are unwrapped; the inner body is what emote offsets index.
18978        assert_eq!(strip_ctcp_action("\u{1}ACTION Kappa\u{1}"), "Kappa");
18979        assert_eq!(strip_ctcp_action("\u{1}ACTION \u{1}"), "");
18980        // Plain messages and malformed wrappers pass through untouched.
18981        assert_eq!(strip_ctcp_action("hello"), "hello");
18982        assert_eq!(strip_ctcp_action("\u{1}ACTION no-suffix"), "\u{1}ACTION no-suffix");
18983    }
18984
18985    #[test]
18986    fn action_message_emote_offsets_align_after_strip() {
18987        // `/me Kappa` → stored `\x01ACTION Kappa\x01`; after stripping, offset 0-4
18988        // must land on "Kappa", not on the control-char-prefixed wrapper.
18989        let stripped = strip_ctcp_action("\u{1}ACTION Kappa\u{1}");
18990        let map = HashMap::new();
18991        let segs = build_twitch_segments(stripped, "25:0-4", &map, None);
18992        assert!(matches!(&segs[0], ChatSegment::Emote { name, .. } if name == "Kappa"));
18993    }
18994
18995    // ----- username colour readability -----
18996
18997    #[test]
18998    fn hsl_round_trips_within_one_step() {
18999        for c in [
19000            egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xFF, 0x00, 0x00),
19001            egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0x00, 0xFF),
19002            egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x8A, 0x2B, 0xE2),
19003            egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x12, 0x34, 0x56),
19004            egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x80, 0x80, 0x80),
19005        ] {
19006            let (h, s, l) = rgb_to_hsl(c);
19007            let back = hsl_to_rgb(h, s, l);
19008            // Allow ±1 per channel for rounding.
19009            assert!((c.r() as i32 - back.r() as i32).abs() <= 1);
19010            assert!((c.g() as i32 - back.g() as i32).abs() <= 1);
19011            assert!((c.b() as i32 - back.b() as i32).abs() <= 1);
19012        }
19013    }
19014
19015    #[test]
19016    fn readable_color_lightens_dark_color_on_dark_bg() {
19017        let bg = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x1e, 0x1e, 0x1e); // egui dark panel-ish
19018        let blue = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0x00, 0xFF); // unreadable on dark
19019        assert!(contrast_ratio(blue, bg) < 4.0);
19020        let fixed = readable_color(blue, bg);
19021        assert!(contrast_ratio(fixed, bg) >= 4.0);
19022        // Hue stays blue-ish: blue channel remains the dominant one.
19023        assert!(fixed.b() > fixed.r() && fixed.b() > fixed.g());
19024    }
19025
19026    #[test]
19027    fn readable_color_picks_reachable_direction_on_midtone_bg() {
19028        // On a mid-grey bg, lightening blue toward white never clears 4.0, but
19029        // darkening does — the direction must be chosen by reachable contrast.
19030        let bg = egui::Color32::from_gray(128);
19031        let blue = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x00, 0x00, 0xFF);
19032        let fixed = readable_color(blue, bg);
19033        assert!(contrast_ratio(fixed, bg) >= 4.0);
19034    }
19035
19036    #[test]
19037    fn readable_color_keeps_already_legible_color() {
19038        let bg = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0x1e, 0x1e, 0x1e);
19039        let coral = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xFF, 0x7F, 0x50); // already high-contrast
19040        assert_eq!(readable_color(coral, bg), coral);
19041    }
19042
19043    #[test]
19044    fn readable_color_darkens_pale_color_on_light_bg() {
19045        let bg = egui::Color32::WHITE;
19046        let pale = egui::Color32::from_rgb(0xFF, 0xFF, 0x00); // yellow: invisible on white
19047        assert!(contrast_ratio(pale, bg) < 4.0);
19048        let fixed = readable_color(pale, bg);
19049        assert!(contrast_ratio(fixed, bg) > contrast_ratio(pale, bg));
19050    }
19051
19052    #[test]
19053    fn twitch_default_color_is_deterministic_per_name() {
19054        // Same name → same colour every time (Twitch's stable default assignment).
19055        assert_eq!(twitch_username_color("Kappa"), twitch_username_color("Kappa"));
19056    }
19057
19058    #[test]
19059    fn import_identity_matches_candidate_identity() {
19060        // Dedup keys must equal what the importer produces (lowercased login / UC id).
19061        assert_eq!(monitor_import_identity("https://twitch.tv/CoolStreamer"), "coolstreamer");
19062        assert_eq!(
19063            monitor_import_identity("https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCabcDEF"),
19064            "ucabcdef"
19065        );
19066        assert_eq!(
19067            yt_channel_id("https://youtube.com/channel/UCxyz/live"),
19068            Some("ucxyz".to_string())
19069        );
19070        // A handle URL has no /channel/UC… id → None (won't false-match a sub).
19071        assert_eq!(yt_channel_id("https://youtube.com/@handle"), None);
19072    }
19073
19074    #[test]
19075    fn build_twitch_combines_first_party_offset_and_thirdparty_word() {
19076        // First-party "Kappa" by offset (no file on disk → name fallback), and a
19077        // third-party "POGGERS" by word match in the trailing gap.
19078        let mut map = HashMap::new();
19079        let pog = PathBuf::from("/x/poggers.webp");
19080        map.insert("POGGERS".to_string(), pog.clone());
19081        // "Kappa POGGERS": Kappa at cp 0-4; POGGERS is the gap word.
19082        let segs = build_twitch_segments("Kappa POGGERS", "25:0-4", &map, None);
19083        // Expect: Emote(Kappa, None) then Text(" ") then Emote(POGGERS, Some).
19084        assert!(matches!(&segs[0], ChatSegment::Emote { name, file, .. } if name == "Kappa" && file.is_none()));
19085        assert!(segs.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, ChatSegment::Emote { name, file, .. } if name == "POGGERS" && file.as_ref() == Some(&pog))));
19086    }
19087
19088    fn rec_with_output(path: &str) -> crate::models::Recording {
19089        crate::models::Recording {
19090            id: 1,
19091            monitor_id: 1,
19092            started_at: 0,
19093            ended_at: None,
19094            status: "recording".into(),
19095            bytes: 0,
19096            exit_code: None,
19097            output_path: path.into(),
19098            went_live_at: None,
19099            went_live_approx: false,
19100            lost_secs: None,
19101            stream_id: None,
19102            take_group: None,
19103            ad_count: 0,
19104            ad_secs: 0,
19105            meta_change_count: 0,
19106            title: String::new(),
19107            category: String::new(),
19108            log_excerpt: String::new(),
19109            notes: String::new(),
19110            vod_id: None,
19111            vod_state: None,
19112            vod_muted_secs: None,
19113        }
19114    }
19115
19116    #[test]
19117    fn finds_youtube_live_chat_append_form() {
19118        // yt-dlp appends `.live_chat.json` to the -o value, so the sidecar keeps the
19119        // video extension: `<output_path>.live_chat.json`.
19120        let dir = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("sa-chat-{}", std::process::id()));
19121        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19122        std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).unwrap();
19123        let out = dir.join("clip.mkv");
19124        std::fs::write(format!("{}.live_chat.json", out.to_string_lossy()), "{}").unwrap();
19125
19126        let found = chat_file_for_recording(&rec_with_output(&out.to_string_lossy()));
19127        assert_eq!(found.as_deref(), Some(out.with_extension("mkv.live_chat.json").as_path()));
19128
19129        // Twitch native logger uses the extension-replace form.
19130        let tout = dir.join("vod.mkv");
19131        std::fs::write(tout.with_extension("chat.jsonl"), "{}").unwrap();
19132        let tfound = chat_file_for_recording(&rec_with_output(&tout.to_string_lossy()));
19133        assert_eq!(tfound.as_deref(), Some(tout.with_extension("chat.jsonl").as_path()));
19134
19135        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19136    }
19137
19138    #[test]
19139    fn actions_col_index_is_actions() {
19140        // The optional-Actions logic skips this index in the builder/header/rows;
19141        // guard that it actually points at the Actions column.
19142        assert_eq!(
19143            super::STREAM_COLUMNS[super::STREAM_ACTIONS_COL].title,
19144            "Actions"
19145        );
19146    }
19147
19148    #[test]
19149    fn stream_meta_aggregation_dedups_rebaseline() {
19150        let smc = |id, at, old: &str, new: &str| StreamMetaChange {
19151            id,
19152            recording_id: 0,
19153            at_secs: at,
19154            kind: "title".into(),
19155            old_value: old.into(),
19156            new_value: new.into(),
19157        };
19158        // Take 1 (started 1000): initial "A", then A -> B at +300s.
19159        let t1 = vec![smc(1, 0, "", "A"), smc(2, 300, "A", "B")];
19160        // Take 2 (started 2000): re-observes "B" (the duplicate), then B -> C at +120s.
19161        let t2 = vec![smc(3, 0, "", "B"), smc(4, 120, "B", "C")];
19162
19163        let agg = aggregate_stream_changes(&[(1000, t1), (2000, t2)]);
19164        // All rows kept, offsets rebased onto the stream timeline (min start 1000)
19165        // and sorted: 0, 300, (2000-1000)+0=1000, (2000-1000)+120=1120.
19166        assert_eq!(
19167            agg.iter().map(|c| c.at_secs).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
19168            vec![0, 300, 1000, 1120]
19169        );
19170        // The displayed list drops both initial values — including take 2's
19171        // re-baseline of "B" (the omitted duplicate) — and keeps the real changes.
19172        let lines = meta_change_lines(&agg);
19173        assert_eq!(lines.len(), 2, "{lines:?}");
19174        assert!(lines[0].contains("A → B"), "{:?}", lines[0]);
19175        assert!(lines[1].contains("B → C"), "{:?}", lines[1]);
19176    }
19177
19178    #[test]
19179    fn date_fmt_parse_roundtrip() {
19180        for f in DateFmt::ALL {
19181            assert_eq!(DateFmt::parse(f.as_str()), f);
19182        }
19183        // Unknown / empty falls back to the ISO default.
19184        assert_eq!(DateFmt::parse("bogus"), DateFmt::Iso);
19185        assert_eq!(DateFmt::parse(""), DateFmt::Iso);
19186    }
19187
19188    #[test]
19189    fn active_date_fmt_roundtrip() {
19190        for f in DateFmt::ALL {
19191            set_active_date_fmt(f);
19192            assert_eq!(active_date_fmt(), f);
19193        }
19194        set_active_date_fmt(DateFmt::Iso); // restore default for other tests
19195    }
19196
19197    #[test]
19198    fn fmt_polled_shows_interval() {
19199        // Never polled -> just the interval, so the cadence is still visible.
19200        assert_eq!(fmt_polled(None, 60), "(60s)");
19201        assert_eq!(fmt_polled(Some(0), 30), "(30s)");
19202        // Polled -> "<timestamp> (Ns)"; the timestamp is local/tz-dependent, so
19203        // assert only the stable suffix and that a timestamp is present.
19204        let s = fmt_polled(Some(1_700_000_000), 45);
19205        assert!(s.ends_with(" (45s)"), "got {s:?}");
19206        assert!(s.len() > " (45s)".len());
19207    }
19208
19209    #[test]
19210    fn browser_profile_roundtrip() {
19211        // No profile.
19212        assert_eq!(split_browser_profile("firefox"), ("firefox".into(), String::new()));
19213        assert_eq!(compose_browser_profile("firefox", ""), "firefox");
19214
19215        // Named profile.
19216        assert_eq!(
19217            split_browser_profile("firefox:dmrf6eed.YouTube"),
19218            ("firefox".into(), "dmrf6eed.YouTube".into())
19219        );
19220        assert_eq!(
19221            compose_browser_profile("firefox", "dmrf6eed.YouTube"),
19222            "firefox:dmrf6eed.YouTube"
19223        );
19224
19225        // Absolute-path profile: the drive-letter colon stays in the profile
19226        // (split on the FIRST colon only, matching yt-dlp).
19227        let raw = r"firefox:C:\Users\Blu\AppData\Roaming\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\dmrf6eed.YouTube";
19228        let (b, p) = split_browser_profile(raw);
19229        assert_eq!(b, "firefox");
19230        assert_eq!(p, r"C:\Users\Blu\AppData\Roaming\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\dmrf6eed.YouTube");
19231        assert_eq!(compose_browser_profile(&b, &p), raw);
19232
19233        // Empty browser -> empty (no cookies), even with a profile.
19234        assert_eq!(compose_browser_profile("", "whatever"), "");
19235    }
19236
19237    // ----- "Stream in player" target probing (incl. mid-SABR captures) -----
19238
19239    /// A fresh scratch dir mimicking a channel output dir with a `.cache\` inside.
19240    fn probe_dir(tag: &str) -> (PathBuf, PathBuf) {
19241        let dir = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!(
19242            "sa_probe_{tag}_{}_{}",
19243            std::process::id(),
19244            crate::models::now_unix()
19245        ));
19246        let cache = dir.join(".cache");
19247        std::fs::create_dir_all(&cache).unwrap();
19248        (dir, cache)
19249    }
19250
19251    /// Write `name` in `cache` with `len` filler bytes.
19252    fn put(cache: &PathBuf, name: &str, len: usize) {
19253        std::fs::write(cache.join(name), vec![0u8; len]).unwrap();
19254    }
19255
19256    const BIG: usize = 64 * 1024; // SPLIT_AV_MIN_BYTES
19257
19258    #[test]
19259    fn probe_finds_single_growing_ts() {
19260        let (dir, cache) = probe_dir("ts");
19261        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.ts", BIG);
19262        let out = dir.join("Chan - 2026.mkv");
19263        assert_eq!(
19264            stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()),
19265            Some(StreamTarget::Growing(cache.join("Chan - 2026.ts")))
19266        );
19267        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19268    }
19269
19270    #[test]
19271    fn probe_finds_dash_companion_ts() {
19272        let (dir, cache) = probe_dir("dash");
19273        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.dash.ts", BIG);
19274        // The companion recording's own output path carries the .dash infix.
19275        let out = dir.join("Chan - 2026.dash.mkv");
19276        assert_eq!(
19277            stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()),
19278            Some(StreamTarget::Growing(cache.join("Chan - 2026.dash.ts")))
19279        );
19280        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19281    }
19282
19283    #[test]
19284    fn probe_ignores_empty_single_file() {
19285        let (dir, cache) = probe_dir("empty");
19286        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.ts", 0);
19287        let out = dir.join("Chan - 2026.mkv");
19288        assert_eq!(stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()), None);
19289        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19290    }
19291
19292    #[test]
19293    fn probe_sabr_split_format_id_after_template_ext() {
19294        // Naming variant A (seen 2026-07-01): {stem}.mkv.f<id>.mp4.sq0.part
19295        let (dir, cache) = probe_dir("sabr_a");
19296        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.mkv.f400.mp4.sq0.part", 4 * BIG); // video (bigger)
19297        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.mkv.f140.mp4.sq0.part", BIG); // audio
19298        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.mkv.f400.mp4.state", 52); // resume sidecars: excluded
19299        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.mkv.f140.mp4.state", 51);
19300        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.log", 9999); // tool log: excluded
19301        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.thumbnail.jpg", 9999); // no f<id> segment: excluded
19302        let out = dir.join("Chan - 2026.mkv");
19303        assert_eq!(
19304            stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()),
19305            Some(StreamTarget::SplitAv(vec![
19306                cache.join("Chan - 2026.mkv.f400.mp4.sq0.part"), // largest first
19307                cache.join("Chan - 2026.mkv.f140.mp4.sq0.part"),
19308            ]))
19309        );
19310        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19311    }
19312
19313    #[test]
19314    fn probe_sabr_split_format_id_before_container_ext() {
19315        // Naming variant B (seen 2026-06-30): {stem}.f<id>.mkv.sq0.part
19316        let (dir, cache) = probe_dir("sabr_b");
19317        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.f303.mkv.sq0.part", 4 * BIG);
19318        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.f140.mkv.sq0.part", BIG);
19319        let out = dir.join("Chan - 2026.mkv");
19320        assert_eq!(
19321            stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()),
19322            Some(StreamTarget::SplitAv(vec![
19323                cache.join("Chan - 2026.f303.mkv.sq0.part"),
19324                cache.join("Chan - 2026.f140.mkv.sq0.part"),
19325            ]))
19326        );
19327        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19328    }
19329
19330    #[test]
19331    fn probe_sabr_prefers_bare_over_part_and_higher_sequence() {
19332        let (dir, cache) = probe_dir("sabr_pref");
19333        // f303: bare (merge phase) outranks the leftover .part.
19334        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.f303.mkv", 4 * BIG);
19335        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.f303.mkv.sq0.part", 4 * BIG);
19336        // f140: sq1 (post-resume sequence) outranks sq0.
19337        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.f140.mkv.sq0.part", BIG);
19338        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.f140.mkv.sq1.part", BIG);
19339        // In-flight ffmpeg merge output must never be picked.
19340        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.mkv.temp.mp4", 8 * BIG);
19341        let out = dir.join("Chan - 2026.mkv");
19342        assert_eq!(
19343            stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()),
19344            Some(StreamTarget::SplitAv(vec![
19345                cache.join("Chan - 2026.f303.mkv"),
19346                cache.join("Chan - 2026.f140.mkv.sq1.part"),
19347            ]))
19348        );
19349        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19350    }
19351
19352    #[test]
19353    fn probe_sabr_single_format_is_growing() {
19354        // Audio-only (or video-only) SABR capture: one growing file → Growing.
19355        let (dir, cache) = probe_dir("sabr_one");
19356        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.f140.mkv.sq0.part", BIG);
19357        let out = dir.join("Chan - 2026.mkv");
19358        assert_eq!(
19359            stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()),
19360            Some(StreamTarget::Growing(cache.join("Chan - 2026.f140.mkv.sq0.part")))
19361        );
19362        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19363    }
19364
19365    #[test]
19366    fn probe_sabr_ignores_tiny_files() {
19367        // First seconds of a capture: files below the init-segment floor.
19368        let (dir, cache) = probe_dir("sabr_tiny");
19369        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.f303.mkv.sq0.part", 1024);
19370        put(&cache, "Chan - 2026.f140.mkv.sq0.part", 512);
19371        let out = dir.join("Chan - 2026.mkv");
19372        assert_eq!(stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()), None);
19373        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19374    }
19375
19376    #[test]
19377    fn probe_does_not_leak_across_stems() {
19378        // Another recording's SABR files in the same .cache must not match.
19379        let (dir, cache) = probe_dir("stems");
19380        put(&cache, "Other - 2025.f303.mkv.sq0.part", 4 * BIG);
19381        put(&cache, "Other - 2025.f140.mkv.sq0.part", BIG);
19382        let out = dir.join("Chan - 2026.mkv");
19383        assert_eq!(stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()), None);
19384        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19385    }
19386
19387    #[test]
19388    fn probe_does_not_leak_into_prefix_stems() {
19389        // A sibling recording whose stem EXTENDS this stem after a dot
19390        // ("Chan - Movie Night" vs "Chan - Movie Night. Part 2") must not
19391        // leak into the shorter stem's probe: the segments before the f<id>
19392        // token would be title text, not container decoration.
19393        let (dir, cache) = probe_dir("prefix");
19394        put(&cache, "Chan - Movie Night. Part 2.f303.mkv.sq0.part", 4 * BIG);
19395        put(&cache, "Chan - Movie Night. Part 2.f140.mkv.sq0.part", BIG);
19396        let out = dir.join("Chan - Movie Night.mkv");
19397        assert_eq!(stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()), None);
19398        // With the shorter stem's own files present, only they are picked.
19399        put(&cache, "Chan - Movie Night.f303.mkv.sq0.part", 4 * BIG);
19400        put(&cache, "Chan - Movie Night.f140.mkv.sq0.part", BIG);
19401        assert_eq!(
19402            stream_target_for_active(&out.to_string_lossy()),
19403            Some(StreamTarget::SplitAv(vec![
19404                cache.join("Chan - Movie Night.f303.mkv.sq0.part"),
19405                cache.join("Chan - Movie Night.f140.mkv.sq0.part"),
19406            ]))
19407        );
19408        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19409    }
19410
19411    #[test]
19412    fn split_av_player_command_uses_appending_and_audio_file() {
19413        let parts = vec![
19414            PathBuf::from(r"A:\streams\Nitya Ch. 【Phase】\.cache\a b.f303.mkv.sq0.part"),
19415            PathBuf::from(r"A:\streams\Nitya Ch. 【Phase】\.cache\a b.f140.mkv.sq0.part"),
19416        ];
19417        let cmd = super::build_player_command(
19418            r"C:\Progs\mpv\mpv.exe",
19419            &StreamTarget::SplitAv(parts.clone()),
19420        );
19421        assert_eq!(cmd.get_program().to_string_lossy(), r"C:\Progs\mpv\mpv.exe");
19422        let args: Vec<String> =
19423            cmd.get_args().map(|a| a.to_string_lossy().into_owned()).collect();
19424        // Live-view flags, then the largest (video) file as the appending://
19425        // main file, then the audio as an external appending:// track.
19426        // Backslashes are converted to forward slashes inside the URLs.
19427        assert!(args.contains(&"--keep-open=yes".to_string()));
19428        let fwd = |p: &PathBuf| p.to_string_lossy().replace('\\', "/");
19429        assert_eq!(args.last().unwrap(), &format!("--audio-file=appending://{}", fwd(&parts[1])));
19430        assert!(args.contains(&format!("appending://{}", fwd(&parts[0]))));
19431
19432        // Growing single file under mpv also uses appending://; other players
19433        // and finished files get the plain path.
19434        let g = StreamTarget::Growing(PathBuf::from(r"A:\x\.cache\y.ts"));
19435        let cmd = super::build_player_command(r"C:\Progs\mpv\mpv.exe", &g);
19436        assert!(cmd.get_args().any(|a| a.to_string_lossy() == "appending://A:/x/.cache/y.ts"));
19437        let cmd = super::build_player_command(r"C:\VLC\vlc.exe", &g);
19438        let args: Vec<String> =
19439            cmd.get_args().map(|a| a.to_string_lossy().into_owned()).collect();
19440        assert_eq!(args, vec![r"A:\x\.cache\y.ts".to_string()]);
19441    }
19442
19443    #[test]
19444    fn player_kind_sniffing() {
19445        assert!(player_is_mpv(r"C:\Progs\mpv\mpv.exe"));
19446        assert!(player_is_mpv(r"C:\Apps\mpv.net\mpvnet.exe"));
19447        assert!(player_is_mpv("mpv"));
19448        assert!(!player_is_mpv(r"C:\Program Files\VideoLAN\VLC\vlc.exe"));
19449        assert!(!player_is_mpv(""));
19450
19451        let split = StreamTarget::SplitAv(vec![PathBuf::from("v"), PathBuf::from("a")]);
19452        assert!(playable_with(&split, r"C:\Progs\mpv\mpv.exe"));
19453        assert!(!playable_with(&split, r"C:\VLC\vlc.exe"));
19454        let single = StreamTarget::Growing(PathBuf::from("x.ts"));
19455        assert!(playable_with(&single, r"C:\VLC\vlc.exe"));
19456        assert!(playable_with(&StreamTarget::Finished(PathBuf::from("x.mkv")), ""));
19457    }
19458}
